NARRATIVE: ON 5/28/2026, EQT WAS CONDUCTING MOWING OPERATIONS OUTSIDE THE CALLISTO CS FENCELINE, NEAR THE PAJUPG075 (FKA NIJUS021) AND PAJUPG081 (FKA NIJUS029) RIGHT-OF-WAY. WHILE MOWING, THE EQT OPERATOR (EMPLOYEE “EE” #1) WAS INJURED BY SUDDEN FIRE. THE EQT OPERATOR ESCAPED FURTHER INJURY BY REMOVING HIMSELF FROM THE SCENE AND SEEKING HELP INSIDE THE CALLISTO CS OFFICE. THE INJURED OPERATOR INFORMED EE #2 OF WHAT HAPPENED.  AFTER ASSESSING THAT HIMSELF AND NO OTHER EES OR PUBLIC WERE IN IMMEDIATE DANGER, EE #2 CALLED THE 9-1-1 EMERGENCY CALL CENTER AT ~12:21PM. ADDITIONALLY, EE #2 INSTRUCTED A CONTRACTOR WHO WAS PRESENT TO CONTACT THE INJURED EE #1’S SUPERVISOR, EE #3, AS WELL AS EE #2’S SUPERVISOR (EE #4).  AT ~12:26PM, EE #2 CALLED MIDSTREAM GAS CONTROL (MGC) TO REPORT A FIRE NEAR PIPELINE PAJUPG081, REQUESTING THE ISOLATION OF THE PIPELINE. EE #2 ADVISED MGC THAT AN EE HAD SUSTAINED BURN INJURIES TO HIS HAND, AND THAT FIRE/EMS WAS ENROUTE TO THE SITE. MGC CONTACTED THE PRODUCTION CONTROL CENTER (PCC) AND REQUESTED THAT THE UPSTREAM CLEMENTE MM BE SHUT-IN. ADDITIONALLY, MGC SHUT DOWN COMPRESSOR UNIT #4 DOWNSTREAM OF THE SITE.  AT ~12:28PM, EE #4 CONTACTED THE EQT HOTLINE AND WAS CONNECTED WITH EE #5 FROM EHS AT 12:32PM, WHOM HE MADE AWARE OF THE SITUATION. AT ~12:33PM, MGC NOTIFIED EE #6 FROM MIDSTREAM COMPLIANCE AND SUPERINTENDENT EE #3. EE #3 ADVISED PIPELINE OPERATORS AND SUPERVISORS TO HEAD TO THE SITE.  TWO OPERATIONS SUPERVISORS (EES # 7 AND 8) ARRIVED ON-SITE AT ~12:45PM, WITH EE #7 SERVING AS THE OPERATIONS COORDINATOR FOR THE EVENT. AT APPROXIMATELY THE SAME TIME, EMERGENCY RESPONDERS ARRIVED ON-SITE. EE #7 ASSESSED THE SITUATION, AND INSTRUCTED THE FIRE CHIEF TO STAGE THEIR EQUIPMENT, DEPLOY HOSES, AND AWAIT FURTHER INSTRUCTION. THE FIRE CHIEF CONFIRMED THAT NO SUPPRESSION ACTIVITIES WOULD BE INITIATED UNTIL AUTHORIZATION WAS GIVEN. EMS PERSONNEL WERE DIRECTED TO THE CALLISTO CS OFFICE TO CARE FOR THE INJURED EE.  AT ~12:55PM, EE #7, WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF EE #8, BEGAN TO COORDINATE ON-SITE EFFORTS TO ISOLATE THE SOURCE OF THE LEAK, IN CONJUNCTION WITH MGC. EE #7 DIRECTED PIPELINE OPERATORS TO BEGIN CLOSING MULTIPLE VALVES ON PAJUPG081, PAJUPG075, AND PAJUPG086 (FKA NIJUS041) TO ISOLATE POTENTIAL SOURCES OF GAS. FACILITY OPERATORS INSIDE CALLISTO CS WERE INSTRUCTED TO CONFIGURE THE YARD SO THAT ANY PIPELINE BLOWDOWN OR STATION ESD BLOWDOWN WOULD OCCUR ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE STATION, OPPOSITE THE LOCATION OF THE LEAK. DURING THESE ACTIVITIES, MGC REMOTELY CLOSED THE HCA VALVE TO CLEMENTE MM AT ~1:00PM TO FURTHER ISOLATE THE SYSTEM. AT ~1:05PM, LIFE FLIGHT ARRIVED ON THE SCENE. THE FINAL VALVE WAS SHUT ON PAJUPG086 AT ~1:40PM.   FOLLOWING THE FINAL ISOLATION VALVE CLOSURE, EE #7 PERFORMED A FINAL EVALUATION OF THE SCENE FOR THE SAFE BLOWDOWN OF THE PIPELINE ASSETS. EE # 7 NOTED THAT WINDS WERE BLOWING SOUTHWARD, AWAY FROM THE LEAK SITE. WITH CONDITIONS BEING SAFE, THE BLOWDOWN OF PAJUPG075 AND PAJUPG081 COMMENCED AT THAT SOUTHERN END OF THE CALLISTO CS SITE.  EE #7 CONSULTED WITH THE FIRE CHIEF AND AUTHORIZED FIRE SUPPRESSION ACTIVITIES TO COMMENCE ONCE BOTH LINES WERE FULLY DEPRESSURIZED. AT ~1:58PM, BLOWDOWNS ON BOTH PIPELINES HAD COMPLETED. THE FIRE DEPARTMENT EXTINGUISHED THE FIRE WITH WATER.  AT ~3:16PM, COMPLIANCE ON-CALL WAS CONTACTED TO CONFIRM THAT THE INJURED EE WAS ADMITTED TO THE HOSPITAL AS AN IN-PATIENT. WITH CONFIRMED DISCOVERY OF A REPORTABLE INCIDENT, COMPLIANCE’S EE #6 NOTIFIED THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER OF THE INCIDENT AT ~3:25PM (NRC# 1463661).  EQT IS WORKING WITH A 3RD PARTY TO DETERMINE AN APPARENT CAUSE AND WILL PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT UPON COMPLETION OF THE INVESTIGATION.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 12, 2026, LINE D-600 EXPERIENCED AN UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS INTO THE ATMOSPHERE, RESULTING IN A MATERIAL LOSS EXCEEDING 3,000 MCF. LINE D-600 IS A TRANSMISSION PIPELINE OPERATED AND MAINTAINED BY COLUMBIA GAS TRANSMISSION, LLC (THE COMPANY), A TC ENERGY ENTITY, LOCATED IN PAULDING COUNTY, OHIO.  AT 11:50 EDT, COMPANY MONITORING CENTER RECEIVED NOTIFICATION FROM OHIO GAS COMPANY REGARDING A POTENTIAL RELEASE AND ARRIVED ON-SITE AT 12:12 EDT. SUBSEQUENT INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT THE RELEASE ORIGINATED FROM A DAMAGED 20-INCH PIPELINE CAUSED BY A THIRD PARTY DURING AGRICULTURAL TILING ACTIVITIES.  THE PRELIMINARY REPAIR COSTS WERE ESTIMATED TO EXCEED $150,000; THEREFORE, THE INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC). THE COMPANY AND LOCAL DISTRIBUTION COMPANY PERSONNEL TOOK ALL NECESSARY ACTIONS TO MAINTAIN GAS FLOW IN THE SYSTEM FOR AS LONG AS PRACTICABLE.  AT 17:30 EDT, LINE D-600 WAS ISOLATED. FOLLOWING COMPLETION OF THE REQUIRED ENGINEERING ASSESSMENT, A PRE-TESTED REPLACEMENT PIPE SEGMENT WAS INSTALLED. SERVICE TO LINE D-600 WAS SUCCESSFULLY RESTORED ON MAY 16, 2026, AT 09:39 EDT.  THE INITIAL NRC REPORT NO. 1462250 AND THE 48-HOUR NRC REPORT NO. 1462402 WERE SUBMITTED ON MAY 12, 2026, AND MAY 14, 2026, RESPECTIVELY.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 7, 2026, UNIT 8 AT STATION 4 IN DAMON, TEXAS, EXPERIENCED A RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS WHEN THE BLOWDOWN VALVE OPENED WHILE THE SUCTION AND SIDE-GATE VALVES REMAINED OPEN. THE EVENT WAS LIMITED TO THE AFFECTED UNIT AND DID NOT IMPACT THE OPERATION OF THE REMAINING COMPRESSOR STATION FACILITIES. A TOTAL OF 4,301 MCF OF NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED.  AT APPROXIMATELY 4:45 A.M., OPERATIONS PERSONNEL RECEIVED A CALLOUT REGARDING ABNORMAL VALVE OPERATION. PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 5:40 A.M. THE INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT AN ELECTRICAL SHORT CAUSED A FUSE FAILURE ON THE ANALOG OUTPUT CARD WITHIN THE CONTROL CIRCUIT. THE FAILED FUSE CAUSED THE BLOWDOWN VALVE TO OPEN AS DESIGNED, RESULTING IN THE RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS.

NARRATIVE: ON APRIL 8, 2026, AT APPROXIMATELY 12:30 CDT, WHILE THE NASH COMPRESSOR STATION WAS SHUT DOWN FOR ROUTINE MAINTENANCE, THE FUEL GAS SKID ALARMED ON HIGH PRESSURE. A COMMUNICATION/MEASUREMENT TECHNICIAN RESPONDED, ISOLATED THE VALVE SUPPLYING THE FUEL GAS SKID, AND BLED PRESSURE BELOW THE ALARM SET POINT. THE FUEL GAS SUPPLY REMAINED ISOLATED BECAUSE THE STATION WAS NOT OPERATING AT THE TIME.    THE AFFECTED BLOW-OFF VALVES ARE SPRING-ACTUATED, FAIL-OPEN VALVES HELD CLOSED BY CONTROL PRESSURE SOURCED FROM THE FUEL GAS HEADER. BETWEEN APPROXIMATELY APRIL 8 (MIDDAY) AND APRIL 10, 2026, AT APPROXIMATELY 04:00 CDT, PRESSURE IN THE ISOLATED FUEL GAS SKID GRADUALLY DECLINED FROM APPROXIMATELY 150 PSIG TO 25 PSIG. ONCE PRESSURE FELL BELOW THAT REQUIRED TO HOLD THE VALVES CLOSED, THE VALVES AUTOMATICALLY OPENED AS DESIGNED, VENTING GAS THROUGH THE ESD STACK.     AT APPROXIMATELY 04:44 CDT ON APRIL 10, 2026, SOUTHERN STAR CENTRAL GAS PIPELINE, INC. (SSCGP) GAS CONTROL RECEIVED SCADA ALARMS INDICATING THAT THE SUCTION, DISCHARGE, AND FUEL GAS EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD) BLOW-OFF VALVES AT THE NASH COMPRESSOR STATION HAD OPENED. A LOW-LOW PRESSURE ALARM ON THE GENERATOR FUEL GAS SUPPLY ACTIVATED SHORTLY THEREAFTER, AND DECREASED PRESSURE WAS OBSERVED ON THE MAINLINE.    SSCGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED AND ARRIVED ON SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 05:27 CDT, CONFIRMING NATURAL GAS WAS VENTING THROUGH THE ESD VENT STACK. THE AFFECTED BLOW-OFF VALVES WERE MANUALLY ISOLATED AND CLOSED AT APPROXIMATELY 05:48 CDT, STOPPING THE RELEASE. THE INITIAL NRC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE AT 06:23 CDT, WITH A FOLLOW-UP CALL AT 06:39 CDT TO PROVIDE LOCATION INFORMATION. BASED ON SYSTEM CALCULATIONS, APPROXIMATELY 6.3 MMCF OF NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED.    UPON ARRIVAL, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ISOLATED THE AFFECTED SYSTEMS, STOPPED VENTING, PURGED THE SYSTEM, AND RESET THE ESD CONTROLS. THE ESD SYSTEM WAS FULLY RESTORED TO SERVICE AT APPROXIMATELY 12:00 CDT ON APRIL 10, 2026.

NARRATIVE: AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT, THE KMTP UNIT 2 SUCTION AND DISCHARGE VALVES FAILED IN THEIR LAST COMMANDED STATE (OPEN), WHILE THE VENT VALVE FAILED TO ITS DESIGNED FAIL-SAFE POSITION (OPEN). THE STATION SUCTION AND DISCHARGE VALVES ALSO REMAINED OPEN. UPON OCCURRENCE OF THE UNIT 2 PROCESSOR MAJOR NON-RECOVERABLE FAULT (MNRF), THE LAST VALID PROCESS VALUES AND EQUIPMENT STATUSES WERE RETAINED IN BOTH THE STATION CONTROLLER AND SCADA SYSTEM, RESULTING IN UNIT 2 CONTINUING TO DISPLAY A “RUNNING” STATUS DESPITE THE LOSS OF PROCESSOR FUNCTIONALITY.  A SUBSEQUENT INSPECTION OF THE UNIT 2 PLC ENCLOSURE IDENTIFIED THE PRESENCE OF A RODENT WITHIN THE ENCLOSURE. EVIDENCE OF RODENT URINE CONTAMINATION WAS OBSERVED ON THE PLC HARDWARE AND ASSOCIATED ELECTRICAL COMPONENTS. THE CONTAMINATION CAUSED AN ELECTRICAL SHORT CIRCUIT, RESULTING IN COMMUNICATION FAILURES AND LOSS OF PROCESSOR FUNCTIONALITY. THE APPARENT ROOT CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS IMPROPER ENCLOSURE INSTALLATION AND SEALING, WHICH ALLOWED ENVIRONMENTAL AND WILDLIFE INTRUSION THROUGH UNSEALED OR INADEQUATELY SEALED ENCLOSURE OPENINGS.

NARRATIVE: ON THURSDAY, MARCH 19, 2026, AT APPROXIMATELY 07:52 HOURS, GAS DETECTION EQUIPMENT IN THE AUXILIARY BUILDING AT THE CONSUMERS ENERGY MUSKEGON RIVER COMPRESSOR STATION, PLANT 2, ACTIVATED AN ALARM. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL RESPONDED AND CONDUCTED AN INITIAL LEAK INVESTIGATION, CONFIRMING THE PRESENCE OF GAS IN THE PIT AREA OF THE AUXILIARY BUILDING. THE AREA WAS VENTILATED BY OPENING THE PIT AND AUXILIARY BUILDING DOORS. THE FACILITY IS LOCATED AT 8613 PINE ROAD, MARION, MICHIGAN, IN CLARE COUNTY.    GAS OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CONTINUED THE LEAK INVESTIGATION WHILE MONITORING SYSTEM CONDITIONS IN AND AROUND THE FACILITY. A REDUCTION IN SUCTION PRESSURE WAS OBSERVED, PROMPTING FURTHER EVALUATION OF THE 24-INCH SUCTION LINE. BAR HOLE TESTING IN THE AREA IDENTIFIED INDICATIONS OF GAS, AND THE 24-INCH SUCTION LINE WAS ISOLATED AND BLOWN DOWN TO ZERO AT APPROXIMATELY 20:00 HOURS.    LEAK INVESTIGATION ACTIVITIES CONTINUED OVER MULTIPLE DAYS AS ADDITIONAL GAS INDICATIONS WERE IDENTIFIED. PRESSURE WAS INTRODUCED TO THE 24-INCH SUCTION LINE TO AID LEAK DETECTION EFFORTS, WHICH LED TO GAS READINGS AT A BURIED DRIP LOG VESSEL ASSOCIATED WITH THE LINE. THE VESSEL WAS EXCAVATED AND REMOVED FROM SERVICE. INSPECTION IDENTIFIED MULTIPLE PINHOLE LEAKS ON THE UNDERSIDE OF THE VESSEL, WITH VISUAL OBSERVATIONS CONSISTENT WITH INTERNAL CORROSION.    TEMPORARY REMEDIATION INCLUDED REMOVAL OF THE AFFECTED DRIP LOG VESSEL AND ASSOCIATED PIPING TO ELIMINATE THE SOURCE OF THE GAS RELEASE. THE VESSEL WAS ISOLATED, REMOVED, AND REPLACED WITH 24-INCH STEEL PIPE, AND THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON APRIL 9, 2026. A NEW VESSEL WILL BE INSTALLED AS PART OF PLANNED PERMANENT CORRECTIVE ACTIONS PRIOR TO THE 2026 STORAGE WITHDRAWAL SEASON.    A REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE OF THE VESSEL WAS SUBMITTED TO THE CONSUMERS ENERGY LABORATORY FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. THE CATHODIC PROTECTION GROUP IS ALSO EVALUATING THE FINDINGS TO SUPPORT ROOT CAUSE DETERMINATION AND IDENTIFICATION OF CORRECTIVE AND PREVENTIVE ACTIONS.

NARRATIVE: ON 3/21/2026, EQT WAS COMPLETING A VOLUNTARY EMISSIONS FLYOVER AND DETECTED A SOURCE OF HIGH EMISSIONS OVER AN ASSET IN THE MORGAN TOWNSHIP, GREENE COUNTY, PA.  THE EQT MIDSTREAM CONTROL CENTER WAS NOTIFIED OF THIS AND DISPATCHED AN OPERATOR TO INVESTIGATE AT APPROXIMATELY 6:36PM.  EQT PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT THE SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 7:30PM AND FOUND TREE HAD FALLEN ON THE PRATT (LEWIS, M. #2 3635) STORAGE WELL.  PERSONNEL CONFIRMED THAT ABOVE GROUND EQUIPMENT WAS DAMAGED, AND GAS WAS ACTIVELY VENTING.  THE SITE WAS SECURED AND PERSONNEL BEGAN TO DETERMINE THE SOURCE OF THE LEAK.    THE PRATT 3635 STORAGE WELL HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN ISOLATED FROM THE PIPING SYSTEM VIA CLOSURE OF THE “C” VALVE AT THE WELL SITE.  IT WAS DETERMINED THAT GAS WAS VENTING FROM THE 4” HORIZONTAL RUN COMING FROM THE WELL HEAD DUE TO A CRACK THAT RESULTED FROM THE TREE’S IMPACT.  THE CRACK WAS LOCATED BETWEEN THE “C” VALVE AND “A” VALVE.  PERSONNEL WERE ABLE TO SUCCESSFULLY ISOLATE THE LEAK WITH THE CLOSURE OF THE “A” VALVE AT APPROXIMATELY 9:00PM.  EQT CONFIRMED THIS TO BE A REPORTABLE INCIDENT AT APPROXIMATELY 10:15PM.  AN INITIAL GAS LOSS ESTIMATE OF 12.8 MMSCF WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC (REPORT # 1457845) AT APPROXIMATELY 10:38PM.    ON 3/23/26, THE TREE WAS REMOVED FROM THE ABOVE GROUND EQUIPMENT.  IT WAS NOTED THAT THE RTU ON THE WELL WAS DAMAGED FROM THE FALLING TREE.  A REFINED GAS LOSS ESTIMATE OF 17.7 MMSCF WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC (REPORT #1458019) AS PART OF EQT’S 48-HOUR FOLLOW-UP CALL AT APPROXIMATELY 9:00PM ON 3/23/26.  AT APPROXIMATELY 10:45PM, THE 1ST TEMPORARY PLUG WAS SET IN THE WELL.  AT APPROXIMATELY 1:45PM ON 3/24/26, THE 2ND TEMPORARY PLUG WAS SET IN THE WELL, FOLLOWED BY A LAYER OF CONCRETE.    THE LAST RELIABLE SIGNAL FROM THE RTU ON THE WELL WAS AT 8:01PM ON 3/13/26, SHOWING 158.26 PSIG.  EQT BELIEVES THE DAMAGE TO THE RTU OCCURRED AT APPROXIMATELY THIS TIME FROM THE FALLEN TREE.  THE RTU EXISTS FOR STORAGE MODELING PURPOSES ONLY.  CRITICAL POINTS IN THE SCADA SYSTEM EXIST DOWNSTREAM OF THE “C” VALVE, WHICH HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN SHUT, ISOLATING THE WELL FROM THE DOWNSTREAM SYSTEM.  EQT HAS CONCLUDED THAT THE APPARENT CAUSE OF THE FAILURE IS NATURAL FORCE DAMAGE FROM HIGH WINDS CAUSING THE TREE TO FALL ON THE ABOVE GROUND EQUIPMENT.    SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT SUBMITTED TO INCORPORATE ESTIMATED COSTS AND KNOWN ABOVE GRADE PIPING COMPONENT ATTRIBUTES.

NARRATIVE: ON FEBRUARY 11, 2026, AT APPROXIMATELY 06:54, A CONSUMERS ENERGY GAS CONTROLLER RECEIVED A LOW LOW TEMPERATURE ALARM AT THE MARION CITY GATE DEHYDRATION UNIT LOCATED AT 19323 N. 5TH STREET, MARION, MICHIGAN (OSCEOLA COUNTY). THE GAS OPERATIONS STORAGE SUPERVISOR WAS NOTIFIED AND DISPATCHED A STORAGE MECHANIC TO THE SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 07:01.    UPON ARRIVAL, THE MECHANIC OBSERVED AN ACTIVE FIRE AT THE DEHYDRATION BUILDING, NOTIFIED THE SUPERVISOR, AND REQUESTED ADDITIONAL RESOURCES TO ISOLATE THE PIPELINE SEGMENT SUPPLYING THE UNIT. THE AFFECTED SEGMENT WAS ISOLATED FROM LATERAL 50 BY CLOSING VALVES. THE FIRE CONTINUED DURING PIPELINE DEPRESSURIZATION AND WAS SUBSEQUENTLY EXTINGUISHED. THE MARION FIRE DEPARTMENT RESPONDED AND COOLED RESIDUAL HOT SPOTS TO ALLOW A PRELIMINARY SITE EVALUATION TO BE PERFORMED. THERE WERE NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES AS A RESULT OF THE INCIDENT.    FOLLOWING ISOLATION AND COMPLETION OF A SAFETY EVALUATION, STORAGE EMPLOYEES INSTALLED A TEMPORARY BYPASS LINE TO MAINTAIN SERVICE TO THE VILLAGE OF MARION AND MITIGATE CUSTOMER IMPACT TO APPROXIMATELY 522 RESIDENTIAL AND COMMERCIAL CUSTOMERS. DUE TO THE SEVERITY OF THE EVENT AND THE POTENTIAL CUSTOMER IMPACT, AN INCIDENT COMMAND STRUCTURE (ICS) WAS ESTABLISHED. AS CONDITIONS EVOLVED, A DECISION WAS MADE TO ISOLATE THE CITY GATE INLET AND DISCONTINUE SERVICE TO THE VILLAGE OF MARION AS A PRECAUTIONARY MEASURE.     AFTER THE SERVICE INTERRUPTION, CONSUMERS ENERGY IMPLEMENTED A COORDINATED RESTORATION PLAN TO SAFELY RESTORE SERVICE TO IMPACTED CUSTOMERS OVER THE NEXT SEVERAL DAYS. CREWS FOLLOWED ESTABLISHED SAFETY PROCEDURES, AND COORDINATION WITH LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS AND MUNICIPAL OFFICIALS WAS MAINTAINED TO SUPPORT RESTORATION ACTIVITIES. CUSTOMERS WERE NOTIFIED THROUGH ESTABLISHED COMMUNICATION CHANNELS, AND RESOURCES WERE DEPLOYED TO PROVIDE ASSISTANCE AND MINIMIZE INCONVENIENCE RESULTING FROM THE SERVICE DISRUPTION.    IN PARALLEL WITH RESTORATION EFFORTS, A ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION TEAM IS CURRENTLY WORKING TO EVALUATE THE EVENT, IDENTIFY CONTRIBUTING FACTORS, AND DEVELOP CORRECTIVE ACTIONS TO PREVENT RECURRENCE.

NARRATIVE: ON 2/3/2026 AT APPROXIMATELY 5:24PM LOCAL TIME AN UNEXPLAINED PRESSURE DECREASE OCCURRED. LOCAL OPERATIONS HEARD A LOUD NOISE AND WENT OUTSIDE OF THE FIELD OFFICE TO ASSESS WHAT MAY HAVE OCCURRED. AT 5:32PM LOCAL OPERATIONS VISUALLY CONFIRMED A FAILURE OCCURRED AND THE STATION WAS ESD AT 5:34PM. AT 5:55PM MANUAL ISOLATION VALVES LEAVING THE STATION WERE ISOLATED. ISOLATION VALVE ON LINE 300 WAS NOT HOLDING AT THE STATION AND VALVE WAS LEAKING BY FROM THE MAINLINE TO THE STATION. PERMANENT HARDENING SEALANT WAS INJECTED INTO THE LINE 300 LEAKING VALVE AND WAS SUCCESSFUL IN HOLDING PRESSURE. SOUTHERN PINES ENERGY CENTER (SPEC) WAS FULLY BLOWDOWN TO ATMOSPHERE ON 2/4/2026 APPROXIMATELY 6:00PM.     THE LOCATION OF FAILURE WAS SPECIFIC TO THE 2 INCH LOW POINT DRAIN COMING OFF OF LINE 300 AT THE 6 O CLOCK POSITION WHICH CAUSED DAMAGE TO THE LINE 400 2" INCH DRAIN WHEN THE FAILURE OCCURRED. NOTE: DAMAGE WAS LOCALIZED TO THE LOW POINT DRAIN AND THE PARENT (CARRIER) LINE PIPE WAS NOT DAMAGED, BUT OPTED FOR A FULL REMOVAL AS A CYLINDER AND REPLACEMENT WITH STRAIGHT PIPE, FULLY REMOVING THE LOW POINT DRAIN AT THESE LOCATIONS.      DNV ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS CONCLUDED 3 CASUAL FACTORS: LOW POINT DRAIN DESIGN, MONITORING OF LOW POINT DESIGN, AND MECHANICAL / CHEMICAL MITIGATION OF LOW POINT.     PREVENTIVE MEASURES TAKEN TO AVOID REPEATED ISSUE IN THE FUTURE: 1) REMOVAL OF 7 LOW POINT DRAINS THAT WERE ASSOCIATED WITH LOW VOLUME / UNNECESSARY DEAD LEG 2) MONITORING OF LOW POINT DRAINS THROUGH CORROSION COUPON AT APPLICABLE SAMPLE LOCATION AND ADDING A UT SENSOR AT A COMMON HEADER LOCATION FOR MONITORING OF WALL THICKNESS OF PIPE AND 3) DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF AN INTERNAL CORROSION PROGRAM TO MITIGATE CONDITIONS OF POSSIBLE INTERNAL CORROSION.

THE CAUSE OF THE ESD BEING INCOMPLETE WAS DUE TO THE ESD ALARM SIGNAL IN THE ESD PANEL BEING AN UNLATCHED ALARM. THE UNLATCHING ALARM WAS MOVED TO A LATCHING ALARM PRIOR TO THE STATION BEING PUT BACK IN-SERVICE, AS WELL AS A THOROUGH POINT-TO POINT ON THE ANNUNCIATING SIGNALS.    KEWASKUM COMPRESSOR STATION WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE ON DECEMBER 27, 2025, AT 2:00 PM CST.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE INCIDENT WAS NOT DUE TO CONTROLLER OR CONTROL ROOM ACTIONS, AS OPERATING PRESSURES REMAINED WITHIN ALLOWABLE LIMITS AND DID NOT EXCEED MAOP.
NARRATIVE: AT 6:23 P.M. ON DECEMBER 10, 2025, A CONTRACTOR WAS NOTIFIED OF A POSSIBLE PIPELINE INCIDENT BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC. THE CONTRACTOR CALLED THE OPERATOR, AND THE OPERATOR CHECKED LINE PRESSURE IN SCADA TO INVESTIGATE LEAK REPORT. OPERATOR IDENTIFIED UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES BASED ON APPROXIMATE LOCATION REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC. AT 6:34 P.M., OPERATOR CONTACTED THE CONTROL ROOM AND INSTRUCTED THEM TO REMOTELY CLOSE THE UPSTREAM VALVE DUE TO A POTENTIAL LINE BREAK AND STARTED DRIVE DOWNSTREAM MANUAL VALVE LOCATION. CONTROL ROOM PERSONNEL EXECUTED THE COMMAND AND CONFIRMED THE UPSTREAM VALVE WAS SUCCESSFULLY CLOSED. FOLLOWING VALVE CLOSURE, LINE PRESSURE DROPPED SIGNIFICANTLY, AND UPSTREAM PRESSURE WAS CONTINUOUSLY MONITORED. AT 7:34 P.M., OPERATOR PERSONNEL CLOSED THE MANUAL DOWNSTREAM VALVE TO FURTHER ISOLATE THE AFFECTED SEGMENT. OPERATOR ARRIVED AT THE INCIDENT SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 8:00 P.M. TO CONDUCT A PRELIMINARY ON-SITE EVALUATION AND BEGIN PIPELINE REPAIRS. AT 9:01 P.M., THE OPERATOR DETERMINED THE EVENT MET THE PHMSA AND RAILROAD COMMISSION OF TEXAS (RRC) IMMEDIATE NOTICE REPORTING THRESHOLDS (CONFIRMED DISCOVERY) DUE TO AN UNINTENTIONAL LOSS EXCEEDING 3 MMCF. CREWS MOBILIZED TO THE SITE TO CUT OUT AND REPLACE FAILED SECTION OF PIPELINE. ON DECEMBER 16, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 7:00 P.M., REPAIRS WERE COMPLETED AND THE PIPELINE WAS PURGED. THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON DECEMBER 17, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 5:00 A.M.  FOLLOWING THE INCIDENT, THE FAILED PIPE SEGMENT WAS REMOVED AND SENT TO BAKER RISK FOR A METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. THE FINAL REPORT IS EXPECTED BY THE END OF JANUARY, AND A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED WITH INFORMATION ON THE CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT.  2/3/2026 SUPPLEMENTAL - THE METALLURGICAL LAB REPORT FROM BAKER RISK WAS RECEIVED ON 1/21/2026. IT WAS DETERMINED EXTERNAL CORROSION WAS THE CAUSE OF THE FAILURE. THE FOLLOWING SUMMARIZES THE CONCLUSIONS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS REPORT:  1. THE PIPELINE WAS DAMAGED BY PITTING CORROSION.  2. THE PITTING CORROSION WAS LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF THE 6 O’CLOCK POSITION ON THE PIPELINE.  3. THE PITTING CORROSION AREAS COINCIDED WITH THE LONGITUDINAL WELD SEAM STARTING ~36 INCHES DOWNSTREAM OF GIRTH WELD 12460 OVER A LENGTH OF ~84 INCHES.  4. THE WELD SEAM WAS PREFERENTIALLY ATTACKED WITHIN THE PITTED AREA.  5. THE RUPTURE INITIATED DUE TO WALL LOSS AT THE INITIATION SITE.  6. ONCE INITIATED, THE RUPTURE PROPAGATED BY DUCTILE OVERLOAD FAILURE.  7. THE PIPELINE RUPTURE FOLLOWED THE WELD SEAM IN THE JOINT BETWEEN GIRTH WELD 12460 AND GIRTH WELD 12470.  8. THE RUPTURE PROPAGATED IN A DUCTILE MANNER BOTH UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM FROM THE ORIGIN, TERMINATING ~ 4-FEET UPSTREAM OF GIRTH WELD 12460 AND JUST BEYOND GIRTH WELD 12470 ON THE DOWNSTREAM END.  9. THE ILI DATA IS GENERALLY CONSISTENT WITH THE PRESENT OBSERVATIONS AND MEASUREMENTS BUT UNDERESTIMATED THE SEVERITY OF THE PITTING. AT LEAST 56% WALL LOSS WAS PHYSICALLY MEASURED, WHEREAS THE ILI DATA ESTIMATED A MAXIMUM LOSS OF 39%.  NOTE: CAPROCK ACQUIRED THIS PIPELINE ON OCTOBER 1, 2025, AND IMMEDIATELY INITIATED A 5-WEEK REMEDIATION PROJECT (10/15/2025-11/20/2025) BASED ON THE RESULTS OF THE PREVIOUS OWNERS ILI RUN IN OCTOBER 2024.  AS A CORRECTIVE ACTION, CAPROCK HAS SCHEDULED ANOTHER ILI RUN ON THIS PIPELINE FOR THE WEEK OF FEBRUARY 9TH, 2026, AND WILL REVIEW AND ADDRESS DEFECTS OR ANOMALIES IN ACCORDANCE WITH 49 CFR 192.485(A)-(B).

AN OPERATOR RESPONDED TO EAST FORK COMPRESSOR STATION FOR AN EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD). A SECOND OPERATOR WAS DISPATCHED TO ISOLATE THE PIPELINE AT A NEARBY BLOCK VALVE. THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED, AND OPERATIONS CONFIRMED THE RELEASE LOCATION. THE AREA AROUND THE POINT OF RELEASE WAS BARRICADED TO PREVENT ENTRY. WE DEEMED A REPORTABLE EVENT HAD OCCURRED.  THE DAMAGED PIPE MATERIAL WAS SENT FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. THERE WERE REPAIRS MADE TO THE AFFECTED SECTION. THE PIPELINE OPERATING PRESSURE WAS DE-RATED PENDING ANALYSIS RESULTS. A SUPPLEMENTAL WILL BE SUBMITTED WAS ONCE RESULTS FROM THE INVESTIGATION HAVE BEEN RECEIVED AND REVIEWED.     SUPPLEMENTAL- THE RESULTS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSES REVEALED THAT THE IN-SERVICE FAILURE WAS ASSOCIATED WITH LATENT MECHANICAL DAMAGE ON THE PIPE BODY. THE FAILURE ORIGINATED WITHIN A GOUGE THAT WAS 1.7 FEET LONG AT AN ORIENTATION OF 7:30. SHEAR CRACKS DEVELOPED IN THE GOUGE WHICH ULTIMATELY CAUSED THE FAILURE. THE SOURCE OF THE GOUGE IS UNKNOWN.

NARRATIVE: AT 0551 HOURS, NOVEMBER 9, 2025, THE MOWER COUNTY SHERIFF’S DEPARTMENT REPORTED A RELIEF VALVE VENTING GAS AT THE LA CROSSE BRANCH LINE REGULATOR STATION (MNR-0102) NEAR WALTHAM, MINNESOTA. AT 0559 HOURS, A LOW LOW INLET PRESSURE AT THE LACROSSE MAOP REGULATOR STATION WAS IDENTIFIED BY GAS CONTROLLERS.    TECHNICIANS ARRIVED ON SITE AT 0700 HOURS AND CONFIRMED THE RELIEF VALVE WAS NO LONGER VENTING. FIRST RESPONDERS INFORMED THE TECHNICIANS THAT THE RELIEF VALVE VENTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES AND THEN STOPPED.    TECHNICIANS ISOLATED THE RELIEF VALVE AND BEGAN TROUBLESHOOTING. DURING INSPECTION OF THE PILOT, ICE BUILT UP WAS DISCOVERED IN THE TOP HALF OF THE PILOT ASSEMBLY. AFTER CLEANING, TECHNICIANS PUT ALL EQUIPMENT BACK TOGETHER AND TESTED THE RELIEF TO ENSURE PROPER SETPOINT. AT 0850 HOURS, NOVEMBER 9, 2025, THE RELIEF VALVE WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE.

NARRATIVE: DURING THE PURGING OPERATION OF A 16-INCH OFFSHORE PIPELINE (40-B-03) SCHEDULED FOR ABANDONMENT, A 2-INCH TEMPORARY HOSE USED FOR GAS VENTING BECAME DISCONNECTED FROM THE 16-INCH LINE APPROXIMATELY TWO HOURS INTO THE CONTROLLED PURGE. THIS DISCONNECTION RESULTED IN UNCONTROLLED UNDERWATER VENTING FOR AN ESTIMATED FIVE ADDITIONAL HOURS.  THE VALVE AND TEMPORARY HOSE ASSEMBLY WERE SENT TO A LABORATORY FOR DETAILED FAILURE ANALYSIS. THE ANALYSIS DETERMINED THAT THE 2-INCH NIPPLE EXPERIENCED AN AXIAL LOAD COMBINED WITH BENDING FORCES. THESE FORCES CAUSED OVALIZATION OF THE NIPPLE CONNECTION AND ULTIMATELY LED TO SHEARING OF THE THREADED CONNECTION.  THE PIPELINE (40-B-03) HAD SHUT DOWN AND ISOLATED SINCE OCTOBER.

PRELIMINARY INDICATIONS WERE A BATTERY CHARGER FAILED. FURTHER INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THE PRIMARY CAUSE OF THE ESD WAS THE FAULT ON THE SAFETY PLC PROCESSOR. A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR WAS BOTH THE SUCTION AND DISCHARGE VALVE POWER GAS PILOT VALVES WERE IN THE CLOSED POSITION NOT ALLOWING THE VALVES TO CLOSE. NO EMERGENCY RESPONDERS WERE INVOLVED. NO CUSTOMERS WERE AFFECTED. THE ARIZONA CORPORATION COMMISSION MADE AN ANNOUNCED VISIT TO FLAGSTAFF AREA OFFICE AND WILLIAMS STATION ON 11/3/2025 TO INVESTIGATE THE EVENT.

NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 22, 2025, AT 6:30 A.M. WHILE INVESTIGATING LINE SHUT-INS AND COMPRESSOR SHUTDOWNS DUE TO HIGH O2 ISSUES AN OPERATIONS (OPS) TECH OBSERVED THAT THE HEADLEE DISCHARGE LINE HAD 40 PSIG WHEN THE NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURE IS 1250 PISIG. THE EMPLOYEE NOTIFIED THE OPS SUPERVISOR WHO THEN VERIFIED THE LINE PRESSURE WITH ANOTHER OPS TECH LOCATED AT THE LUPIN STATION. AT 6:40 A.M. THE OPS TEAM BEGAN A LEAK INVESTIGATION ON THE 7406642 - LUPIN TO HEADLEE. AT 09:37 A.M. FIELD PERSONNEL PINPOINT THE LOCATION THE LEAK. THE LINE WAS SHUT IN, AND FIELD PERSONNEL BEGAN TO INVESTIGATE THE SEVERITY OF THE LEAK. AT 11:15 A.M. OPERATIONS MADE AN INITIAL DETERMINATION THAT THE LEAK MAY EXCEED THE REPORTABLE THRESHOLD AND NOTIFIED DOT COMPLIANCE. THE NRC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE AT 12:06 P.M. TXRRC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE AT 12:15 P.M. CONFIRMED DISCOVERY WAS AT 9:39 P.M. WHEN OPERATIONS DETERMINED THE VOLUME LOST TO BE 7.784 MMCF.     ON NOVEMBER 25, 2025, ACUREN INSPECTION INC. COMPLETED THE METALLURGICAL TESTING. THE TESTS CONCLUDED:    1. THE FAILURE COINCIDED WITH A REGION OF INTERNAL CORROSION PRESENT ALONG THE TOP OF THE PIPE. THE INTERNAL CORROSION OCCURRED IN A MESA-LIKE PATTERN WITH SMALL, CIRCULAR PITS.   2. THE OBSERVED INTERNAL CORROSION OCCURRED IN AN ENVIRONMENT CONTAINING CARBON DIOXIDE AND WATER, COMBINING TO FORM CARBONIC ACID RESULTING IN SIDERITE.   3. THE PIPE WAS CONSISTENT WITH NOMINAL 12-INCH OD X 0.250-INCH WT, API 5L GRADE X52 MATERIAL MANUFACTURED VIA THE HF-ERW PROCESS WITH A POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT.     ENERGY TRANSFER'S INTERNAL INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THAT THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS LIQUID/WATER CONTENT FROM PRODUCERS ENTERING THE PIPELINE.     PLAN TO PREVENT RECURRENCE:  IMPLEMENT A CLEANING PIG SCHEDULE TO SWEEP ANY LIQUID BUILD UP IN THE PIPELINE AND A CORROSION TREATMENT/MONITORING PROGRAM.

NARRATIVE: ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 16, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 20:30, CHANDELEUR PIPE LINE, (THIRD COAST), WAS CONTACTED BY THE UNITED STATES COAST GUARD REGARDING ALLEGED BUBBLES WITH NO SHEEN ON THE SURFACE OF THE WATER IN BLOCK 42 OF THE BRENTON SOUND AREA IN THE GULF OF AMERICA NEAR THE CHANDELEUR 16” NATURAL GAS PIPELINE AND A NATURAL GAS PIPELINE OWNED AND OPERATED BY ENBRIDGE. THIRD COAST INITIATED ITS INVESTIGATION REGARDING THE NRC REPORT. FOLLOWING THE INITIAL INVESTIGATION, THIRD COAST DETERMINED THE REPORTED NATURAL GAS RELEASE DID NOT APPEAR TO ORIGINATE FROM THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE BECAUSE OF THE LACK OF ANY MATERIAL REDUCTIONS IN PRESSURE AND NO APPARENT OPERATIONAL ABNORMALITIES ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. THIRD COAST THEN CONTACTED THE USCG TO RELAY THIRD COAST’S CONCLUSION THAT ITS INITIAL INVESTIGATION DID NOT APPEAR TO INDICATE THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE WAS THE SOURCE OF THE REPORTED NATURAL GAS RELEASE. ON SATURDAY, OCTOBER 18, THIRD COAST WAS CONTACTED BY ENBRIDGE, WHICH STATED THAT, DURING ITS INVESTIGATION RELATED TO THE NRC REPORT, ENBRIDGE DETERMINED THE SOURCE OF THE REPORTED NATURAL GAS RELEASE WAS LIKELY TO BE THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. IN RESPONSE, THIRD COAST CONTACTED ALL PRODUCERS ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE TO INITIATE SHUT-IN PROCEDURES OF THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE AND TO DIVERT FLOWS OF NATURAL GAS FROM THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE TO ALTERNATE PIPELINES. ON SUNDAY, OCTOBER 19, AT APPROXIMATELY 04:00, THIRD COAST COMPLETED THE SHUT-IN OF THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. THIRD COAST PERFORMED A 10-HR. STATIC PRESSURE TEST ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE THAT WAS COMPLETED ON SUNDAY AFTERNOON. THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE HELD PRESSURE WITH NO PRESSURE DROPS FOR THE FULL DURATION OF THE STATIC PRESSURE TEST. FOLLOWING THE COMPLETION OF THE STATIC PRESSURE TEST, THIRD COAST NOTIFIED USCG AND BSEE TO PROVIDE THE RESULTS OF THE STATIC PRESSURE TEST AND RESTARTED THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 21, BSEE CONTACTED THIRD COAST REGARDING ONGOING BUBBLES ON THE SURFACE OF THE WATER IN VICINITY OF THE LOCATION IDENTIFIED IN THE NRC REPORT. IN RESPONSE, THIRD COAST SHUT-IN THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. DUE TO ADVERSE WEATHER AND SEA CONDITIONS, THIRD COAST WAS UNABLE TO RETURN TO THE LOCATION OF THE REPORTED ONGOING NATURAL GAS RELEASE UNTIL MONDAY, OCTOBER 27. ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 27, DIVERS IDENTIFIED NATURAL GAS SEEPING FROM THE CONCRETE COATING ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 10 FT FROM A JUMPER LINE CONNECTION BETWEEN THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE AND A PIPELINE TEXAS EASTERN TRANSMISSION PIPELINE (“TETCO”). THE DIVERS CLOSED THE JUMPER LINE VALVE TO FULLY ISOLATE THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE AND BEGAN TO REMOVE THE CONCRETE COATING TO CONFIRM THE LOCATION. THE DIVING VESSEL WAS FORCED TO LEAVE LOCATION DUE TO WEATHER BUT WAS ABLE TO RETURN THE FOLLOWING MORNING ON OCTOBER 28. ON OCTOBER 28, THE DIVERS COMPLETED THE REMOVAL OF THE CONCRETE COATING ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE AND CONFIRMED THE LOCATION OF THE SEEPING NATURAL GAS AT THE 6 O’CLOCK POSITION OF THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE. ON OCTOBER 28, AT APPROXIMATELY 11:16, THIRD COAST PLACED A CALL TO THE NRC IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE NRC SYSTEM WAS DOWN, AND NO REPORTS COULD BE TAKEN OR GENERATED. ON OCTOBER 28, AT APPROXIMATELY 16:35 , THIRD COAST PLACED ANOTHER CALL TO THE NRC (NRC# 1446522). ON OCTOBER 30, THIRD COAST PROVIDED THE NRC WITH A 48-HOUR UPDATE (NRC# 1446736). ON OCTOBER 31, THIRD COAST INSTALLED A PLIDCO CLAMP AT THE LOCATION OF THE SEEPING NATURAL GAS ON THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE, AND THE VALVES WERE THEN OPENED, AND THIRD COAST PERFORMED A LEAK TEST. THIRD COAST DID NOT OBSERVE ANY FURTHER SEEPAGE OF NATURAL GAS FROM THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE DURING THE LEAK TEST. THIRD COAST THEN INSTALLED SANDBAGS AND CONCRETE MATS ON THE AREA OF THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE AT ISSUE. APPROXIMATELY 784 MCF OF NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED FROM THE CHANDELEUR PIPELINE.

NARRATIVE: COLUMBIA GAS TRANSMISSION LLC (COLUMBIA), A TC ENERGY ENTITY, OPERATES AND MAINTAINS FORREST HILLS REGULATOR STATION LOCATED IN BROOKSVILLE, KENTUCKY. ON TUESDAY OCTOBER 14TH, 2025, 3RD PARTY NDE TECHNICIAN (CONTRACTOR), AND A COLUMBIA M&R TECHNICIAN WERE HAND PREPPING CORRODED SURFACE AREAS OF A 1-INCH DIAMETER FITTING FOR EVALUATION. DURING HAND PREPARATION THE FITTING BEGAN LEAKING. COLUMBIA'S AREA OPERATIONS MANAGER WAS CONTACTED AND INSTRUCTED THE COLUMBIA M&R TECHNICIAN TO EVACUATE THE BUILDING AND LEAVE THE DOORS OPEN FOR VENTING. A COLUMBIA PIPELINE TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ON SITE TO PERFORM LEL READINGS AROUND THE BUILDING. AFTER ASSESSING THE EVENT, CONFIRMING THAT NO GAS WAS BEING DETECTED BEYOND THE METER BUILDING, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT POTENTIAL IMPACTS TO RESIDENTIAL LIFE AND PROPERTY DID NOT WARRANT RESIDENTIAL EVACUATION AT THE TIME. THE FACILITY WAS MANNED AROUND THE CLOCK WITH HOURLY INSTRUMENTED LEAK SWEEPS FROM OCTOBER14TH TO 19TH. THE LEAK WAS STABLE WITH CORRECTIVE ACTION INCLUDING IMPLEMENTATION OF A PRESSURE REDUCTION TO 320 PSIG. THE LEAK SOURCE WAS IDENTIFIED AT THE SIDE WALL OF THE 1-INCH DIAMETER FITTING. A ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION IS UNDERWAY. THE FITTING HAS BEEN REPAIRED, AND THE FACILITY WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON OCTOBER 19TH, 2025.

NARRATIVE: THE PUTNAM WELL CONNECT INCIDENT OCCURRED ON OCTOBER 8, 2025, WHEN A LEAK DEVELOPED ON A 2014-ERA X52 STEEL GATHERING LINE IN NORTHEAST OHIO. THE FAILURE WAS IDENTIFIED AT A LOW POINT IN THE PIPELINE, WHERE A THROUGH-WALL PIT (1/4” PINHOLE) ALLOWED NATURAL GAS AND NATURAL GAS LIQUIDS TO BE RELEASED WHICH MIGRATED TOWARDS A DRY TRIBUTARY. THE IMMEDIATE RESPONSE INCLUDED ISOLATING AND BLOWING DOWN THE LINE, DEPLOYING CONTAINMENT AND VACUUM TRUCKS, AND INITIATING SOIL REMEDIATION AND ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING.    THE INVESTIGATION IS ON-GOING, HOWEVER PRELIMINARILY THE MOST PROBABLE ROOT CAUSE WAS INTERNAL CORROSION, LIKELY MICROBIAL-INDUCED CORROSION (MIC). ON DECEMBER 13, 2025, A SMART TOOL (ILI) WAS RUN ON THE PUTMAN LATERAL AND IDENTIFIED FIVE TOTAL AREAS, INCLUDING THE FAILURE SEGMENT, TOTALING APPROXIMATELY 1,000 FEET OF PIPELINE THAT WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED IN APRIL/MAY 2026. THE 3-FOOT SECTION CONTAINING THE FAILURE LOCATION WAS SENT TO ACUREN IN MAGNOLIA, TEXAS FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS ON MAY 15, 2026. THE PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK IN SERVICE ON JUNE 12, 2026.

NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 5TH, 2025 AT 12:10 P.M. GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A CALL FROM A LANDOWNER REPORTING A LEAK IN AN ET ROW. OPERATIONS WAS DISPATCHED AT 12:15PM AND CONFIRMED AT 2:31 P.M. THAT THE LINE WAS OUR 1090 SUGAR VALLEY PIPELINE. THE LINE WAS ISOLATED BY VALVE CLOSURE BY 3:56 P.M.    THE NEXT MORNING OCTOBER 6TH 2025 AT 11:22 A.M. CALCULATIONS CONFIRMED THE INCIDENT TO BE REPORTABLE, DUE TO THE GAS LOSS EXCEEDING THE REPORTING THRESHOLD.   PERMANENT REPAIRS (PIPE REPLACEMENT) WERE COMPLETED BY OCTOBER 10TH 2025. THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON OCTOBER 24TH, 2025 AT 2:00 P.M. THE PIPE HAS BEEN SENT TO A LAB WHERE THE CAUSE IS PENDING THE RESULTS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS.    **THE RESULTS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS DETERMINED THAT THE CAUSE WAS FROM AN ORIGINAL MANUFACTURING DEFECT IN THE LAP SEAM WELD.    TO PREVENT THIS FROM REOCCURRING THE 1090 SUGAR VALLEY 12IN FIELD WILL UNDERGO A PERMANENT MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE OPERATING PRESSURE (MAOP) REDUCTION TO TWO-HUNDRED AND FIFTY (250) PSIG.  THE ORIGINAL MAOP WAS ESTABLISHED AT FIVE-HUNDRED (500) PSI; FOLLOWING THE RUPTURE EVENT, THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE AT A TEMPORARY MAOP OF THREE-HUNDRED AND SIXTY (360) PSIG. AFTER FURTHER COMMERCIAL ANALYSIS, IT WAS DETERMINED THE MAOP COULD BE FURTHER REDUCED TO 250 PSIG. THIS MITIGATIVE MEASURE TO PERMANENTLY REDUCE WILL DECREASE THE MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE TENSILE HOOP STRESS THAT THIS PIPE SEGMENT MAY EXPERIENCE, WHICH WILL REDUCE BY HALF THE RISK OF INTERNAL PRESSURE THAT COULD AFFECT THE PIPE SEAM.

NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 4 AT APPROXIMATELY 09:50, CONSUMERS ENERGY GAS TRANSMISSION EMPLOYEES WERE EXCAVATING TO EXPOSE A WELLHEAD FOR ANNULAR MONITORING WHEN THEY INADVERTENTLY STRUCK AN ABOVE-GRADE CASING VALVE AND NIPPLE. THIS CONTACT CAUSED A LEAK AT THE NIPPLE/WELLHEAD INTERFACE OF CRANBERRY LAKE WELL C-243, LOCATED IN SUMMERFIELD TOWNSHIP, CLARE COUNTY, MICHIGAN. INSPECTION REVEALED THAT THE CASING VALVE AND NIPPLE WERE COMPROMISED AT THE CASING HEAD.    THE SITE WAS IMMEDIATELY SECURED, AND PERSONNEL ENSURED PUBLIC SAFETY BY MAINTAINING A SAFE PERIMETER. FOLLOWING INTERNAL ASSESSMENT, THE SITUATION WAS CLASSIFIED AS A LEVEL ONE WELL CONTROL EVENT, WITH CONTINGENCY PLANS IN PLACE TO ESCALATE TO LEVEL TWO IF NECESSARY.    A THIRD-PARTY CONTRACTOR WAS ENGAGED TO SAFELY KILL THE WELL BY PUMPING BRINE INTO THE TUBING TO EQUALIZE PRESSURE AND STOP GAS FLOW. ONCE THE WELL WAS CONFIRMED TO BE SAFELY KILLED, THE DAMAGED COMPONENTS WERE REMOVED, THE CASING HEAD THREADS WERE VISUALLY INSPECTED, AND A NEW VALVE AND NIPPLE WERE INSTALLED. THE WELL REMAINED OUT OF SERVICE PENDING INTEGRITY VERIFICATION AND FOLLOW-UP WORK.    FOLLOWING THE WELL CONTROL EVENT, WELL C 243 WAS REMEDIATED THROUGH THE ACTIONS OUTLINED BELOW. THE WELLSITE WAS SECURED, PROPERLY BACKFILLED, AND SUPPORTED FOR THE CE WORKOVER RIG. ONCE THE RIG ARRIVED ON LOCATION, STANDARD WELL KILL OPERATIONS WERE CONDUCTED; HOWEVER, THE WELL HAD ALREADY BEEN KILLED DURING THE INITIAL CONTROL EFFORTS. BLOW OUT PREVENTERS (BOP) WERE INSTALLED, AND PRESSURE TESTING WAS COMPLETED. THE TUBING WAS THEN REMOVED AND THE WELLBORE WAS ISOLATED.    ADDITIONAL SURFACE LEVEL NDT METHODS AND EXAMINATIONS WERE PERFORMED TO VERIFY COMPONENT INTEGRITY. THE WELLSITE WAS SUBSEQUENTLY EXCAVATED FOR INSTALLATION OF THE ANNULUS MONITORING PORT. THE RIG THEN REMOVED THE WELLBORE PLUG AND RAN TUBING BACK INTO THE WELL. FOLLOWING THIS, THE WELL WAS TESTED AND NORMAL OPERATIONS RESUMED ON OCTOBER 28, 2025.

NARRATIVE: ON SEPTEMBER 22, 2025, AT 7:55 A.M. A 3RD PARTY CONTRACTOR WAS BLOWING DOWN A TEMPORARY BYPASS LINE CONNECTED TO THE 3902_05 WAHA COMP TO DUAL OASIS 30-INCH NATURAL GAS PIPELINE. A 3RD PARTY CONTRACT EMPLOYEE LOCATED AT THE UPSTREAM LINE STOP ACTUATOR WAS INJURED WHEN 2-INCH NIPPLE AND VALVE UTILIZED AS PART OF THE BLOWDOWN PROCEDURE CAME OFF THE LINE STOP ACTUATOR STRIKING THE EMPLOYEE ON HIS LEFT ARM AND TORSO.       AT 8:09 A.M. EMERGENCY SERVICES WERE CALLED. THE 3RD PARTY CONTRACTOR EMPLOYEE WAS TRANSPORTED TO MEDICAL CENTER HOSPITAL IN ODESSA. AT 4:41 P.M. THE 3RD PARTY CONTRACTOR'S SAFETY REPRESENTATIVE NOTIFIED ENERGY TRANSFER'S DOT COMPLIANCE DEPARTMENT THAT AN INJURED CONTRACTOR WAS BEING ADMITTED TO MEDICAL CENTER HOSPITAL.      NRC (REPORT #1443444) AND TX RRC (INCI# 3411) WERE NOTIFIED.    IT WAS CONCLUDED THAT THE ROOT CAUSE WAS THE IMPROPER ASSEMBLY OF THE 2” BLOW DOWN NIPPLE ON THE WELDFIT STOPPLE. WELDFIT'S PLAN TO PREVENT RECURRENCE IS TO DEVELOP A PROCEDURE FOR THE PROPER USE AND APPLICATION OF THREAD SEALANT TAPE AND ESTABLISH CRITERIA FOR VERIFYING PROPER THREAD MAKEUP IS ACHIEVED. ENERGY TRANSFER SOP A.18 OPERATOR QUALIFICATION PLAN SECTION 7.7.1 "REQUIREMENTS TO PERFORM OQ TASKS" REQUIRES MEETING WITH THE CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL TO REVIEW SCOPE OF WORK AND REVIEW OF APPLICABLE SOPS PRIOR TO PERFORMING COVERED TASKS. BEFORE WELDFIT PERFORMS COVERED TASKS ON ANY ENERGY TRANSFER ASSETS, ENERGY TRANSFER WILL REVIEW WELDFIT SOPS TO CONFIRM THE APPROPRIATE UPDATES WERE MADE IN ACCORDANCE WITH WELDFIT’S PLAN TO MITIGATE REOCCURRENCE.

DURING THE TEST, THERE WAS A FAILURE, NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED AND SOME LIQUID PRODUCT SPRAYED OUT ACROSS HIGHWAY 285.  AT 11:59 CST, AN ENTERPRISE TECHNICIAN MADE INTERNAL NOTIFICATION AND CALLED 911 TO REPORT THE GAS RELEASE TO LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS. AT 12:12, ENTERPRISE SHUT OFF GAS FLOW TO THE PIPELINE. AT 12:40 PM, ENTERPRISE ISOLATED THE RELIEF VALVE. AT 12:45, ENTERPRISE DETERMINED THAT THIS EVENT WAS AN INCIDENT DUE TO GAS LOSS. ON 9-19-2026 AT 13:09 AND 13:10, THE TRRC AND NRC WERE NOTIFIED BY ENTERPRISE OF THE INCIDENT, RESPECTIVELY.      AT 15:50, AN ENTERPRISE CONTRACTOR STARTED THE CLEANING OF HIGHWAY 285. AT 20:30, THE CONTRACTOR COMPLETED THE CLEANUP AND HIGHWAY 285 WAS OPENED BY TXDOT IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. ON 9-19-2025 AT 20:11, THE PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE. A DIFFERENT CONTRACTOR TESTED THE RELIEF VALVE AND FOUND IT TO OPERATE WITHIN ACCEPTABLE PARAMETERS.    THE INVESTIGATION OF THE INCIDENT DETERMINED THAT THE CONTRACTOR DID NOT HAVE A WRITTEN PROCEDURE TO TEST THE RELIEF VALVE, AND THE CONTRACTOR WAS NOT FAMILIAR WITH THE RELIEF VALVE CONFIGURATION.

NARRATIVE: ON 9/12/2025 AT APPROXIMATELY 19:32, UP-24 RUPTURED AT STATION 125+05 NEAR THE DRIP AND LAUNCHER / RECEIVER AT THE UW-204 WELL PAD IN FORBES STATE FOREST WHICH IS LOCATED WEST OF FARMINGTON, PA IN FAYETTE COUNTY. GAS CONTROL RECEIVED INITIAL INDICATION OF THE RUPTURE FROM SCADA PRESSURE DROP ALARMS ON UP-2, UP-5, AND UP-7 AT 19:32. UP-24 IS AN 8 INCH DIAMETER TRANSMISSION PIPELINE IN STORAGE FUNCTION APPROXIMATELY 2.3 MILES LONG AND TRANSPORTS GAS BETWEEN OTHER TRANSMISSION PIPELINES IN STORAGE FUNCTION AND UW-204 WHICH IS AN UNDERGROUND STORAGE WELL IN NORTH SUMMIT STORAGE. THE MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE OPERATING PRESSURE OF UP-24 IS 3377 PSIG. UP-24 WAS IN NORMAL OPERATION WITH AN OPERATING PRESSURE OF 3176 PSIG AND NO FLOW WHEN THE INCIDENT OCCURRED. AT APPROXIMATELY 20:20, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CALLED GAS CONTROL AND CONFIRMED THAT NATURAL GAS WAS BLOWING IN NORTH SUMMIT STORAGE. FAYETTE COUNTY 911 CALLED GAS CONTROL AT APPROXIMATELY 20:31 AND PROVIDED A SIMILAR NOTIFICATION. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL MANUALLY CLOSED VALVE UP-24 UPSTREAM OF UP-24 AT APPROXIMATELY 20:55 AND MANUALLY CLOSED THE MASTER GATE VALVE AND LEAD LINES ON UW-204 DOWNSTREAM OF UP-24 AT APPROXIMATELY 21:20 TO COMPLETE THE ISOLATION OF UP-24. GAS CONTROL ALSO INITIATED COMMANDS TO REMOTELY CLOSE VALVES UP-5 AND UP-116 TO SUPPORT THE ISOLATION OF UP-24 AT APPROXIMATELY 20:57. GAS CEASED BLOWING FROM UP-24 AT APPROXIMATELY 21:29. THE RUPTURE RESULTED IN THE LONGITUDINAL AND THEN CIRCUMFERENTIAL FAILURE OF PIPE GOING TO THE DRIP; AN APPROXIMATELY 25 FT BY 30 FT HOLE IN THE GROUND APPROXIMATELY 12 FT TO 15 FT DEEP; APPROXIMATELY 14 FT OF PIPE FOLDING UP AND COMING OUT OF THE GROUND; APPROXIMATELY 1.5 FT OF PIPE BEING EJECTED APPROXIMATELY 73 FT AWAY FROM THE HOLE; DAMAGE TO THE DRIP AND ASSOCIATED PIPING; DAMAGE TO THE LAUNCHER / RECEIVER AND ASSOCIATED PIPING; DAMAGE TO A NEARBY ABOVEGROUND BRINE STORAGE TANK AT THE UW-204 WELL PAD AND ASSOCIATED PIPING; APPROXIMATELY 26,067 MCF GAS LOSS FROM UP-24; APPROXIMATELY 3,092 MCF OF GAS LOSS FROM UW-204; AND APPROXIMATELY 0.05 BARRELS OF BRINE SPILLED TO THE GROUND. THE GAS DID NOT IGNITE AND THERE WERE NO FIRES, INJURIES, FATALITIES, OR EVACUATIONS. INITIAL NOTIFICATION (#1442611) WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 9/12/2025 AT 20:45. FOLLOW UP NOTIFICATION (#1442677) WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 9/13/2025 AT 20:50. FOUR PIECES OF UP-24 PIPE WERE CUT-OUT AT THE RUPTURE AND WERE SENT TO KIEFNER & ASSOCIATES FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS. THE FAILURE ANALYSIS REPORT FROM KIEFNER WAS COMPLETED AND IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT INTERNAL CORROSION WAS THE PRIMARY CAUSE. AS A PRECAUTIONARY MEASURE, THE INTEGRITY OF THE UW-204 WELLHEAD AND ANNULAR VENT WERE INSPECTED ON 9/13/2025 AND IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT UW-204 COULD BE SAFELY RETURNED TO SERVICE ONCE THE REMEDIATION ACTIONS FOR UP-24 WERE COMPLETED AND UP-24 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE. THE ISOLATION OF UP-24 REMAINED IN EFFECT UNTIL THE FOLLOWING REMEDIATION ACTIONS WERE COMPLETED: THE SECTION OF UP-24 CONTAINING THE RUPTURE WAS REPLACED WHICH INVOLVED THE REPLACEMENT OF APPROXIMATELY 180 FT OF 8 INCH PIPE; THE DRIP AND ASSOCIATED PIPING WERE REPLACED; THE NEARBY ABOVEGROUND BRINE STORAGE TANK AND ASSOCIATED PIPING WERE REPAIRED; AND THE SOIL WHERE THE BRINE SPILLED WAS CLEANED UP. IN ADDITION TO THE REMEDIAL ACTIONS ABOVE, THE UW-204 SITE WAS RECONFIGURED TO ALLOW ADDITIONAL PIPING TO BE IN-LINE INSPECTED IN THE FUTURE, IMPROVE THE FUNCTIONALITY OF THE DRIP, AND ADDITIONAL INSPECTIONS OF NORTH SUMMIT STORAGE WERE PERFORMED. THE ESTIMATED COST OF THE RECONFIGURATION OF THE UW-204 SITE AND ADDITIONAL INSPECTIONS OF NORTH SUMMIT STORAGE WAS $2,506,965.  THE LAUNCHER / RECEIVER AND ASSOCIATED PIPING ARE SCHEDULED TO BE REPLACED IN 2026 SO AS TO BE LOCATED CLOSER TO UW-204 TO ALLOW ADDITIONAL PIPING TO BE IN-LINE INSPECTED IN THE FUTURE. UP-24 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON 12/10/2025 AT APPROXIMATELY 11:30.

NARRATIVE: ON SEPTEMBER 12, 2025, AT 13:37 COLUMBIA GAS TRANSMISSION, LLC (COLUMBIA) MONITORING CENTER RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE MARYLAND EMERGENCY SERVICE REGARDING A POTENTIAL NATURAL GAS RELEASE FROM A COLUMBIA FACILITY NEAR TERRA ALTA, WV. COLUMBIA OPERATIONS PERSONNEL RESPONDED AND WERE ON SITE AT 15:25 AND CONFIRMED THAT THE UNINTENDED GAS RELEASE TO BE FROM LINE 8234. THE INVOLVED SECTION OF LINE 8234 IN THIS EVENT IS SUBMERGED WITHIN THE NORTH BRANCH OF THE POTOMAC RIVER ON THE MD-WV LINE, AND THE PIPELINE RESIDES BELOW THE BOTTOM OF THE RIVERBED. WATER LEVELS WERE LOW, AND CURRENT HAD SIGNIFICANTLY SLOWED DOWN, RESULTING IN KAYAKERS SEEING AN AREA OF WATER WITH INCREASED BUBBLING. GARRETT COUNTY 911 RECEIVED A CALL FROM KAYAKERS ABOUT THE LEAK, REPORTING AN ODOR OF GAS. PIPELINE TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ON SITE AT 15:25 AND FOUND A NON-HAZARDOUS LEAK. NEITHER THE TECHNICIAN NOR FIREFIGHTERS WHO WERE AT THE LOCATION SAW ANY DEAD FISH OR NOTABLE EFFECTS ON WILDLIFE. THE TECHNICIAN REPORTED NO SHEEN ON THE WATER. NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) WAS NOTIFIED OF THE LEAK AROUND 16:07 ON THE DAY OF DISCOVERY, AND THE 48-HOUR NRC REPORT NUMBER WAS COMPLETED ON SEPTEMBER 14.  THE NRC 1-HR. REPORT AND 48-HR. REPORT NUMBERS WERE 1442591 AND 1442688, RESPECTIVELY. SITE MONITORING HAS BEEN INCREASED TO ONCE A WEEK TO ENSURE THE NON-HAZARDOUS LEAK CONDITIONS DO NOT WORSEN PRIOR TO BEING PERMANENTLY REPLACED.     NOTE: ALL RECORDED TIMES ARE IN EASTERN DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT).

NARRATIVE: ON SEPTEMBER 10, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 08:49, A CONSUMERS ENERGY EMPLOYEE INVESTIGATING A POTENTIAL LEAK INDICATION CONFIRMED THE PRESENCE OF A LEAK ON LINE 2600 NEAR THE JOHNSON ROAD VALVE SITE. LINE 2600 IS A 12-INCH NATURAL GAS TRANSMISSION PIPELINE LOCATED IN LAKE CITY, MICHIGAN (MISSAUKEE COUNTY).    CONSUMERS ENERGY PROMPTLY INITIATED AN ISOLATION PROCEDURE TO FURTHER ASSESS THE SITUATION. THE AFFECTED SECTION OF LINE 2600 WAS SUCCESSFULLY ISOLATED AND VENTED TO 0 PSIG BY APPROXIMATELY 16:46 THE SAME DAY.    FOLLOWING EXCAVATION, THE LEAK WAS DISCOVERED ON A 12-INCH BALL VALVE (VALVE 2622). THE VALVE WAS PRESERVED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CONSUMERS ENERGY’S FAILURE INVESTIGATION PROCEDURES AND TRANSPORTED TO THE COMPANY’S LABORATORY FOR ANALYSIS.    THE INCIDENT RESPONSE AND REMEDIATION EFFORTS WERE CONDUCTED WITHOUT INJURY TO THE PUBLIC OR OPERATING PERSONNEL.    REMEDIATION ACTIVITIES COMMENCED ON SEPTEMBER 10, 2025. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDED REPLACEMENT OF THE 12-INCH BALL VALVE AND APPROXIMATELY 9-FEET OF PIPELINE. THE PIPELINE SEGMENT WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON SEPTEMBER 23, 2025. FURTHER ANALYSIS OF THE FAILED VALVE IS ONGOING TO DETERMINE THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE FAILURE.    LABORATORY ANALYSIS AND POST-INCIDENT INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THE LEAK ORIGINATED FROM A FAILED O-RING WITHIN THE VALVE STEM ASSEMBLY INSIDE THE BONNET. THE FAILURE ALLOWED PRESSURIZED NATURAL GAS TO MIGRATE FROM THE PRESSURE BOUNDARY INTO THE VALVE STEM GREASE BEARING CAVITY WHICH WAS EQUIPPED WITH A ZERK-TYPE GREASED FITTING THAT INCLUDED A SAFETY VENT FEATURE INTENDED TO ALLOW AIR TO ESCAPE DURING GREASING OPERATIONS. ONCE THE O-RING FAILED IT ALLOWED AND UNOBSTRUCTED PATH FOR GAS TO MIGRATE FROM THE BONNET CAVITY INTO THE BELOW GRADE SPACE RESULTING IN A LEAK TO GRADE.

NARRATIVE: ON AUGUST 28, 2025, THERE WAS A RELEASE OF GAS AT THE SNAPPING TURTLE COMPRESSOR STATION LOCATED IN WIND RIDGE, PA WHICH IS OWNED AND OPERATED BY EQT PRODUCTION. CHIEF INSPECTOR TROY LEEK CALLED THE EQT ENVIRONMENTAL, HEALTH, & SAFETY (EHS) HOTLINE TO REPORT A GAS RELEASE AT THE SNAPPING TURTLE COMPRESSOR STATION. UPON ARRIVAL TO THE LOCATION, HE OBSERVED THE RICHHILL TOWNSHIP FIRE DEPARTMENT (WHO HAD BEEN DISPATCHED TO THE LOCATION UPON COMMUNITY NOTIFICATION), STAGED ~150' AWAY ON THE SHARED ACCESS ROAD. ALL CONTRACT PERSONNEL WERE MUSTERED OUTSIDE OF THE GATE. THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR (JOHN MINCIN) WAS NOTIFIED. GAS CONTROL WAS CONTACTED TO INITIATE A REMOTE SHUT IN OF COMPRESSOR UNIT #3, AND OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED TO THE SITE. UPON EXAMINATION, THERE WAS A TUBING FAILURE ON A RELIEF VALVE INSIDE THE STATION.     UPON EVALUATION, OPERATIONS DETERMINED THAT NO OVERPRESSURE EVENT OCCURRED. PRESSURE WAS ~917PSI WHEN THE PRV LIFTED. IT WAS NOTED THAT THE NUT AND FERRULE OF THE 1/8" STAINLESS STEEL TUBING BROKE AT THE PILOT WHICH, BY DESIGN, CAUSED A LOSS OF PRESSURE TO THE MAIN VALVE DOME THUS TRIGGERING A FAIL-OPEN CONDITION (CAUSED THE RELIEF VALVE TO LIFT). THE SUSPECTED FAILURE IS EXCESSIVE VIBRATION, SO NEW TUBING WAS INSTALLED IN A CONFIGURATION THAT ALLEVIATED THE VIBRATION ISSUE. EQT PRODUCTION EXAMINED OTHER STATIONS WITH SIMILAR TUBING CONFIGURATIONS AS THE FAILED TUBING AND FOUND THEM TO BE FULLY OPERATIONAL FOR MANY YEARS AND WITHOUT VIBRATION ISSUES. EQT PRODUCTION BELIEVES THAT THIS WAS AN ISOLATED EVENT AND PARTICULAR TO THE RELIEF VALVE TUBING AT THIS SPECIFIC RELIEF VALVE AT SNAPPING TURTLE COMPRESSOR STATION.

NARRATIVE: AS REPORTED AN ALLIANCE PIPELINE TECHNICIAN DETECTED A SMALL LEAK DURING A SITE CALL OUT WHICH WAS SIGNIFIED BY BUBBLING GROUND WATER AFTER A SIGNIFICANT RAIN EVENT ON AUGUST 19, 2025.  THE SITE WAS DEEMED SAFE BY ACTIVE LEAK MONITORING ON SITE FROM AUGUST 19-21 AND A THIRD-PARTY LEAK DETECTION TEAM CONDUCTING A SITE SURVEY ON AUGUST 22 WITH INSTRUMENTATION THAT DETERMINED NO MIGRATION OF GAS LEAKAGE OFF THE BLOCK VALVE LOCATION.  AN ADDITIONAL SURVEY WAS CONDUCTED BY THE THIRD PARTY AGAIN ON SEPTEMBER 23 WITH DETERMINATION THAT GAS LEAKAGE WAS NOT MIGRATING OFF SITE.  THE LEAK VOLUME WAS NOT ABLE TO BE DETERMINED UTILIZING QUALITATIVE AND QUANTITATIVE LEAK DETECTION EQUIPMENT DUE TO THE VERY SMALL NATURE OF THE LEAK.  THE EXCAVATION TO INVESTIGATE THE LEAK WAS CONDUCTED THE WEEK OF OCTOBER 14.  ON OCTOBER 17 THE SMALL LEAK WAS CONFIRMED AND REPAIRED ON THE 1 1/2” BODY PLUG ON THE DISCHARGE SIDE VALVE OF THE BLOCK VALVE SITE.  THE BODY PLUG WAS SAFELY REMOVED AND REPLACED WHILE THE PIPELINE REMAINED IN SERVICE.  IOWA UTILITIES COMMISSION REPRESENTATIVES DANIEL O’CONNOR AND ERIC BROWN WERE ON SITE TO WITNESS SOME OF THE EXCAVATION WORK TO EXPOSE THE SUSPECTED LEAK AREA ON OCTOBER 15-16.  ON OCTOBER 17 COMMUNICATION WAS SENT TO IUC TO CONFIRM DISCOVERY AND REPAIR OF THE LEAKING PLUG ON THE VALVE BODY.  THE EXCAVATION WAS LEFT OPEN FOR SUBSEQUENT LEAK DETECTION SURVEY BY THE THIRD-PARTY CONTRACTOR.  THE WEEK OF OCTOBER 21 ALLIANCE MOBILIZED THE THIRD-PARTY CONTRACTOR TO PERFORM LEAK DETECTION SURVEYS ON SITE WHICH INDICATED NO LEAKS ON THE VALVE BODY.  SANDBLASTING AND COATING OF THE VALVE BODY AND STEM OF THE EXPOSED VALVE WAS FINALIZED THE WEEK OF DECEMBER 1 ALONG WITH BACKFILL OF THE EXCAVATION, SITE CLEANUP, AND DEMOBILIZATION OF CREW AND EQUIPMENT FROM THE LOCATION. EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: SOFT GOODS OF THE RELIEF VALVE GOT DAMAGED AND THE VALVE COULD NOT RESEAT.
NARRATIVE: AT 14:05 HOURS ON AUGUST 6, 2025, A NORTHERN NATURAL GAS OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN DISCOVERED A RELIEF VALVE VENTING AT THE MINNEAPOLIS, MINNESOTA NO. 1R (STATION NO. 753160) IN CARVER, MINNESOTA.  THE OPERATIONS TEAM CONTACTED THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT AND SHERIFFS DEPARTMENT TO SUPPORT WITH THE CLOSING OF COUNTY ROAD 40.  THE ROAD WAS SUCCESSFULLY CLOSED AT 14:24 HOURS UNTIL THE OPERATIONS TEAM WAS ABLE TO DIVERT FLOW THROUGH THE STATION BYPASS  AND SUCCESSFULLY STOPPED THE RELIEF VALVE VENTING AT 14:28 HOURS.  THE COUNTY ROAD WAS THEN REOPENED AT 14:35 HOURS.      DURING THE ONSITE INSPECTION OF THE RELIEF VALVE THE OPERATIONS TEAM, IT WAS BELIEVED THAT THE RELIEF VALVE AND RELIEF PILOT VALVE WERE DAMAGED BY DEBRIS PREVENTING IT FROM RESEATING AND CLOSING.  THE DEBRIS WAS SUSPECTED TO HAVE ORIGINATED FROM RECENT NEARBY CONSTRUCTION ON THE PIPELINE.  WHEN THE REGULATOR STRAINERS WERE EXAMINED, THEY WERE FOUND TO HAVE BECOME DISLODGED, RELEASING ANY DEBRIS THEY HELD.    THE RELIEF VALVE AND RELIEF VALVE PILOT WERE REBUILT ON-SITE AND VERIFIED FOR PROPER OPERATION.  AT 17:00 HOURS ON AUGUST 7, 2025, THE STATION WAS PUT BACK INTO NORMAL OPERATIONS.    FOLLOWING THE INCIDENT, THE RELIEF VALVE PILOT WAS SENT TO THE VENDOR TO BE EXAMINED BY A MATERIAL SPECIALIST.  THE SPECIALIST FOUND THAT THE PILOT DISK HAD WEAKENED CAUSING THE PILOT TO FAIL OPEN DAMAGING THE RELIEF VALVE BOOT DUE TO THE HIGH VELOCITY OF GAS VENTING THROUGH THE RELIEF VALVE.  THIS STYLE OF PILOT DISK WAS KNOWN BY SPECIALIST TO WEAKEN OVER TIME, AND IT IS NO LONGER BEING USED BY THE VENDOR.  NORTHERN NATURAL GAS IS LOOKING AT REPLACING ALL PILOT DISKS OF THIS STYLE.

NARRATIVE: AT 8:05 A.M., A FASKEN OPERATOR NOTIFIED ONEOK PIPELINE CONTROL CENTER OF A GAS RELEASE AT THE FASKEN METER SETTING. UPON RECEIVING THE REPORT, PCC CALLED THE LOCAL MEASUREMENT TECHNICIAN TO HEAD TO THE SITE. THE TECH ARRIVED ON SITE AT 9:25 A.M AND WITNESSED A PRV RELEASING GAS. THE TECH ISOLATED THE PRV AT 9:27 A.M. AFTER BEING ISOLATED, THE PRESSURE DATA WAS TRENDED, AND A PRESSURE CHANGE WAS NOTED TO HAVE OCCURRED AT 5:51 A.M.  THE RELIEF FOR THE VALVE WAS REMOVED AND SENT OFF FOR ANALYSIS. AT THIS TIME THERE ARE NO PLANS TO REINSTALL A NEW RELIEF AS THIS IS A REDUNDANT FORM OF PRESSURE CONTROL FOR THIS SETTING. ONCE ANALYSIS RESULTS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED A SUPPLEMENTAL WILL BE SUBMITTED.    SUPPLEMENTAL- FOLLOWING THE EVENT, OWT CONDUCTED A FAILURE ANALYSIS. THE ANALYSIS CONCLUDED THAT THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE FAILURE WAS THE INSTALLATION OF A PRV WITH A RELIEF TOLERANCE WITHIN THE NORMAL OPERATING RANGE OF THE PIPELINE SYSTEM. THIS ALLOWED THE PVR TO CONTINUE TO RELIEF WITHIN THE NORMAL DESIGN/OPERATING PRESSURE OF THE PIPELINE SYSTEM.  AS A RESULT, THE PVR STEM AND SEATS WERE DAMAGED, NOT ALLOWING THE PVR TO RESEAT. THE PVR IS A REDUNDANT FORM OF PRESSURE CONTROL AND HAS BEEN ISOLATED FROM THE SYSTEM.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 3, 2025, AT 16:24 MDT, NORTHERN BORDER'S GAS CONTROL (GC) RECEIVED AN ALARM AT MONCHY METER STATION AND AT 16:36 MDT, GC RECEIVED A CALL FROM A CUSTOMER ABOUT A PRESSURE DROP ON THEIR COMPRESSOR STATION THAT MAY HAVE IMPACTED MONCHY METER STATION. AT 18:38 MDT, A SECOND CALL WAS RECEIVED FROM THE CUSTOMER ASKING FOR A TECHNICIAN ON OUR SIDE TO INVESTIGATE A RELIEF VALVE AT MONCHY METER STATION. GC DISPATCHED A TECHNICIAN TO INVESTIGATE A POTENTIAL RELEASE AT 18:45 MDT. UPON ARRIVING ON SITE AT 21:17 MDT, THE TECHNICIAN DISCOVERED NATURAL GAS WAS BEING RELEASED TO THE ATMOSPHERE THROUGH AN EIGHT-INCH RELIEF VALVE. IMMEDIATE ACTION WAS TAKEN TO ISOLATE THE VALVE, WHICH ENDED THE RELEASE AT 21:20 MDT. THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS CALCULATED TO BE APPROXIMATELY 217,850.1 MCF. THERE WERE NO DAMAGES, INJURIES OR HOSPITALIZATIONS. THE INVESTIGATION INTO THE CAUSE OF THE VALVE FAILURE IS STILL ON-GOING. THE RELIEF VALVE IS CURRENTLY ISOLATED AND THERE IS A SECONDARY RELIEF VALVE AT THE MONCHY METER STATION THAT IS PROVIDING OVERPRESSURE PROTECTION TO THE PIPELINE SYSTEM.

NARRATIVE: ON JUNE 15, 2025, AN EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD) EVENT OCCURRED AT THE WASHINGTON RANCH COMPRESSOR STATION WHILE THE STATION WAS IN AN IDLE OPERATING STATE. SEVERE WEATHER AND FLASH FLOODING IN THE AREA PREVENTED EPNG PERSONNEL FROM ACCESSING THE STATION UNTIL JUNE 16, 2025.  DURING THIS PERIOD, THE STATION WAS MONITORED THROUGH REMOTE SYSTEM ACCESS WHICH INDICATED ISOLATION VALVES AND ESD VENTS WERE IN THEIR PROPER POSITIONS.     UPON ARRIVING ON SITE, EPNG PERSONNEL CONDUCTED AN ONSITE ASSESSMENT AND FOUND ESD VALVE 5107 IN A PARTIALLY CLOSED POSITION. A ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS DETERMINED THAT VALVE 5107 HAD A DEFECTIVE VALVE ACTUATOR THAT PREVENTED FULL VALVE CLOSURE DURING THE ESD EVENT AND CONTRIBUTED TO A RELEASE OF APPROXIMATELY 84,000 MCF OF NATURAL GAS.    THE EVENT WAS INITIALLY CLASSIFIED AS AN ABNORMAL OPERATION EVENT. SUBSEQUENT REVIEW OF MEASUREMENT DATA IDENTIFIED THAT THE VOLUME OF GAS RELEASE EXCEEDED REPORTABLE THRESHOLDS ON JUNE 19, 2025, AT WHICH TIME KINDER MORGAN NOTIFIED THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER OF A REPORTABLE NATURAL GAS RELEASE.    CORRECTIVE ACTION INCLUDED REPLACEMENT OF THE VALVE ACTUATOR ON ESD VALVE 5107 TO ENSURE FULL CLOSURE DURING FUTURE ESD ACTIVATIONS.

NARRATIVE: OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT RECEIVED INITIAL NOTIFICATION OF THIS INCIDENT VIA A FIRE DEPARTMENT SCANNER REPORT ON 6/8/25 AT APPROXIMATELY 18:00. AT THIS TIME, IT WAS UNKNOWN IF THE INCIDENT INVOLVED AN EGTS ASSET AS THERE ARE OTHER PIPELINE AND UNDERGROUND WELL OPERATORS IN THE AREA SO OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT DISPATCHED OPERATIONS PERSONNEL TO MAKE THIS DETERMINATION. AFTER PICKING UP A UTILITY TERRAIN VEHICLE AT SOUTHERN AREA HEADQUARTERS, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON-SITE IN THE NEARBY VICINITY OF THE INCIDENT ON 6/8/25 AT APPROXIMATELY 18:38. AT THIS TIME, IT REMAINED UNKNOWN IF THE INCIDENT INVOLVED AN EGTS ASSET SO OPERATIONS PERSONNEL BEGAN THE PROCESS OF MANUALLY ISOLATING BW-9832 BY CLOSING VALVES (A GATE & E GATE) AS A PRECAUTIONARY MEASURE. BW-9832 IS AN UNDERGROUND NATURAL GAS STORAGE WELL IN KENNEDY STORAGE AND IS LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF VALLEY CHAPEL, WEST VIRGINIA. THE CLOSEST ROAD TO BW-9832 IS MARE RUN ROAD AND BW-9832 IS CONNECTED TO THE H-19366 PIPELINE. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL COMPLETED THE ISOLATION OF BW-9832 ON 6/8/25 AT APPROXIMATELY 18:55. FOLLOWING THE ISOLATION OF BW-9832, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CONFIRMED DISCOVERY THAT THE INCIDENT INVOLVED BW-9832 ON 6/8/25 AT APPROXIMATELY 19:04. A TREE HAD FALLEN AND SEVERED THE 4” LEAD LINE AND 4” WELL LINE ASSOCIATED WITH BW-9832. IT IS BELIEVED THAT THIS OCCURRED ON 6/8/25 AT APPROXIMATELY 17:46. THE OPERATING PRESSURE OF BW-9832 AT THE TIME OF INITIATION OF THE INCIDENT WAS APPROXIMATELY 924 PSIG AND BW-9832 WAS IN THE INJECTION PHASE. THE MAXIMUM OPERATING STORAGE PRESSURE OF KENNEDY STORAGE IS 1050 PSIG. THE NATURAL GAS THAT WAS RELEASED AS A RESULT OF THE INCIDENT DID NOT IGNITE. ONE RESIDENTIAL BUILDING WITH TWO OCCUPANTS WAS EVACUATED AS A PRECAUTIONARY MEASURE. NO INJURIES WERE SUSTAINED AND THERE WERE NO ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS AS A RESULT OF THE INCIDENT. INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ON 6/8/25 AT 20:03. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1433512 TO THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION OF THE INCIDENT. A FOLLOW-UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ON 6/9/25 AT 21:48 TO CONFIRM THE INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1433620 TO THE FOLLOW UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION. A TEMPORARY BRIDGE PLUG WAS INSTALLED ON BW-9832 TO FACILITATE THE TESTING OF THE INTEGRITY OF THE WELLHEAD CONNECTIONS ON BW-9832. TESTING OF THE INTEGRITY OF THE WELLHEAD CONNECTIONS ON BW-9832 WAS COMPLETED ON 6/23/25 AND IT WAS DETERMINED THAT BW-9832 COULD SAFELY RETURN TO SERVICE ONCE THE 4” LEAD LINE AND 4” WELL LINE WERE REPLACED. THE 4” LEAD LINE, THE 4” WELL LINE, AND THE ASSOCIATED DRIP WERE REPLACED AND BW-9832 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON 11/21/2025 AT APPROXIMATELY 14:00. OTHER TREES IN THE IMMEDIATE AREA OF BW-9832 WERE ALSO REMOVED TO HELP PREVENT RECURRENCE OF THE INCIDENT.

NARRATIVE: NATIONAL FUEL GAS SUPPLY CORPORATION (“NATIONAL FUEL”) IS REPORTING THIS NATURAL GAS INCIDENT AS A RESULT OF AN UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF GAS, WHICH RESULTED IN PROPERTY DAMAGE WITH ESTIMATED COSTS IN EXCESS OF THE APPLICABLE $145,400 THRESHOLD OUTLINED IN 49 C.F.R. § 191.3(1)(II) AND OCCURRING ON NATIONAL FUEL’S F1S PIPELINE IN THE TOWN OF LIMESTONE, CATTARAUGUS COUNTY, NY (“LINE F1S”). ON SUNDAY, JUNE 1, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 12:11PM, NATIONAL FUEL GAS CONTROL OPERATIONS CENTER RECEIVED AN ALARM DUE TO A PRESSURE DROP ON LINE F1S. AT APPROXIMATELY 12:14PM, THE PRESSURE DROP TRIGGERED A COMPRESSOR UNIT SHUTDOWN AT LIMESTONE COMPRESSOR STATION, WHICH ISOLATED THE COMPRESSOR STATION FROM LINE F1S. NATIONAL FUEL PROMPTLY MOBILIZED OPERATIONS TO INVESTIGATE. AT APPROXIMATELY 1:06PM, NATIONAL FUEL ARRIVED AT LIMESTONE COMPRESSOR STATION TO BEGIN THE INVESTIGATION INTO THE CAUSE OF THE ALARM, AND, ALTHOUGH A POTENTIAL SOURCE OF THE ALARM WAS NOT YET DISCOVERED, AS A PRECAUTIONARY ACTION, CLOSED VALVE CPC113 AT LIMESTONE STATION, WHICH IS IMMEDIATELY UPSTREAM OF LINE F1S. AT APPROXIMATELY 1:36PM, THE DOWNSTREAM VALVE ON LINE F1S (VALVE RHC111) WAS CLOSED TO ISOLATE LINE F1S. OUT OF AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION, AT APPROXIMATELY 2:46PM, NATIONAL FUEL NOTIFIED THE NRC OF A POTENTIAL RUPTURE. AFTER LINE F1S WAS ISOLATED, NATIONAL FUEL PATROLLED LINE F1S, AND, AT APPROXIMATELY 3:16PM, CONFIRMED A RUPTURE ON LINE F1S NEAR THE CROSSING OF RICE BROOK WITHIN ALLEGANY STATE PARK (GPS COORDINATES 42.0603, -78.69133). UPON EXCAVATING AND REMOVING THE RUPTURED SECTION OF LINE F1S, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE RUPTURE OCCURRED ALONG THE LONGITUDINAL SEAM OF AN APPROXIMATELY 50-FOOT JOINT OF PIPE AND ARRESTED 2-3 INCHES BEYOND THE GIRTH WELD IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. THE LONGITUDINAL SEAM WAS ORIENTED AT THE 6:00 O’CLOCK PIPE POSITION. THE RICE BROOK FLOWED ABOVE LINE F1S THROUGH TWO PARALLEL CORRUGATED METAL CULVERT PIPES, WHICH WERE DAMAGED AS A RESULT OF THE RUPTURE. THE FLOW OF RICE BROOK WAS TEMPORARILY DISRUPTED, BUT LATER RE-ESTABLISHED WITH MINIMAL ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS. LABORATORY ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS OF THE RUPTURED SECTION OF LINE F1S DETERMINED THE ROOT CAUSE WAS INTERNAL SELECTED SEAM WELD CORROSION (SSWC), INITIATING IN A SAG BEND AT A LOW POINT IN LINE F1S. THE LAB REPORT STATED THAT THE LOW POINT WOULD LIKELY COLLECT WATER AND OTHER POTENTIAL CONTAMINANTS FROM THE SERVICE FLUID, WHICH WOULD AID THE PROGRESS OF INTERNAL SSWC DUE TO LINE F1S'S SEAM HAVING BEEN INSTALLED AT THE 6:00 O'CLOCK ORIENTATION. LINE F1S WAS REPAIRED BY REPLACING APPROXIMATELY 60 FEET OF 6-INCH OD, 0.280-INCH WALL, GRADE X-52 STEEL PIPE WHICH WAS PRESSURE TESTED TO >1.5 X MAOP. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES RESULTED FROM THE RUPTURE.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 25, 2025, AT 7:57 A.M., WHILE MAINTENANCE WAS BEING PERFORMED BY AN ENERGY TRANSFER COMPANY (ETC) EMPLOYEE AND A CONTRACT EMPLOYEE, THE WEST SLASH UNIT 2 COMPRESSOR, CAUGHT FIRE. THE ETC EMPLOYEE NOTIFIED THE OPERATIONS MANAGER WHO THEN NOTIFIED EMERGENCY SERVICES. THE EMPLOYEE AND CONTRACT EMPLOYEE ACTIVATED THE EMERGENCY SHUT DOWN SYSTEM (ESD) SUCCESSFULLY SHUTTING DOWN THE COMPRESSOR STATION. BOTH THE ETC EMPLOYEE AND CONTRACT EMPLOYEE WERE INJURED WHEN THE FIRE IGNITED. WHEN EMERGENCY SERVICES ARRIVED AT THE LOCATION THE TWO INJURED RECEIVED MEDICAL ATTENTION AND THE FIRE WAS EXTINGUISHED.    THE INTERNAL INVESTIGATION FOUND THAT EMPLOYEES FAILED TO PROPERLY FOLLOW THE LOCKOUT/TAGOUT PROCEDURE LOCATED IN S-230 HAZARDOUS ENERGY CONTROL SECTION XII. THE INVESTIGATION ALSO FOUND THAT THE EMPLOYEES WRONGFULLY OPENED A VALVE ALLOWING THE NATURAL GAS LINE PRESSURE TO PURGE DEBRIS FROM THE CYLINDERS.  THE RELEASED GAS THEN FOUND AN IGNITION SOURCE SUPPLIED BY ONE OR MORE OF THE RUNNING NATURAL GAS COMPRESSORS RESULTING IN THE FIRE.      THE PLAN TO PREVENT RECURRENCE IS AS FOLLOWS:  1) SAFETY STAND DOWN FOR THE ENTIRE WESTER DIVISION TO REVIEW LOCKOUT/TAGOUT PROCEDURES AND ACCEPTABLE CLEANING MEDIA.  2) RETRAIN SITE EMPLOYEES ON S-230 WITH AN EMPHASIS ON THE PROPER LOCKOUT/TAGOUT PROCESS.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: YES, SPECIFY INVESTIGATION RESULT(S): (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
NARRATIVE: ON MAY 19, 2025, HARVEST INITIATED A ROUTINE FOAM PIGGING OPERATION ON THE PGGS SYSTEM AT APPROXIMATELY 11:00 AM AS PART OF STANDARD MAINTENANCE ACTIVITIES. THE PIG WAS LAUNCHED WITHOUT INCIDENT; HOWEVER, PRESSURE IN THE PG-02 SEGMENT BEGAN TO RISE UNEXPECTEDLY LATER THAT AFTERNOON. THIS SEGMENT IS EQUIPPED WITH A 6-INCH PRESSURE RELIEF VALVE (PRV) AND EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN VALVE (ESDV-1), MONITORED BY PRESSURE TRANSMITTER PT-201.     DURING THE INVESTIGATION, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE PIG ARRIVED SIGNIFICANTLY EARLIER THAN ANTICIPATED. THE TRAVEL TIME HAD BEEN PROJECTED USING SCADA FLOW DATA. THE PIG WAS EXPECTED TO TAKE UP TO TWO DAYS TO REACH ITS DESTINATION BUT COMPLETED THE RUN WITHIN A FEW HOURS. THIS UNANTICIPATED ARRIVAL LED TO CONDENSATE ACCUMULATION IN THE LINE, WHICH TRAVELED INTO THE ACTUATOR SUPPLY TUBING OF ESDV-1, CONTRIBUTING TO VALVE MALFUNCTION.     AT 5:14 PM, PT-201 REGISTERED A PRESSURE OF 526 PSI. THE HOUSTON CONTROL ROOM DETECTED THE TREND AND CONTACTED PIPELINE OPERATOR, WHO ADVISED THAT THE FOAM PIG WAS LIKELY STILL IN TRANSIT. HE INFORMED THE CONTROLLER THAT THE VALVE HAD PRESSURE RELIEF CAPABILITY AND INSTRUCTED THEM TO RESET THE VALVE IF NECESSARY AND NOTIFY HIM IF PRESSURE EXCEEDED 600 PSI.     AS PRESSURE CONTINUED TO RISE, PIPELINE OPERATOR INITIATED MANUAL SHUT-IN COORDINATION WITH THE HILCORP PLATFORM. AT 5:54 PM, PRESSURE REACHED 550 PSI AND ESDV-1 RECEIVED A CLOSE COMMAND, SHUTTING THE VALVE AS DESIGNED. HOWEVER, CONDENSATE ACCUMULATION IN THE REGULATOR AND ACTUATOR TUBING SUBSEQUENTLY PREVENTED THE VALVE FROM REOPENING.     THE CONTROL ROOM ISSUED MULTIPLE RESET COMMANDS TO ESDV-1 VIA SCADA, BUT THE VALVE REMAINED IN AN 'ERROR' STATE AND FAILED TO REOPEN. AT 6:37 PM, PRESSURE REACHED THE MAOP OF 606.2 PSI, PROMPTING THE CONTROLLER TO ESCALATE THE SITUATION TO OPERATIONS CONTROLS DIRECTOR, FOREMAN, AND PIPELINE OPERATOR.     BY 6:43 PM, SCADA ENGINEER WAS CONTACTED FOR SUPPORT. HE DETERMINED THAT THE ISSUE WAS NOT DUE TO A SCADA MALFUNCTION BUT WAS LIKELY CAUSED BY CONDENSATE IN THE REGULATOR AND ACTUATOR TUBING, WHICH PREVENTED THE VALVE FROM RECEIVING SUFFICIENT ACTUATION PRESSURE. AT 6:50 PM, PRESSURE PEAKED AT 620.9 PSI, EXCEEDING MAOP BUT REMAINING BELOW 110%, AND THE PRV ACTIVATED TO RELIEVE PRESSURE. THE PRV VENTED NATURAL GAS TO ATMOSPHERE FOR APPROXIMATELY 22 MINUTES.     AT 6:57 PM, FLOW THROUGH PG-02 DROPPED TO ZERO. AT 7:00 PM, A MANUAL CLOSE COMMAND WAS ISSUED TO ESDV-1, AND A PERMISSIVE SIGNAL WAS RECEIVED. PIPELINE OPERATORS AGREED TO KEEP THE VALVE CLOSED AND CONDUCT A STAND-UP PRESSURE TEST TO CONFIRM PIPELINE INTEGRITY.     THE TEST BEGAN AT 7:18 PM, WITH PT-201 READING 594.5 PSI. BY 8:01 PM, PRESSURE HAD STABILIZED AT 594.0 PSI. ALTHOUGH THE LINE WAS DEEMED SAFE TO RESTART, REPEATED ATTEMPTS TO REOPEN ESDV-1 WERE UNSUCCESSFUL.     AT 10:19 PM, PIPELINE OPERATOR AND I&E TECHNICIAN DECIDED TO KEEP THE SYSTEM SHUT-IN OVERNIGHT. THE ROOT CAUSE WAS IDENTIFIED AS A LIQUID-FILLED REGULATOR AND ACTUATOR TUBING, WHICH PREVENTED PROPER VALVE ACTUATION. PLANS WERE MADE TO ACCESS THE SITE THE FOLLOWING MORNING AND REPLACE THE AFFECTED COMPONENTS.

IT WAS DETERMINED TO BE A RUPTURE THAT OCCURRED DURING ROUTINE MAINTENANCE PIGGING OPERATIONS.    ON MAY 16, 2025, AT 1230, IT WAS DETERMINED THE REPAIRS WOULD EXCEED THE $145,400.00 REPORTABLE DOLLAR CRITERIA AND THE NRC AND THE TXRRC WERE NOTIFIED.    THE LINE SEPARATION IS STILL UNDER INVESTIGATION AND THE AFFECTED SECTION OF THE PIPE HAS BEEN SENT TO THE LAB FOR ANALYSIS.  A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED WHEN THE LAB ANALYSIS AND INVESTIGATION IS COMPLETE.     **THE LAB RESULTS CONCLUDED THAT THE FAILURE COINCIDES WITH A REGION OF INTERNAL CORROSION PRESENT ALONG THE BOTTOM OF THE PIPE.  THE INTERNAL CORROSION OCCURRED DUE TO HYDROGEN SULFIDE AND CARBON DIOXIDE COMBINING WITH WATER TO FORM AN ACIDIC ENVIRONMENT.    TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE, OPERATIONS WILL IMPLEMENT A PIGGING SCHEDULE AND CORROSION INHIBITOR PROGRAM SPECIFIC FOR THE PIPELINE.  COUPONS WILL BE ADDED BEFORE THE LINE IS PUT BACK INTO SERVICE.     A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED ONCE THE PIPELINE HAS BEEN PUT BACK INTO SERVICE.    **ON SEPTEMBER 19, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 1000 THE LINE WAS PUT BACK INTO SERVICE.

NAME: MISSISSIPPI HUB, LLC
STATUS_WHEN_IDENTIFIED: ROUTINE START-UP
INCIDENT_AREA_SUBTYPE: TYPICAL ABOVEGROUND FACILITY PIPING OR APPURTENANCE
SYSTEM_PART_INVOLVED: ONSHORE COMPRESSOR STATION EQUIPMENT AND PIPING
ACCIDENT_PRESSURE: PRESSURE EXCEEDED MAOP, BUT DID NOT EXCEED THE APPLICABLE ALLOWANCE IN 192.201
INVESTIGATION_STATUS: YES, SPECIFY INVESTIGATION RESULT(S): (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
CAUSE: MATERIAL FAILURE OF PIPE OR WELD
NARRATIVE: AT 20:52 CDT ON 4/12/2025, AN 8-INCH PRESSURE RELIEF VALVE (PRV) ATTACHED TO A 42-INCH HEADER AT THE MISSISSIPPI HUB UNGSF AND COMPRESSOR STATION WAS EJECTED DUE TO A RUPTURE OF THE PIPE SPOOL BELOW THE VALVE. AS A RESULT OF THE EVENT, THE ON-SITE OPERATIONS CONTROLLER EXPERIENCED A LOSS OF COMMUNICATIONS WITH THE SCADA SYSTEM. HOWEVER, THE STATION ESD SYSTEM FUNCTIONED AS DESIGNED AND SHUT IN THE FACILITY AUTOMATICALLY. THE FACILITY WAS IN THE PROCESS OF PREPARING TO WITHDRAW GAS FROM STORAGE WHEN THE EVENT OCCURRED.       THE ON-SITE OPERATOR WAS JOINED BY OTHER LOCAL EMPLOYEES AND BEGAN INVESTIGATING THE SOURCE OF THE FAILURE WHEN THE EJECTED PRV WAS DISCOVERED. THE PRV WAS LOCATED BETWEEN THE GAS HEATERS AND SEPARATORS DOWNSTREAM OF THE PRESSURE REGULATORS FOR THE COMINGLED GAS STREAM WHICH WAS NOT WITHIN THE UNGSF DEMARCATION. IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE EXTENT OF PHYSICAL DAMAGE AND CORRECTIONS TO COMMUNICATIONS COULD NOT BE FULLY EVALUATED UNTIL DAYLIGHT, BUT THERE WAS NO CONCERN FOR THE SAFETY OF EMPLOYEES OR THE PUBLIC. THERE WERE NO INJURIES TO PERSONNEL, IGNITION OF THE GAS, EVACUATIONS, ROAD CLOSURES, OR MEDIA INQUIRIES.     THE FOLLOWING MORNING, PERSONNEL DETERMINED THE PRV HAD BEEN EJECTED VERTICALLY AND THROUGH A CABLE TRAY, RESULTING IN MULTIPLE DAMAGED CABLES. ONCE THE PRELIMINARY EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE HAD BEEN ASSESSED, IT WAS DETERMINED THE FACILITY WOULD BE SHUT IN WHILE REPAIRS WERE MADE. THE EVENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC AT 11:09 CDT ON 4/13/2025 SINCE REPAIRS COULD HAVE EXCEEDED $145,400 AND WE DETERMINED AT THAT TIME THAT THE EVENT RESULTED IN THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN OF AN UNGSF.     THE REPAIR WAS COMPLETED ON 4/16/2025, AND COMMUNICATIONS FOR THE FACILITY WERE RESTORED, ALLOWING THE FACILITY TO BE PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE ON 4/18/2025. THE FAILED PIPE UNDERWENT METALLURGICAL EXAMINATION, AND A ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS WAS UNDERTAKEN TO DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF THE FAILURE.     THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS DETERMINED THE PIPE SPOOL FAILURE WAS THE RESULT OF AN EXTERNAL OVERLOAD OF THE ASSEMBLY CREATED BY A PRESSURE RELEASE EVENT. THE PRESSURE RELEASE CAUSED A DOWNWARD FORCE AND BENDING MOMENT, TRIGGERING BUCKLING INSTABILITY JUST BELOW THE PRESSURE RELIEF VALVE.    THE COMPONENTS OF THE ASSEMBLY ABOVE THE BUCKLE ROTATED NEARLY 45° FROM VERTICAL BEFORE A PARTING FAILURE OCCURRED.     A BULGE WAS PROGRESSIVELY GENERATED AS THE STRUCTURE BENT OVER, DISSIPATING SIGNIFICANT ENERGY. MICROGRAPHS SHOW THAT THE MATERIAL EXHIBITED SIGNIFICANT PLASTIC FLOW BEFORE FAILURE.     THE OVERALL PLASTIC BEHAVIOR OF THE MATERIAL LOCAL TO THE BULGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH MECHANICAL TESTING RESULTS. MECHANICAL TESTING SHOWS AN ELONGATION OF 32% TO 36% AND A REDUCTION IN AREA OF 66% TO 72%. SUBSIZE CHARPY V-NOTCH TEST SPECIMENS (5 MM X 10 MM) RETURNED AN AVERAGE TOUGHNESS OF 98 FT-LBS  AT 72°F(CONVERTED TO FULL SCALE).    THE FINAL FRACTURE INITIATION WAS CAUSED BY SHEAR OVERLOAD AT THE BUCKLE BULGE TIP ON THE OD.    NO SIGNS OF PREEXISTING ID OR OD CRACKS, DAMAGE, CORROSION, OR OTHER STRESS RISERS WERE FOUND. NO EVIDENCE EXISTED THAT PRIOR DAMAGE WEAKENED THE BUCKLING RESISTANCE OF THE STRUCTURE.     A FINITE ELEMENT ANALYSIS OF THE PIPE ASSEMBLY WAS UNDERTAKEN TO SUPPORT THE CONCLUSION THE PIPE SPOOL FAILURE WAS THE RESULT OF AN EXTERNAL OVERLOAD OF THE ASSEMBLY CREATED BY A PRESSURE RELEASE EVENT. THE PLASTIC COLLAPSE OVERLOAD IS CONSISTENT WITH THE FEA ENGINEERING ANALYSIS RESULTS.

NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 25TH, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 09:51 EASTERN DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT), THE PRESENCE OF NATURAL GAS WAS IDENTIFIED BY TC ENERGY PROJECT PERSONNEL WHILE PERFORMING A CASING MITIGATION PROJECT, A SUBSEQUENT LEAKAGE SURVEY WAS CONDUCTED TO IDENTIFY THE LOCATION OF THE SOURCE OF THIS GAS RELEASE. ON MARCH 26TH AT APPROXIMATELY 10:00 EDT, AFTER FURTHER INVESTIGATION, IT WAS IDENTIFIED THE LEAK WAS FROM A VALVE STEM ON LINE D-76 NEAR THE OHIO STATE ROUTE19 HIGHWAY.  AT APPROXIMATELY 13:26 EDT IT WAS CONFIRMED THE COST OF REPAIR MAY EXCEED $145,400 AND THE INCIDENT WAS PROMPTLY REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC). THE INITIAL PLAN FOR REPAIR WAS TO INSTALL STOPPLES, BYPASS THE VALVE, THE CASING AND GAS PIPELINE LOCATED UNDER THE HIGHWAY.  ON APRIL 3RD, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 15:00 EDT A MECHANICAL FITTING THAT WAS LOCATED A FEW FEET DOWNSTREAM OF THE LEAKING VALVE FAILED AND CAUSED THE RELEASE OF APPROXIMATELY 7,254 MCF OF NATURAL GAS INTO THE ATMOSPHERE. THE PROJECT TEAM EVACUATED THE SITE OF THE INCIDENT AND CALLED THE EMERGENCY RESPONDERS. OHIO SR 19 HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED AT 15:25 EDT, AND 129 RESIDENCES WERE EVACUATED. A COURTESY CALL WAS MADE TO THE PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION OF OHIO CHIEF ' FACILITY AND OPERATIONS FIELD DIVISION AT 15:27 EDT AND PHMSA ACCIDENT INCIDENT DIVISION AT 15:39 EDT. THERE WAS NO FIRE, EXPLOSION, INJURY, OR FATALITY. AT 18:49 PM EDT THE AFFECTED SECTION OF THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED AND BLOWN DOWN.  THERE WAS A LOSS OF GAS SERVICE TO TWO COLUMBIA GAS OF OHIO POINTS OF DELIVERY. THE REPAIR WAS COMPLETED BY REMOVING THE FAILED MECHANICAL FITTING AND LEAKING VALVE. LINE D-76 WAS RETURNED INTO SERVICE ON APRIL 6, 2025, AT 16:45 EDT.   THE FAILED MECHANICAL FITTING AND LEAKING VALVE WERE PROTECTED AND PRESERVED AS EVIDENCE AND SENT TO A LOCAL TC ENERGY OFFICE FOR FURTHER INVESTIGATION.  THE ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS IS COMPLETE, AND TC ENERGY IS ACTIONING THE CORRECTIVE ACTIONS.

RELEASE_TYPE_DETAILS: NO UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF PRODUCT.  MECHANICAL FAILURE OF COMPRESSOR FRAME, CRANKCASE, AND ASSOCIATED EQUIPMENT RESULTING IN SMALL, CONTAINED COMPRESSOR LUBRICANT LEAK.  PRODUCT INTENTIONALLY RELEASED DURING STATION SHUTDOWN AND VENTING.
ACCIDENT_IDENTIFIER: SCADA-BASED INFORMATION (SUCH AS ALARM(S), ALERT(S), EVENT(S), AND/OR VOLUME OR PACK CALCULATIONS)
INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 20, 2025, COMPRESSOR #2 WAS PLACED IN MAINTENANCE MODE FOR TROUBLESHOOTING AN ESD VENT VALVE STATUS ISSUE. LOOSE TERMINALS IN THE ACTUATOR CONTROL BOARD WERE IDENTIFIED AND REPAIRED. HOWEVER, MAINTENANCE MODE WAS ACCIDENTALLY LEFT ACTIVE OVERNIGHT.    THE MAINTENANCE MODE SHOULD HAVE TRIGGERED LOCAL ALARMS AND SMS NOTIFICATIONS AT 2, 12, AND 24 HOURS, BUT SMS ALERTS FAILED TO GENERATE. OPERATORS MISSED THE MAINTENANCE STATUS ON LOCAL SCADA DISPLAYS THE MORNING OF MARCH 21.    AT 12:24 ON MARCH 21, COMPRESSOR #2 SUCTION AND DISCHARGE BLOCK VALVES UNEXPECTEDLY CLOSED. NORMALLY THIS WOULD TRIGGER UNIT SHUTDOWN, BUT MAINTENANCE MODE BLOCKED ALL SHUTDOWN SIGNALS, FORCING THE COMPRESSOR TO RUN WITH CLOSED VALVES UNTIL 13:05 WHEN OPERATORS RECEIVED A LOSS OF RUN STATUS ALARM.    PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT 13:14 AND ASSESSED DAMAGE. DURING INSPECTION, A SMALL OIL FIRE WAS DETECTED BY THE SAFETY SYSTEM, CAUSING STATION ESD AT 13:23. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL IMMEDIATELY EXTINGUISHED THE FIRE.    POST-INCIDENT SCADA ANALYSIS REVEALED BOTH VALVES OPENED WITHOUT COMMAND AFTER THE COMPRESSOR STOPPED. THE ENGINE CONTROL PANEL INDICATED SHUTDOWN DUE TO MECHANICAL OVERLOAD.    THE VALVE CLOSURE CAUSED CATASTROPHIC MECHANICAL FAILURE REQUIRING COMPLETE REPLACEMENT OF FRAME, CRANKCASE, CRANKSHAFT, CYLINDERS, AND ASSOCIATED COMPONENTS. THE MAINTENANCE MODE CAPABILITY HAS BEEN ELIMINATED FROM STATION PLC PROGRAMMING.    ROOT CAUSE WAS IDENTIFIED AS MALFUNCTION IN VALVE CONTROL CIRCUITRY, HARDWARE, WIRING, AND CONNECTIONS BETWEEN PLC AND VALVE ACTUATORS. DUE TO THE INTERMITTENT NATURE, SPECIFIC COMPONENT ISOLATION WAS IMPOSSIBLE DURING ONLINE OPERATION. INVESTIGATION ELIMINATED PLC PROGRAMMING AND HARDWARE, NARROWING THE FAULT TO PHYSICAL CONNECTIONS INCLUDING I/O BOARDS, CABINET CONNECTIONS, AND FIELD WIRING. HISTORICAL REVIEW REVEALED SIMILAR VALVE CLOSURES PREVIOUSLY MISDIAGNOSED AS PNEUMATIC ISSUES.    PRIOR TO RESTART, ALL PLC I/O BOARDS FOR BLOCK VALVE CONTROL WERE REPLACED, WIRING CONTINUITY VERIFIED, AND ACTUATOR TERMINATIONS INSPECTED. SINCE RETURNING TO SERVICE APRIL 29, 2025, NO UNEXPECTED VALVE POSITION CHANGES OCCURRED. COMBINED WITH REMOVAL OF PROBLEMATIC MAINTENANCE FUNCTIONS AND NEW MAINTENANCE MODE SAFEGUARDS, WE ARE CONFIDENT THE ROOT CAUSE IS RESOLVED AND SIMILAR ELECTRICAL FAILURES WOULD NOT RESULT IN THE SAME INCIDENT TYPE.

NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 8TH AT ROUGHLY 1924 AN INCREASE IN BUILDING LEL WAS OBSERVED BY PIPELINE CONTROL. AS A RESPONSE TO THE RISING LEL PERCENTAGE, PIPELINE CONTROL SHUTDOWN STATION REMOTELY AND SHORTLY AFTER REMOTELY EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN THE STATION TO COMBAT THE RISING LEL PERCENTAGE IN THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING. FOLLOWING THE COMMAND FROM PIPELINE CONTROL THE DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE FAILED TO CLOSE. OPERATIONS RESPONDED TO INCIDENT AND FOUND AIR PRESSURE HAD NOT ALLEVIATED FROM DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE AS DESIGNED, WHICH HELD THE VALVE IN THE OPEN POSITION. OPERATIONS COMPLETED TROUBLESHOOTING ON DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE AND BELIEVED TO HAVE BLOCK VALVE CLOSED WHICH OPENED ESD VENT VALVES. IT WAS THEN DETERMINED THAT DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE HAD NOT CLOSED ENTIRELY SO PRESSURE CONTINUED TO BE ALLEVIATED BY ESD VENT VALVES UNTIL OPERATORS WERE ABLE TO MANIPULATE VALVES IN THE FIELD COMPLETELY ISOLATING THE SYSTEM. THIS ALLOWED FOR STATION TO DEPRESSURIZE AS PLANNED AND COMPRESSOR BUILDING LEL ISSUE TO BE LOOKED INTO FURTHER. FINDING WAS A BROKEN SITE GLASS PIPE NIPPLE ON UNIT 4 SCRUBBER BOTTLE.    WILLIAMS OPERATIONS AND I&E PERSONNEL COMPLETED TROUBLESHOOTING ON DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE AND FOUND THAT VERSA VALVE ON INSTRUMENT AIR SYSTEM WAS OPERATING SPORADICALLY. VERSA VALVE ASSOCIATED WITH DISCHARGE ESD BLOCK VALVE WAS REPLACED WITH NEW BEFORE BRING STATION BACK INTO NORMAL OPERATIONS. FULL SYSTEM TEST WAS COMPLETED ON THURSDAY MARCH 13TH TO ENSURE THINGS WERE FUNCTIONING AS INTENDED.     FURTHER INVESTIGATION IS ONGOING.

NARRATIVE: BASED ON THE PRELIMINARY CRIMINAL INVESTIGATION CONDUCTED BY THE REEVES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE, SOMETIME ON THE EVENING OF MARCH 5TH, TWO VACUUM TRUCKS NOT AFFILIATED WITH WESTERN MIDSTREAM, ATTEMPTED TO STEAL PETROLEUM FROM A PIPELINE FACILITY DESIGNATED FOR MAINTENANCE. DURING THE ATTEMPTED THEFT, THE PIPELINE SYSTEM IGNITED, CAUSING A FIRE INCIDENT ACROSS 6 WESTERN MIDSTREAM PIPELINES.     IN RESPONSE TO THE INCIDENT, GATHERING PIPELINES SUPPLYING THE AFFECTED TRUNKLINE WERE ISOLATED. THE PRODUCT WITHIN THE PIPELINE WAS DEPRESSURIZED AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE, AND THE REMAINING PRODUCT WAS ALLOWED TO BURN OFF, AS THIS WAS THE SAFEST AND MOST EFFICIENT METHOD TO EXTINGUISH THE FIRE. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE REPORTED. INITIAL AGENCY NOTIFICATIONS MADE:      - REEVES COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS NOTIFIED AT 11:25 PM ON MARCH 5, 2025   - REEVES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE WAS NOTIFIED AT 11:25 PM ON MARCH 5, 2025.   - NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) WAS NOTIFIED AT 2:01 AM ON MARCH 6, 2025.   - RAILROAD COMMISSION OF TEXAS (RRC) WAS NOTIFIED AT 2:04 AM AND AGAIN AT 7:08 AM ON MARCH 6, 2025. NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 2, 2025, AT APPROXIMATELY 16:03, LINE SM-116 WAS SUBJECT TO AN UNINTENDED RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS TO ATMOSPHERE DUE TO A FAILURE IN THE BODY OF THE PIPELINE.  LINE SM-116, A CARBON STEEL TRANSMISSION PIPELINE WITH 16-INCH NPS (NOMINAL PIPE SIZE), 0.281-INCH WALL THICKNESS AT LOCATION OF FAILURE IS OPERATED AND MAINTAINED BY COLUMBIA GAS TRANSMISSION LLC (COLUMBIA), A SUBSIDIARY OF TC ENERGY.    THE PIPELINE FAILURE WAS NOTED BY SCADA ALARMS AND WAS ACKNOWLEDGED AND RESPONDED TO BY COLUMBIA’S GAS CONTROLLER. FIELD OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WERE NOTIFIED AND RESPONDED TO THE FAILED PIPELINE’S LOCATION FOR INVESTIGATION. COLUMBIA FIELD OPERATIONS PERSONNEL COMPLETED THE ISOLATION OF THE FAILED SEGMENT OF SM-116 BY CLOSING TWO MAINLINE VALVES.    THE FAILED PIPELINE EVIDENCE WAS REMOVED AND TRANSPORTED TO A METALLURGICAL FAILURE ANALYSIS (MFA) LABORATORY FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS. UPON RECEIPT THE FAILED PIPELINE EVIDENCE WAS INSPECTED AND PRESENTED NO VISUAL INDICATIONS TO EITHER EXTERNAL OR INTERNAL CORROSION. METALLURGICAL EXAMINATION DETERMINED THAT TOPSIDE MECHANICAL DAMAGE WAS THE DIRECT CAUSE OF THE SM-116 RUPTURE. MULTIPLE DENTS AND GOUGES WERE FOUND ON THE TOP OF THE PIPE MOST LIKELY CAUSED BY MACHINERY, SUCH AS AN EXCAVATOR. THERE WAS NO EVIDENCE OF FATIGUE CRACK GROWTH,  SCC, OR MATERIAL DEFECTS AS CONTRIBUTING FACTORS. IN ADDITION, A ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS REPORT WAS COMPLETED BY AN INDEPENDENT THIRD PARTY DETERMINING THE DIRECT CAUSE OF THE SM-116 RUPTURE WAS DUE TO THIRD PARTY MECHANICAL DAMAGE.     LESSONS LEARNED AND RECOMMENDATIONS WERE PROPOSED TO IMPROVE WORKING RELATIONSHIPS WITH STATE AND/OR LOCAL AUTHORITIES TO HELP WITH OUTREACH TO BUSINESSES THAT HAVE AN ENHANCED THIRD-PARTY EXTERNAL INTERFERENCE (TPEI) RISK BASED ON EQUIPMENT USED ONSITE AND THE ACTIVITIES CONDUCTED.     ALL TIMES ARE REPORTED IN EASTERN STANDARD TIME.
