NARRATIVE: OPERATIONS DISCOVERED GAS LEAK AT MP 97.15 ON SVL-T-T-T WITH HANDHELD LEAK SURVEY EQUIPMENT.  AFTER CONFIRMATION, LINE PRESSURE WAS REGULATED TO 80% OF OPERATING PRESSURE AND EXISTING PRESSURE WAS DRAWN DOWN BY A DOWNSTREAM CUSTOMER.  AFTER EXCAVATION, A TRANSVERSE CRACK WAS VISUALLY IDENTIFIED ON THE PIPE BODY.  THE LINE WAS SUBSEQUENTLY ISOLATED, FURTHER DRAWN DOWN BY CUSTOMERS WITH REMAINING PRESSURE BLOWN DOWN.  THE PIPE SEGMENT WAS THEN CUT OUT AND REPLACED WITH PRETESTED PIPING.  THE CUTOUT PIPE WAS SENT TO A METALLURGICAL LAB FOR ANALYSIS.  LAB ANALYSIS CONCLUDED THE FOLLOWING:  THE PRIMARY FAILURE MECHANISM WAS STRESS CORROSION CRACKING (SCC), DRIVEN BY RESIDUAL TENSILE STRESSES FROM PREEXISTING BENDING DURING CONSTRUCTION IN COMBINATION WITH A MILDLY CORROSIVE, OXYGEN-DEPLETED ENVIRONMENT.  SCC MAY NOT HAVE OCCURRED IN THE ABSENCE OF PIPE BENDING DURING CONSTRUCTION, HIGHLIGHTING THE CRITICAL ROLE OF MECHANICAL LOADING IN THIS FAILURE.  UNINTENTIONAL LOSS VOLUME WAS UPDATED AND REDUCED AFTER FURTHER ANALYSIS.

NARRATIVE: MONTANA DAKOTA UTILITIES (MDU) EMPLOYEE RESPONDED TO A NO-HEAT/GAS ODOR CALL AT THE FARMHOUSE NEAR THE LEAK AT 3:49 AM MT/ 4:49 AM CT AM MONDAY 2/17/25.  CUSTOMER'S FURNACE WOULD RUN FOR A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME AND THEN SHUT DOWN. MDU EMPLOYEE SAID THE PRESSURE AT THE CUSTOMER'S HOUSE WAS ZERO. CUSTOMER ALSO SAID SHE HEARD A LOUD HISSING, FIRST HEARING THE HISSING ABOUT 3:00 PM MT/4:00 PM CT SUNDAY 2/16/25. MDU EMPLOYEE WENT TO THE FARM TAP TAKE-OFF AND PRESSURE WAS ZERO ON THE MDU SIDE THERE AS WELL.  THE SOUND OF HISSING WAS LOUD, AND MDU EMPLOYEE CALLED WBI GAS CONTROL AND REQUESTED WBI EMPLOYEES TO ASSIST, WBI EMPLOYEES RESPONDED TO THE SCENE.     MDU EMPLOYEE ALSO SAID THAT HE WAS RESPONDING TO GAS ODOR CALLS IN SIDNEY ON 2-16-25, HIS FIRST CALL WAS 2:00 AM MT/3:00 AM CT. THE GAS ODOR/LEL AND CALLS VARIED IN NUMBER WITH WIND DIRECTION; WHEN THE WIND WAS BLOWING TOWARD TOWN CALLS INCREASED.  WBI EMPLOYEES RESPONDED TO THE LOCATION AND CONFIRMED SUSPECTED AREA OF THE LEAK TO WBI GAS CONTROL AT 7:30AM MST ON 2/17/25. PIPELINE WAS CONTINUALLY MONITORED UNTIL IT WAS SHUT IN AND BLOWN DOWN AT 2:00PM MST ON 2/17/25. LINE WAS REPAIRED AND RETURNED TO SERVICE BY 12:30AM MST ON 2/18/25.    SUPPLEMENTAL INFORMATION 6/13/2025:  THE FAILED SECTION WAS SENT INTO A LAB FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. THE RESULTS FROM THIS ANALYSIS CONFIRMED THAT THE CAUSE OF THE FAILURE WAS LACK OF PENETRATION AND MISALIGNMENT OF THE WELD WHEN THE WELD WAS MADE DURING THE INITIAL CONSTRUCTION OF THE PIPELINE IN 1947.

NARRATIVE: ON 2/15/2025 THE VERNON COMPRESSOR STATION LOST COMMERCIAL POWER. IN ADDITION, THE EMERGENCY GENERATOR FAILED TO START, AND THE STATION BATTERY UPS SYSTEM FAILED TO SWITCH TO BATTERY BACKUP. THE UPS IS USED TO MAINTAIN POWER TO STATION NETWORKS, PLC'S, AND HMIS FOR SHORT PERIODS OF TIME UNTIL EITHER COMMERCIAL OR EMERGENCY GENERATOR POWER BECOMES AVAILABLE. WITH NO POWER, A NORMAL STOP COMMAND WAS ISSUED TO ALL THREE STATION TURBINES. THE TURBINES STOPPED, THE UNIT BLOWDOWN VALVES OPENED, AND THE FUEL SUPPLY VALVES CLOSED. THE STATION ESD BLOWDOWN VALVES ARE DESIGNED TO DEFAULT TO THE FAIL-OPEN POSITION AND MUST BE MANUALLY RESET. STATION BLOWDOWN VALVES WERE SET TO OPEN AT 55PSI. THE ESD PRESSURE DROPPED BELOW 50PSI, WHICH ACTIVATED THE VALVES, AND BEGAN RELIEVING THE STATION PRESSURE. TECHNICIANS ARRIVED ON LOCATION AND BLOCKED IN THE STATION DURING THE BLOWDOWN PROCESS, PRIOR TO A FULL STATION BLOWDOWN. CORRECTIVE ACTIONS TAKEN TO HAVE THE STATION BACK ONLINE BY 2/16/2025 AT 4:00AM.     REMEDIAL ACTIONS TAKEN, SMALL LEAK WAS FOUND ON THE ESD SYSTEM. THE ESD PILOTS ON BLOWDOWN VALVES WERE RESET TO 30PSI AND THE SIDE GATE ESD PILOTS WERE REST TO 45PSI. ORIGINAL CONSTRUCTION DESIGN WAS CHANGED FROM UTILIZING ESD GAS AS PILOT PRESSURE TO POWER GAS. ALL REGULATORS WERE TESTED AND TRENDED TO ENSURE PROPER OPERATION. GENERATOR LOGIC ISSUES WERE IDENTIFIED AND CORRECTED, THEN MULTIPLE SIMULATED POWER LOSS SCENARIOS WERE RUN TO ENSURE THE GENERATOR TRANSFERRED PROPERLY AND CARRIED THE STATION LOAD.

NARRATIVE: ON 02/10/2025, A LANDOWNER CONTACTED A CNX REPRESENTATIVE TO INQUIRE ABOUT AN AUDIBLE POTENTIAL GAS RELEASE AT THE MOR40 VALVE SETTING. IT WAS INITIALLY ASSUMED THE OBSERVED NOISE WAS FROM CONTEMPORANEOUS CNX PIGGING OPERATIONS BLOWING DOWN THE BARREL AT BAILEY VALVE SET TO LAUNCH A PIG.  THE CNX REPRESENTATIVE CONTACTED THE LANDOWNER BACK TO RELAY THAT INFORMATION AND THE LANDOWNER THANKED THE CNX REPRESENTATIVE FOR THE RESPONSE.  DURING THIS PIGGING OPERATION THAT WAS CONDUCTED PURSUANT TO CNX PIGGING PROCEDURES AND OPERATIONAL PARAMETERS,  THE PRESSURE INCREASED IN THE 1260 MOR40 U2 PIPELINE (UPSTREAM OF THE PIPELINE BEING PIGGED). BASED ON A TREND OF THE DATA, THE PRESSURE APPEARS TO HAVE REMAINED BELOW THE MAOP OF THE PIPELINE AND THE SET-POINT FOR THE PRV LOCATED UPSTREAM AT THE MOR40 WELL PAD.  HOWEVER, THE PRV OPENED AT THIS TIME. THE PRV REMAINED OPEN AND RELEASED GAS UNTIL A PRODUCTION OPERATOR ARRIVED ONSITE APPROXIMATELY TWO HOURS LATER AND FOUND THE PRV RELIEVING. THE PRODUCTION OPERATOR SECURED THE RELEASE WITHIN APPROXIMATELY 9 MINUTES OF HIS FINDING.  THE PRV WAS SET TO RELIEVE AT 1300 PSIG; BASED ON THE PRESSURE DATA TREND IT APPEARS TO HAVE RELIEVED AT APPROXIMATELY 1126 PSIG. THE PRV WAS TESTED ON 02/11/2025 AND FOUND TO RELIEVE AT 1148 PSIG; IT WAS THEN RESET TO 1300 PSIG. THE PRV WAS LATER THOROUGHLY INSPECTED BY THE MANUFACTURER (MERCER) WHO FOUND EVIDENCE OF NORMAL WEAR ON VALVE INTERNAL COMPONENTS. THE MANUFACTURER STATED THAT THIS WEAR COULD CAUSE A POTENTIAL LEAK, WHICH IN TURN COULD CAUSE A VALVE TO ACTIVATE BELOW ITS SET PRESSURE; IN ADDITION, THE MANUFACTURER TESTED THE SPRING FOR PROPER FUNCTION AND FOUND IT TO BE WITHIN TOLERANCE. AFTER COMPLETING THEIR INSPECTION, NO DEFINITIVE CAUSE FOR THE SUB-SET PRESSURE ACTIVATION WAS IDENTIFIED BY THE MANUFACTURER.  CNX HAS TAKEN THIS VALVE OUT OF SERVICE AND REPLACED IT WITH A NEW VALVE TO AVOID A RECURRENCE.

DURING THE SECOND ATTEMPT, THEY WERE UNABLE TO TOUCH THE PIPE AS THE INITIAL EXPOSURE SILTED BACK IN DUE TO DYNAMIC CONDITIONS OF THE MS RIVER. THE PIPELINE HAS BEEN CUT AND CAPPED ON BOTH ENDS AND TEXAS GAS IS IN THE PROCESS OF ABANDONING THE MLS 16-4 PIPELINE. THERE ARE NO INTENTIONS OF RETURNING THIS PIPELINE TO SERVICE AND IT IS NOT EXPECTED THAT A CAUSE WILL BE DETERMINED. A FINAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED WHEN THE ABANDONMENT PROCESS IS COMPLETE.

STATUS_WHEN_IDENTIFIED: IDLE
UPSTREAM_OPRTNL_CNTRL_DETAIL: UTILIZED AIR MOVING EQUIPMENT TO REMOVE RESIDUAL VAPORS
DOWNSTREAM_OPRTNL_CNTRL_DETAIL: UTILIZED AIR MOVING EQUIPMENT TO REMOVE RESIDUAL VAPORS
INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
OPERATION_DETAILS: INCORRECT OPERATION OF GAS MONITORING EQUIPMENT
NARRATIVE: A PROJECT WAS INITIATED TO INSTALL A PIG TRAP APPROXIMATELY ONE MILE FROM THE IRION COMPRESSOR STATION.  ORIGINALLY DELAYED DUE TO INCLEMENT WEATHER, THE PIPELINE WAS SUCCESSFULLY ISOLATED AND VENTED TO A FLARE ON JANUARY 29, 2025.  A CONTRACT INSPECTOR WORKING FOR APPLIED CONSULTANTS, ISSUED THE WORK PERMIT, AUTHORIZING THE NIGHT CONTRACT CREW TO COMMENCE COLD-CUTTING AND WELDING OPERATIONS WITHIN A BELL HOLE. TO MITIGATE THE PRESENCE OF FUMES EMANATING FROM BOTH ENDS OF THE PIPELINE INSIDE THE BELL HOLE, CONTRACTORS INSTALLED PLUGS TO FACILITATE HOT WORK. A FORMANS PLUG WAS PLACED AT THE OPPOSITE END OF THE WORKSITE, WHILE A MUD PLUG WAS POSITIONED BEHIND THE AREA WHERE THE WELDER WOULD BE BEVELING AND WELDING. ONCE THE VISIBLE GAS FUMES DISSIPATED FROM THE BELL HOLE, THE WELDER AND HIS ASSISTANT ENTERED THE SPACE TO PREPARE FOR BEVELING. THE FOUR-GAS  MONITOR WAS CHECKED TO CONFIRM THE ABSENCE OF LOWER EXPLOSIVE LIMITS (LELS) IN THE AREA. AT APPROXIMATELY 3:00 AM, THE WELDER IGNITED HIS TORCH AT THE END OF THE PIPE TO VERIFY THE PRESENCE OF ANY RESIDUAL GAS.  MOMENTS LATER, A FLASH FIRE ERUPTED AROUND THE WELDER, IGNITING HIS CLOTHING AND RESULTING IN BURN INJURIES. THE INVESTIGATION SHOWED THAT THE MUD PLUG LOCATED BEHIND THE WELDER WAS LEAKING GAS FUMES INTO THE IMMEDIATE AREA. THE FOUR-GAS MONITOR USED TO DETECT THE LELS, WAS SITTING AT THE TOP OF THE BELL HOLE WITH A THREE-FOOT HOSE HANGING INTO THE SIX-FOOT HOLE, APPROXIMATELY SIX TO EIGHT FEET FROM THE WORK LOCATION. AT THIS DISTANCE AND LOCATION, THE MONITOR WAS UNABLE TO DETECT THE ATMOSPHERE IN THE WORK AREA. THE CAUSE WAS DETERMINED TO BE INCORRECT OPERATION OF GAS MONITORING EQUIPMENT. TARGA HAS INITIATED MULTIPLE EFFORTS TO MITIGATE AND PREVENT RECURRENCE. A MUD PLUG INSTALLATION PROCEDURE HAS BEEN DEVELOPED AS GUIDANCE FOR CONTRACTORS WORKING FOR TARGA. ADDITIONALLY ALL CONTRACT INSPECTORS HAVE BEEN RETRAINED ON SAFETY ENFORCEMENT EXPECTATIONS AND PROPER USE OF GAS MONITORING.

NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 29, 2024, BATESVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION 63 ON THE TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE (TGP) SYSTEM EXPERIENCED AN ESD EVENT. GAS CONTROL IMMEDIATELY CONTACTED TGP OPERATIONS AT 01:45 CST. THE OPERATIONS TEAM ARRIVED ON SITE AT 2:05 CST AND CONFIRMED THAT GAS WAS VENTING. THE KM TECHNICIAN IDENTIFIED VALVE 63-1S TO BE UNEXPECTEDLY OPEN AND CLOSED THE VALVE AND SECURED THE GAS RELEASE. THE NRC WAS CALLED AT 3:04 CST BY KM PERSONNEL AND THE INITIAL REPORT WAS CAPTURED AS RECEIVED BY 3:07 CST. A 48-HOUR NRC REPORT WAS MADE TO UPDATE THE GAS RELEASE VOLUME TO 49,399 MCF. INITIAL INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT LIGHTNING HAD STRUCK THE BUILDING PROTECTION SYSTEM IN COMPRESSOR BUILDING "B" CAUSING A LOSS OF COMMUNICATION WITH THE STATION CONTROLS. THIS LOSS OF COMMUNICATION ACTIVATED THE TGP ESD FAIL SAFE SYSTEM.     FOLLOWING AN INVESTIGATION REGARDING THE VALVE OPERATION, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE ISSUE WAS THE FAILURE OF AN OBSOLETE ACTUATOR WHICH RESULTED IN THE VALVE ACTUATING TO THE OPEN POSITION RATHER THAN THE INTENDED CLOSED POSITION. THE FAILURE MAY HAVE BEEN ATTRIBUTED TO INTERNAL DEBRIS OR WORN COMPONENTS WITHIN THE ACTUATOR. UPON FURTHER REVIEW OF THE ESD SYSTEM, IT WAS CONCLUDED THAT SIX VALVES, INCLUDING VALVE 63-1S, WERE EQUIPPED WITH ESD ACTIVATION THAT WAS NOT FUNCTIONALLY REQUIRED. AS A RESULT, AN INITIATIVE WAS IMPLEMENTED TO RECONFIGURE THESE VALVES WITHIN THE SYSTEM TO REDUCE THE LIKELIHOOD OF MECHANICAL FAILURES.

NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 25, 2024, A FIRE OCCURRED AT THE NORTH BIG SPRING COMPRESSOR STATION, 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF BIG SPRING, TEXAS IN HOWARD COUNTY.    AT APPROXIMATELY 0605 SCADA NOTIFIED OPERATIONS ABOUT AN ISSUE AT THE NORTH BIG SPRING COMPRESSOR STATION.    0645, IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT COMPRESSOR UNIT #6 WAS ON FIRE, ESD'D THE STATION AND CALLED THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT TO THE STATION.    AT 0936, IT WAS DETERMINED THE REPAIRS AND DAMAGES WOULD EXCEED THE $145,400.00 REPORTABLE DOLLAR CRITERIA AND THE NRC AND THE TXRRC WAS NOTIFIED.    LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT EXTINGUISHED THE FIRE.    THIS IS A LEASED COMPRESSOR FROM A 3RD PARTY    THE LEAK OF THE GAS AND THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE IS STILL UNDER INVESTIGATION. A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED ONCE INVESTIGATION IS COMPLETE.    **THE FINAL INVESTIGATION FOUND THAT COMPRESSOR CYLINDER #1 DISCHARGE FLANGE BOLTS WERE BROKEN AND THE GASKETED FLANGE CONNECTION FAILED WHICH RESULTED IN THE RELEASE OF GAS. COMPRESSOR CYLINDER #1 IS LOCATED WITHIN CLOSE PROXIMITY TO THE ENGINE AND ASSOCIATED EXHAUST COMPONENTS WHICH LIKELY RESULTED IN THE IGNITION OF THE GAS. ADDITIONAL FINDINGS INDICATED THAT UNIT #6 HAD EXPERIENCED SIGNIFICANT SEVERE VIBRATION. FINDINGS INCLUDE; MOVEMENT/SHIFTING OF COMPRESSOR SKID ON GRAVEL FOUNDATION, NUMEROUS FAILED, LOOSE, OR MISSING BOLTS/FASTENERS ON 1ST STAGE COMPRESSOR CYLINDER ASSEMBLIES AND CYLINDER SUPPORTS, LOOSE CROSS TIE BARS ON COMPRESSOR FRAME, FAILED THROUGH BOLTS ON DISCHARGE BOTTLE SUPPORTS, FAILED PROCESS PIPING CLAMP ON CYLINDER #2.  THIS COMPRESSOR WAS PERMANENTLY REMOVED AND BYPASSED WITH NO REPLACEMENT.  FACILITY STARTED BACK UP WITH UNIT #5 AT 1300 ON DECEMBER 25, 2024.     TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE, THE FOLLOWING WILL BE CONSIDERED:  - VISUALLY INSPECT ENGINE MOUNTING HARDWARE FOR LOOSENESS OR ABNORMALITIES ON ALL ARCHROCK LEASED UNITS IN THE REGION AND REPORT ANY FINDINGS TO ARCHROCK FOR EVALUATION. ETC OPERATIONS  - VERIFY PROPER TORQUE AND MOUNTING ON ARCHROCK LEASED AND MAINTAINED COMPRESSOR PACKAGES. ARCHROCK  - ENSURE COMPRESSOR PACKAGE VIBRATION SHUTDOWNS ARE ADJUSTED PROPERLY AND FULLY FUNCTIONAL ON ARCHROCK LEASED AND MAINTAINED COMPRESSOR PACKAGES. ARCHROCK  - AVOID OPERATING OR RESTARTING UNITS THAT HAVE EXPERIENCED CRITICAL SHUTDOWNS UNTIL A THOROUGH EVALUATION CAN BE PERFORMED BY A SUBJECT MATER EXPERT AND THE EQUIPMENT HAS BEEN DEEMED SAFE TO OPERATE. NARRATIVE: ON 11/24/24 AT APPROXIMATELY 20:56, A PIPELINE RUPTURE OCCURRED ON GULF SOUTH'S BISTINEAU STORAGE FIELD WELL LINE A-11, ALSO REFERRED TO AS INDEX 900-8. THE RUPTURE WAS INITIALLY IDENTIFIED BY A GULF SOUTH OPERATIONS EMPLOYEE WHO LIVES ONSITE. THE BI-DIRECTIONAL 8-INCH O.D. WELL LINE CONNECTS THE SINGLE A-11 WELL TO THE A LATERAL PIPELINE TRUNK BETWEEN THE COMPRESSOR STATION FIELD HEADER AND THE STORAGE FIELD.    THE A-11 WELL WAS SHUT IN, ALONG WITH THE CORRESPONDING VALVES LOCATED ON THE A LATERAL TRUNK PIPELINE, ALLOWING GULF SOUTH TO TOTALLY ISOLATE THE AFFECTED WELL LINE FROM THE STORAGE FIELD AND COMPRESSOR STATION FIELD HEADER. THIS ENABLED THE AFFECTED LINE SEGMENT TO BE FULLY DEPRESSURIZED. THE LINE SEGMENT REMAINED ISOLATED AND DEPRESSURIZED UNTIL A PIPE REPLACEMENT WAS COMPLETED. REPAIR WAS COMPLETED ON 12/11/24 BY PIPE REPLACEMENT. THE FAILED SECTION OF PIPE AND A SHORT PUP FROM EACH END OF THE REMAINING PIPE WERE INSPECTED ON SITE BY GULF SOUTH'S METALLURGIST, WRAPPED, AND SENT TO A LAB FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS WHICH HAS NOT YET BEEN COMPLETED. THE FIELD EXAMINATION DETERMINED THE PRELIMINARY CAUSE OF THE FAILURE TO BE INTERNAL CORROSION.     ADDITIONALLY, PHMSA ASKED HOW THIS PIPELINE IS TREATED. IT IS TREATED USING A CONTINUOUS INJECTION PUMP. A CORROSION INHIBITOR, SPECIFICALLY COASTAL CHEMICAL 1141-C, IS INJECTED WHEN THE FIELD IS IN WITHDRAWAL MODE. ON AVERAGE, 0.25 TO 0.5 GALLONS PER DAY OF CHEMICAL IS PUMPED.    1/24/25 UPDATED RESPONSE TO C3G AND PART H PER PHMSA REQUEST.

CAUSE_DETAILS: EXCAVATION DAMAGE BY OPERATOR'S CONTRACTOR (SECOND PARTY)
EX_PARTY_TYPE: EXCAVATION DAMAGE BY OPERATOR'S CONTRACTOR (SECOND PARTY)
ROOT_CAUSE_TYPE: FACILITY MARKED INACCURATELY DUE TO INCORRECT FACILITY RECORDS/MAPS
NARRATIVE: SUMMARY OF EVENT  ON SATURDAY, NOVEMBER 9, 2024, AT 1:33 PM LOCAL TIME (CENTRAL STANDARD TIME) A CONTRACTOR PERFORMING WORK FOR PEOPLE'S GAS LIGHT & COKE (PGL) WAS DOING EXCAVATION WORK ADJACENT TO COUNTY ROAD 2700 NORTH AT APPROXIMATE LATITUDE/LONGITUDE 40.266609/-88.432686.  WHILE PERFORMING THE EXCAVATION WORK WITH A BACKHOE, AT THE COMPLETION OF A SWIPE, THE OPERATOR HEARD A POP/HISS AND OBSERVED GAS BLOWING INTO THE AIR.  THE NATURAL GAS TRANSMISSION LINE BEING EXCAVATED WAS AT APPROXIMATELY 1,750 PSIG.  THE CONTRACTOR PERSONNEL CONTACTED PGL EMPLOYEES WORKING IN THE VICINITY.  PGL RESPONDED WITH ON SHIFT PERSONNEL AUGMENTED BY OFF WORK EMPLOYEES.  THE AREA WAS MADE SAFE AND THE LEAK WAS ISOLATED USING MANUAL TRANSMISSION LINE VALVES.  THE AFFECTED PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED AT APPROXIMATELY 3:00 PM AND WAS DEPRESSURIZED TO ATMOSPHERIC PRESSURE AT 3:30 PM.  AT 4:50 PM, PGL ESTIMATED THE RELEASE TO EXCEED 3,000,000 STANDARD CUBIC FEET (SCF).  PGL NOTIFIED THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) AT 5:06 PM AND THE ILLINOIS COMMERCE COMMISSION (ICC) AT 5:29 PM.  A FOLLOW UP CALL WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 11, 2024 AT 2:43 PM, DURING WHICH PGL REPORTED A CALCULATED RELEASE VOLUME OF 4.953 MMCF.    CAUSES  THE DIRECT CAUSE OF THE RELEASE WAS DAMAGE CAUSED BY THE BACKHOE SHOVEL CONTACT TO SURROUNDING SOIL AND/OR INSTALLED EQUIPMENT INCLUDING THE FOLLOWING:  A 1" 3,000 PSIG RATED, THREADED W-K-M DYNASEAL QUARTER TURN BALL VALVE INSTALLED AT THE 12 O'CLOCK POSITION ON THE 12" CARBON STEEL TRANSMISSION PIPELINE. ON ONE END (TOWARD ATMOSPHERE) OF THIS VALVE WAS A 1" HEX HEAD PLUG AND ON THE OTHER END (LEADING TO THE 12" PIPELINE), WAS A 1"X3" SCHEDULE 160 NIPPLE THAT WAS SCREWED INTO A THREAD-O-LET THAT WAS WELDED TO THE PIPELINE.     THE ROOT CAUSE WAS AS-BUILT DRAWINGS WERE NOT UPDATED TO REFLECT THE ABOVE EQUIPMENT FOLLOWING INSTALLATION IN MARCH 2024.  DRAWINGS WERE NOT UPDATED BECAUSE THE INSTALLATION WAS THOUGHT TO BE TEMPORARY IN NATURE TO SUPPORT FUTURE PURGING OPERATIONS THAT WOULD BE NEEDED FOR PLANNED CONSTRUCTION.  BECAUSE DRAWINGS WERE NOT UPDATED, INFORMATION REGARDING INSTALLATION OF THE EQUIPMENT WAS NOT AVAILABLE TO THE PERSONNEL PERFORMING EXCAVATION ACTIVITIES.  CONTRIBUTING TO THE EVENT WAS THE BACKHOE OPERATOR PERFORMING EXCAVATION WITHIN THE TOLERANCE ZONE OF 18" OF THE PIPELINE.  THE BACKHOE OPERATOR'S OPERATOR QUALIFICATION WAS REMOVED BECAUSE OF THE INCIDENT AND IS PENDING REMEDIATION AND REQUALIFICATION.      AMOUNT OF GAS RELEASED:  BASED ON FURTHER CALCULATIONS USING RELIEF VALVE CAPACITY METHODOLOGY, PGL HAS REVISED THE CALCULATED RELEASE VOLUME TO BE 5,163,905 SCF. THIS INCLUDES 4,842,969 SCF PRIOR TO ISOLATION AND 320,936 SCF FOLLOWING ISOLATION.  A 1.25" OPENING WAS USED DURING THE CALCULATION.  THIS OPENING PROVIDES A CONSERVATIVE CALCULATION RELATED TO THE 1" THREADOLET CONNECTION, WHICH WAS FORCIBLY REMOVED BY THE BACKHOE AND/OR ADJACENT SOIL BEING COMPRESSED DURING EXCAVATION.      ACTIONS TAKEN  1. ALL WORK ASSOCIATED WITH THE PROJECT WAS SHUTDOWN PENDING PGL INVESTIGATION.   2. DIRECTION PROVIDED TO PGL AND CONTRACT RESOURCES THAT W-K-M DYNASEALVALVES WILL NOT BE BURIED IN THE FUTURE. A PLAN WAS DEVELOPED TO ADDRESS OTHER LOCATIONS WHERE THESE STYLE VALVES HAVE BEEN INSTALLED.  (UPDATE: OTHER LOCATIONS WITH VALVES INSTALLED WITH THE SAME CONFIGURATION HAVE BEEN REMOVED).    3. DIRECTED PGL AND CONTRACT RESOURCES THAT ANYTHING BEING INSTALLED ON PGL FACILITIES IS REQUIRED TO BE DOCUMENTED IN THE AS-BUILT DRAWINGS.  4. DIRECTED CONTRACT RESOURCES THAT ONLY HAND DIGGING WILL OCCUR INSIDE OF THE 18 INCH TOLERANCE ZONE.     5. INFORMED CONTRACT RESOURCES THAT ANY HIT THAT RESULTS IN A RELEASE OF GAS THE EXCAVATOR MUST CALL 911 AND JULIE MUST BE NOTIFIED.

NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 31ST, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 2:00 AM MST, WILLIAMS HAD A CATERPILLAR 3606 COMPRESSOR UNIT (UNIT) GO DOWN AT THE LOCHBUIE COMPRESSOR STATION ("FACILITY"). THE 3RD PARTY LEASED COMPRESSOR SUPPLIER, KODIAK GAS SERVICES (KODIAK), HAD AN OPERATION TECHNICIAN GO TO THE SITE AND RESTART THE UNIT, WHICH REQUIRES A PURGE TO SAFELY BRING THE UNIT BACK ONLINE.  FOR THE SITE-SPECIFIC UNIT PURGE, NATURAL GAS IS VENTED TO ATMOSPHERE FROM A 2" BLOWDOWN VALVE THROUGH A SILENCER STACK. AFTER THE UNIT IS COMPLETELY PURGED, KODIAK'S STARTUP PROCESS IS TO CLOSE THE BLOWDOWN VALVE BEFORE LOADING THE UNIT; HOWEVER, THE KODIAK TECHNICIAN DID NOT PERFORM THIS STEP AND AFTER LOADING THE UNIT AT APPROXIMATELY 4:00 AM MST, THE KODIAK TECHNICIAN LEFT THE FACILITY.  WILLIAMS NOTICED THAT THE FACILITY'S VOLUME THROUGHPUT WAS LESS THAN WHEN THE UNIT WAS RUNNING EARLIER, BUT THERE WAS NO INDICATION OF A LEAK NOR OF AN ISSUE ON GOING. WILLIAMS CONTINUED ITS INVESTIGATION THROUGH THE DAY OF OCTOBER 31ST. AT APPROXIMATELY 4:00 PM MST A WILLIAMS OPERATION TECHNICIAN IDENTIFIED THE IMPROPER VALVE ALIGNMENT OF THE 2" BLOWDOWN VALVE (VALVE IN OPEN POSITION) AND REALIZED THAT IT WAS THE CAUSE OF THE FACILITY'S VOLUME THROUGHPUT DECREASE. THE OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN IMMEDIATELY CLOSED THE VALVE.

NARRATIVE: A TRANSMISSION LINE LEAK WAS IDENTIFIED IN EGO'S GAS STORAGE SYSTEM (GROUP 8) AT APPROXIMATELY 16:15 EDT ON 10/18/2024 NEAR 9232 MUDBROOK ST NW IN JACKSON, OH AFTER A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC CALLED IN AN ODOR COMPLAINT. LOCAL PERSONNEL UTILIZED VALVES TO SHUT IN THE LEAKING LINE AROUND 18:00 EDT THAT SAME DAY. AN ORIGINAL ASSESSMENT OF THE COSTS TO REPLACE THE LINE WERE UNDER THE $145,400 OPERATOR LOSS THRESHOLD FOR AN INCIDENT PER 191.3. ON 11/5/2024 AT APPROXIMATELY 9:20 EST, THE ENBRIDGE GAS OHIO PROJECT MANAGER FOR THE REPLACEMENT OF THE SECTION OF PIPE WITH THE LEAK INFORMED PIPELINE SAFETY THAT COSTS WERE NOW LIKELY TO EVENTUALLY EXCEED THE $145,400 THRESHOLD DUE TO HIGHER THAN EXPECTED ENVIRONMENTAL AND CONTRACTOR COSTS. THE LEAK WAS CALLED IN AS AN INCIDENT AT THAT TIME TO THE OHIO PUBLIC UTILITY COMMISSION AND THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER. THE SECTION OF PIPE WITH THE LEAK HAS BEEN REMOVED AND SENT TO A 3RD PARTY FOR ANALYSIS. A NEW SECTION OF PIPE HAS BEEN INSTALLED TO REPLACE THE OLD LINE BUT IS NOT YET IN SERVICE. A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE PROVIDED WITH THE IN-SERVICE DATE AND RESULTS OF THE 3RD PARTY LAB ANALYSIS.    4/25/25 SUPPLEMENTAL UPDATE: LABORATORY ANALYSIS HAS BEEN COMPLETED. THE RESULTS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS INDICATE THAT THE LEAK OCCURRED AT AN AREA OF DISCRETE PITTING FROM INTERNAL CORROSION AT THE BOTTOM OF THE PIPE (6:17 ORIENTATION). THE LEAK WAS LIKELY CAUSED BY THE PRESENCE OF AN ACIDIC BRINE (PH 2.92, MEASURED AT AMBIENT PRESSURE) WITH HIGH SALINITY. THE LOCALIZED CORROSION LIKELY OCCURRED AT BREAKS IN AN OTHERWISE THICK PROTECTIVE SCALE OR AT AREAS UNDER DEPOSITS AT THE BOTTOM OF THE PIPE. IT IS POSSIBLE THAT THERE WAS A GAS PHASE CONTRIBUTION (I.E., FROM CO2, H2S, AND/OR 02 CORROSION), HOWEVER, THE GAS PHASE CONTRIBUTION WAS LIKELY SECONDARY TO THE PRESENCE OF THE ACIDIC BRINE. THE CAUSE OF THE BRINE ACIDITY AT THIS LOCATION IS UNKNOWN, BUT A PH VALUE OF 2.92, MEASURED AT AMBIENT PRESSURE, IS IN EXCESS OF WHAT IS POSSIBLE FROM CO2 GAS DISSOLVED IN THE PROCESS STREAM. ENBRIDGE GAS OHIO HAS PLACED PERMANENT UT GAUGES ON THE LINE FOR FUTURE INTERNAL CORROSION MONITORING.    PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE ON 1/7/25.    5/12/2025 UPDATED FINAL REPORT: A14 WAS CHANGED TO "ONSHORE PIPELINE" TO REFLECT THAT PIPE WAS CONSIDERED A TRANSMISSION ASSET FOR REGULATORY PURPOSES.    5/20/2025: FINALIZING SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WITH CHANGES TO A14.    1/12/2026: UPDATED FINAL REPORT: C1 WAS CHANGED FROM "INTERSTATE" TO "INTRASTATE".

NARRATIVE: ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 9, 2024, ASPEN DEVELOPERS CORPORATION (ASPEN DEVELOPERS) WAS OPERATING A TRACK HOE TO COMPLETE ROAD WORK (GRADING AND DRAINAGE) IN PREPARATION FOR A WATER LINE INSTALLATION IN THE SIERRA NEVADA MOUNTAINS, APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES EAST OF THE WASHOE COUNTY CENSUS-DESIGNATED PLACE OF INCLINE VILLAGE, NEVADA. WHILE PERFORMING WORK ON THE EXISTING DIRT ROAD, THE TRACK HOE OPERATOR PUNCTURED THE TOP OF GREAT BASIN GAS TRANSMISSION COMPANY'S (GREAT BASIN) 8-INCH DIAMETER NORTH TAHOE LATERAL GAS PIPELINE WITH A TOOTH OF THE TRACK HOE BUCKET.  THE DAMAGE OCCURRED AT THE APPROXIMATE MILEPOST LOCATION OF NT-13.56 AND RESULTED IN THE UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF GAS FROM THE STEEL TRANSMISSION FACILITY. GREAT BASIN RECEIVED NOTIFICATION OF THE DAMAGE AT APPROXIMATELY 16:00 PDT    ON OCTOBER 9, 2024, BY TRUCKEE MEADOWS FIRE AND RESCUE, AND PROMPTLY DISPATCHED EMERGENCY RESPONSE PERSONNEL TO THE SCENE FOR ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION AND REMEDIATION.      THE PIPELINE AND HAZARDOUS MATERIAL SAFETY ADMINISTRATION (PHMSA) NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) ON OCTOBER 9, 2024, AT 20:14 PDT AND ASSIGNED INCIDENT #1413294 DUE TO THE NATURAL GAS RELEASE ANTICIPATED TO EXCEED THREE MILLION CUBIC FEET. GREAT BASIN DID NOT SHUTDOWN THE FACILITY TO AVOID SERVICE INTERRUPTION TO A DOWNSTREAM SHIPPER (LOCAL DISTRIBUTION COMPANY).  A REPAIR CLAMP WAS USED TO CONTROL THE FLOW OF GAS ON OCTOBER 10, 2024, AT 20:49 PDT. A SUPPLEMENTAL NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON OCTOBER 14, 2024, AT 09:08 PDT AND ASSIGNED INCIDENT #1413672. GREAT BASIN CALCULATED THE TOTAL RELEASE OF GAS TO TOTAL 7,304 MCF  .     GREAT BASIN PERFORMED A PRELIMINARY INTERNAL INVESTIGATION TO DETERMINE CONTRIBUTING FACTORS ASSOCIATED WITH THE INCIDENT ON OCTOBER 16, 2024, AND DETERMINED THAT THE FACILITY LOCATE MARKS, ESTABLISHED ON AUGUST 28, 2024, DEVIATED FROM THE ACTUAL FACILITY LOCATION BY 19 FEET AND THE LOCATOR INCORRECTLY MARKED THE FACILITY DUE TO BLEED OFF ONTO A NEARBY, UNMARKED STEEL WATER LINE. NARRATIVE: RE: OMO RD COMPRESSOR STATION INCIDENT  NRC INCIDENT REPORT #1413461 & FOLLOW UP #1413594    ON OCTOBER 5, 2024 AT APPROXIMATELY 3:09 AM, EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD) VENT VALVES (BDV 9010) AND (BDV 9011) OPENED BECAUSE OF A LOSS OF POWER GAS PRESSURE USED TO HOLD THE ESD VALVES CLOSED.  PIPELINE ISOLATION (SDV-9001) WAS ALSO OPEN, RESULTING IN A GAS RELEASE OF ~5.2 MMSCF TO ATMOSPHERE.   THE LOSS OF POWER GAS WAS CAUSED BY OF A COMBINATION OF AN ISOLATION OF THE POWER GAS HEADER AND A LEAKING REGULATOR IN THE POWER GAS SYSTEM.  ON OCTOBER 3, 2024, AT 10:56 A.M., THE FUEL GAS SUPPLY VALVE (SDV 9008) TRIPPED / CLOSED DUE TO HIGH PRESSURE (>475 PSIG).  THE HIGH PRESSURE WAS CAUSED BY DAYTIME HEATING AND THERMAL EXPANSION OF THE GAS IN THE FUEL GAS SYSTEM. THE OMO ROAD COMPRESSOR STATION WAS SHUTDOWN AT THE TIME, MINIMIZING FUEL GAS USE AND CAUSING FUEL GAS HEADER PRESSURE TO FLUCTUATE WITH AMBIENT TEMPERATURE. IN ADDITION TO ITS PRIMARY FUNCTION TO SUPPLY FUEL TO THE NATURAL GAS FIRED ENGINES USED TO DRIVE THE GAS COMPRESSORS AT THE STATION, THE FUEL GAS SYSTEM ALSO PROVIDES POWER GAS TO THE POWER GAS HEADER.  TYPICALLY, POWER GAS PRESSURE IS HIGH ENOUGH THAT THE GAS PRESSURE OVERCOMES THE SPRING TENSION USED TO OPEN THE BLOWDOWN VALVES IN THE EVENT OF AN ESD.   FOLLOWING THE OVERPRESSURE CONDITION, SDV 9008 WAS NOT REOPENED BECAUSE THE COMPRESSOR STATION WAS IDLE AND THE NEED FOR FUEL GAS WAS MINIMAL.  WITH SDV 9008 CLOSED AND THE POWER GAS HEADER ISOLATED, A LEAKING REGULATOR ON BDV 9011 EVENTUALLY BLED OFF ENOUGH PRESSURE FROM THE POWER GAS HEADER TO ALLOW BDV 9010 AND BDV 9011 TO FAIL OPEN AS DESIGNED. THE LEAKING REGULATOR WAS IDENTIFIED FOLLOWING THE RELEASE ON OCTOBER 5, 2024 AND REPAIRED ON OCTOBER 17, 2024.    CORRECTIVE ACTION:  BLUEWATER WILL IMPLEMENT CORRECTIVE ACTIONS TO PRECLUDE REPETITION OF THE INCIDENT, MINIMIZE THE AMOUNT OF VENTED GAS, AND IMPROVE REPORTING TIMELINESS.  1. UPDATE PROCEDURES TO AVOID THE ISOLATION OF THE POWER GAS HEADER:  A. OPTION 1: SDV 9008 (FUEL GAS SUPPLY) WILL BE OPEN DURING NORMAL AND STAND BY OPERATIONS  B. OPTION 2: (TBD) VB-9071 CHANGED TO 'NORMALLY OPEN' TO PROVIDE AN UNINTERRUPTED SUPPLY TO THE POWER GAS HEADER  2. CONTROL LOGIC AT THE SITE ALLOWED FOR THE PIPELINE VALVE (SUPPLY GAS) TO REMAIN OPEN WHEN THE VENT VALVES BDV 9010 AND BDV 9011 WERE OPEN.   A. BLUEWATER HAS UPDATED LOGIC AT THE FACILITY, IF A VENT VALVE IS IN ANY POSITION OTHER THAN "CLOSED" THE PIPELINE VALVE FEEDING THE FACILITY (SDV 9001) WILL CLOSE.  3. SCADA ALARMS TO IMPROVE RESPONSE TIME WILL BE ADDED.    A. "VALVES OUT OF SEQUENCE" NOTIFYING THE OPERATOR THAT THE VENT VALVE AND THE PIPELINE VALVES ARE OPEN OR IN TRAVEL.   B. "LOW PIPELINE PRESSURE" AN ADJUSTABLE ALARM THE OPERATORS CAN SET FOR THE CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS ON THE PIPELINES.   C. "LOW LOW PIPELINE PRESSURE" A NON-ADJUSTABLE "HIGH PRIORITY" ALARM SET AT 650 PSIG.  4. REVISE EMERGENCY PROCEDURES TO INCLUDE VOLUMETRIC THRESHOLDS FOR REPORTING NATURAL GAS RELEASES TO FEDERAL AND STATE AGENCIES CONSISTENT WITH EXISTING RULES AND REGULATIONS.   5. TRAINING: ADDITIONAL/REFRESHER TRAINING ON REPORTING REQUIREMENTS I.E. TIME TO REPORT, AND THRESHOLDS FOR REPORTING. BLUEWATER COMPLETED THE TRAINING FOR APPROPRIATE EMPLOYEES ON OCTOBER 15, 2024.

NARRATIVE: ON FRIDAY AFTERNOON 9/20/24 AT 13:21 CT, A TECHNICIAN AT THE MONROE GAS STORAGE COMPRESSOR STATION OBSERVED SMALL BUBBLES RISING WITHIN A PUDDLE OF WATER WHILE DOING REGULAR INSPECTION ROUNDS WITHIN THE FENCED FACILITY. AFTER CONFIRMING THE PRESENCE OF NATURAL GAS AT THE LOCATION AND SUSPECTING A PINHOLE LEAK, THE 18" EASTSIDE GAS TRANSMISSION LINE WAS ISOLATED BY MANUALLY CLOSING THE DOWNSTREAM VALVES. BECAUSE THE LINE WAS NOT FLOWING AT THE TIME, THE UPSTREAM VALVES WERE ALREADY CLOSED. THE EFFECTED LINE SEGMENT WAS ISOLATED BY 14:13 CT.  AFTER INITIAL COORDINATION REGARDING POSSIBLE COST OF REPAIR, IT WAS DETERMINED TO CLASSIFY THE EVENT AS AN INCIDENT AND NOTIFY THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC). THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED BY THE WILLIAMS SECURITY OPERATIONS CENTER AT 15:27 CT. THE PIPELINE WAS SAFELY BLOWN DOWN WITH LOCK OUT / TAG OUT COMPLETED BY 19:20 CT THAT EVENING.    DUE TO THE PIPELINE'S 10' DEPTH IN THE AREA, IT TOOK MULTIPLE DAYS FOR CONTRACTORS TO CLEAN THE LINE THROUGH CLEANING PIGS, PURGE WITH NITROGEN AND POTHOLE THROUGH HYDROVACING TO PINPOINT THE LEAK LOCATION. ONCE IDENTIFIED (THROUGH DETECTING THE PRESENCE OF NITROGEN), THE LEAK LOCATION WAS FULLY AND SAFELY EXCAVATED (CONSIDERING THE SIGNIFICANT DEPTH) ON 10/14/24.  AS OF 10/15/24, INTERNAL CORROSION WAS FOUND AT THE PINHOLE LEAK LOCATION. THE EFFECTED PIPE IMMEDIATELY NEAR THE LEAK LOCATION WAS CUT-OUT ON 10/17/24 AND SENT TO A LAB FOR ANALYSIS.     WILLIAMS OPERATIONS, PIPELINE INTEGRITY AND CONSTRUCTION TEAMS ESTABLISHED A CONSERVATIVE PLAN TO REPLACE NECESSARY PIPE AND RETURN THE LINE TO SERVICE. APPROXIMATELY 350 FEET OF 18-INCH PIPE IN THE LOW AREA OF THE PIPELINE (WITHIN THE FACILITY) WAS REPLACED AND HYDROTESTED. THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON 11/9/2024.    THE CAUSE IS SUSPECTED TO BE SULFATE REDUCING BACTERIA MICROBIOLOGICALLY INFLUENCED CORROSION (SRB-MIC) BASED ON LAB RESULTS. REPRESENTATIVES FROM OPERATIONS, MANAGEMENT AND PIPELINE INTEGRITY HAVE ESTABLISHED AN INTERNAL CORROSION MITIGATION PLAN TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE. COUPONS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED AT EACH WELL HEAD LOCATION AND OTHER LOCATIONS, BASED ON INTERNAL CORROSION DATA GATHERED, OPERATIONS, MANAGEMENT, COMPLIANCE AND CORROSION DEPARTMENT WILL DEEM IF MORE LOCATIONS ARE NEEDED. A PIGGING PROGRAM THAT INCLUDES ALL 13 LATERALS IS FORTHCOMING, WITH ADDITIONAL FOCUS ON THE 18" LATERAL.

OPERATION_DETAILS: PIPELINE CLEARING OPERATIONS INCLUDED A CONTROLLED TRANSFER (FLASHING) OF PRESSURIZED HYDROCARBON LIQUIDS AND WATER FROM A PIPELINE AT 60 PSIG INTO A TEMPORARY, ATMOSPHERIC LIQUID STORAGE VESSEL. THE ASSOCIATED INCORRECT OPERATION CONSISTED OF NOT SECURING THE AREA IMMEDIATELY AROUND THE STORAGE VESSEL AND ALLOWING VEHICLES (IGNITION SOURCES) TO BE PARKED TOO CLOSE TO THE STORAGE VESSEL.
NARRATIVE: DURING ROUTINE PIGGING OPERATION TO REMOVE HYDROCARBON LIQUIDS AND WATER FROM A 60-PSIG GAS GATHERING PIPELINE, THE FLUIDS WERE DECANTED IN A CONTROLLED MANNER INTO A TEMPORARY ATMOSPHERIC STORAGE (FRAC) TANK. OPERATING PROCEDURES DID NOT ADDRESS THE NEED TO ESTABLISH A SAFETY ZONE (VISUAL WARNINGS AND WINDSOCKS) AROUND THE PIPELINE PIGGING OPERATION AREA TO HELP TO ELIMINATE RANDOM IGNITION SOURCES IN AN AREA EXPECTED TO HAVE TEMPORARY EXPOSURE TO HYDROCARBON VAPORS. ULTIMATELY, IT WAS A VEHICLE WHICH WAS PARKED TOO CLOSE TO THE WORK AREA WHICH PROVIDED THE IGNITION SOURCE WHEN AN EMPLOYEE ENTERED THE VEHICLE AND TURNED THE IGNITION KEY.    THE RELEASE OF HYDROCARBON VAPORS WAS THE EXPECTED RESULT OF THE CONTROLLED DEPRESSURIZING OF A STREAM OF HYDROCARBON LIQUIDS INTO AN ATMOSPHERIC STORAGE VESSEL. THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE FIRE WAS A FAILURE OF OPERATING PROCEDURES IN ALLOWING A POTENTIAL SOURCE OF IGNITION (A VEHICLE) TO BE LOCATED WITHIN AN AREA EXPECTED TO BE TEMPORARILY EXPOSED TO EVOLVED HYDROCARBON VAPORS. THIS INCIDENT WAS NOT THE RESULT OF ANY PIPELINE MATERIAL FAILURE, ABNORMAL OPERATING CONDITIONS (I.E., OVERPRESSURE CONDITION), OR INCIDENTAL THIRD-PARTY DAMAGE TO THE PIPELINE FACILITIES.

NARRATIVE: ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 15, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 1:23 P.M. PG&E WAS INFORMED BY THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT THAT PG&E TRANSMISSION PIPELINE, L-301F, WAS STRUCK NEAR 50 ESPINOSA ROAD IN CASTROVILLE, CA. WHILE PLOWING THE FIELD, THE LINE WAS HIT BY FARM EQUIPMENT AND PUNCTURED THE PIPELINE RESULTING IN AN UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF GAS.     THE EXCAVATOR DID NOT OBTAIN A USA TICKET FOR THEIR CURRENT FARMING EXCAVATION ACTIVITY THAT RESULTED IN THIS INCIDENT. A USA TICKET (2024050700416) WAS PREVIOUSLY OBTAINED ON 5/7/24. THAT TICKET HAD A LEGAL EXCAVATION START DATE OF 5/10/2024 AND EXPIRED ON 6/4/24 WITH NO REQUEST FOR RENEWAL OR EXTENSION.    THE DAMAGED PG&E PIPELINE WAS WITHIN THE DELINEATED AREA OF THE EXPIRED USA TICKET. DURING THE INCIDENT INVESTIGATION, THE EXCAVATOR SAID THE PG&E USA GAS FLAGS FOR THE USA TICKET WERE NO LONGER VISIBLE PRIOR TO THIS INCIDENT DUE TO PREPPING THE FIELD FOR HARVESTING.     INVESTIGATION ACTIVITIES INCLUDED A FIELD WALK THROUGH. THE PG&E DIRT INVESTIGATOR LOOKED FOR ANY REMNANTS OF PGE USA GAS FLAG MARKERS BUT ONLY OBSERVED ONE PG&E USA GAS FLAG LOCATED NEAR THE PG&E GAS PADDLE MARKER EAST OF THE DAMAGE SITE AREA.     THE FIRE DEPARTMENT AND CA HIGHWAY PATROL WERE ONSITE. NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED. AT APPROXIMATELY 20:00 PG&E INCIDENT ON-CALL PERSONNEL DETERMINED THAT THE UNINTENDED GAS LOSS WAS OVER THREE MILLION CUBIC FEET AND REPORTED TO DOT AND CPUC.

NARRATIVE: DURING A ROUTINE OFFSHORE FLIGHT PATROL, A PIPELINE TECHNICIAN SPOTTED AN AREA OF BUBBLES IN THE GOM NEAR THE HIA 582 PLATFORM. THE BUBBLES WERE SUSPECTED TO BE COMING FROM THE 30" NATURAL GAS PIPELINE SEGMENT 7869. THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED AND MONITORED. NO SHEEN WAS OBSERVED. THE LEAK WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC AND PHMSA.    THE VISION BARGE, CONTRACTED FROM CHET MORRISSON, ARRIVED ON LOCATION. THE PIPELINE LIES AT A SEA DEPTH OF 356 FEET IN CONDITIONS OF VERY POOR VISIBILITY. SATURATION DIVERS FOUND THE PINHOLE NATURAL GAS LEAK ON THE 30" PIPELINE. THE CONCRETE AND SOMASTIC COATING ON THE PIPE WERE REMOVED AND A 30" X 24" PLIDCO CLAMP WAS BOLTED IN PLACE OVER THE LEAK. ALL UT READINGS AROUND THE CLAMP AND LEAK AREA WERE GOOD.    AN ANODE SLED WAS INSTALLED ON THE PIPE TO SUPPORT CURRENT CORROSION MITIGATION PROGRAMS. THE PIPELINE WAS TESTED UP TO 900 PSI, NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURE, AND NO LEAKS WERE OBSERVED BY THE DIVERS. THE PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK IN SERVICE. NARRATIVE: ON JULY 15, 2024, A RUPTURE OCCURRED ON ENERGY TRANSFER COMPANYS K TO COYA 24-INCH PIPELINE LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 15 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MONAHANS, TX.      AT 3:30 A.M. SUPERVISORY CONTROL AND DATA ACQUISITION (SCADA) SYSTEM ALERTED THE COYANOSA GAS PLANT OPERATOR OF A LOSS OF PRESSURE.        AT 4:40 A.M. THE PIPELINE OPERATOR ARRIVED ON LOCATION TO FIND LOCAL FIRST RESPONDERS WERE ALREADY ON SCENE BATTLING A FIRE. THE EMERGENCY PERSONNEL HAD BEEN NOTIFIED OF THE FIRE BY A THIRD PARTY.        AT 5:20 A.M. THE AFFECTED PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED, AND THE FIRE HAD BURNED OUT, ENERGY TRANSFER PERSONNEL WERE ABLE TO CONFIRM THE DISCOVERY OF A RUPTURE ON THE K TO COYA 24. IT WAS DETERMINED THE REPAIRS AND DAMAGES WOULD EXCEED THE $145,400.00 REPORTABLE DOLLAR CRITERIA AND THE NRC AND THE TXRRC WERE NOTIFIED.      THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT ALLOWED THE FIRE TO BURN ITSELF OUT. THE CAUSE OF THE RUPTURE IS STILL UNDER INVESTIGATION.         THE CAUSE OF THE RUPTURE WAS IDENTIFIED AS EXTERNAL CORROSION OCCURRING ALONG THE 3:00 OCLOCK SIDE OF THE PIPE. THE THINNEST MEASURED REMAINING WALL THICKNESS WAS DETERMINED TO BE 0.020-INCH (7% OF THE NOMINAL WALL THICKNESS).     OTHER AREAS OF CORROSION PRESENT ON THE PIPE MATERIAL PROVIDED APPEARED CONSISTENT WITH DC INTERFERENCE CURRENT CORROSION AND UNDER DEPOSIT CORROSION.       PLAN TO PREVENT RECURRENCE, OPERATIONS WILL INSTALL MORE CP TEST STATIONS TO HELP LOCATE EXTERNAL CORROSION TROUBLE AREAS AND INSTALL MORE RECTIFIERS TO IMPRESS CURRENT EQUALLY OVER THE ENTIRETY OF THE PIPELINE. OPERATIONS WILL ALSO UTILIZE CORROSION COUPONS AND CHEMICAL INJECTION TO MONITOR AND MITIGATE ANY INTERNAL CORROSION.

OPERATION_DETAILS: THE CONTRACTOR WELL WORK CREWS FAILED TO EQUALIZE PRESSURE ACROSS THE RETRIEVABLE PACKER PRIOR TO RELEASING IT AS REQUIRED BY THE WELL WORK PROCEDURE.
NARRATIVE: WELL CONTROL WAS TEMPORARILY LOST DURING PLANNED MAINTENANCE OF THE C.V.G.S. 1-L (API10: 0401120925), AN UNGSF GAS STORAGE WELL. AT 11:27 AM LOCAL TIME ON JULY 11TH, 2024, 1,478 FEET OF 2-3/8' WORKSTRING WAS EJECTED FROM THE WELL. WITHIN THIRTY SECONDS, THE WELL WAS CONTROLLED AND SECURED BY THE ONSITE CREWS. THERE WERE NO INJURIES. CVGS BEGAN ITS INCIDENT RESPONSE, INCLUDING NOTIFYING THE NRC AND OTHER REGULATORY AGENCIES IN ACCORDANCE WITH CVGS PROCEDURES AND APPLICABLE REGULATIONS.    PRIOR TO THE LOSS OF WELL CONTROL AND IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE WELL WORK PROCEDURE, THE WELL HAD A RETRIEVABLE PACKER INSTALLED AT 1,507 FEET MD AND A PLUG SET IN THE PACKER AT 2,424 FEET MD INTENDED TO FUNCTION AS TWO DOWNHOLE ISOLATING BARRIERS BETWEEN THE RESERVOIR AND THE SURFACE. A FEW ELEMENTS OF THE SCADA MONITORING SYSTEM WERE PURPOSEFULLY DISCONNECTED TO ISOLATE HAZARDOUS ENERGY AND ACCESS THE WELL FOR THE PLANNED WORK, AND THE SCADA SYSTEM FOR THE REST OF THE FACILITY WAS OPERATIONAL AND FUNCTIONING.    AT SOME TIME PRIOR TO 11:27 AM, POTENTIALLY OVERNIGHT, PRESSURE BUILT BETWEEN THE BARRIERS INSTALLED AT 1,507 FEET MD AND 2,424 FEET MD. AT 11:27 AM, THE WORKOVER CREWS BEGAN TO RELEASE THE RETRIEVABLE PACKER INSTALLED AT 1,507 FEET MD TO RETRIEVE IT FROM THE WELL. AS THE PACKER WAS RELEASED, THE FORCE FROM THE BUILT-UP PRESSURE PUSHED THE WORKSTRING AND RETRIEVABLE PACKER OUT OF THE WELLBORE IN APPROXIMATELY 3 TO 5 SECONDS. THE GAS THAT BUILT UP BETWEEN THE TWO DOWNHOLE BARRIERS WAS RELEASED. IN LESS THAN THIRTY SECONDS, THE ONSITE CREWS SECURED THE WELL WITH MULTIPLE BARRIERS (MASTER VALVE AND BLOWOUT PREVENTER ELEMENTS). THE WORKSTRING CONTACTED THE ELECTRONICS OF AN OFFSET WELL, WHICH SHUT DOWN IN THE INTENDED FAILSAFE CONDITION AND PROVIDED THE INITIAL INDICATIONS OF AN INCIDENT TO THE CONTROL ROOM VIA THE SCADA SYSTEM. THE SURFACE PIPING OF THREE OFFSET WELLS WAS CONTACTED AND THEN TAKEN OUT OF SERVICE UNTIL ASSESSMENTS CONFIRMED THE DAMAGE WAS ONLY COSMETIC.    THE WELL WORK PROCEDURE SPECIFIED TO EQUALIZE PRESSURE ACROSS THE RETRIEVABLE PACKER INSTALLED AT 1,507 FEET MD PRIOR TO RELEASING IT. THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROCEDURAL STEP IS TO MITIGATE THE POSSIBILITY OF A FAILURE OF THE ISOLATING BARRIER INSTALLED AT 2,424 FEET MD. THE WELL WORK CREWS SKIPPED THIS STEP. THE FAILURE OF THE ISOLATING BARRIER INSTALLED AT 2,424 FEET MD IS A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR.    THE ESTIMATED RELEASE VOLUME WAS CALCULATED USING THE WELL'S INFLOW PERFORMANCE RELATIONSHIP, THE RESERVOIR PRESSURE, AND THE PRESSURE IDENTIFIED AT SURFACE DURING THE RELEASE. AN ALTERNATIVE METHOD RESULTS IN A LOWER RELEASE VOLUME.

NARRATIVE: ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 10, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 21:04 HOURS, PG&E INCIDENT ON-DUTY PERSONNEL CONFIRMED A RELEASE OF GAS AT KETTLEMAN COMPRESSOR STATION RESULTED IN AN INJURY NECESSITATING IN-PATIENT HOSPITALIZATION.     FOLLOWING COMPLETION OF CONSTRUCTION, CLEARANCE ACTIVITIES TO RE-INTRODUCE GAS AND PURGE AIR FROM THE SYSTEM, INITIATED:   - THE APPROVED CLEARANCE REQUIRED V-56 TO BE "CHECKED OPEN" FOR PURGING, HOWEVER, IT HAD BEEN CLOSED FOR STEM SEAL REPLACEMENT WORK ON JULY 8, 2024, AND ONLY PARTIALLY OPENED PRIOR TO THE PURGE - OPERATIONS THAT HAD NOT BEEN DOCUMENTED NOR APPROVED AS PART OF THE SEQUENCE OF OPERATIONS IN THE CLEARANCE.   - GAS WAS RE-INTRODUCED TO THE SYSTEM FROM A 34'' CONTROL VALVE (V-90), A CLEARANCE POINT WITH 618 PSI. THERE ARE TWO WAYS TO OPERATE V-90, MANUAL HYDRAULIC AND MANUAL PNEUMATIC.   - WHEN ATTEMPTING TO MANUALLY OPERATE V-90 HYDRAULICALLY, OIL UNEXPECTEDLY DISCHARGED FROM THE ACTUATOR'S MANUAL HYDRAULIC OVERRIDE SYSTEM RELIEF VALVE AND THE VALVE FAILED TO OPERATE.   - V-90 WAS THEN PARTIALLY OPENED USING THE MANUAL PNEUMATIC CONTROLS. THIS METHOD IS NOT EFFECTIVE FOR FINE THROTTLING AS REQUIRED FOR PURGING IN DESIGN STANDARD A-38.    GAS FROM V-90 BEGAN TO DISPLACE AIR AT MULTIPLE VENT LOCATIONS PER THE ESTABLISHED CLEARANCE PLAN. IT IS SUSPECTED THAT AS A RESULT OF THE PARTIAL OPEN POSITION OF V-56, A GREATER AMOUNT OF GAS FLOW WAS DIRECTED TOWARD V-78. INSTEAD OF GAS EXITING THE 1/2'' VERTICAL VENT VALVE DOWNSTREAM OF V-78 AS APPROVED IN THE CLEARANCE, GAS EXITED THE FULL 6'' PIPE OPENING HORIZONTALLY WHERE THE BLIND FLANGE HAD BEEN REMOVED ON JULY 9. GAS FLOWED DIRECTLY INTO AN OPPOSING BLIND FLANGE ROUGHLY 20'' AWAY AT V-79. THIS RESULTED IN DEFLECTION IN ALL DIRECTIONS, INCLUDING INTO THE EXCAVATION BELOW. A HAZARDOUS AIR-GAS PLUME DEVELOPED. AT APPROXIMATELY 18:42 HOURS, THE AIR-GAS PLUME IGNITED, RESULTING IN SERIOUS BURNS TO ONE COWORKER AND MINOR INJURIES TO OTHERS. CWS IN THE AREA IMMEDIATELY RESPONDED, ATTENDING TO THE INJURED CW AND EXTINGUISHING VARIOUS SPOT FIRES USING PRE-STAGED FIRE EXTINGUISHERS. A CW CLOSED V-90 TO SHUT IN THE GAS BEFORE IGNITION, ALLOWING THE FLAME TO EXTINGUISH IN ABOUT ONE MINUTE.     PROCEDURE USE & ADHERENCE WAS THE APPARENT CAUSE OF THIS INCIDENT. CONFIGURATION CONTROL WAS NOT APPLIED WHEN CLEARANCE WORK WAS EXECUTED. PRIOR TO PURGING INTO SERVICE, CWS FAILED TO RECOGNIZE A HIGH ENERGY ESSENTIAL CONTROL HAD BEEN DISABLED DURING THE PURGE OUT OF SERVICE. PROCEDURES INVOLVING PURGING GAS FACILITIES, INSTALLATION AND OPERATION OF AIR MOVERS, AND TEMPORARY VENT STACKS PROVIDE A BARRIER IN THE FORM OF REQUIREMENTS TO CALCULATE AND MONITOR PURGE DRIVE PRESSURE, PROPERLY SIZE AIR MOVERS AND FRESH AIR SOURCES, AND INSTALL TEMPORARY VENT STACKS TO SAFELY DISPERSE GAS TO ATMOSPHERE. PROCEDURE INVOLVING NEW CLEARANCES FOR GT FACILITIES PROVIDES A BARRIER IN THE FORM OF CHANGE MANAGEMENT FOR THE CLEARANCE PROCESS AND ENDORSEMENT REQUIREMENTS, WHICH FAILED AS THE CLEARANCE TEAM DID NOT FOLLOW THEM.     THE PRECISE IGNITION SOURCE AND LOCATION COULD NOT BE DETERMINED TO A CERTAINTY; HOWEVER, THE FOLLOWING POTENTIAL IGNITION SOURCES WERE ASSESSED AND COULD NOT BE RULED OUT. AN ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE FROM THE GENERATED DUST CLOUD. AN ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE FROM EITHER THE PIPELINE ITSELF (DUE TO CHARGING FROM THE VENTING GAS) OR AN ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE FROM AN EMPLOYEE IN THE VICINITY OF THE VENTING GAS (DUE TO ELECTROSTATIC ACCUMULATION ON THEIR PERSON). A MECHANICAL SPARK FROM DEBRIS EXITING THE PIPELINE AT HIGH VELOCITY OR KICKED UP BY THE VENTING GAS. ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT IN THE VICINITY OF THE VENTING GAS WAS LOCKED OUT AND TAGGED OUT AND WAS RULED OUT AS A POTENTIAL IGNITION SOURCE.    THE INJURED EMPLOYEE WAS TRANSFERRED BY AMBULANCE TO A LOCAL HOSPITAL AND THEN MEDEVACKED TO A FRESNO AREA HOSPITAL TO RECEIVE CARE. THIS INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO THE DOT AND CPUC DUE TO A RELEASE OF GAS RESULTING IN AN INJURY REQUIRING HOSPITAL ADMISSION FOR OVERNIGHT STAY.

NARRATIVE: AT 22:54 ON 7/4/2024, COMPLIANCE RECEIVED A CALL INDICATING THAT GAS WAS LEAKING PAST A CHECK VALVE ON THE DISCHARGE SIDE OF THE COMPRESSOR, WHICH CAUSED A RELIEF VALVE TO GO OFF, AND GAS TO MIGRATE TO A UNIT THAT HAD SHUT DOWN EARLIER IN THE DAY. THAT UNIT WAS SHUT DOWN FOR A DIFFERENT REASON UNRELATED TO THE CAUSE OF THIS INCIDENT. THE SHUT DOWN UNIT HAS A SAFETY FEATURE THAT VENTS ALL GAS THROUGH THE BLOWDOWN STACK. THE VENTING STARTED TO OCCUR AT APPROXIMATELY 4:30 AND STOPPED AT APPROXIMATELY 10:35, RESULTING IN AN APPROXIMATE LOSS OF 7,771 MCF OF GAS LOST (UPDATED FROM 7,500 MCF ON THE NRC REPORTS). WHEN OPERATIONS ARRIVED ON SITE, A BALL VALVE WAS SHUT UPSTREAM OF THE UNIT TO BLOCK ANY MORE GAS FROM BEING VENTED. ON MONDAY, 7/8, OPERATIONS INSPECTED THE CHECK VALVE AND FOUND A TEFLON BOLT STUCK IN THE VALVE. ONCE THE BOLT WAS REMOVED, THE VALVE WAS ABLE TO OPERATE NORMALLY.     THE TEFLON BOLT HOLDS THE COMPRESSOR VALVE CAGES IN THE CYLINDER OF THE COMPRESSOR. THESE BOLTS ARE KEPT IN THE COMPRESSOR PER THE COMPRESSOR MANUFACTURER'S DESIGN. THE BOLTS ARE NOT MEANT TO BE REMOVED UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCE. GOING FORWARD, THE TECHNICIANS WILL INSPECT THE BOLTS DURING COMPRESSOR MAINTENANCE FOR SECURE FIT, WEAR, AND DAMAGE. THEY WILL BE REPLACED AS NEEDED. THE COMPANY WILL REVIEW ITS PROCEDURES TO DETERMINE IF ANY CHANGES ARE NECESSARY.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 2, 2024, A FIRE OCCURRED AT THE OASIS WAHA COMPRESSOR STATION, 2.26 MILES NORTH OF COYANOSA, TEXAS IN PECOS COUNTY.    AT APPROXIMATELY 0500 SCADA NOTIFIED OPERATIONS ABOUT A UNIT DOWN ON FIRE DETECTION.    0520, IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT COMPRESSOR UNITS 3 & 4 WERE ON FIRE, ESDD THE STATION AND CALLED THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT TO THE STATION.    AT 0543, IT WAS DETERMINED THE REPAIRS AND DAMAGES WOULD EXCEED THE $145,400.00 REPORTABLE DOLLAR CRITERIA AND THE NRC AND THE TXRRC WAS NOTIFIED.    LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT EXTINGUISHED THE FIRE.    THE DAMAGED COMPRESSORS WERE BYPASSED, AND THE STATION WAS BACK ONLINE ON JULY 5.    FUEL GAS AND LINE OIL FUELED THE FIRE.  THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE IS STILL UNDER INVESTIGATION. A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED ONCE INVESTIGATION IS COMPLETE.      **DURING THE PHYSICAL INSPECTION THERE WERE ALSO FOUND TO BE BROKEN BOLTS GATHERED FOR ANALYSIS.  THE COUPLING COVER BOLT, AS WELL AS A MISSING BOLT FROM THE TURBINE DRAIN FLANGE WERE FOUND ON THE SKID FLOOR.  THESE BOLTS WERE ALSO SENT TO METALLURGIST ALSO AND WERE DETERMINED TO HAVE FAILED DUE TO FATIGUE.      THE SHEARED BOLT AND STAINING ON THE DRAIN FLANGE SUGGEST A POSSIBLE ROOT CAUSE OF THE FIRE.  THE FAILURE OF THE FLANGE AS AN OIL SEALING MECHANISM WOULD LEAD TO OIL DRAINING DIRECTLY ONTO THE EXHAUST BLANKET LEADING TO VAPORIZATION AND IGNITION OF THE OIL.    THIS WAS AN ISOLATED INCIDENT.     THE MITIGATION STEPS TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE ARE AS FOLLOWED:  -EVALUATE FIRE EYES/GAS DETECTOR PLACEMENT ON THESE PACKAGES.    -CHANGE FASTENERS ON ENGINE SWINGS.     -COORDINATE AN EVALUATION OF ALL UNITS TO CONFIRM NO MISSING/BROKEN FASTENERS, THIS IS NOW INCLUDED IN OUR 4000-HOUR PREVENTATIVE MAINTENANCE PLAN.

AN INVESTIGATION DETERMINED A CONTRACTOR WORKING ON BEHALF OF AUTUMN VISTA DAIRY USING A TRACTOR AND TANKER TO SPREAD FERTILIZER, HAD BACKED INTO THE WELLHEAD, CAUSING A RELEASE OF GAS, AND DAMAGING THE WELLHEAD AND SURFACE COMPONENTS, MAKING THE WELL INCAPABLE OF ISOLATION. UPON DETERMINING THE LOSS OF CONTROL, PERSONNEL BEGAN EVALUATING THE SITE TO DETERMINE IMMEDIATE NEEDS AND REMAINED ONSITE UNTIL COMPLETE ISOLATION OF THE WELL WAS ACHIEVED.    CONSUMERS ENERGY CONTACTED CUDD   EMERGENCY WELL RESPONSE (CONTRACTOR), INFORMING THEM OF THE INCIDENT AND REQUESTING ASSISTANCE. BOTH CONSUMERS ENERGY AND CUDD MOBILIZED AT THE SITE ON JUNE 16, 2024, TO BEGIN PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTING THE WELL KILL OPERATION.     TWO ATTEMPTS TO KILL THE WELL BY SUPPRESSING IT WITH LIQUIDS WERE UNSUCCESSFUL, LEADING TO A DECISION TO USE A VENDOR SPECIALIZED BLOW OUT PREVENTER (BOP) TO ISOLATE AND GAIN CONTROL OF THE WELL. ON JUNE 19, 2024, THE WELLBORE WAS SUCCESSFULLY ISOLATED USING A CUDD BLOWOUT PREVENTER AND AN ASI-X PACKER WAS SET AND TESTED. CUDD'S BOP WAS REMOVED AND REPLACED WITH A COMPANY BOP.      FURTHER ACTION INCLUDES PREPARATION FOR THE COMPLETE WELL REMEDIATION, WHICH WILL INVOLVE INTEGRITY DIAGNOSTIC EVALUATIONS THROUGH LOGGING AND MECHANICAL INTEGRITY TESTING. THE WELL COMPLETION WILL ALSO INCLUDE INSTALLING NEW TUBING, NEW API 6A SURFACE EQUIPMENT, AND CONNECTING PIPELINE.    ON JULY 24TH, 2024, THE WELL WAS LOGGED WITH BOTH GAMMA RAY NEUTRON AND CORROSION LOGGING TOOLS TO VALIDATE DOWNHOLE INTEGRITY BEFORE COMPLETING WORKOVER ACTIVITIES. THIS WAS THE SECOND TIME THE WELL HAS BEEN LOGGED SINCE ITS BASELINE ASSESSMENT WITH ZERO NOTABLE CHANGES. A NEW WELLHEAD WAS THEN PROCURED AND INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CONSUMERS ENERGY PROCEDURES. THE WELL WAS THEN COMPLETED WITH A 2-3/8-INCH TUBING STRING AND NEW WELLHEAD COMPONENTS AND VALVES.     THE REMAINING WORK INCLUDES FINAL PIPELINE CONNECTION TO BE COMPLETED BY LOCAL OPERATIONAL AND WELDING CREWS PER THEIR RESOURCE AND PROJECT WORK PLAN. THE FINAL STATE OF THE WELL, ONCE RETURNED TO SERVICE WILL INCLUDE NEW API 6A WELLHEAD AND VALVES, REPLACED PIPELINE CONNECTION, UPDATED DOWNHOLE INTEGRITY LOGS, WELLHEAD PROTECTION AND SIGNAGE.     THE FINAL PIPELINE CONNECTION WAS COMPLETED BY LOCAL OPERATIONAL AND WELDING CREWS, AND NORMAL OPERATIONS RESUMED ON APRIL 24, 2025. PROPER PROTECTION WAS ALSO INSTALLED TO SAFEGUARD THE PIPELINE FACILITIES FROM IMPACT AND EXCAVATION DAMAGE.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 30, 2024, A BODY VENT FITTING ON AN ABOVE-GROUND 12-INCH VALVE BEGAN LEAKING TO ATMOSPHERE AFTER IT WAS OPERATED AS PART OF AN IN-LINE INSPECTION OF A NATURAL GAS TRANSMISSION PIPELINE.  THE 12-INCH VALVE WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THE ISOLATION OF A PIG RECEIVER LOCATION INVOLVED IN THE IN-LINE INSPECTION PROJECT. THE LEAK WAS CONTINUOUSLY MONITORED BY UGI PERSONNEL UNTIL THE VALVE WAS REPLACED ON JUNE 18, 2024.    ON JULY 7, 2025, A SUPPLEMENTAL AND FINAL REPORT IS BEING FILED TO PROVIDE UPDATED INCIDENT INFORMATION.     UGI CONSULTED WITH THE VALVE MANUFACTURER WHO EXAMINED AND INVESTIGATED THE VALVE. UGI SUBSEQUENTLY PERFORMED A ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS OF THE EVENT. UGI CONCLUDED THAT THE ROOT CAUSE OF THIS EVENT WAS ATTRIBUTABLE TO THE VALVE SEALS THAT WERE EXPOSED TO DEBRIS SUCH AS WELD SLAG. WELD SLAG WAS FOUND EMBEDDED INTO THE SEALS AND DID NOT ALLOW THE VALVE TO SEAL COMPLETELY. THE BODY BLEED VALVE ALSO DID NOT SEAL AND WAS STRIPPED WHILE BEING OPERATED AND LEAKED CONTINUOUSLY TO ATMOSPHERE FOR A SHORT DURATION. UGI WAS ABLE TO STOP THE LEAK UNTIL THE FINAL REPAIR COULD BE MADE. BASED ON THE SMALL OPENING OF THE BODY BLEED VALVE, THE ESCAPING GAS WAS NEGLIGIBLE.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 20, 2024, AN INCIDENT OCCURRED ON PIPELINE SR- 538 IN BENTON, OHIO, DURING A SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE PROJECT. AS PART OF THE PROJECT, FOUR (4) TC ENERGY TECHNICIANS AND TWO (2) THIRD PARTY CONTRACTORS WERE WORKING TO INSTALL A SKILLET BLIND FLANGE TO ISOLATE THE AFFECTED PIPELINE SEGMENT. THE CREW OBSERVED A GAS LEAK FROM A 6-INCH VALVE AT THAT TIME AND DECIDED TO GREASE THE VALVE TO REMEDIATE THE LEAK USING A MOTORIZED GREASE GUN. AN IGNITION/FLASH FIRE ENSUED THAT INJURED TWO (2) OF THE TC ENERGY TECHNICIANS AND BOTH OF THE THIRD-PARTY CONTRACTORS. THE INJURED WORKERS WERE IMMEDIATELY TAKEN FOR MEDICAL EVALUATION AND TREATMENT. TC ENERGY COMPLETED AN INVESTIGATION TO IDENTIFY THE FACTORS THAT MAY HAVE CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO THE INCIDENT. THE RESULTS OF THAT INVESTIGATION IDENTIFIED THE FOLLOWING PROPOSED RECOMMENDATIONS:   1. DEVELOP AND PUBLISH AN OPERATING PROCEDURE, OR UPDATE EXISTING PROCEDURES, TO OUTLINE THE REQUIREMENTS FOR INSTALLING A SKILLET BLIND.    2. CONDUCT A CONTROL OF WORK ASSESSMENT FOR INSTALLING SKILLET BLINDS TO PRODUCE A HAZARD CONTROLS DIAGRAM FOR REVIEW BY PERSONNEL WITH THE JOB SAFETY ANALYSIS (JSA).    3. IMPLEMENT IMPROVEMENTS TO PERSONNEL TRAINING MATERIALS TO PROVIDE MORE EMPHASIS ON THE ADEQUATE RESPONSE TO UNEXPECTED LEAKS.    4. CONDUCT RISK ASSESSMENT ON THE USAGE OF CSN SOLUTIONS BLACK WIDOW 20V GREASE GUNS.    5. UPDATE THE JSA FORM TO HAVE OPERATOR CONFIRM THE SPECIFIC PERSONAL PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT (PPE) NEEDED FOR THE TASK (E.G., FLAME-RESISTANT CLOTHING, ETC.)    6. UPDATE APPLICABLE PROCEDURES TO REQUIRE A HOT WORK PERMIT TO BE COMPLETED WHEN INSTALLING A SKILLET BLIND DOWNSTREAM OF A SINGLE POINT OF ISOLATION REGARDLESS OF WHETHER A GAS LEAK OCCURS.    7. COMPLETE ONGOING EFFORT TO ISSUE PERSONAL DIFFUSIVE 4-GAS MONITOR GAS MONITORS TO ALL TC ENERGY FIELD TECHNICIANS AND OPERATIONS SPECIALISTS.   8. UPDATE JSA FORM TO CLARIFY THAT CONTRACTORS MUST ALSO SIGN THE JSA IF THEY ARE ALSO INVOLVED IN THE TASK BEING COVERED.   9. SHARE ADDITIONAL DETAILS AND LEARNINGS FROM THE INCIDENT TO THE ORGANIZATION.   10. UPDATE GAS HANDLING PROCEDURES TO CLARIFY THE AMOUNT OF TIME SINCE THE LAST VALVE INSPECTION / SERVICING THAT WOULD TRIGGER ADDITIONAL INSPECTION / SERVICING BEFORE COMPLETING A LOCKOUT TAG OUT ACTIVITY.      TC ENERGY SENT THE VALVE ASSOCIATED WITH THE INCIDENT TO A LABORATORY FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS.  THE LAB RESULT CONFIRMED THE SOURCE OF THE GAS LEAK WAS THE VALVE. THE FOLLOWING ARE THE RESULT OF TESTS;  1-THE SEAT TESTS CONDUCTED IN GENERAL ACCORDANCE WITH THE 24TH EDITION OF API 6D, SPECIFICATION FOR PIPELINE AND PIPING VALVES, SHOWED THAT THERE WAS A DETECTABLE LEAK AT THE VALVE SEAT OF THE SUBJECT PLUG VALVE AT PRESSURES AS LOW AS 70 PSI.   2-THE SHELL TESTS CONDUCTED IN GENERAL ACCORDANCE WITH THE 24TH EDITION OF API 6D SHOWED THAT THERE WAS A DETECTABLE LEAK AT THE FLANGED CONNECTION BETWEEN THE ROTATING SHAFT OF THE PLUG AND THE SHELL OF THE VALVE AT PRESSURES AS LOW AS 800 PSI.

NARRATIVE: ON THURSDAY, MAY 9TH AT APPROXIMATELY 1:25PM CENTRAL TIME, EMMET OPERATIONS RECEIVED A CALL FROM A THIRD PARTY ABOUT A POSSIBLE LINE STRIKE WITH A GAS RELEASE. AT 1:38PM, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CONFIRMED THAT A THIRD PARTY HAD STRUCK LINE SM-23 WITH A BULLDOZER CAUSING A GAS RELEASE. NO ONE WAS INJURED AND THE GAS DID NOT IGNITE. AN OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN REMAINED ON SITE TO MONITOR THE RELEASE WHILE ONE TECHNICIAN WENT TO THE UPSTREAM VALVE SITE AND ANOTHER TECHNICIAN MOBILIZED TO THE DOWNSTREAM VALVE SITE. PRESSURE AT THE TIME OF THE STRIKE WAS 550PSIG; PRESSURE WAS LOWERED TO 350PSIG AND MONITORED. THE DECISION TO MONITOR PRESSURE WAS MADE TO AVOID SIGNIFICANT OUTAGES TO RESIDENTIAL CUSTOMERS. ONCE CUSTOMER NOTIFICATIONS WERE FINALIZED, THE PIPELINE WAS SHUNT IN AND BLOWN DOWN. A TYPE B SLEEVE WAS INSTALLED OVER THE DEFECT AND THE PIPELINE WAS PURGED AND RETURNED TO SERVICE ON FRIDAY MAY 10TH AT APPROXIMATELY 4:40PM CENTRAL TIME.    THE PIPELINE WAS STRUCK BY A THIRD PARTY LOGGING COMPANY. THE CONTRACTORS WORKING FOR THE LOGGING COMPANY DID NOT COMPLETE A ONE CALL AND WERE NOT AUTHORIZED TO PERFORM WORK ON THE SM-23 RIGHT OF WAY.

NARRATIVE: AFTER REVIEWING THE TIMELINE OF EVENTS AND THE FACTUAL EVIDENCE, THE INVESTIGATION TEAM CONCLUDED THAT WHILE  THERE WAS NOT DEFINITIVE EVIDENCE THAT CIRCULATION OF THE WELL WOULD HAVE PREVENTED THIS INCIDENT, IT  APPEARS THAT THE GAS RELEASE OCCURRED AS A RESULT OF WELL FLUID IMBALANCE. HAD THE WELL REMEDIATION PROCESS  REQUIRED CIRCULATION OF THE WELL FLUID, CONDITIONS IN THE WELL MAY NOT HAVE REACHED A STATE OF IMBALANCE,  WHICH ULTIMATELY LED TO THE LOSS OF WELL CONTROL. THEREFORE, THE WELL REMEDIATION PROCEDURE WAS  DETERMINED TO BE INADEQUATE.    THE FULL ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS WAS SUBMITTED TO PHMSA ON NOVEMBER 19, 2024.

THE MITIGATIVE AIR FLOW DID NOT MAINTAIN FLAMMABLE GAS CONCENTRATION IN THE STATION YARD PIPING BELOW THE LEL. THE MITIGATIVE AIR FLOW ALSO DRIED OUT SLUDGE DEPOSITS INSIDE THE STATION YARD PIPING. SUSPECTED ACCUMULATION OF IRON SULFIDE IN THE SLUDGE DEPOSITS AUTO-IGNITED WHEN EXPOSED TO OXYGEN. THE AUTO-IGNITION OCCURRED IN THE PRESENCE OF A FLAMMABLE GAS CONCENTRATION ABOVE THE LEL.
AUTHORIZER_TITLE: DIRECTOR PIPELINE SAFETY AND ASSET INTEGRITY
NARRATIVE: AN ACCIDENTAL IGNITION OCCURRED AT STATION 60 ON MAY 8, 2024, AN INTERSTATE TRANSMISSION COMPRESSOR STATION OPERATED BY TRANSCONTINENTAL PIPE LINE COMPANY, LLC (TRANSCO) NEAR JACKSON, LOUISIANA. AT THE TIME, STATION 60 WAS OFFLINE AND ISOLATED FROM OPERATING PIPELINES FOR SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE TO REPLACE INTERNAL VALVE COMPONENTS CONNECTING THE COMPRESSOR UNITS TO THE STATION'S COMMON SUCTION AND DISCHARGE HEADERS. THE INCIDENT HAPPENED AT 6:32 AM, JUST BEFORE SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE WORK WAS TO BEGIN FOR THAT DAY.   THE FACILITY'S CONSTRUCTION PROVIDED FOR SINGLE VALVE ISOLATION BETWEEN EACH PIPELINE AND FACILITY PIPING. WORK PLANS ANTICIPATED LEAKAGE AT THESE ISOLATION POINTS AND INCORPORATED AN AIR PURGE PLAN TO MITIGATE POTENTIAL FLAMMABLE ENVIRONMENTS WITHIN PIPING.     THE ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT THE FOLLOWING CAUSES DIRECTLY LED TO THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS INCIDENT:     1. FLAMMABLE MATERIAL (NATURAL GAS) LEAKED INTO THE STATION PIPING AROUND THE PROCESS ISOLATION BETWEEN THE STATION AND TRANSMISSION PIPELINES;  2. THE MITIGATIVE AIR FLOW DID NOT MAINTAIN FLAMMABLE GAS CONCENTRATION BELOW THE LEL;  3. THE MITIGATIVE AIR FLOW DRIED INTERNAL SLUDGE DEPOSITS INSIDE THE STATION PIPING;  4. SUSPECTED ACCUMULATION OF PYROPHORIC IRON SULFIDE IN THE INTERNAL SLUDGE DEPOSITS OF THE STATION PIPING IGNITED UPON EXPOSURE TO OXYGEN; AND  5. A PERSON WAS PRESENT WITHIN THE FIRE ZONE AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENTAL IGNITION.    TO PREVENT SIMILAR ISSUES FROM RECURRING, WILLIAMS IS COMMITTED TO FOLLOWING THE RECOMMENDATIONS DERIVED FROM THE ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS. THIS INCLUDES PROVIDING COMPREHENSIVE GUIDANCE AND CONDUCTING REFRESHER TRAINING FOR ALL PERSONNEL INVOLVED. BY REINFORCING THE KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS NECESSARY FOR SAFE OPERATIONS, WILLIAMS AIMS TO ENSURE THAT PERSONNEL ARE EQUIPPED TO RECOGNIZE POTENTIAL HAZARDS AND RESPOND APPROPRIATELY.    PHMSA REVIEWED WILLIAMS RETURN TO SERVICE PLAN AND APPROVED ON JUNE 18TH, 2024. WILLIAMS STATION 60 RETURNED TO SERVICE ON JUNE 22ND AT 9:55 AM

NARRATIVE: ON 5/6/2024 AN EF4 TORNADO STRUCK THE CITY OF BARNSDALL, OKLAHOMA AT APPROXIMATELY 9:45 PM CDT. SSCGP METER FACILITIES FOR THE CITY OF BARNSDALL SETTING 3417 AND THE WAX REFINERY 3416 (BOTH SETTINGS ARE AT THE SAME LOCATION) WERE DAMAGED. SSCGP EMPLOYEES WERE ABLE TO ACCESS THE SITE AT 12:14 AM. A LEAK WAS DISCOVERED ON THE METER RUN AND THE ABOVE GROUND VALVES WERE IMMEDIATELY CLOSED. DUE TO THE DAMAGE OF THE PRIMARY METER RUNS, THE BY-PASS TO THE CITY WAS OPENED TO PROVIDE UN-INTERRUPTED SERVICE TO BARNSDALL AND IS BEING UTILIZED UNTIL TEMPORARY METERING FACILITIES ARE INSTALLED. SERVICE TO THE WAX REFINERY WAS SHUT OFF DUE TO EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE WAX REFINERY. TEMPORARY METERING FACILITIES WILL BE INSTALLED TO SERVE THE WAX REFINERY WHEN NATURAL GAS SERVICE IS REQUESTED BY THE REFINERY. THE FULL SCALE OF THE DAMAGE TO THE METER FACILITIES WAS NOT ABLE TO BE EVALUATED IMMEDIATELY DUE TO DEBRIS AND SITE CONDITIONS. AT APPROXIMATELY 8:00 AM ON 5/7/2024, A PRELIMINARY EVALUATION WAS MADE, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE OPERATOR'S PROPERTY DAMAGE WOULD EXCEED THE INCIDENT THRESHOLD OF $139,700. A NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 5/7/2024 AT 8:47 AM. SSCGP IS CURRENTLY SCOPING A PROJECT TO REPLACE ALL OF THE ABOVE GROUND FACILITIES AT THIS LOCATION.

NARRATIVE: ON APRIL 26, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 05:20 AM, A RUPTURE OCCURRED INVOLVING A THIRD-PARTY GAS PIPELINE FACILITY.  THIS EVENT OCCURRED APPROXIMATELY 270 FEET FROM ATMOS ENERGYS ABOVE GROUND STATION AT THE TERMINUS OF ITS TRANSMISSION LINE, LINE X2-7, WHICH WAS NOT CONNECTED TO THE SUBJECT RUPTURED FACILITIES. THE RUPTURE DAMAGED A PIPE SUPPORT AT ATMOS ENERGYS ABOVE GROUND STATION AND CAUSED IT TO SINK INTO THE SOIL, WHICH THEN CAUSED A 12-INCH VALVE FLANGE TO MISALIGN RESULTING IN A RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS AND SUBSEQUENT FIRE.  AT 07:15 AM, ATMOS ENERGY WAS NOTIFIED OF THE EVENT BY ANOTHER INVOLVED THIRD-PARTY OPERATOR.  THE FIRST ATMOS ENERGY TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ON SITE AND DETERMINED THE CONDITION TO EXIST AT APPROXIMATELY 07:49 AM.  ALTHOUGH ATMOS ENERGY WAS UNABLE TO APPROACH THE STATION YARD AT THIS TIME BECAUSE OF THE ONGOING EVENT, CONFIRMED DISCOVERY WAS DETERMINED AT 09:17 AM BASED UPON THE ANTICIPATED STATION DAMAGE COST.  ATMOS ENERGYS GAS CONTROLLERS, IN COORDINATION WITH ATMOS ENERGYS ONSITE EMPLOYEES, CLOSED VALVES TO ISOLATE LINE X2-7 AND THE UPSTREAM LATERAL LINE X2 FROM THE ATMOS ENERGY TRUNKLINE AT 8:10 AM. BOTH CONNECTED LINES WERE ALLOWED TO BLOW DOWN AT A SAFE DISTANCE FROM THE DAMAGED FACILITY. THE FIRE AT THE STATION WAS ALLOWED TO BURN OUT, WHICH OCCURRED AT APPROXIMATELY 12:45 PM.    ON MAY 3, 2024, THE X2 VALVE AT LINE X2-7 WAS CLOSED SEPARATING THESE TWO LINES. ON MAY 22, 2024, THE UPSTREAM LATERAL LINE X2 WAS PLACED BACK IN SERVICE. THE DAMAGED STATION AND THE ENTIRE 263 FEET OF LINE X2-7 WILL NOT BE REPLACED OR PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE.

NARRATIVE: ON 04-26-2024, AT 5:37 AM CENTRAL TIME, ENTERPRISE OBSERVED A PRESSURE DROP ON THE 20-INCH MIVIDA TRUNK C PIPELINE IN WARD COUNTY, TEXAS. THE INCIDENT OCCURRED SOUTHWEST OF PYOTE, TEXAS. ON 5:38 AM, ENTERPRISE PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED TO THE PIPELINE TO DETERMINE ITS STATUS. AT 05:57 AM, ENTERPRISE PERSONNEL REPORTED A FIRE IN THE GENERAL DIRECTION OF THE MIVIDA TRUNK C PIPELINE. THE ACTUAL TIME OF IGNITION IS UNKNOWN.    AT 6:15 AM, ENTERPRISE ARRIVED AT THE LEASE ROAD NEAR THE PIPELINE SCENE. LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS WERE ALREADY AT THE SCENE. IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE FIRE WAS LOCATED AT A CROSSING WITH A 3RD PARTY PIPELINE, WHICH WAS ALSO ON FIRE. AT 6:19 AM, ENTERPRISE PERSONNEL DETERMINED THAT THE FIRE IMPACTED ENTERPRISE MIVIDA TRUNK C PIPELINE. BETWEEN 6:20 AM AND 7:17 AM, THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED. AT 7:15 AM, ENTERPRISE DETERMINED THAT THE FIRE FROM THE PIPELINE MET INCIDENT REPORTING CRITERIA AND THE NRC/TRRC WERE NOTIFIED AT 7:33 AM AND 7:47 AM RESPECTIVELY. A REVIEW OF SCADA TRENDS INDICATED THE PRESSURE DROP STARTED AT 05:15 AM.      ON 04-30-2024, AT 15:45 PM, THE FIRE SELF-EXTINGUISHED. THE PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE ON 05-21-2024.     A METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS DETERMINED THAT THE CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS INTERNAL CORROSION OF THE PIPELINE AT THE 6:00 O'CLOCK POSITION.

NARRATIVE: ON 4/23/2024 WILLIAMS REPORTED AN EVENT TO THE NRC DUE TO THE TOTAL VOLUME OF GAS RELEASE EXCEEDING 3 MMSCF. THE TOTAL VOLUME RELEASED THAT DAY WAS 17.76 MMSCF TOTALING BETWEEN TWO PSV'S ON MAINLINE 40 AT THE JL40-47 JUNCTION AND GREEN RIVER METER STATION, BUT BOTH RELEASED GAS DUE TO THE SAME CAUSE. AT THE TIME OF THE INITIAL RELEASE OPERATIONS WAS ON SITE PERFORMING OTHER WORK AT THE JL40-47 JUNCTION.      AT THE TIME OF THE RELEASE THERE WERE TWO COMPRESSOR STATIONS THAT WERE DOWN FOR MAINTENANCE, THIS LEFT ONLY ONE COMPRESSOR STATION AVAILABLE IN THE AREA TO MOVE GAS. AN ISSUE ON THE FUEL CONTROL VALVE WITH THE COMPRESSOR THAT WAS RUNNING CAUSED REPEATED SHUTDOWNS. THESE REPEATED SHUTDOWNS CAUSED GAS TO BACKUP ON THE SYSTEM AND PRESSURES BUILT UP, ULTIMATELY CAUSING THE TWO PSV'S TO LIFT ON THE MAINLINE RESULTING IN THE GAS RELEASE THAT WAS REPORTED. THE FUEL VALVES WERE OPERATED IN SAFE MODE TO SUBVERT A POWERED HOUR LIMITATION SET BY SOLAR TO KEEP FLOW THROUGH ON THE LINE, HOWEVER THEY HAD TO BE RESTARTED EVERY HOUR TO MAINTAIN THIS.

NARRATIVE: ON APRIL 17TH 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 12:00 ENERGY TRANSFER RECEIVED A NOTIFICATION FROM THE PRODUCER SM ENERGY, OF A POSSIBLE LEAK IN THE AREA. ET PERSONNEL PROCEEDED WITH ISOLATING THE LINE AND AT 14:03 THE VALVES WERE LOCKED OUT. AT APPROXIMATELY 14:45, ENERGY TRANSFER PERSONNEL CONFIRMED A LEAK ON THE SME L11 12-INCH PIPELINE   (28.122918, -99.967259) AND PROCEEDED WITH BLOWING DOWN THE LINE. THIS IS A 12-INCH TYPE C GAS GATHERING LINE IN A RURAL AREA LOCATED IN WEBB COUNTY NEAR LAREDO, TX    AN ESTIMATED 22 MCF OF UNINTENTIONAL GAS WAS RELEASED. IT WAS DETERMINED UPON CONFIRMED DISCOVERY THAT THIS INCIDENT WOULD BE REPORTABLE DUE TO THE ESTIMATE OF PROPERTY DAMAGE AND REPAIR COST. THIS INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC ON APRIL 17TH 2024, AT 2:57PM    THE LINE WAS EXCAVATED AND A COUPON (36-INCHES LONG: 0.250-INCH WT) WAS SENT INTO ADV INTEGRITY FOR 3RD PARTY ANALYSIS.  FAILURE WAS FOUND TO BE AT THE 6 OCLOCK POSITION; PIT WAS MEASURED TO BE 0.5 INCHES IN DIAMETER.  SEDIMENT WAS FOUND, REMOVED, AND ANALYZED AT THE 6 OCLOCK POSITION OF THE PIPE.  THE FINDINGS SHOWED THE SEDIMENT HAD PRESENCE OF SIDERITE. AFTER REVIEWING OPERATIONAL AND HISTORICAL RECORDS ALONG WITH A LAB ANALYSIS WE HAVE CONCLUDED THAT THE COMBINATION OF SIDERITE, SAND, IRON SULFIDE, AND OTHER DEBRIS LED TO UNDER DEPOSIT CORROSION AND TO AN EVENTUAL FAILURE.  OUR EFFORTS TO MITIGATE THIS ISSUE IS TO MODIFY OUR MAINTENANCE PLAN BY INCORPORATING CHEMICAL TREATMENT FOR IRON SULFIDE AND SOLID DEPOSITS ALONG WITH OUR ROUTINE MAINTENANCE AND PIGGING

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE OPERATOR DID NOT FIND THAT AN INVESTIGATION OF THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WAS NECESSARY DUE TO: (PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION FOR WHY THE OPERATOR DID NOT INVESTIGATE)
INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: INCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY A IMPROPERLY INSTALLED LIMIT DEVICE ON THE GATE VALVE.
NARRATIVE: ON 4/16/2024, OPERATIONS WERE PERFORMING VALVE MAINTENANCE ON PL-115 GATE VALVE (WKM VALVE WITH AN EIM ACTUATOR). DURING THE CLOSE ACTUATION, THE VALVE FAILED TO STOP CLOSING WHEN COMMANDED AND RESULTED IN THE ACTUATOR BEING PUSH OFF THE STEM AND INTO THE GROUND.     THE END OF STROKE/LIMIT VALVE FAILED TO ISOLATE POWER GAS AFTER CLOSE, RESULTING IN ADDITIONAL TORQUE ENERGY BEING APPLIED TO THE GEARS HOUSING. ALL THAT ENERGY RESULTED IN THE FAILURE OF THE CAST IRON WELDS BETWEEN THE ACTUATOR AND YOKE/STEM HOUSING. WITH THE GEAR HOUSING OFF THE SHAFT, THE GATE VALVE COULD NOT REMAIN CLOSED AND PIPELINE PRESSURE SLAMMED IT OPEN AGAINST THE BONNET.     AS A RESULT OF THE ACTUATOR FAILURE, A STAINLESS POWER GAS LINE BROKE OFF AND VENTED.    THE EIM ACTUATOR CONTROLS WERE TESTED ON A BENCH WITH PRESSURIZED AIR AND DISCOVERED TO SUCCESSFULLY ACHIEVE THE OPEN LIMIT BUT NOT THE CLOSE LIMIT. THE CAUSE FOR THIS WAS THE CLOSE LIMIT VALVE WAS INSTALLED BACKWARDS, WHICH RESULTED IN THE POPPET BEING UNABLE TO ENGAGE WITH THE CAM AND FAILURE TO ISOLATE POWER GAS TO THE MOTOR ONCE THE VALVE WAS CLOSED.

AT 3:19PM ISOLATED SECTION WAS BLOWN FLAT.    ON 4/11/2024 PIPE WAS EXPOSED, AND LEAK IDENTIFIED AS A CRACKED WELD. A 44-FOOT PIPE SEGMENT WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED.  A SPECIMEN WAS SENT TO THE LAB FOR EVALUATION AND DETERMINATION OF CAUSE.     THE LAB REPORT WAS COMPLETED ON 11/1/2024 AND FOUND THAT THE LEAK WAS CAUSED BY A COMBINATION OF A PREEXISTING DELAYED HYDROGEN CRACK ON THE INTERNAL PIPE SURFACE AT THE TOE OF THE GIRTH WELD, THE PRESENCE OF A CONCENTRATED AXIAL STRESS AT THE BOTTOM OF THE PIPE, AND THE EXCEEDANCE OF THE GIRTH WELD'S TENSILE STRAIN CAPACITY.  THE CRACK MEASURED 34 INCHES LONG CIRCUMFERENTIALLY ALONG THE BOTTOM SIDE OF THE GIRTH WELD.

NARRATIVE: THE INCIDENT TOOK PLACE AT FARMER BROTHERS METERING AND REGULATING STATION BEGINNING AT 20:41 ON MARCH 25, 2024. AT THE FACILITY, NATURAL GAS NORMALLY FLOWS THROUGH A FILTER SEPARATOR WHERE ANY LIQUIDS FLOWING THROUGH THE SEPARATOR GO TO A SUMP. WHEN THE SUMP LEVEL EXCEEDS A CERTAIN THRESHOLD, THE CONTROLLER ACTUATES THE CONTROL VALVE TO THE OPEN POSITION TO RELEASE LIQUIDS TO A WASTE TANK. THE CONTROL VALVE'S MECHANICAL LEVEL CONTROLLER FAILED TO REVERT POSITION ONCE THE LIQUID LEVEL IN THE SUMP HAD DROPPED BELOW THE DESIGN THRESHOLD. GAS FLOWED FROM THE SEPARATOR THROUGH A 2" NPS PIPE INTO A WASTE TANK AND THEN ENTERED THE ATMOSPHERE THROUGH THE TANK BLOWOFF VALVES UNTIL THE AFTERNOON OF MARCH 26, 2024 AT 14:45 WHEN THE INCIDENT WAS DISCOVERED.    UPON ARRIVAL AT THE FACILITY, PERSONNEL IMMEDIATELY SHUT THE MANUAL VALVE TO THE LIQUIDS TANK WHICH CEASED EMISSIONS OF NATURAL GAS TO THE ATMOSPHERE AT 15:07 ON MARCH 26, 2024. THE CONTROLLER ON THE CONTROL VALVE WAS FOUND TO BE MISALIGNED BECAUSE THE SPRING TENSION WAS TOO HIGH, NOT ALLOWING THE VALVE TO CLOSE. THE VALVE'S CONTROLLER WAS IMMEDIATELY RECALIBRATED, RESET, AND TESTED TO ENSURE FUNCTIONALITY.

NARRATIVE: ON 03/09/2024, AT 2:45 AM, THE CISCO COMPRESSOR STATION SUFFERED A POWER OUTAGE ON THE ESD SYSTEM STRING, TRIGGERING AN UNINTENDED EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD) RESULTING IN A RELEASE OF 45 MMCF OF NATURAL GAS. WILLIAMS GAS CONTROL RECEIVED AN ALARM INDICATING THE CISCO COMPRESSOR STATION EXPERIENCED A FACILITY ESD EVENT. THE COMPRESSOR STATION WAS PRESSURIZED BUT NOT RUNNING AT THE TIME. PIPELINE CONTROL IMMEDIATELY NOTIFIED OPERATIONS, WHO DECIDED TO WAIT UNTIL DAYLIGHT TO RESPOND. THE DECISION WAS BASED ON PERSONAL SAFETY RISKS FROM NAVIGATING THE TERRAIN, AND THE BELIEF THAT THE STATION WAS STABILIZED DUE TO FAIL-SAFE VENT VALVES AND THE SUCTION AND DISCHARGE ISOLATION VALVES ON THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM SIDE OF THE STATION. THE SCADA SYSTEM INDICATED GAS PRESSURE ON THE MAINLINE SEGMENT AT CISCO COMPRESSOR STATION WAS DECLINING AND AT 3:45 AM, THE CONTROLLER CONTACTED OPERATIONS TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE. AN OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ONSITE ABOUT AN HOUR LATER. THE TECHNICIAN IDENTIFIED THE STATION DISCHARGE VALVE OPEN AND GAS VENTING THROUGH THE 8" BLOWDOWN. PIPELINE CONTROL REMOTELY CLOSED A MAINLINE BLOCK VALVE TO LIMIT THE RELEASE OF GAS AT 6:10 AM. THE ONSITE TECHNICIAN MANUALLY SHUT IN THE FACILITY TO STOP THE GAS RELEASE AT 6:18 AM.    THE OPERATIONS TEAM PERFORMED SIGNIFICANT TESTING AND TROUBLESHOOTING TO IDENTIFY THE CAUSE OF THE ESD AND THE FAILURE OF THE STATION DISCHARGE VALVE. THIS REVEALED A LOOSE CONNECTION ON A POWER JUMPER WIRE THAT SUPPLIES POWER TO THE ESD-RELATED RELAY STRINGS, WHICH GAVE THE INDICATION OF A POWER LOSS AND TRIGGERED THE ESD.  ONE OF THE STATION'S DISCHARGE VALVES FAILED TO PROPERLY CLOSE DURING THE ESD DUE TO A SOLENOID MALFUNCTION ON THE DISCHARGE VALVE. AFTER THESE CAUSES WERE IDENTIFIED, FUNCTION TESTS WERE COMPLETED PRIOR TO RETURNING THE COMPRESSOR STATION TO SERVICE.

EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: SOLENOID VALVE FAILED TO ALLOW ACTUATOR TO FUNCTION PROPERLY
NARRATIVE: ON 2/29/24, NATURAL GAS COMPRESSOR UNIT B (UNIT B) WAS STOPPED DURING ROUTINE SHUTDOWN; THIS ACTION ISSUES A "CLOSE" COMMAND TO UNIT B'S REMOTE ACTUATED SUCTION VALVE SD2401B. SDV2401B'S ACTUATOR USES A SOLENOID VALVE (VERSA VALVE) WHICH IS RESPONSIBLE FOR SDV2401B'S ACTUATOR OPERATION. THE SOLENOID VALVE MALFUNCTIONED PARTWAY THROUGH THE CLOSURE OF SDV2401B. SHORTLY AFTER, THE SCADA SYSTEM ALERTED THE CONTROLLER THAT SDV2401B FAILED TO CLOSE. THE ALERT WAS ACCOMPANIED BY ADDITIONAL ALARMS, 38 ALARMS IN A 40 SECOND PERIOD, WHICH INCLUDED NUISANCE ALARMS RELATED TO THE COMPRESSOR SHUTDOWN AS WELL AS ALARMS FROM UNRELATED FACILITY ACTIVITIES. ALL ALARMS WERE ACKNOWLEDGED BY THE CONTROLLER. SDV2401B REMAINED IN ALARM STATE AND SDV2401B WAS LEFT PARTIALLY OPEN.    A SHORT TIME LATER, THE CONTROLLER ISSUED A SCADA COMMAND TO STOP THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING FAN SYSTEM, WHICH, BY DESIGN, AUTOMATICALLY TRIGGERS A BLOW-DOWN OF ALL GAS PIPING INSIDE THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING VIA THE FACILITY BLOW-DOWN SYSTEM BY ISSUING AN 'OPEN' COMMAND TO BYPASS VALVE XXV2404B AND BLOW-DOWN VALVE BDV2411B. THIS BLOW-DOWN PROCESS IS NECESSARY TO ENSURE THE BUILDING SAFE TO WORK IN.    WITH SDV2401B STILL IN A PARTIALLY OPEN POSITION AND XXV2404B AND BDV2411B IN THE OPEN POSITION, A FLOW PATH WAS PRESENT FOR GAS TO CONTINUOUSLY FLOW FROM THE SUCTION SIDE OF THE COMPRESSOR UNIT B TO THE FACILITY BLOW-DOWN SYSTEM, RESULTING IN A CONTINUOUS RELEASE OF GAS @ 600 MSCF/H. ON 3/1/2024, THE MEASUREMENT TECH RECOGNIZED AN ISSUE WITH A GAS FLOW TOTALIZER IMBALANCE AND THEN DISCUSSED THE ISSUE WITH THE ON-SHIFT CONTROLLER. THE OPERATIONS TEAM INSPECTED THE FACILITY BLOW-DOWN SYSTEM AND IDENTIFIED GAS VENTING FROM THE TOP OF THE BLOW-DOWN SILENCER. IMMEDIATE ACTION WAS TAKEN TO CLOSE THE FACILITY'S BLOW-DOWN SYSTEM AND HALT THE GAS RELEASE.     SDV2401B WAS STROKED TO TROUBLESHOOT AND TEST WHAT SHOULD HAVE BEEN NORMAL FUNCTION. THE ACTUATOR SOLENOID VALVE (VERSA VALVE) WAS SERVICED, AND PROPER OPENING/CLOSURE WAS VERIFIED WITH SDV2401B.

NARRATIVE: ON FEBRUARY 17TH, 2024, TC ENERGY WAS NOTIFIED BY A LANDOWNER OF A POTENTIAL GAS RELEASE AT THE CLENDENIN COMPRESSOR STATION. TC ENERGY OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT CLENDENIN COMPRESSOR STATION, LOCATED IN KANAWHA WEST VIRGINIA, AND DISCOVERED NATURAL GAS BEING RELEASED TO ATMOSPHERE. THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS RELEASE WAS CONFIRMED BY THE ONSITE TC ENERGY OPERATIONS. A ESD ACTUATOR FAILED DUE TO FREEZING CONDITIONS, A VALVE ACTUATOR ON THE ESD SYSTEM FAILED AND TRAVELED OPEN. THE PILOT AIR VENT FROZE DUE TO CONDENSATION SENDING A FALSE SIGNAL TO THE TRIGGER, CAUSING THE VALVE TO TRAVEL TO THE OPEN POSITION.  THE SWITCH MALFUNCTION CAUSED NO ALARM TO BE CONVEYED TO THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN DEVICE (ESD) PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC CONTROLLER (PLC) AND THEREFORE, IS THE REASON THE GAS CONTROLLER DID NOT RECEIVE A "CALL OUT" ALARM FROM THE SCADA SYSTEM. TC ENERGY CONFIRMED THAT THE CLENDENIN STATION CONTINUED TO OPERATE UNDER NORMAL CONDITIONS. TC ENERGY OPERATIONS INVESTIGATED THE OCCURRENCE, CONFIRMED THE CAUSE, CLEARED ALL OBSTRUCTIONS AND ESD ACTUATOR WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE. NARRATIVE: THE ANDERSON COUNTY 12" OR TC-12 PIPELINE SYSTEM IS A NATURAL GAS TRANSMISSION PIPELINE LOCATED IN ANDERSON, HENDERSON AND HOUSTON COUNTIES IN TEXAS. ON FEBRUARY 10, 2024, M6 MIDSTREAM LLC FIELD PERSONNEL WERE NOTIFIED BY OUR CONTROL CENTER THAT THERE WERE SOME PRESSURE DISCREPANCIES ON THE TC-12 PIPELINE SYSTEM LOCATED IN ANDERSON COUNTY, TEXAS. UPON INVESTIGATION, M6 FIELD PERSONNEL ASSUMED THAT THERE COULD BE A PIPELINE LEAK ON THE TC-12 PIPELINE SYSTEM AND BEGIN ISOLATING THE PIPELINE SYSTEM. THE TC-12 PIPELINE SYSTEM WAS ISOLATED AND FIELD PERSONNEL COMMENCED BLOW DOWN OPERATIONS. DUE TO SEVERE WEATHER CONDITIONS (HIGH WINDS, THUNDERSTORM, LIGHTNING AND LOCALIZED FLOODING), AND NO AMBIENT LIGHTING, THE LEAK LOCATION WAS NOT CONFIRMED UNTIL FEBRUARY 11, 2024.ON FEBRUARY 11, 2024, UPON VISUAL INSPECTION OF FAILURE SITE IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE LEAK WOULD BE ABOVE THE REPORTABLE THRESHOLD AMOUNT OF GAS LOSS (> 3MMCFD) PER 191.3, SO NOTIFICATIONS WERE MADE AT THIS POINT. THE PIPELINE SEGMENT INVOLVED AT THE SITE OF THE FAILURE WAS SENT TO A THIRD-PARTY TESTING FACILITY TO DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF FAILURE. THE LAB RESULTS CONCLUDED THE LOCALIZED FAILURE WAS DUE TO ENVIRONMENTALLY ASSISTED CRACKING CAUSED BY EXTERNAL HYDROGEN EMBRITTLEMENT.

NARRATIVE: ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 9TH, 2024, WILLIAMS CALLED THE NRC TO REPORT AN INCIDENT NORTH OF STATION 190 NEAR MILEPOST 1655 ON TRANSCO MAINLINE A, ORIGINAL NRC# 1391164. A GAS SMELL WAS REPORTED THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 8TH, 2024. WILLIAMS EMPLOYEES HEADED TO THE SCENE TO INVESTIGATE THE SAME DAY BUT WERE UNABLE TO LOCATE THE LEAK. OPERATIONS CONTINUED TO LOOK FOR THE LEAK AND LOCATED IT THE FOLLOWING MORNING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 9TH, 2024. THE AFFECTED SECTION OF THE LINE WAS SUBSEQUENTLY ISOLATED WITHIN 3 HOURS AFTER THE LEAK WAS IDENTIFIED. RELEASE RATE IS CURRENTLY ESTIMATED AT 2342 SCF PER DAY. EQUIPMENT WAS MOBILIZED TO SITE TO DRAWDOWN AND EXCAVATE. DRAWDOWN BEGAN SUNDAY, FEBRUARY 11TH, 2024 AND WAS COMPLETED ON THE 13TH. EXCAVATION WAS SUBSEQUENTLY STARTED THE SAME DAY. EXCAVATION AND DRAW DOWN WAS COMPLETED ON THE 14TH. BLOWDOWN OCCURRED ON THE 14TH AND CUT-OUT/TIE-IN WAS DONE ON THE 15TH. ON FEBRUARY 16TH, 2024 THE COATING PROCESS WAS COMPLETED AND THE LINE WAS PLACED BACK IN SERVICE. AN INVESTIGATION AND ROOT CAUSE WAS CONDUCTED TO IDENTIFY THE CAUSE OF THE FAILURE. AFTER THE INVESTIGATION AND RESULTING METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS, THE LEAK ORIGINATED WITHIN AN ORIGINAL MANUFACTURING MILL WELD REPAIR OR "SQUIRT WELD" PASS APPLIED TO THE DSAW SEAM WELD IMMEDIATELY UPSTREAM OF THE GIRTH WELD PROVIDED. THE LEAK WAS THE RESULT OF SOLIDIFICATION CRACKS PRESENT WITHIN THE EXTERNAL AND INTERNAL SEAM WELD PASS THAT WERE POSITIONED IMMEDIATELY ADJACENT TO ONE ANOTHER.

NARRATIVE: OPERATOR RECEIVED A CALL AT 1:06AM ON 2/3/2024 FROM A RESIDENT NEAR SARDIS MS. RESIDENT STATED THAT THEY HEARD NOISE COMING FROM THE DIRECTION OF THE PIPELINE. PERSONNEL WAS DISPATCHED TO THE SITE. DISCOVERY OF THE INCIDENT OCCURRED AT 2:11AM. SEGMENT WAS ISOLATED AND BLOWN DOWN. UPON EXCAVATION, THREE WRINKLE BENDS WERE FOUND AT THE INCIDENT SITE. INVESTIGATION IS ONGOING; THE FAILED PIPE WAS SENT FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. PHMSA HAS ISSUED CPF2-2024-009-CAO.    7/3/2024: THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS WAS COMPLETED IN APRIL 2024, WITH THE FINAL REPORT BEING ISSUED MAY 29, 2024. THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS CONCLUDED THAT THE LEAK EVENT WAS CAUSED BY THE FORMATION OF A CIRCUMFERENTIAL CRACK RUNNING ALONG THE HIGHEST POINT OF A WRINKLE BEND. METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS CONSISTED OF VISUAL EXAMINATION, TESTING OF MECHANICAL PROPERTIES, CHEMISTRY, AND CHARPY IMPACT TOUGHNESS. BASED ON THE TESTS, ALL MECHANICAL PROPERTIES WERE FOUND TO BE CONSISTENT WITH API STANDARDS AT THE TIME OF PIPELINE CONSTRUCTION. THE TWO KEY MECHANISMS THAT CAUSED THE INITIATION AND GROWTH OF THESE CRACKS ARE HIGHER DENSITY OF HYDROGEN TRAPS WITHIN THE ID MICROSTRUCTURE, AS SUBSTANTIATED BY THE PRESENCE OF ID SECONDARY CRACKING, IN ADDITION TO POSSIBLY LOW TEMPERATURES.     6/2/2025: THE ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS (RCFA) AND REMEDIAL WORK PLAN WAS COMPLETED AND SUBMITTED TO PHMSA ON 7/10/2024.     ON 3/10/2025, THE CAO DOCUMENTATION REPORT (CDR) WAS COMPLETED AND SUBMITTED TO PHMSA. ON   3/28/2025 TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE COMPANY (TGP) RECEIVED A CLOSURE LETTER FROM PHMSA OF THE CPF 2-2024-009-CAO.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: PUBLIC DISCOVERY OF LEAK.
CAUSE: MATERIAL FAILURE OF PIPE OR WELD
NARRATIVE: KINDER MORGAN TEJAS GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A CALL FROM A HOMEOWNER OF A POTENTIAL NATURAL GAS LEAK.  KM OPERATIONS PERSONNEL RESPONDED AND VERIFIED THE PRESENCE OF GAS ABOVEGROUND ON ITS 202-600 LINE.  NOTIFICATIONS WERE MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER AND THE TXRRC.  OPERATIONS WERE DISPATCHED TO THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES AND LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS (HOUSTON FIRE DEPARTMENT) WERE CONTACTED AND DISPATCHED.  UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES WERE CLOSED IN ORDER THAT THE PIPELINE SEGMENT COULD BE ISOLATED, AND THE PIPELINE BLOWN DOWN.  THE LEAK SITE WAS EXCAVATED, AND EVALUATION AND REPAIR OF THE LEAK BEGAN.  DURING EVALUATION THE CAUSE OF THE LEAK WAS DETERMINED TO BE FROM A CRACK AT A TAP WELD (TAP HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN ABANDONED). THE REPAIR OF THE LEAK CONSISTED OF THE SEGMENT OF PIPE CONTAINING THE ABANDONED TAP BEING CUT OUT AND REPLACED WITH A PIECE OF STRAIGHT PIPE.  THE KINDER MORGAN METALLURGY DEPARTMENT IS CURRENTLY IN THE PROCESS OF COMPLETING A FAILURE ANALYSIS OF THE FAILED TAP CONNECTION.  UPON THE COMPLETION OF THE FAILURE ANALYSIS INVESTIGATION THIS REPORT WILL BE UPDATED AND FINALIZED.      ON MAY 9, 2024; KINDER MORGAN'S METALLURGICAL ENGINEER COMPLETED THE FAILURE ANALYSIS OF THE TAP CONNECTION.  THE FINAL CONCLUSIONS AS TO THE CAUSE OF THE FAILURE WERE: 1) THE LEAK WAS LOCATED AT THE TOE OF THE FILLET WELD ATTACHING A LOCALIZED SADDLE TO THE NPS 20 PIPE; 2) THE HOT TAP CONNECTION DID NOT HAVE A FULL ENCIRCLEMENT SADDLE, AND 3) THE FRACTURE WAS A DUCTILE OVERLOAD CAUSED BY A DOWNWARD BENDING MOMENT ON THE NPS 2 BRANCH.    PLEASE NOTE THAT CURRENT KINDER MORGAN ENGINEERING STANDARD E1100 WOULD NOT PERMIT NEW CONSTRUCTION WITH THIS NPS 2 ON NPS 20 HOT TAP CONFIGURATION WITH ONLY A LOCALIZED SADDLE AND NO FULL ENCIRCLEMENT SLEEVE.
