INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WERE NOT A CAUSE OF OR A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR TO THE INCIDENT.
NARRATIVE: OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT RECEIVED INITIAL NOTIFICATION OF THIS INCIDENT VIA A CALL FROM ANOTHER OPERATOR ON 1/25/24 AT APPROXIMATELY 18:06 REPORTING THAT THERE WAS A FIRE AT BIG ISAAC COMPRESSOR STATION (CS) (GA-1081). GAS CONTROL RECEIVED SECONDARY NOTIFICATION OF THIS INCIDENT FROM 911 AT APPROXIMATELY 18:10 REPORTING THAT A FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS IN THE PROCESS OF RESPONDING TO BIG ISAAC CS. AT THIS POINT IN TIME, THE EXTENT AND SEVERITY OF THE FIRE AT BIG ISAAC CS WAS NOT KNOWN. BIG ISAAC CS RECEIVES NATURAL GAS FROM H-21912 (PRODUCTION PIPELINE OWNED AND OPERATED BY HOPE GAS), COMPRESSES IT USING A SINGLE ENGINE UNIT (AJAX MODEL #DPC-360LE INSTALLED IN 1999 WITH 360 HP), AND DISCHARGES IT INTO TL-413 (GATHERING PIPELINE OWNED AND OPERATED BY EGTS WITH A MAOP OF 500 PSIG). GAS CONTROL NOTIFIED OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT OF THE SITUATION AT APPROXIMATELY 18:15. OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT DISPATCHED PERSONNEL TO BIG ISAAC CS AT APPROXIMATELY 18:20. GAS CONTROL LOST COMMUNICATIONS WITH BIG ISAAC CS AT APPROXIMATELY 17:19. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT BIG ISAAC CS AT APPROXIMATELY 19:24, ASSESSED THE SITUATION, BEGAN UTILIZING FIRE EXTINGUISHERS TO EXTINGUISH THE FIRE ON THE ENGINE UNIT, AND IMPLEMENTED A PLAN TO SAFELY ISOLATE THE FIRE. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL MANUALLY CLOSED VALVE 1081-A AT APPROXIMATELY 19:26. VALVE 1081-A IS LOCATED WHERE THE DISCHARGE OF BIG ISAAC CS CONNECTS TO TL-413 WHICH IS DOWNSTREAM OF THE FIRE. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL MANUALLY CLOSED VALVE 1081-B AT APPROXIMATELY 19:27. VALVE 1081-B IS LOCATED WHERE THE SUCTION OF BIG ISAAC CS CONNECTS TO H-21912 WHICH IS UPSTREAM OF THE FIRE. THE CLOSURE OF VALVES 1081-A AND 1081-B COMPLETED THE ISOLATION OF THE FIRE. THE FIRE WAS EXTINGUISHED AT APPROXIMATELY 19:36. THE RESPONDING FIRE DEPARTMENT DEPARTED THE FACILITY AT APPROXIMATELY 20:20. THE RESPONDING OPERATIONS PERSONNEL SECURED THE FACILITY AND DEPARTED THE FACILITY AT APPROXIMATELY 23:20. NO EVACUATIONS WERE NECESSARY, NO INJURIES WERE SUSTAINED, AND THERE WERE NO ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS AS A RESULT OF THE INCIDENT. BASED ON REVIEW OF THE SCADA DISCHARGE PRESSURE AND FLOW HISTORIES, IT IS BELIEVED THAT THE FIRE ON THE ENGINE UNIT INITIALLY IGNITED AT APPROXIMATELY 17:19 AT WHICH POINT IN TIME BIG ISAAC CS WAS IN NORMAL OPERATION WITH A DISCHARGE PRESSURE OF 197 PSIG AND A DISCHARGE FLOW RATE OF 832 MSCFD. UPON EXAMINATION OF THE ENGINE UNIT FOLLOWING THE INCIDENT, IT IS BELIEVED THAT THE FIRE INITIATED IN THE LOW STAGE DISTANCE PIECE AND WAS SUSTAINED BY THE FLOW OF NATURAL GAS. THE CROSSHEAD PIN AND BUSHING SEIZED CAUSING INCREASED FRICTION ON THE SLIDE ASSEMBLY. THE ADDITIONAL FRICTION INCREASED THE TEMPERATURE ON THE COMPONENTS TO THE FLASHPOINT OF THE OIL (459 DEG F). THE CROSSHEAD RUNNING POSITION WAS ALTERED BY MELTING BABBITT CAUSING THE COMPRESSOR PISTON ROD TO RUB THE PRESSURE AND WIPER PACKING CASES. EXCESSIVE RUBBING OF THE COMPRESSOR PISTON ROD GENERATED EXCESSIVE HEAT INSIDE A GASEOUS ENVIRONMENT WHICH ALLOWED COMBUSTION TO OCCUR. THE INTERNAL FIRE MELTED THE PLEXIGLASS COVER ON THE CROSSHEAD DISTANCE PIECE HOUSING WHICH ALLOWED ADDITIONAL OXYGEN TO ENTER THE DISTANCE PIECE AND SUSTAINED THE COMBUSTION. THE RESULTING DAMAGE TO THE ENGINE UNIT AND THE ASSOCIATED FRAME, SKID, PROTECTIVE STRUCTURE, CONTROL PANEL, COMPRESSOR BOTTLE, FUEL LINE VALVE, AND CONDUIT WAS EXTENSIVE. INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ON 1/25/24 AT 19:28. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1389962 TO THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION OF THE INCIDENT. A FOLLOW UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ON 1/27/24 AT 09:04 TO CONFIRM THE INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1389962 TO THE FOLLOW UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION. THE DECISION WAS MADE TO ABANDON BIG ISAAC CS AS AN ENGINE UNIT AT LAW CS IS CAPABLE OF MOVING THE NATURAL GAS THAT WAS MOVED BY THE ENGINE UNIT AT BIG ISAAC CS.

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 24, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 01:57, ENERGY TRANSFER (ET) PERSONNEL WERE NOTIFIED BY GAS CONTROL OF A COMPRESSOR SHUT DOWN AT THE GALVAN G5L CDP COMPRESSOR STATION. THIS STATION IS TIED TO THE G5LTS-8 LINE, WHICH IS A TYPE C GAS GATHERING LINE LOCATED IN WEBB COUNTY. ET PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE AT 02:43 AND NOTICED THAT A UNIT WAS ON FIRE. ET PERSONNEL IMMEDIATELY INITIATED AN EMERGENCY AREA SHUT DOWN BY CLOSING THE FUEL LINE SUCTION HEADER VALVE AND DISCHARGE VALVE AT 02:45, WHICH ALLOWED THE FIRE TO BURN OUT.  DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO THE INDIVIDUAL ENGINE.  THE UNIT WITH THE DAMAGED ENGINE WAS ISOLATED FROM THE COMMON HEADER, WHICH ALSO HAS 3 OTHER UNITS CONNECTED TO IT.  THE FACILITY WAS STARTED UP AND PUT BACK IN SERVICE ON JANUARY 24TH AT APPROXIMATELY 06:41 ON JANUARY 24TH.  THE DAMAGED ENGINE HAS BEEN ASSESSED AND A REPLACEMENT ENGINE HAS BEEN ORDERED.  INVESTIGATION REVEALED THAT THE MOST APPARENT CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS THE STARTER/FUEL GAS SUPPLY DEVELOPED A LEAK AT OR NEAR THE VICINITY OF THE SEALCO VALVE. THE START/FUEL GAS LEAKAGE FOUND AN IGNITION SOURCE. THE HIGH HEAT CAUSED THE SEALCO VALVE TO LOSE ITS INTEGRITY AND SEPARATE INTO PIECES. THIS ALLOWED THE 120# OF FUEL GAS TO FUEL THE FIRE.

NARRATIVE: WBI ENERGY RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE GOLVA, ND FIRE CHIEF, WHO LIVES CLOSE BY THE GOLVA COMPRESSOR STATION, AT 9:32AM TO NOTIFY US THAT THERE WAS GAS BLOWING AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION.  TWO WBI OPERATORS ARRIVED ON SITE AT 9:58AM AND ESD'D THE STATION.  WHEN THE OPERATORS ASSESSED THE SITUATION, THEY FOUND THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING FROM THE PILOT THAT HOLDS THE RELIEF VALVE CLOSED HAD FAILED WHICH CAUSED THE RELIEF VALVE TO OPEN AND VENT GAS TO THE ATMOSPHERE.  THE OPERATORS REPLACED THE FAILED TUBING AND PUT THE STATION BACK IN SERVICE AT 11:50AM.      THIS SAME INCIDENT HAPPENED ON 1/7/23 AT THE GOLVA COMPRESSOR STATION ALSO.  WBI PERFORMED AN INVESTIGATION OF THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING FAILURE THAT OCCURRED ON 1/7/24 AND 1/23/24. WBI'S VIBRATION TECHNICIAN TOOK VIBRATION INTENSITY READINGS ON THE RELIEF VALVE AND ASSOCIATED TUBING AND FOUND AMPLITUDES EXCEEDING ALLOWABLE LIMITS. WBI REPLACED THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING WITH HEAVIER WALL TUBING AND HAS DEVELOPED A PLAN FOR INSTALLING DIAGONAL SUPPORTS TO THE RELIEF VALVE AND ASSOCIATED PIPING. THE INSTALLATION OF THE ADDITIONAL SUPPORTS IS CURRENTLY BEING PLANNED AND TENTATIVELY SCHEDULED FOR THE FIRST WEEK IN MARCH. AFTER THE INSTALLATION OF THE SUPPORTS, ADDITIONAL VIBRATION ANALYSIS WILL BE PERFORMED TO CONFIRM THE VIBRATION HAS BEEN DAMPENED TO AN ALLOWABLE MAGNITUDE.    UPDATE - INSTALLATION OF ADDITIONAL SUPPORTS WERE ADDED TO UNIT #1 ON 2/28 AND TO UNIT #2 ON 2/29

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 21, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 10:30 A.M. ET, ENBRIDGE WAS NOTIFIED BY THE LOCAL SHERIFF DEPARTMENT THAT A STRANGE NOISE WAS ORIGINATING FROM THE REGULATOR STATION. THE RESPONDING TECHNICIAN DISCOVERED THAT A 3-INCH RELIEF VALVE WAS VENTING, EVEN THOUGH STATION PRESSURES WERE WITHIN NORMAL OPERATING RANGE BELOW THE MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE OPERATING PRESSURE (MAOP).  AT APPROXIMATELY 10:45 A.M. THE RELIEF VALVE WAS ISOLATED ELIMINATING THE GAS FROM VENTING TO ATMOSPHERE.     THE INITIAL INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THE RELIEF VALVE LIKELY BEGAN VENTING AROUND 4:30 A.M. ET.  THE STATION IS LOCATED OFF A RURAL COUNTY ROAD WITH LIMITED TRAFFIC.  THE NEAREST DWELLING WAS APPROXIMATELY 1/2 MILE FROM THE STATION.  THE PRELIMINARY GAS LOSS CALCULATIONS WERE COMPLETED AT APPROXIMATELY 1:10 P.M. ET CONFIRMING THAT THE VOLUME WOULD LIKELY EXCEED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD.  THE NRC WAS SUBSEQUENTLY NOTIFIED AT 1:55 P.M. ET.     THE RESPONDING TECHNICIAN DETERMINED THAT THE RELIEF VALVE MALFUNCTIONED AS THE RESULT OF A BLOCKED PILOT LINE (I.E. FROZEN).  THE TECHNICIAN REPAIRED THE PILOT AND PLACED THE RELIEF VALVE BACK IN SERVICE.

NARRATIVE: ON 01/18/24, MIDWESTERN GAS TRANSMISSION PARIS COMPRESSOR STATION PERSONNEL WERE PERFORMING A ROUTINE INSPECTION OF THE STATION YARD PIPING WHEN THEY OBSERVED DAMAGE TO THE VALVE ACTUATOR ON MAINLINE VALVE 2113. AS THEY APPROACHED THE AREA, THEY NOTICED THE MAINLINE VALVE ACTUATOR HAD DISLODGED STRIKING THE MAINLINE VALVE 2113 BYPASS LINE. THE PARIS STATION WAS NOT RUNNING AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT. OPERATIONS CONFIRMED THAT THE DAMAGE DID NOT CAUSE A RELEASE. AN INVESTIGATION FOUND THAT WATER ENTERED THE INSIDE OF THE STEM EXTENSION HOUSING. BELOW ZERO TEMPERATURES CAUSED THIS WATER TO FREEZE AND BLOCK THE STEM EXTENSION VENT. DURING THE RESPONSE OPERATIONS FOUND A SMALL STEM LEAK ON MAINLINE VALVE 2113. THE STEM WAS LEAKING INTO THE VALVE EXTENSION HOUSING. THE FROZEN VENT DID NOT ALLOW THE PRESSURE TO EVACUATE THE STEM EXTENSION HOUSING WHICH CAUSED PRESSURE TO BUILD AND DISLODGE THE VALVE STEM EXTENSION. WHEN THE VALVE STEM DISLODGED, IT DAMAGED THE VALVE ACTUATOR AND BYPASS LINE. PROPERTY DAMAGE COSTS TO THE VALVE ACTUATOR AND BYPASS LINE EXCEEDED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE SWITCH MALFUNCTION CAUSED NO ALARM TO BE CONVEYED TO THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN DEVICE (ESD) PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC CONTROLLER (PLC) AND THEREFORE, IS THE REASON THE GAS CONTROLLER DID NOT RECEIVE A "CALL OUT" ALARM FROM THE SCADA SYSTEM.
NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 17, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 06:20 HRS. CST, TC ENERGY WAS NOTIFIED BY THE PUBLIC OF A POTENTIAL GAS RELEASE AT THE CORINTH COMPRESSOR STATION. TC ENERGY OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT THE CORINTH COMPRESSOR STATION, LOCATED IN ALCORN COUNTY, MISSISSIPPI, AND DISCOVERED NATURAL GAS BEING RELEASED TO ATMOSPHERE. THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS RELEASE WAS CONFIRMED BY TC ENERGY OPERATIONS AT APPROXIMATELY 07:16 HRS. CST. IT WAS LATER CONFIRMED THAT THE BLOWDOWN VALVE'S (5SH-2D BD) STRATIX SWITCH, WHICH COMMUNICATES THE VALVE STATUS TO THE PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC CONTROLLER (PLC), HAD MALFUNCTIONED. THIS LOSS OF COMMUNICATION CAUSED THE BLOWDOWN VALVE TO LOSE ITS HOLD CLOSED COMMAND FROM THE PLC. THE VALVE TRAVELED OPEN DUE TO THE LOSS OF SIGNAL WHEN THE SWITCH FAILED. THE MALFUNCTIONING SWITCH ALSO CAUSED NO ALARM TO BE CONVEYED TO THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN DEVICE (ESD) PLC AND THEREFORE, IS THE REASON GAS CONTROLLER DID NOT RECEIVE A "CALL OUT" ALARM FROM THE SCADA SYSTEM. TC ENERGY OPERATIONS MANUALLY CLOSED THE BLOWDOWN VALVE AT 08:05 HRS. CST. THE STRATIX SWITCH WAS REPLACED AND THE ESD SYSTEM WAS PLACED BACK INTO NORMAL SERVICE AT APPROXIMATELY 12:30 HRS. CST. THE CORINTH COMPRESSOR STATION WAS NOT SHUT DOWN DUE TO THIS INCIDENT.

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 12, 2024, SEVERE WEATHER INCLUDING HEAVY WINDS AND SNOW PASSED THROUGH THE GAYLORD, MICHIGAN AREA. WHEN THE POWER FAILURE ALARM WAS DETECTED AT 7:23 PM. EST, THE MONITORING CENTER CALLED THE TECHNICIAN ON SITE TO INVESTIGATE THE ALARM. THE TECHNICIAN COULD AUDIBLY HEAR BLOWING GAS AT 7:47 P.M. EST AND DISCOVERED NATURAL GAS WAS BEING RELEASED THROUGH THE UNIT 1101 BLOWDOWN STACK. IMMEDIATE ACTION WAS TAKEN BY VERIFYING NO FIRE EXISTED, A SECOND TECHNICIAN WAS DISPATCHED AND ASSISTED IN SAFELY ISOLATING THE GAS SOURCE BY CLOSING THE UNIT VALVE WHICH ENDED THE RELEASE AT 8:15 P.M. EST. UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS CALCULATED TO 3.61 MILLION CUBIC FEET (MMCF).     NO PROPERTY DAMAGE, INJURIES OR MANDATORY EVACUATIONS WERE NEEDED. THE CAUSE OF THE UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE WAS ATTRIBUTED TO LOSS OF COMMERCIAL POWER AT THE STATION. THE BACKUP GENERATOR DID NOT START, AND THE VENT VALVE OPENED DUE TO THE LOSS OF CONTROL AIR PRESSURE.  THE VENT VALVE IS DESIGNED TO FAIL OPEN IN THE CASE OF LOW CONTROL AIR AND THE SUCTION AND DISCHARGE VALVES REMAINED IN THEIR CURRENT OPEN OPERATING STATE. NO OVERPRESSURE OCCURRED.     AFTER FURTHER INVESTIGATION OF THE AUXILIARY POWER UNIT (APU), IT WAS FOUND TWO LOOSE PRIMARY IGNITION WIRES CAUSED THE ENGINE TO BACKFIRE WHICH RESULTED IN A SMALL TEAR IN THE LEFT BANK SECONDARY FUEL REGULATOR DIAPHRAGM. A LOW HORSEPOWER CONDITION ENSUED. APU WAS REPAIRED AND PLACED BACK IN SERVICE ON JANUARY 19, 2024.    MODIFIED SECTIONS A5, G2 AND G6 PER PHMSA'S REQUEST.

NARRATIVE: WBI ENERGY RECEIVED A CALL FROM A CONCERNED CITIZEN AT 4:41 PM ON 1/7/24 THAT THERE WAS A LOUD GAS BLOWING SOUND AT THE GOLVA COMPRESSOR STATION.  A WBI OPERATOR ARRIVED ON SITE AT 5:14 PM AND ASSESSED THE SITUATION.  THEY FOUND THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING FROM THE PILOT THAT HOLDS THE RELIEF VALVE CLOSED HAD FAILED WHICH CAUSED THE RELIEF VALVE TO OPEN AND VENT GAS TO THE ATMOSPHERE.  THE OPERATOR WAS ABLE TO PARTIALLY ISOLATE THE STATION AND REPLACE THE BROKEN SUPPLY LINE TUBING.  THE STATION WAS RETURNED TO NORMAL SERVICE AT 6:34 PM.      THE VOLUME OF GAS RELEASED TO ATMOSPHERE WAS NOT CALCULATED ON THE DAY OF THE INCIDENT.  A SIMILAR INCIDENT HAPPENED AT THE GOLVA COMPRESSOR STATION ON 1/23/24 AND WHEN SCADA DATA WAS BEING REVIEWED, THE VOLUME RELEASED ON 1/7/24 WAS IDENTIFIED AND CALCULATED.  AFTER THE VOLUME WAS CALCULATED AND IDENTIFIED AS BEING OVER THE 3,000 MCF THRESHOLD, THE INCIDENT WAS TELEPHONED TO THE NRC.    WBI PERFORMED AN INVESTIGATION OF THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING FAILURE THAT OCCURRED ON 1/7/24 AND 1/23/24.  WBI'S VIBRATION TECHNICIAN TOOK VIBRATION INTENSITY READINGS ON THE RELIEF VALVE AND ASSOCIATED TUBING AND FOUND AMPLITUDES EXCEEDING ALLOWABLE LIMITS.  WBI REPLACED THE SUPPLY LINE TUBING WITH HEAVIER WALL TUBING AND HAS DEVELOPED A PLAN FOR INSTALLING DIAGONAL SUPPORTS TO THE RELIEF VALVE AND ASSOCIATED PIPING.  THE INSTALLATION OF THE ADDITIONAL SUPPORTS IS CURRENTLY BEING PLANNED AND TENTATIVELY SCHEDULED FOR THE FIRST WEEK IN MARCH.  AFTER THE INSTALLATION OF THE SUPPORTS, ADDITIONAL VIBRATION ANALYSIS WILL BE PERFORMED TO CONFIRM THE VIBRATION HAS BEEN DAMPENED TO AN ALLOWABLE MAGNITUDE.    UPDATE - INSTALLATION OF ADDITIONAL SUPPORTS WERE ADDED TO UNIT #1 ON 2/28 AND TO UNIT #2 ON 2/29.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: NO SCADA ALARMS WERE INITIATED AS A RESULT OF THE EVENT.
NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 30, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 05:04 HOURS A CONSUMERS ENERGY WHITE PIGEON COMPRESSOR STATION EMPLOYEE RESIDING NEAR THE STATION REPORTED HEARING WHAT SEEMED TO BE VENTING GAS. THE WHITE PIGEON COMPRESSOR STATION IS IN WHITE PIGEON, MICHIGAN (ST. JOSEPH COUNTY).    THE EMPLOYEE ARRIVED AT THE SITE AT APPROXIMATELY 05:15 HOURS AND DISCOVERED GAS VENTING FROM A BLOWDOWN VALVE (VALVE 623) AND PROMPTLY ACTIVATE THE PLANT 2 FIREGATE SYSTEM, CLOSING THE BLOCK VALVE AND SUCCESSFULLY STOPPED GAS FROM VENTING. AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT, PLANT 2 WAS AVAILABLE FOR SERVICE, BUT THE UNITS WERE OFFLINE AND THERE WAS NO GAS FLOW IN THE PIPE SEGMENT. IN RESPONSE TO THE INCIDENT THE PLANT WAS TAKEN OUT OF SERVICE FOR A THOROUGH EVALUATION.    ON JANUARY 2, 2024, DURING FURTHER INVESTIGATION, IT WAS DETERMINED THE PNEUMATIC PRESSURE SWITCH'S PILOT VALVE DEDICATED TO VALVE 623, O-RING WAS DAMAGED, CAUSING THE BLOWDOWN VALVE 623 TO ACTUATE.     BECAUSE OF THE INDIVIDUAL COMPONENT FAILURE, THERE WAS NO DECREASE IN THE OVERALL PLANT 2 PILOT SYSTEM PRESSURE.  BECAUSE THERE WAS NO LOSS IN PILOT SYSTEM PRESSURE, THE FIREGATE SYSTEM DID NOT ACTIVATE WHICH ALLOWED GAS TO CONTINUOUSLY VENT UNTIL THE STATION MECHANIC MANUALLY INITIATED IT.     SCADA INFORMATION WAS UTILIZED TO CONFIRM THE FAILURE. HOWEVER, THERE ARE NO ALARMS THAT WOULD BE RECEIVED BY CONSUMERS ENERGY GAS CONTROL TO INDICATE THIS SPECIFIC FAILURE HAD OCCURRED.     THE ROOT CAUSE WAS DETERMINED TO BE INADEQUATE MAINTENANCE. OUR INSPECTION PLAN DID NOT INCLUDE DISASSEMBLING THE EQUIPMENT AND REPLACING THE SOFT GOODS (O-RING) EVERY TWO YEARS AS SUGGESTED BY THE MANUFACTURER.     TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE, THE SOFT GOODS OF THE PNEUMATIC SWITCH WERE REPLACED, AND THE SWITCH WAS REASSEMBLED AND TESTED. THREE OTHER PNEUMATIC SWITCHES WITHIN THE PLANT WERE ALSO DISASSEMBLED, INSPECTED AND THE SOFT GOODS REPLACED AND TESTED BEFORE RETURNING THE EQUIPMENT TO NORMAL OPERATIONS ON JANUARY 2, 2024, AT APPROXIMATELY 14:30 HOURS. ADDITIONALLY, MAINTENANCE PLANS WILL BE CREATED TO INSPECT FOR DAMAGE AND REPLACE THE SOFT GOOD AT A SPECIFIED FREQUENCY AS SUGGESTED BY THE MANUFACTURER.

NARRATIVE: AT 6:50AM ON DECEMBER 26TH, AN OFF-SITE EMPLOYEE RECEIVED A CALLOUT FROM THE AUTOMATED SYSTEM FOR AN EQUIPMENT FAILURE ALARM. AT 7:02AM, WHILE THE EMPLOYEE WAS RESPONDING TO THE ALARM, HE RECEIVED A CALL FROM GAS CONTROL. GAS CONTROL INFORMED THE EMPLOYEE OF A PERCENT CHANGE ALARM ON THE #3-LINE SUCTION PRESSURE TRANSMITTER. SEPARATE OPERATOR RESOURCES ARRIVED AT THE STATION AND DISCOVERED THAT THE STATION WAS VENTING GAS THROUGH BOTH SUCTION BLOWDOWN VALVES. THE #3 SUCTION SIDE GATE VALVE WAS OPEN AND ALLOWED GAS TO VENT. AT 7:55AM, THE #3 SUCTION SIDE GATE VALVE WAS CLOSED MANUALLY BY THE EMPLOYEE AND THE STATION COMPLETED THE BLOW DOWN PROCESS. INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT THE ROBERTSHAW RELAY, WHICH KEEPS THE 1-INCH ESD BLOCK VALVE IN THE CLOSED POSITION, WAS FAULTY. THIS ALLOWED THE 1-INCH ESD BLOCK VALVE TO LEAK THROUGH THE VENT AND CAUSED THE VALVE TO PARTIALLY OPEN AND PRESSURE UP THE ESD SUPPLY GAS SYSTEM, THUS TRIGGERING A STATION ESD. ADDITIONALLY, MOISTURE HAD ACCUMULATED IN THE ESD SENSING LINES (NORMALLY DEPRESSURIZED) AND SHAFER TRIGGER VALVE THAT SENDS A CLOSE SIGNAL TO THE ESD SUCTION SIDE GATE VALVE. THE MOISTURE FROZE DURING THE COLD AMBIENT TEMPERATURES EXPERIENCED AT NGPL 305 ON THE MORNING OF 12/26/2023. THE PRESENCE OF ICE IN THE ESD SENSING LINES PREVENTED THE #3 SUCTION SIDE GATE VALVE FROM CLOSING AS DESIGNED. NGPL REPLACED THE ROBERTSHAW RELAY AND USED NITROGEN TO BLOW OUT ANY WATER IN THE ESD SYSTEM. THE ESD SYSTEM WAS PRESSURED UP AND SUCCESSFULLY CHECKED FOR LEAKS.

NARRATIVE: NIPSCO CONTRACTED WITH CAMPOS EPC TO COMPLETE ENGINEERING WORK FOR A FUTURE NIPSCO TRANSMISSION MAINLINE REPLACEMENT. CAMPOS EPC SUBCONTRACTED WITH TERRA TESTING LLC TO COMPLETE SOIL SAMPLING ANALYSIS AS PART OF ITS ENGINEERING WORK.     TERRA TESTING LLC REQUESTED A LOCATE TICKET ON 11/30/23. NIPSCO WAS ONLY ABLE TO LOCATE A PORTION OF THE SCOPE OF THE LOCATE REQUEST DUE TO ACCESS ISSUES ON THE PROPERTY. IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE INDIANA DIG LAW 8-1-26-18(K), NIPSCO PROVIDED NOTIFICATION TO TERRA TESTING VIA MULTIPLE PHONE CALLS AND POSITIVE RESPONSE TO THE EXCAVATOR THAT NIPSCO WAS UNABLE TO COMPLETE THE LOCATE REQUEST DUE TO ACCESS ISSUES ON THE PROPERTY. THESE NOTIFICATIONS WERE MADE ON THE FOLLOWING DATES: 12/4/23, 12/5/23, 12/8/23, 12/14/23 (10:27AM PHONE CALL). DUE TO ACCESS ISSUES WITH THE PROPERTY, TERRA TESTING LLC WAS INFORMED NOT TO EXCAVATE THE AREA AND AGREED TO THIS ON 12/14/23, DURING THE REFERENCED 10:27AM PHONE CALL WITH NIPSCO. ON 12/14/2023 (AT 11:38 AM), A TERRA TESTING LLC CREW STRUCK AN UNMARKED SECTION OF 12" STEEL HIGH PRESSURE TRANSMISSION MAIN WITH VERTICAL SOIL BORING EQUIPMENT. TERRA TESTING LLC DID NOT HAVE AUTHORIZATION FROM NIPSCO TO START EXCAVATION.    UPDATE 02/05/2024  NIPSCO REPAIRED THE PIPE AFTER ISOLATING THE DAMAGED SECTION. ISOLATION OCCURRED WITH A 12"  LINE VALVE UTILIZED TO THE SOUTH OF THE DAMAGE, AND A 12" STOPPLE FITTING INSTALLED TO THE NORTH. ONCE ISOLATED, THE DAMAGED PORTION OF PIPE WAS CUT OUT AND A NEW SECTION WAS WELDED IN PLACE.      IN AN EFFORT TO PREVENT TRANSMISSION DAMAGE FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE, AND WHERE POSSIBLE, WE PLAN TO POST "DO NOT DIG" SIGNS IN THE PROPOSED AREA OF EXCAVATION THAT CANNOT BE LOCATED. THESE SIGNS INCLUDE THE COMPANY NAME AND LOCATOR PHONE NUMBER. THIS IS ABOVE AND BEYOND THE REQUIRED NOTIFICATION TO THE REQUESTOR OF THE LOCATE.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE EVENT WAS CAUSED BY A SMALL LEAK THAT WAS IDENTIFIED BY A TECHNICIAN WHO NOTICED STUNTED GROWTH IN A WHEAT FIELD. THE EVENT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED BY THE CONTROLLER.
NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 07, 2023, A FIELD TECHNICIAN IDENTIFIED AN AREA APPROXIMATELY 75 FEET IN DIAMETER OF STUNTED GROWTH IN A WHEAT FIELD ON THE 20-INCH-DIAMETER M730A SUBLETTE-TO-FOWLER A-LINE AT MP 06.10.  THE FIELD TECHNICIAN COMPLETED A LEAK INVESTIGATION UTILIZING A SOUTHERN CROSS 46 HAWK LASER DIODE METHANE LEAK DETECTOR AND CONFIRMED THERE WAS A LEAK PRESENT.  THE LEAK DETECTOR REGISTERED 380 PPM AT THE LOCATION. REPAIR EFFORTS WERE DELAYED SEVERAL WEEKS DUE TO INCLEMENT WEATHER FROM HEAVY RAIN, SNOWSTORMS, AND BLIZZARD CONDITIONS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA, LIMITING ACCESS TO THE SITE.     INITIAL EXCAVATION BEGAN ON JANUARY 2, 2024. FIVE FEET OF THE M730A LINE WAS EXPOSED WHERE THE LEAK WAS THOUGHT TO HAVE ORIGINATED. AFTER INVESTIGATING THE EXPOSED AREA, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE LEAK WAS NOT IN THIS AREA. FIELD OPERATIONS BEGAN PROBING THE AREA WEST AND EAST OF THE EXPOSED PORTION OF THE LINE TO INVESTIGATE FURTHER. BOTH AREAS THAT WERE PROBED AND BAR-HOLE TESTED WITH THE 46 HAWK LEAK DETECTOR. ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE EXCAVATION THE READING WAS 8% GAS WHILE THE WEST SIDE READING WAS 14% GAS. THE READINGS WERE ABOVE THE PERMISSIBLE 10% LEL LIMIT FOR OPERATION OF THE TRACK HOE IN THIS AREA. A SAFETY STAND DOWN WAS INITIATED AND IT WAS DETERMINED THAT IT WAS NOT SAFE TO PROCEED WITH THE EXISTING CONDITIONS. FIELD OPERATIONS WORKED WITH FACILITY PLANNING TO DEVELOP A PLAN TO LOWER THE OPERATION PRESSURE OF THE M730A TO AS LOW AS 50 PSIG IN ORDER TO SLOW THE LEAK AS WELL AS KEEP FARM TAP CUSTOMERS IN SERVICE.     ON JANUARY 5, 2024, THE DECISION WAS MADE TO HIRE A CONTRACTOR TO ASSIST WITH ACCESSING THE SITE AND REPAIR THE LEAK. THE INITIAL ESTIMATE FOR MATTING THE ROUTE INTO THE LEAK SITE EXCEEDED THE PROPERTY DAMAGE THRESHOLD OF A REPORTABLE INCIDENT, SO THE LEAK WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC.     ON JANUARY 16, 2024, THE LINE PRESSURE HAD REACHED 174 PSIG AND THE SITE COULD BE SAFELY ACCESSED BY EQUIPMENT AND WELDING RIGS. EXCAVATION BEGAN ON THE LEAK SITE. A DRESSER COUPLING WAS FOUND TO BE LEAKING. REPAIRS WERE COMPLETED ON JANUARY 18, 2024, BY WELDING A STYLE 220 PUMPKIN SLEEVE OVER THE LEAKING COUPLING.     BACKFILL AND TOPSOIL RESTORATION WAS COMPLETED ON JANUARY 23, 2024. SUBSEQUENT GAS MONITORING DETECTED NO FURTHER LEAKS, AND A GAS SURVEY EXTENDING 500 FEET UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM OF THE EXCAVATIONS REVEALED NO ADDITIONAL LEAKS.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 27, 2023 AT 11:07 (CST), STATION 527 IN PORT SULPHUR, LA EXPERIENCED AN EMERGENCY SHUT DOWN (ESD) EVENT. THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION WAS COMPLETED BY LOCAL TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WHO WERE ONSITE AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT. UPON STATION WALKTHROUGH, IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE STATION BLOCK VALVE WAS NOT FULLY CLOSED, ALLOWING FOR VENTING OF GAS THROUGH THE BLOW-DOWN VALVE. AT 11:20 (CST), TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CLOSED THE BLOW-DOWN VALVE TO STOP VENTING OF THE GAS. AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT, BOTH STATION 527 AND THE ASSOCIATED SCADA SYSTEM WERE OFFLINE. THE SCADA SYSTEM WAS UNDERGOING MAINTENANCE DUE TO A FLOW-REVERSAL PROJECT OCCURRING AT THE STATION. SINCE THIS EVENT, STATION 527 HAS REMAINED IDLE.     THE INTERNAL INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT WHILE DOWNLOADING THE RTU, THE ESD TEST STAND WAS NOT REMOVED FROM SERVICE. UPON DOWNLOADING THE RTU, THE STATION ESD ACTIVATED. IT WAS DETERMINED THAT ONCE THE ESD ACTIVATED, THE STATION BLOCK VALVE WAS NOT ABLE TO FULLY CLOSE DUE TO A ROCK FILTER BECOMING CLOGGED ON THE PILOT GAS. THE GAS LOSS DUE TO THIS INCIDENT 16,319 MCF.     IN ORDER TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE, WRITTEN PROCEDURES ARE BEING DEVELOPED FOR TAKING THE ESD TEST STAND OUT OF SERVICE AND FOR DOWNLOADING THE RTU SAFETY. ADDITIONALLY, THE VALVE OPERATOR IS BEING ADJUSTED SO THAT THE POWER GAS DOES NOT HAVE REDUNDANT FILTRATION.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 25TH, 2023, AT 10:45 A.M. ET, SALTVILLE GAS STORAGE COMPANY, LLC ("SALTVILLE GAS" OR THE "COMPANY") RECEIVED A CALL FROM A LANDOWNER REPORTING A HISSING SOUND EMANATING FROM HIS PRIVATE WATER WELL. THIS WAS THE EARLIEST INDICATION THAT SALTVILLE GAS HAD OF A GAS RELEASE POTENTIALLY RELATED TO ITS STORAGE FIELD, WHICH IS WHY IT IS IDENTIFIED AS THE INCIDENT DATE/TIME IN A4 AND THE DATE/TIME THAT A "FAILURE" WAS IDENTIFIED IN A13 OF THIS REPORT. THE COMPANY'S INVESTIGATION IS ONGOING, HOWEVER, AND IT HAS NOT YET IDENTIFIED AN EQUIPMENT OR OTHER FAILURE AS A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR TO THIS EVENT.     SALTVILLE GAS TOOK PROMPT ACTION TO INVESTIGATE THE NOVEMBER 25, 2023, NOTIFICATION FROM THE LANDOWNER, AS WELL AS THE LANDOWNER'S NOVEMBER 26, 2023, REPORT OF INTERMITTENT BUBBLING AT COVE CREEK. THE COMPANY DID NOT CONFIRM THAT THE STORAGE FIELD WAS LIKELY THE SOURCE OF THE GAS UNTIL JANUARY 30, 2024, THE DATE AN INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) IN ACCORDANCE WITH PHMSA REPORTING CRITERIA.      ON NOVEMBER 26, 2023, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL BEGAN AN EARLY WITHDRAWAL FROM THE UNDERGROUND STORAGE FIELD TO REDUCE THE PRESSURE IN THE FIELD, A DAY EARLIER THAN WITHDRAWAL HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN PLANNED. IN THE SUCCEEDING WEEKS, SALTVILLE GAS CLOSELY MONITORED FIELD PRESSURE AND ANY EVIDENCE OF GAS AT THE LANDOWNER'S PROPERTY, AND IT GATHERED AND ANALYZED GAS SAMPLES FROM THE LANDOWNER'S WATER WELL TO CONFIRM THE RELEASE SOURCE. ON JANUARY 30TH, 2024, A GAS SAMPLE ANALYSIS REPORT CONFIRMED THAT THE GAS DISCOVERED IN THE LANDOWNER'S WATER WELL LIKELY ORIGINATED FROM THE STORAGE FIELD. AT THAT TIME, SALTVILLE GAS DEEMED THE EVENT SIGNIFICANT WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE PHMSA "INCIDENT" DEFINITION (SEE 49 CFR 191.3(3)) EVEN THOUGH IT DID NOT MEET THE OTHER INCIDENT CRITERIA UNDER 49 CFR 191.3(1) OR (2). THEREFORE, ON JANUARY 30TH, 2024, AT 9:59 A.M. ET, SALTVILLE STORAGE NOTIFIED NRC.    SUPPLEMENTAL 07/16/2025  ON APRIL 22, 2024, SALTVILLE GAS CONDUCTED NITROGEN PRESSURE TESTS ON STORAGE WELLS EH-87, EH-90 AND EH-93. RESULTS FROM THESE TESTS WERE NOT AVAILABLE IN TIME TO BE INCLUDED IN THE INITIAL 30-DAY INCIDENT REPORT SUBMITTED ON FEBRUARY 28TH. BOTH WELLS EH-87 AND EH-90 WERE ABLE TO MAINTAIN PRESSURE FOR THE DURATION OF THE TESTING PERIOD, INDICATING EACH WELLS' PRODUCTION CASING WAS INTACT. HOWEVER, INITIAL TEST PRESSURES AT WELL EH-93 WOULD NOT STABILIZE, AND PRESSURE DROPPED OFF STEADILY, INDICATING THAT EH-93'S PRODUCTION CASING WAS COMMUNICATING WITH SUBSURFACE. (PLEASE NOTE THAT SINCE DECEMBER 10, 2023, EH-93 HAS BEEN ISOLATED BY DOWNHOLE TEMPORARY PLUGS).    ON APRIL 7, 2025, SALTVILLE GAS COMPLETED ALL PLUG AND ABANDONMENT WORK ON WELL EH-93. THE AFFECTED CASING WAS REMOVED AND SENT TO A THIRD-PARTY LABORATORY FOR ANALYSIS. THIRD-PARTY METALLURGICAL TEST RESULTS CONFIRMED THAT THE THROUGH-WALL DEFECT WAS PRIMARILY CAUSED BY MICROBIOLOGICALLY INFLUENCED EXTERNAL CORROSION.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 7, 2023, A TECHNICIAN IDENTIFIED A GRADE 3 LEAK WHILE PERFORMING A LEAK SURVEY ON A CASED CROSSING. NO PPM WAS MEASURED IN THE ATMOSPHERE, BUT THE TECHNICIAN WAS ABLE TO DETECT 580 PPM WHEN THE CAP FOR THE VENT ON THE CASED CROSSING WAS REMOVED AND A PROBE WAS INSERTED IN THE VENT STACK. ON NOVEMBER 8, 2023, AN ETHANE DETECTOR WAS USED TO CONFIRM WHETHER THE PPM WAS NATURAL GAS OR NOT. ETHANE WAS CONFIRMED AND THE PPM HAD INCREASED TO 3,037 INSIDE THE VENT STACK AND 56 PPM AT ATMOSPHERE DUE TO AN INCREASE IN FLOW FROM THE PREVIOUS DAY. A PLAN TO ISOLATE THE SEGMENT WAS IMPLEMENTED WHILE THE ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT STARTED TO DEVELOP A REPAIR PLAN. ON NOVEMBER 9, 2023, THE ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT CONCLUDED THAT REPAIR COSTS WOULD EXCEED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD AND THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED AS SOON AS POSSIBLE AFTER THAT DETERMINATION. THE LEAK WAS STOPPED ON NOVEMBER 22, 2023, AFTER A LINE STOP WAS INSTALLED DOWNSTREAM OF THE LEAK, TO LIMIT THE AMOUNT OF GAS RELEASED TO THE ENVIRONMENT. THE CROSSING WILL BE REPLACED WITH A NEW HDD AND IS SCHEDULED TO BE COMPLETED MID-DECEMBER 2023.    LAB REPORT INDICATES CRACKLIKE INDICATIONS ON THE ORIGINAL 1948 CONSTRUCTION SLEEVES AND WELDS.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 8, 2023 AT 1:09 PM WILLIAMS OPERATIONS WAS NOTIFIED BY AN EMERGENCY RESPONDER OF A POTENTIAL LINESTRIKE ON THE 12" LEWISTON LATERAL LINE LOCATED NEAR PULLMAN, WA. OPERATIONS IMMEDIATELY DISPATCHED TECHNICIANS TO THE SITE AS WELL AS THE TWO VALVES UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM OF THE SITE (VALVES 34-4 AND 34-5). OPERATIONS TECHNICIANS WERE ABLE TO CLOSE BOTH THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES AT 1:50 PM WHILE OTHER TECHNICIANS WERE AT THE RUPTURE LOCATION AND CONFIRMED THAT INCIDENT WAS LEGITIMATE AND CAUSED BY A LANDOWNER USING A DRAIN TILE PLOW ABOVE THE PIPELINE RIGHT-OF-WAY. THE LANDOWNER WAS NOT INJURED, AND THERE WERE NO OTHER INJURIES ASSOCIATED WITH THE INCIDENT. SCADA SYSTEMS, LOCATED IN HOUSTON, TX, WERE ALSO ABLE TO CONFIRM THE PRESENCE OF THE RUPTURE VIA RAPID PRESSURE DROP SEEN ACROSS THE LINE. AS A RESULT OF THE INCIDENT, WILLIAMS LOST SERVICE AT A COLLECTION OF METER STATIONS DOWNSTREAM OF THE RUPTURE, NAMELY PULLMAN, MOSCOW, COLTON, UNIONTOWN, GENESEE, LEWISTON EAST, ALBION, LEWISTON WEST, AND LEWISTON PFI METER STATIONS.    OVER THE COURSE OF THE NEXT HOUR PRESSURE AT THE RUPTURE LOCATION CONTINUALLY RELIEVED THROUGH THE HOLE CREATED BY THE LANDOWNER'S EXCAVATOR. OPERATIONS OBSERVED THAT ALL PRESSURE HAD BEEN RELIEVED AND NOTED A 0 PSI PRESSURE READING AT NEARBY PULLMAN METER STATION AT 2:27 PM. THROUGHOUT THE NEXT HOURS OPERATIONS WORKED TO IDENTIFY EMERGENCY PRE-TESTED STOCK PIPE TO SEND TO THE SITE AS QUICKLY AS POSSIBLE. OPERATIONS AND CONSTRUCTION CREWS WERE QUICKLY MOBILIZED ON-SITE THAT NIGHT AND BEGAN WORKING TO GET THE RUPTURED LINE REPLACED WITH THE NEW PRE-TESTED STOCK PIPE. THE NEW PIPE WAS INSTALLED AND X-RAY EXAMINATION WAS COMPLETED ON THE TIE-IN WELDS AT 2:53 PM ON NOVEMBER 9TH, 2023. THROUGHOUT THE NEXT DAY, OPERATIONS WORKED TO PREPARE THE AFFECTED METER STATION TO BEGIN PROVIDING GAS TO CUSTOMERS AGAIN. AT 7:15 PM ON NOVEMBER 10TH, 2023 THE ENTIRE LEWISTON LATERAL WAS PACKED TO PRESSURE AND DOWNSTREAM CUSTOMERS BEGAN RECEIVING GAS SHORTLY THEREAFTER.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 8, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 12:11 HOURS CONSUMERS ENERGY GAS CONTROL RECEIVED AN ALARM NOTIFICATION WHICH INDICATED A POSSIBLE FIRE AT THE ST. CLAIR COMPRESSOR STATION (PLANT 4 DEHYDRATION SYSTEM BUILDING). THE CONSUMERS ENERGY ST. CLAIR COMPRESSOR STATION IS IN IRA, MICHIGAN (ST. CLAIR COUNTY).    A STATION MECHANIC AT THE SITE WAS NOTIFIED AND CONFIRMED ACTIVATION OF THE PLANT FIRE-GATE SYSTEM OCCURRED DUE TO THE FIRE DETECTION ALARM. FOLLOWING THE EVENT, THE PLANT WAS REMOVED FROM SERVICE AND THE SCENE WAS MADE SAFE. ADDITIONAL PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED TO THE STATION TO DETERMINE THE POTENTIAL CAUSE OF THE ACTIVATION.     DURING THE INCIDENT INVESTIGATION, NO NOTABLE INDICATIONS OF FAILURES WERE PRESENT IN THE FACILITY, BUT UPON ARRIVAL, IT WAS NOTED THAT A GAP IN THE INSULATION ON THE REBOILER EXHAUST STACK WAS CLEARLY VISIBLE AND WAS PRESUMED TO BE THE SOURCE OF A FALSE TRIP INDICATION TO THE FIRE-EYE (IN DIRECT LINE OF SIGHT) DUE TO THE INFRARED IRRADIATION FROM THE PIPE (OPERATING AS NORMAL UP TO 1500 DEGREE F). THE INSULATION ON THE EXHAUST SYSTEM WAS INSPECTED WITH NO NOTABLE ISSUES OBSERVED, REPAIRED, AND RETURNED TO SERVICE ON NOVEMBER 9, 2023.    ON NOVEMBER 10, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 11:17 HOURS, LESS THAN 24 HOURS AFTER THE RETURN TO SERVICE CONSUMERS ENERGY GAS CONTROL RECEIVED AN ALARM NOTIFICATION WHICH INDICATED A POSSIBLE FIRE AT THE ST. CLAIR COMPRESSOR STATION (PLANT 4 DEHYDRATION SYSTEM BUILDING). THE EQUIPMENT WAS TAKEN OFF-LINE, AND ALL INSULATION WAS REMOVED TO PERMIT PHYSICAL INSPECTION. SEVERAL CRACKED WELDS WERE IDENTIFIED ON THE EXHAUST LINE OF THE THERMAL OXIDIZER, SUPER HEATER, AND CONDENSATE COLLECTION SYSTEM OF THE THERMAL OXIDIZER. LOOSE BOLTS WERE FOUND IN THE FLANGED CONNECTIONS OF THE SYSTEM, AND AN EXHAUST PIPE SLIP JOINT CONNECTION TO THE EXHAUST STACK WAS FOUND TO NO LONGER BE INSERTED INTO THE FITTING.    BOTH EVENTS WERE REPORTED TO MPSC AS REQUIRED WHEN AN EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN SYSTEM IS ACTIVATED, AND GAS RELEASED. HOWEVER, A MORE IN-DEPTH VISUAL INSPECTION CONFIRMED ADDITIONAL EQUIPMENT DAMAGE ANTICIPATED TO EXCEED PHMSA REPORTING CRITERIA OF $139,700.     ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATIONS AFTER THE EVENT HAVE IDENTIFIED A CONCERN RELATIVE TO INSUFFICIENT BOLT TORQUE SPECIFICATIONS USED DURING A 2020 REPAIR TO THE SYSTEM, PIPE FABRICATION FAILURE RESULTING IN PRE-STRESS BEING APPLIED TO THE FAILED SYSTEM CONNECTION POINTS, AND POTENTIAL PINNED PIPE IN A HIGH THERMAL GROWTH AREA OF THE SYSTEM EXACERBATING STRESSES IMPARTED TO THOSE SAME CONNECTION POINTS. FURTHER PROBLEM SOLVING IS REQUIRED TO DETERMINE THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE FAILURE. HOWEVER, WITH THE CONCERNS DEFINED, CONSUMERS ENERGY HAS TAKEN ACTION TO REMEDIATE EACH CONCERN THROUGH A REFABRICATION OF THE SUPER HEATER VESSEL FAILED COMPONENTS, PIPE ASSEMBLIES FOUND TO BE CREATING THE PRE-STRESS SCENARIO, SLIP JOINT INSERTION WAS INCREASED TO AVOID RISK OF THE JOINT FAILING, APPLICATION OF TORQUE SPECIFICATIONS FROM THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT MANUFACTURER, AND IMPLEMENTATION OF A PREVENTATIVE MAINTENANCE SCHEDULE FOR RE-VALIDATING TORQUE ON BOLTED CONNECTIONS FOLLOWING RETURN TO SERVICE.    FURTHER ANALYSIS IS REQUIRED TO SUPPORT CONFIRMATION OF THE PIPE FAILURE MODES IDENTIFIED IN THE FIELD AND IS PRESENTLY UNDERWAY. THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT MANUFACTURER HAS BEEN ENGAGED IN THE REVIEW AND REPAIR PLAN AND IS SUPPORTING REVIEW OF THE STRESS ANALYSIS. ENGINEERING SUPPORT HAS BEEN CONTRACTED TO SUPPORT MODEL DEVELOPMENT OF THE SYSTEM AND EXECUTE A PIPE STRESS ANALYSIS. FOLLOWING THE OUTCOME OF THE MODELING AND VALIDATION OF THE FAILURE MODES, ADDITIONAL STEPS MAY BE NECESSARY TO IMPROVE THE INTEGRITY OF THE SYSTEM AND WILL BE IMPLEMENTED ACCORDINGLY.    FURTHER ANALYSIS CONFIRMED THE FAILURE WAS A RESULT OF INSUFFICIENT BOLT TORQUE SPECIFICATIONS PROVIDED BY THE MANUFACTURER DURING A PREVIOUS REPAIR TO THE SYSTEM. THE FAILURE WAS A RESULT OF THE BOLTS YIELDING AND TORQUE WAS LOST THROUGH THE THERMAL CYCLE CAUSING BOLTS TO LOOSEN.

NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 5, 2023 AT 06:58 (EST), STATION 267 IN HOPKINTON, MA EXPERIENCED AN ESD EVENT. THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION WAS RECEIVED VIA SCADA ALARMS. GAS CONTROL PERSONNEL NOTIFIED TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WHO WERE IN ROUTE TO THE STATION FOR DAILY ACTIVITIES. TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED ONSITE AT 07:11 (EST). THE HOPKINTON FIRE DEPARTMENT HAD BEEN NOTIFIED BY A THIRD-PARTY AND ARRIVED ONSITE AT 07:18 (EST).  ONCE TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED ONSITE, THEY OBSERVED THAT STATION 267 WAS VENTING, BUT THE COMPRESSOR STATION WAS NOT FULLY ISOLATED FROM THE MAINLINE. DURING A STATION WALKTHROUGH BY OPERATIONS PERSONNEL, IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE COMPRESSOR STATION SIDE GATE VALVES WERE PARTIALLY OPEN. THUS GAS FROM THE MAINLINE WAS VENTING THROUGH THE STATION. AT 7:38 (EST), TGP OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CLOSED THE COMPRESSOR STATION BLOWOFF VALVES TO STOP VENTING OF THE GAS. AT 3:00 (EST), STATION 267 WAS CONFIRMED TO BE ISOLATED FROM THE MAINLINE. SINCE THIS EVENT, STATION 267 HAS REMAINED ISOLATED.   FOLLOWING THE INTERNAL INVESTIGATION, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT A POWER FAULT IN THE AREA RESULTED IN THE FAILURE OF THE STATION PLC. THE PLC FAILURE ACTIVATED THE BUILDING PROTECTION SYSTEM, TRIGGERING THE ESD. DUE TO THE ESD COMMAND, THE COMPRESSOR STATION VENT VALVES WERE OPENED TO BLOW DOWN THE UNITS. HOWEVER, THE COMPRESSOR SIDE GATE VALVES FAILED TO PROPERLY CLOSE AND ISOLATE THE STATION. THE GAS LOSS DUE TO THIS EVENT WAS CALCULATED TO BE 8664 MCF.  IN ORDER TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE, NEW PLCS HAVE BEEN ORDERED AND WILL BE INSTALLED. ADDITIONALLY, ALL OF THE COMPRESSOR STATION SIDE GATE VALVES HAVE BEEN EVALUATED TO ENSURE CORRECT FUNCTIONALITY.  AS AN ADDED PRECAUTION, OPERATIONS HAS DEVELOPED A PROCEDURE FOR ISOLATING THE STATION FROM THE MAINLINE DURING EXTENDED PERIODS WHEN THE STATION IS NOT IN DEMAND.  THE ACTION ITEMS ABOVE ARE INTENDED TO REDUCE THE POTENTIAL FOR REOCCURRENCE OF THIS EVENT.

NARRATIVE: ON WEDNESDAY, 08/23/2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 3:07 PM EST (15:07), OPERATIONS WAS NOTIFIED BY THE CHIEF INSPECTOR FOR THE TRYON LATERAL ANOMALY DIGS  AND INSPECTIONS THAT THEY HAD EXCAVATED, SAND BLASTED, AND WERE INSPECTING ANOMALY 24520 WHEN THEY STARTED SMELLING GAS. THE CREW STOOD DOWN AND AWAITED OPERATIONS ARRIVAL.  TRYON LATERAL PIPELINE HAD ALREADY BEEN ISOLATED AT STATION 140 AND DRAWN DOWN TO 557 PSI ON 8/22/2023 IN PREPARATION FOR THE ANOMALY DIGS PLANNED  TO BE STARTED ON 8/23/2023.   OPERATIONS ARRIVED AT SITE WITH ADDITIONAL LEAK DETECTION EQUIPMENT AT APPROXIMATELY 3:50 PM ET (15:50) AND BEGAN SURVEYING FOR GAS USING AN INFRARED  OPTICAL GAS DETECTOR WITH A PUMP NEAR THE ANOMALY INSPECTION AREAS, DETECTING A LEAK AT APPROXIMATELY 3:58 PM ET (15:58). THE LEAK WAS VERY SMALL BUT  REGISTERED GAS WITH INFRARED LEAK DETECTOR CUP IMMEDIATELY OVER REGION OF CRACK AREA AND CAUSED READILY VISIBLE BUBBLING OF THE SNOOP  THE OPS TECHS NOTIFIED OPS MANAGER AT 4:10 PM ET (16:10).  IT WAS DECIDED THAT THE ISOLATED PIPELINE PRESSURE WOULD BE FURTHER REDUCED TO LESS THAN 400 PSI WHICH WOULD RESULT IN CALCULATED STRESS LEVEL OF 24.6%  SMYS. AND IN ADDITION TO THE ADDITIONAL REDUCTION IN PRESSURE, TEMPORARY REPAIR CLAMPS WOULD BE INSTALLED, AND ONCE INSTALLED, IT WOULD BE SAFE TO  COMPLETE THE REMAINING ANOMALY DIGS BEFORE COMING BACK TO CUT OUT THE LEAKING FEATURE.   DUE TO THE PROXIMITY TO A NEARBY HOME, IT WAS DECIDED TO INFORM THE RESIDENT OF THE SITUATION AND OFFER FOR THEM TO GO TO A LOCAL HOTEL FOR THE NIGHT. THE RESIDENT ACCEPTED AND VACATED THEIR HOME FOR APPROXIMATELY 24 HOURS UNTIL THE TEMPORARY REPAIR CLAMPS WERE INSTALLED. ONCE IT WAS DECIDED THAT REPAIR CLAMPS WOULD BE UTILIZED AND THAT THE LOCAL RESIDENT WAS LEAVING THEIR HOME, THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED OF THE EVENT.   STATION 140 WAS UTILIZED TO COMPLETE DRAWING DOWN THE TRYON LATERAL PRESSURES TO 387 PSI AT 8:33 PM ET (20:33). THE LATERAL REMAINED ISOLATED  OVERNIGHT.  THE FOLLOWING MORNING, THURSDAY, 8/24/2023, THE TEMPORARY CLAMPS ARRIVED AT STATION 140 AT AROUND 8:30 AM ET AND WERE TRANSPORTED TO THE DIG SITE TO  BE INSTALLED. AT 11:30 AM ET, CLAMP 1 INSTALLATION WAS COMPLETED, LEAK CHECKED, AND NO LEAKS PRESENT AFTER INSTALLATION. AT 1:00 PM ET (13:00), CLAMP 2  INSTALLATION WAS COMPLETED, LEAK CHECKED, AND NO LEAKS PRESENT AFTER INSTALLATION.  DIG #14 (ANOMALY 24520) IS BEING VISITED BY OPERATIONS / CONSTRUCTION DAILY UNTIL THE CUTOUT AND REPLACEMENT TO VERIFY DIG SITE IS SECURE, FENCING IS IN GOOD SHAPE, AND TO PERFORM LEAK SURVEYS TO VERIFY NO LEAKS.    FINAL:  A CUT-OUT AND REPLACEMENT OF THE AFFECTED AREA WAS COMPLETED ON 05/29/2024 FOR A PERMANENT REPAIR.    METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS FOR NEAR IDENTICAL ANOMALIES IN THE AREA WERE RECENTLY CONDUCTED TO SUPPORT SCC DETERMINATION. DESTRUCTIVE TESTING WAS NOT CONDUCTED ON THIS SPECIFIC CUT OUT SO AS NOT TO RUIN THE SAMPLE FOR OTHER LAB ANALYSIS

NARRATIVE: AT 04:14 ON 08/19/23 THE CONTROL CENTER RECEIVED A CRITICAL ALARM INDICATING THE STATION ESD WAS ACTIVATED.  ALL COMPRESSORS WERE SHUTDOWN AND ISOLATION VALVES WERE CLOSED IN THE STATION.  UPON ACTIVATION OF THE ESD THE COMPRESSOR STATION BEGAN AN ENTIRE BLOWDOWN OF NATURAL GAS.  THE ONSITE OPERATOR WENT TO THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING TO DETERMINE IF THERE WAS A FIRE OR ANY GAS DETECTED WITHIN THE BUILDING. ONCE CONFIRMED THERE WAS NO IMMEDIATE DANGER, THE OPERATOR BEGAN CLOSING OFF THE MANUAL BLOCK VALVES TO REDUCE THE VENTING OF GAS EMISSIONS TO THE ATMOSPHERE.  ADDITIONAL OPERATORS WERE CALLED OUT TO ASSIST THE ONSITE OPERATOR AND ARRIVED AT 05:00.  THE OPERATORS REVIEWED ALL ALARMS AND FOUND ESD 9832U WAS ACTIVATED.  DURING THE TROUBLESHOOTING OF THE ESD SWITCH, IT WAS DETERMINED THE ESD RIO FUSE WAS BLOWN WHICH CAUSED THE ESD TO ACTIVATE.  THE FUSE WAS REPLACED AND ALL CIRCUITS WERE VERIFIED AND THE ESD CLEARED.  AT 05:39 THE COMPRESSOR UNITS AND STATION PIPING WERE PRESSURED UP TO NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURE.  THE FIRST COMPRESSOR UNIT WAS STARTED AND PUT BACK INTO SERVICE AT 07:00 AND THE SECOND COMPRESSOR UNIT WAS BACK IN SERVICE AT 07:30.  ALL OTHER COMPRESSOR UNITS ARE PRESSURED UP AND ARE ON STANDBY.  ALL REQUIRED NOTIFICATIONS WERE SUBMITTED AND THE COMPRESSOR STATION WAS RETURNED TO NORMAL OPERATIONS AT 09:45.    FINAL REPORT

NARRATIVE: ON AUGUST 18, 2023, AT 08:00, AN EXPLOSION AND FIRE OCCURRED AT THE CENTERVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION 860 IN THE "A" COMPRESSOR BUILDING WHICH LED TO MAJOR EQUIPMENT DAMAGE WITHIN THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING. THERE WAS A GAS RELEASE AND IGNITION ON UNIT 10A AT TGP 860. UNIT 10A IS A CLARK TLA-8, WITH (4) ENBLOC COMPRESSORS. THE #2 COMPRESSOR HEAD END UNLOADER (HEU) FLANGE CRACKED IN THE FILLET AND RELEASED THE GAS THAT IGNITED CAUSING A SUBSEQUENT FIRE.    AT 08:16, AN OPERATIONS EMPLOYEE PULLED THE MANUAL ESD HANDLE TO ACTIVATE THE STATION ESD SYSTEM.  ANOTHER EMPLOYEE CALLED 911.  ALL EMPLOYEES AND CONTRACTORS WORKING AT THE STATION SAFELY EVACUATED THE FACILITY AND MET AT THE EVACUATION POINT LOCATED AT THE STATION SECURITY GATE TO CONFIRM ALL PERSONNEL WERE ACCOUNTED FOR. IN AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS EVACUATED THE SURROUNDING AREA FOR A FEW HOURS. THERE WERE NO OFF SITE IMPACTS.     THERE WERE NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES. ONE EMPLOYEE WAS TRANSPORTED TO THE HOSPITAL WITH SYMPTOMS THAT WERE NOT DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE INCIDENT. THE INVESTIGATION TEAM DETERMINED THAT THE #2 HEU ASSEMBLY HAD AN INHERENT DESIGN FLAW FROM THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT MANUFACTURER (OEM), WITH A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR OF OVER TORQUEING THE HEU FLANGE DURING REASSEMBLY ON MONDAY, AUGUST 14, 2023 BY THE LOCAL MECHANICAL CREW. THE INCIDENT INVESTIGATION REPORT HAS BEEN PROVIDED TO PHMSA.

NARRATIVE: ON AUGUST 16, 2023, AT 8:07 MT, TALLGRASS OCC RECEIVED A CALL FROM KEITH COUNTY 911 INDICATING A RELEASE OF GAS ON A PIPELINE LOCATED NEAR A MILE MARKER ON HIGHWAY 30. TALLGRASS OCC PERSONNEL LOCATED THE GENERAL AREA OF THE RELEASE AND NOTIFIED TALLGRASS FIELD OPERATIONS PERSONNEL OF THE RELEASE WHO MOBILIZED TO THE SITE. TALLGRASS OCC INITIATED EMERGENCY RESPONSE PROCEDURES AND BEGAN SHUT DOWN OF COMPRESSOR STATIONS TO LIMIT THE RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS. TALLGRASS FIELD OPERATIONS ARRIVED ONSITE AND CONFIRMED LOCATION OF RELEASE AND THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT HAD ALREADY BLOCKED AND ISOLATED HIGHWAY 30. TALLGRASS FIELD OPERATIONS PERSONNEL THEN CLOSED VALVES TO FURTHER ISOLATE THE RELEASE. ONCE THE RELEASE WAS STOPPED, HIGHWAY 30 WAS REOPENED.     THE IMPACTED PIPELINE WAS EXPOSED FOR EVALUATION AND REPAIR. PIPE ON EITHER SIDE OF RELEASE LOCATION ALONG WITH PIECES OF THE PIPE IN THE EXCAVATION WERE COLLECTED AND HAVE BEEN SENT FOR METALLURGICAL EVALUATION. REPLACEMENT PIPE, THAT WAS ALREADY PRESSURE TESTED, WAS WELDED INTO PLACE AND UNDERWENT A UT SURVEY, BLAST AND RECOAT, AND COATING INSPECTION WITH NO HOLIDAYS FOUND. THE PIPELINE WAS PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE ON AUGUST 17, 2023 AT 19:25 MT.    04/17/2024 UPDATE    UPDATED E.11 WITH RESULTS FROM CONTROL ROOM INVESTIGATION  UPDATED EQUIPMENT IN G3.5   UPDATED WORK PERFORMED IN G3.6  INVESTIGATION RESULTS CONCLUDED THAT THE THIRD-PARTY EXCAVATOR FAILED TO USE ONE CALL SYSTEM PRIOR TO EXCAVATION AND STRUCK THE PIPELINE, RESULTING IN A PIPELINE RUPTURE.  THIRD PARTY METALLURGICAL REPORT CONFIRMED THE PIPELINE FAILED DUE TO MECHANICAL DAMAGE AND DID NOT HAVE PREEXISTING DEFECTS AND DID NOT FAIL DUE TO OVERPRESSURE.

NARRATIVE: ON AUGUST 16, 2023, NNG GAS CONTROL RECEIVED ALARMS OF LOW PRESSURE AND ZERO FLOW AT MULTIPLE STATIONS IN THE SEMINOLE SUBSYSTEM, UPSTREAM OF THE KERMIT, TX, COMPRESSOR STATION. THE GAS CONTROLLERS CONTACTED CUSTOMERS IN THE AREA CONFIRMING THE PRESSURE AND FLOW DEVIATIONS. NNG OPERATIONS COMMUNICATIONS CENTER CONTACTED THE FIELD OPERATIONS TEAM ON-CALL TECHNICIAN TO INVESTIGATE THE ALARMS. NNG WAS NOTIFIED BY LOCAL AUTHORITIES OF BLOWING GAS AND FIRE NEAR COYANOSA, TX. THIS CONFIRMED THE INCIDENT WITH NNG GAS CONTROL. NNG OPERATIONS COMMUNICATION CENTER INITIATED AN ALL-CALL TO THE KERMIT, TX, FIELD OPERATIONS TEAM TO RESPOND TO THE EMERGENCY. NNG GAS CONTROL COMPLETED SHUTTING-IN ALL RECEIPT AND DELIVERY POINTS. THE FIELD OPERATIONS TEAM CLOSED BLOCK VALVE 3 WHICH COMPLETED THE ISOLATION OF THE AFFECTED SEGMENT. FIRST RESPONDERS CLOSED TWO ROADS IN THE PROCESS. THE FIRE FROM THE FAILURE WAS REDUCED TO BURNING RESIDUAL GAS WITHIN 75 MINUTES OF THE SEGMENT BEING ISOLATED.    BASED ON LAB RESULTS, THE PIPELINE RUPTURED INITIALLY IN APPROXIMATELY THE 6 O'CLOCK POSITION IN AN AREA, ABOUT A FOOT LONG, OF SEVERE, MOSTLY CIRCULAR, PITTING AND CORROSION. THIS PITTING BEARS ALL THE HALLMARKS OF  MICROBIOLOGICALLY INFLUENCED CORROSION (MIC), A PROCESS WHERE ONE OR SEVERAL STRAINS OF BACTERIA  CORRODE STEEL. THE MATERIAL HAD BEEN CORRODED TO APPROXIMATELY 0.041" WALL THICKNESS AT THE  RUPTURE ORIGIN, APPROXIMATELY 12% OF THE ORIGINAL MATERIAL THICKNESS. FURTHER EXAMINATIONS OF  THE MATERIAL DID NOT LOCATE ANY OTHER FAILURE MECHANISM.

NARRATIVE: ON THE MORNING OF AUGUST 8, 2023, TEXAS EASTERN BEGAN AN ANOMALY DIG TO EXPOSE A GEOMETRY FEATURE (BUCKLE/DENT) AT THE 6 O-CLOCK POSITION.  THE LINE IN THIS AREA WAS VERY DEEP (>11-FEET).  THE UPSTREAM VALVE WAS CLOSED AT 09:39 E.T. TO LOWER THE LINE PRESSURE FOR THE EXCAVATION AND REPAIR.  DOWNSTREAM COMPRESSION WAS USED TO HELP PULL THE PRESSURE DOWN.  WHILE EXCAVATING THE ANOMALY, THE CREW IDENTIFIED INDICATIONS OF A LEAK AT APPROXIMATELY 17:00 ET.  AN OPERATIONS TEAM WAS THEN CALLED IN TO INVESTIGATE THE SUSPECTED LEAK. AT 19:28 ET THE DOWNSTREAM VALVE WAS CLOSED TO FULLY BLOWDOWN THE LINE. AT APPROXIMATELY 20:30 E.T. IT WAS DECIDED THAT REPAIRS COULD LIKELY EXCEED THE PHMSA REPORTING COST THRESHOLD.  AT 21:09 ET THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER WAS NOTIFIED.      THE BLOWDOWN WAS COMPLETED ON AUGUST 9, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 12:30 ET.  THE LINE WAS THEN EXCAVATED AND THE PIPING IN AREA OF THE SUSPECTED LEAK WAS CUT OUT AND REMOVED.  AN AIR PRESSURE TEST WAS THEN PERFORMED ON THE CUT-OUT SEGMENT AND A SMALL THROUGH WALL FEATURE ON THE WELD (12 O-CLOCK) ADJACENT TO THE GEOMETRY FEATURE WAS IDENTIFIED.  THE PIPE WAS THEN TRANSPORTED TO A LAB FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS AS TO THE CAUSE.    04/24/2024 SUPPLEMENTAL  UPDATED PART G - APPARENT CAUSE AND PART J - INTEGRITY INSPECTIONS  THE CAUSE OF THE LEAK WAS TENSILE OVERLOADING OF THE PIPE RESULTING IN A 1.5-INCH DUCTILE FAILURE AT TOE OF THE GIRTH WELD. A BENDING MOMENT EXERTED HIGH TENSILE STRESS NEAR THE 12:00 O'CLOCK LOCATION AND HIGH COMPRESSIVE STRESS THE 6:00 O'CLOCK LOCATION, CAUSING THE LEAK AT THE 12:00 O'CLOCK LOCATION AND A BUCKLE AT THE 6:00 O'CLOCK LOCATION.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 28, 2023 KINDER MORGAN OPERATIONS WAS MADE AWARE OF AUDIBLE GAS LEAK.  A KINDER MORGAN CRUDE AND CONDENSATE (KMCC) CORROSION TECHNICIAN WAS WORKING AT A SHARED SITE WITH INDEX 145 MLV 759 AND HEARD WHAT APPEARED TO SOUND LIKE A GAS LEAK. OPERATIONS WERE DISPATCHED TO VALVE SITE.  OPERATIONS MEASURED THE LEAK AND DETERMINED IT WAS DUE TO A VALVE STEM LEAK. OPERATIONS GATHERED FURTHER INFORMATION AND SECURED THE SITE. PLANS WERE MADE TO BEGIN EXCAVATION ON JULY 31, 2023 TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE. INVESTIGATION DETERMINED A VALVE PACKING REPAIR WOULD NOT BE SUFFICIENT. THE VALVE WOULD NEED TO BE REPLACED DUE AGE AND INABILITY TO REPACK THE VALVE STEM. THE STYLE OF VALVE IS AN OLDER MODEL WALWORTH GATE VALVE. THE ORIGINAL COST ESTIMATE TO REPLACE THE VALVE WAS $120,000. THE PIPELINE INDEX 145 SERVES SEVERAL MAJOR POWER PLANTS. DUE TO LOW SEVERITY OF THE LEAK AND ERCOT GUIDANCE, AN OUTAGE TO REPLACE THE VALVE WAS SCHEDULED FOR SEPTEMBER WHEN DEMAND WOULD NOT BE AS HIGH.    DUE TO UNFORESEEN INSTALLATION CHANGES IN SCOPE OF WORK TO REPLACE THE NEW VALVE, THE COST AS OF SEPTEMBER 15, 2023 WERE FORECASTED TO EXCEED $139,700. THIS INCIDENT AND REPAIR IS NOW DEEMED PHMSA REPORTABLE DUE TO THE REPAIR COST EXCEEDING $139,700.  TOTAL UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS OF 240 MCF.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 27TH KMTP WAS CURRENTLY CONSTRUCTING A TIE IN ONTO THE PERMIAN HIGHWAY PIPELINE. IN ORDER TO TIE-IN THE NEWLY CONSTRUCTED LINE, KMTP ELECTED TO USE A LINE STOP TO KEEP THE GAS FLOWING IN THE MAINLINE WHILE TYING IN THE NEW LINE. WHILE OPERATORS WERE ALREADY ON SITE, THEY DISCOVERED THAT THE GASKET ON THE LINE STOP HAD FAILED AT 7:20 PM. AN EMERGENCY RESPONSE CALL WAS HELD AT 8:00 PM, AND IT WAS DETERMINED THAT KMTP WOULD REPORT THE INCIDENT OUT OF AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION SINCE THE RELEASE VOLUME AND REPAIR COSTS WERE UNKNOWN. IT WAS LATER DETERMINED THAT THE RELEASE VOLUME DID NOT EXCEED THE 3000 MCF THRESHOLD UNTIL 8:37 PM. KMTP ISOLATED AND SHUT DOWN THE LINE AT 9:15 PM AND SUBSEQUENTLY PROCEEDED TO BLOW DOWN 12 MILES OF PIPE. APPLICABLE REPAIRS WERE MADE, AND THE LINE WAS BACK IN SERVICE ON JULY 31ST AT 1:00 PM. KMTP'S INCIDENT INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THAT THE DOUBLE POSITION BYPASS BISEP OPERATION PROCEDURE'S TORQUING SAFETY FACTOR WAS INSUFFICIENT, AND IT LACKED DETAILS REGARDING THE APPLICATION OF MOLY LUBRICANT TO BOLTS AND NUTS PRIOR TO THE TORQUING PROCESS. THESE FINDINGS WERE   VALIDATED WHEN ADDITIONAL PROJECTS WERE CONDUCTED WITH THE REVISED PROCEDURES CHANGES LISTED BELOW AND NO REOCCURRENCES WERE FOUND. KMTP'S INVESTIGATION AND THESE PROCEDURE IMPROVEMENTS LED KMTP TO CONCLUDE THAT THIS INCIDENT WAS CAUSED BY AN INADEQUATE PROCEDURE. TO MINIMIZE THE REOCCURRENCE OF THIS INCIDENT, THE FOLLOWING PROCEDURE IMPROVEMENTS WERE IMPLEMENTED.   -IMPROVED CONTROLS OVER BOLT TORQUING (PARTICULARLY FITTING TO SLAB VALVE) INCLUDING:          O DOCUMENTED PROCEDURE REVIEW          O      RECORDING PUMP PRESSURES & CALCULATING TORQUE VALUES, INCLUDING ON SITE SIGN OFF.          O MOLY LUBRICANT AS REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED UNDER ASME PCC-1-2022          O FULL DOCUMENTATION AND OVERSIGHT  -USE SPIRAL WOUND GASKET WITH INNER RING ON ALL FLANGED CONNECTIONS.  -INCREASED TORQUE TO 50% OF YIELD IN ACCORDANCE WITH GASKET PARAMETERS AND PRESSURE REQUIREMENTS.

NARRATIVE: AT APPROXIMATELY 8:41 A.M. EASTERN DAYLIGHT TIME ON JULY 25, 2023, CGT'S LINE VB RUPTURED (THE INCIDENT). THE INCIDENT OCCURRED NEAR INTERSTATE HIGHWAY 81 (I-81) IN STRASBURG, VA, AND THE RESULTING FIRE OF THE RELEASED GAS CAUSED I-81 TO CLOSE IN BOTH DIRECTIONS FOR ABOUT TWO HOURS. THE INCIDENT RESULTED IN THE EJECTION OF SEVERAL PIECES OF 26-INCH PIPE, WHICH LANDED ON THE PIPELINE RIGHT-OF-WAY. THERE WERE NO REPORTED INJURIES, FATALITIES, OR EVACUATIONS.    THE LINE VB FAILURE WAS CAUSED BY STRESS CORROSION CRACKING (SCC) DETERMINED BY METALLURGICAL TESTING COMPLETED BY AN INDEPENDENT TESTING FACILITY. THE ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS (RCFA) CONCLUDED THE CAUSE OF THE RUPTURE WAS DUE TO NEAR-NEUTRAL PH (NNPH) SCC BECAUSE OF THE LOCAL CONDITIONS SUPPORTED THE INITIATION AND GROWTH. CONDITIONS INCLUDE THE OPERATING STRESS, SUSCEPTIBLE MATERIAL TYPICAL FOR THE PIPE VINTAGE, THE AREA OF COATING DISBONDMENT AND/OR ROCKY OUTCROP SHIELDING THE PIPE FROM CORROSION PROTECTION, EXTERNAL CORROSION SERVING AS A CRACK RAISER AND THE ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS AT THE PIPE SURFACE PROMOTING THE DEVELOPMENT AND GROWTH PF THE NNPH SCC (E.G. WET-DRY CONDITIONS, NEUTRAL PH, BICARBONATE IONS).

SAID MANAGER WAS ASSISTED BY ONE OF HIS AVAILABLE TECHNICIANS TO MAKE NOTIFICATIONS.     21:30-HOURS; KMTP GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE KM CO2 TECHNICIAN STATING THERE MAY BE A LEAK OR RELEASE EVENT AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION ACCORDING TO INFORMATION FROM THE REAGAN COUNTY SHERRIFF'S DEPARTMENT.        21:34-HOURS; A KINDER MORGAN TEXAS PIPELINE (KMTP) OPERATIONS SPECIALIST WAS CONTACTED BY THE CO2 OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN PROVIDING THE POTENTIAL EVENT INFORMATION.        21:39-HOURS; THE KMTP OPERATIONS SPECIALIST CONTACTED GAS CONTROL AND THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR WHILE IN ROUTE TO CONFIRM AWARENESS AND INFORMATION VERIFICATION.        21:50-HOURS; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR NOTIFIED GAS CONTROL OF THE OPERATIONS SPECIALIST DISPATCHED TO THE COMPRESSOR STATION.     22:13 HOURS; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR CONTACTED THE OPERATIONS MANAGER, USING PROCEDURE OM-159, THEY INITIATED USE OF THE EMERGENCY RESPONSE LINE (ERL) NOTIFICATION.    22:15-HOURS; THE OPERATIONS SPECIALISTS ARRIVED ON SITE, CONFIRMED THE VENTING OF NATURAL GAS INVOLVING UNIT #2 WHICH WAS DOWN AND BEGAN ISOLATING THE PIPING OF UNIT #2 TO TERMINATE THE GAS VENTING EVENT.     22:22-HOURS; OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT PROVIDED INFORMATION TO GAS CONTROL FOR THE DISTRIBUTION OF THE ERL CALL NOTIFICATION.       22:22-HOURS; UNIT #2 PIPING WAS ISOLATED, AND THE VENTING OF NATURAL GAS WAS TERMINATED.    22:35-HOURS; THE ERL CALL BEGAN AS SCHEDULED AND THE EVENT WAS DEEMED REPORTABLE.     22:55-HOURS, UNIT #1 WAS TAKEN DOWN FOR OPERATOR CONVENIENCE.        01:25-HOURS; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR CALLED GAS CONTROL DESCRIBING THE UNIT #2 POWER ISSUE.      INVESTIGATION HAD FOUND THE UNIT VALVES FAILED OPEN AS DESIGNED AFTER A 30-AMP FUSE LOCATED DOWNSTREAM OF THE POWER SUPPLY BLEW, THEREFORE NATURAL GAS VENTED THROUGH THE STATION VENT BLOWDOWN SYSTEM.    OPERATIONS SPECIALISTS REPLACED THE BLOWN 30-AMP FUSE, HOWEVER UNIT #2 WOULD NOT BE ABLE FOR RESTART UNTIL 08:00-HOURS, BECAUSE OF THE UNIT SHUTDOWN BY PROGRAMMED LOCK OUT.       07:00-HOURS; A SOLAR FIELD SERVICE REPRESENTATIVE ARRIVED AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION AND BEGAN INVESTIGATING THE BLOWN FUSE ISSUE.    08:00-HOURS; UNIT #2 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE.    10:15-HOURS; UNIT #1 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE.      10:45-HOURS; FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL CONSULTATION WITH THE SOLAR REPRESENTATIVE, UNIT #2 WAS TAKEN DOWN.  WIRING CONNECTIONS AND BATTERIES WERE CHECKED.  SYSTEM AMPS WERE MEASURED.  FUSE CONNECTIONS AND WIRING CONNECTIONS WERE TIGHTENED AS NEEDED.    23:45-HOURS; UNIT #2 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE.  UNIT #2 WAS RUN FOR TWO HOURS WITH ADDITIONAL AMP CHECKS CONDUCTED; NO ISSUES WERE FOUND.     12:45-HOURS; FOR PRECAUTION, UNIT #1 WAS TAKEN DOWN. WIRING CONNECTIONS AND BATTERIES WERE CHECKED.  SYSTEM AMPS WERE MEASURED.  FUSE CONNECTIONS AND WIRING CONNECTIONS WERE TIGHTENED AS NEEDED.    13:45-HOURS; UNIT #1 WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE WITH A FINAL AMP CHECK CONDUCTED, NO ISSUES WERE FOUND.  AT THIS TIME, THE COMPRESSOR STATION HAD RETURNED TO NORMAL OPERATIONAL STATUS.      SUPPLEMENT REPORT CREATED TO UPDATE A-7 TO 14,912 MCF; THE ORIGINAL CALCULATION DID NOT CONSIDER RESTRICTION PLATES IN THE VENT SYSTEM AND VOLUME WAS OVER CALCULATED; D-7F ADJUSTED ACCORDINGLY.  ALSO REVISED THE VALVE CLOSURE TIME WITHIN THIS NARRATIVE TO 22:22-HOURS.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 15, 2023 AT 10:27 A.M. CST, A THIRD PARTY DISCOVERED BUBBLES IN THE GULF OF MEXICO AND CONTACTED WITT O'BRIEN'S RESPONSE, WITT O'BRIEN SUBSEQUENTLY MADE THE NOTIFICATION TO THE NRC AT 11:29 A.M CST. WITT O'BRIEN'S NOTIFIED ENBRIDGE FIELD PERSONNEL OF THE EVENT AND PROVIDED THE COORDINATES OF THE LEAK. TETLP BEGAN INVESTIGATING THE LEAK AND NOTIFYING PRODUCERS TO SHUT IN PRODUCTION ON THE SYSTEM AT APPROXIMATELY 11:00 A.M ON JULY 15, 2023.  TETLP MADE THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION TO THE NRC ON JULY 15, 2023 AT 1:33 P.M.CST UPON CONFIRMED DISCOVERY (BASED ON THE COORDINATES, ASSUMPTION THAT THE LEAK WAS ON TETLP'S LINE 40-B-4 AND ESTIMATED REPAIR COSTS WHICH EXCEEDED THE PHMSA REPORTING THRESHOLD OF AN INCIDENT.    AT APPROXIMATELY 2:37 P.M. CST ON MAY 16 TETLP CONFIRMED THAT THE LEAK WAS ON TETLP'S 24 INCH, LINE 40-B-4.  TETLP IMMEDIATELY MOBILIZED A LIFT BOAT TO START ISOLATION AND BLOWDOWN. LINE 40-B-4 WAS ISOLATED AND DEPRESSURIZED ON JULY 22, 2023. THE LEAK WAS REPAIRED BY INSTALLING A MECHANICAL COUPLING ON JULY 31, 2023. A LEAK TEST WAS SUCCESSFULLY PERFORMED ON THE MECHANICAL COUPLING AND THE LINE RE-PRESSURIZED AND RETURNED BACK TO SERVICE ON JULY 31, 2023.    THE LEAK WAS A PINHOLE LOCATED AT THE 6 O'CLOCK POSITION, THAT WAS CAUSED BY INTERNAL CORROSION.  THE ROOT CAUSE IS BELIEVED TO BE DUE TO INTERNAL CORROSION ON THE LOW POINT OF THE LINE.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 10, 2023, A WELDER WAS WELDING A LINE-STOP FITTING ON NORTHERN NATURAL GAS COMPANY'S WIB14801 LADYSMITH BRANCH LINE AT MILEPOST 1.15 TO ACCOMMODATE A NEW PIPELINE SEGMENT TIE-IN. AT 1100 HOURS, THE WELDER BEGAN WELDING THE "BUTTER TEMPER BEAD PASS" REQUIRED PRIOR TO WELDING THE LINE-STOP FITTING TO THE PIPELINE. SEVEN MINUTES LATER, THE WELDER BURNED THROUGH THE PIPELINE, CREATING A 1/16-INCH HOLE. WHEN THE LEAK STARTED, ALL PARTIES EXITED THE BELL HOLE AND STOPPED ALL IGNITION SOURCES.    THE BEST LOCATION FOR THE LINE STOP FITTING WAS FOUND TO BE 0.150" WALL THICKNESS. THIS IS WHERE THE BURN THROUGH OCCURRED.    THE INCIDENT'S ROOT CAUSE WAS THE WELDER DID NOT FOLLOW THE PROCEDURE FOR WELDING ON THIN-WALL PIPE WHILE IN-SERVICE. THE WELDER WAS OUT OF POSITION AND DID NOT HAVE THE MINIMUM EXPERIENCE NORTHERN REQUIRES TO COMPLETE THIS CHALLENGING WELDING PROCESS.    ON JULY 16, 2023, AT 14:50 THE PROJECT WAS COMPLETED, AND THE LADYSMITH BRANCH LINE WAS RETURNED TO NORMAL SERVICE.

NARRATIVE: AT APPROXIMATELY 1:25 AM TARGA GAS CONTROL WAS NOTIFIED BY A 3RD PARTY OF A FIRE AT TARGA'S BIG LIZARD COMPRESSOR STATION AND THAT 911 HAD BEEN CALLED. THE CONTROLLER CALLED THE FIELD OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR AND NOTIFIED HIM OF THE POTENTIAL FIRE AND DISPATCHED HIM TO THE LOCATION. IN ROUTE TO THE LOCATION OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR CONTACTED ADDITIONAL FIELD OPERATORS TO ASSIST IN CONTACTING GAS WELL PRODUCERS TO CLOSE GAS WELLS SUPPLYING GAS TO BIG LIZARD STATION. AT APPROXIMATELY 2:45 AM OPERATION PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE AND CONFIRMED ONE OF THE ENGINE/COMPRESSORS WAS INVOLVED IN A FIRE. THE OPERATOR BEGAN CLOSING THE MANUAL BLOCK VALVES AT THE PERIMETER OF THE STATION TO ISOLATE THE STATION. ONCE THE STATION WAS ISOLATED, BLOWDOWNS WERE OPEN TO ATMOSPHERE, TAKING INTO CONSIDERATION WIND DIRECTION, TO REMOVE FUEL SOURCES TO FIRE. AT AROUND 6:10 AM THE CITY OF JAL FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS ALLOWED TO ENTER PROPERTY TO EXTINGUISH AN ANCILLARY FIRE AND THE AREA WAS DEEMED SAFE AT 6:20 AM. THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE WAS DUE TO A 6" X 1/2" NIPPLE THAT CRACKED AT THE THREADS CAUSING A GAS LEAK WHICH LED TO AN IGNITION. THE GAS COMPRESSOR IS A RENTAL UNIT OWNED BY KODIAK, WHO INSTALLED THE NIPPLE ON THE PULSATION BOTTLE ON THE DISCHARGE SIDE OF THE COMPRESSOR. THE NIPPLE THAT WAS INSTALLED WAS FOUND TO NOT BE HEAVY WALL AND NORMAL ENGINE VIBRATION CAUSED THE NIPPLE TO CRACK AT THE THREADS WHERE IT WAS SCREWED INTO THE BUSHING.  TO MITIGATE THIS FROM RECURRING KODIAK REPLACED SIMILAR NIPPLES WITH HEX PLUGS AT THE BIG LIZARD STATION BEFORE THE STATION WAS PUT BACK ONLINE. ADDITIONAL MEASURES BEING TAKEN BY KODIAK ON ALL OTHER SIMILAR RENTAL GAS COMPRESSORS IS THAT THEY WILL BE REPLACING ALL SIMILAR NIPPLES WITH EITHER PLUGS, OR 1" X 1/2" SCHEDULE 160 NIPPLES WITH THICKER WALL AND DESIGNED FOR GAS COMPRESSOR USE. TARGA WILL ALSO BE INSPECTING SIMILAR GAS COMPRESSORS ACROSS OUR GATHERING SYSTEM WHETHER THEY ARE TARGA OWNED OR RENTAL UNITS FOR SIMILARLY INSTALLED NIPPLES THAT MAY NEED TO BE REPLACED, AND REPLACE IF DEEMED NECESSARY. NOTE: ALL DAMAGES ASSOCIATED WITH THIS, DUE TO THE NIPPLE FAILURE, WERE ATTRIBUTABLE AND CONTAINED TO THE KODIAK OWNED GAS COMPRESSOR BEING RENTED BY TARGA. NO DAMAGE TO TARGA OWNED ASSETS OCCURRED.

INVEST_OTHER_IND_DETAILS: THE STATION ISOLATION VALVES ARE INTERLOCKED WITH THE STATION BLOWDOWN VALVES. HOWEVER, TWO FAILURES ON THE INTERLOCKING SYSTEM RESULTED IN BOTH THE DISCHARGE HEADER BLOWDOWN VALVE AND THE DISCHARGE HEADER ISOLATION VALVE TO REMAIN OPEN.
NARRATIVE: ON 06/27/2023 AT 12:00 PM, MIDCONTINENT EXPRESS PIPELINE (MEP) HAD A REPORTABLE GAS RELEASE THAT OCCURRED AT THE MEP 2 COMPRESSOR STATION WHEN THE ESD SYSTEM WAS ACTIVATED AND THE DISCHARGE SIDE OF THE ESD SYSTEM DID NOT OPERATE AS DESIGNED. THE ESD WAS ACTIVATED AS A RESULT OF LOSS OF UPS POWER SUPPLY. THE USE OF THE UPS POWER WAS INITIATED BY A TRIPPED ELECTRICAL BREAKER THAT WAS CAUSED BY AN ELECTRICALLY SHORTED AIR COMPRESSOR.      THE ESD BLOCK VALVE THAT IS HELD CLOSED BY AN ENERGIZED SOLENOID, WHEN SUPPLIED BY COMMERCIAL OR UPS POWER. WHEN UPS BATTERIES WERE DRAINED, THE STATION LOST CONTROL POWER TO THE STATION COMPUTERS, PLCS AND UNIT HMIS. THE LOSS OF COMMERCIAL POWER DUE TO TRIPPED BREAKER AND THE LOSS OF UPS POWER ALLOWED THE ESD VALVE TO OPEN CAUSING THE STATION TO BLOWDOWN THROUGH A FULLY PNEUMATIC SYSTEM.    WHEN RESPONSE PERSONNEL ARRIVED THEY FOUND THE STATION DISCHARGE SIDE GATE AND DISCHARGE BLOWDOWN VENT VALVE IN AN OPEN POSITION WHICH LED TO THE 50,086.93 MCF RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS.  THE ESD SYSTEM BY DESIGN  HAS AN INTERLOCK PROCESS SO THAT THE BLOWDOWN VALVES WILL NOT OPEN UNLESS THE ASSOCIATED STATION SIDE GATE VALVES CLOSE. THIS IS ACCOMPLISHED THROUGH A SERIES OF TRIGGER VALVES.  THE SUCTION SIDE OF THE STATION WORKED AS DESIGNED DURING THE ESD.  HOWEVER, THE DISCHARGE SIDE EXPERIENCED FAILURES IN TWO SHAFER TRIGGER VALVES WHICH ALLOWED THE BLOWDOWN VENT VALVE TO OPEN WITH THE DISCHARGE SIDE GATE OPEN.  THE INVESTIGATION FOUND EXCESSIVE CORROSION/RUST WITHIN THE INTERNAL WORKINGS OF THE TWO SHAFER VALVES. THE SHAFER TRIGGER VALVES WERE REPLACED, THE TRIPPED BREAKER WAS RESET, THE SHORTED AIR COMPRESSOR WAS REMOVED FROM SERVICE UNTIL THE MOTOR WAS REPLACED, THE DISCHARGE SIDE GATE AND EDS DISCHARGE BLOWDOWN VALVE WERE RETURN TO THEIR NORMAL STATE AND THE STATION WAS PLACED BACK ONLINE.

IN ORDER TO INSTALL THE SLEEVE, THE EXISTING GIRTH WELD CAP NEEDED TO BE GROUND DOWN TO ACCOMMODATE THE SLEEVE.  THE NON-DESTRUCTIVE EXAMINATION REPORTS DID NOT SHOW ANY INDICATIONS OF CRACKING OR LACK OF FUSION.  AT APPROXIMATELY 9:15 A.M. CT, WHILE GRINDING THE GIRTH WELD CAP, THE WELDER HEARD AN UNUSUAL NOISE AND STOPPED GRINDING.  UPON INSPECTION, THE WELDER IDENTIFIED A SMALL LEAK ON THE WELD AND EXITED THE EXCAVATION AREA.  AT APPROXIMATELY 10:50 A.M. CT, VALVE SECTION 5 WAS ISOLATED AND EMERGENCY OFFICIALS WERE NOTIFIED. THE LINE WAS THEN BLOWN DOWN FROM 721 TO APPROXIMATELY 300 P.S.I.G IN ORDER KEEP METER STATIONS ONLINE. AN ONSITE CREW MONITORED THE SYSTEM CONDITIONS AROUND THE CLOCK.  ON JUNE 29, 2023, THE LINE WAS COMPLETELY BLOWN DOWN AND A CUT OUT REPAIR WAS COMPLETED.  THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE LATER THAT DAY AT APPROXIMATELY 3:15 CT. P.M.  THE LEAK WAS THE RESULT OF GRINDING INTO EXISTING WORM HOLE POROSITY ON A VINTAGE WELD (1943).  THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED AT APPROXIMATELY 12:00 P.M. WHEN IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT REPAIRS WERE LIKELY TO EXCEED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD.     07/24/2023  INCIDENT DATE WAS MISTAKENLY ENTERED AS JUNE 23RD. DATE FIELDS IN PART A WERE UPDATED TO JUNE 26, 2023.

THE EMPLOYEES DISCOVERED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS HAD CAUSED A TREE, LOCATED OUTSIDE THE SITE, TO FALL ACROSS THE SITE FENCE.  A BRANCH OF THE FALLEN TREE STRUCK THE 1.5-INCH VALVE AND PIPE NIPPLE LOCATED ON THE SIDE OF THE SEPARATOR SHEERING THE NIPPLE IN THE THREADS ALLOWING GAS TO UNINTENTIONALLY VENT TO ATMOSPHERE. THE SEPARATOR SERVES THE TEJAS HAWKINS LATERAL LINE, INDEX 520-115P.      BY APPROXIMATELY 05:22-HOURS, THE VALVES ISOLATING THE SEPARATOR WERE CLOSED.  REPAIRS OF THE DAMAGED VALVE AND PIPE NIPPLE WERE COMPLETED ON 06/16/2023 AND THE SEPARATOR WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE AT 09:30-HOURS ON THE SAME DAY.  IN ADDITION, THE DAMAGED FENCE AND NON-GAS STRUCTURES WERE REPAIRED OR CLEARED AWAY AS APPROPRIATE.

OPERATIONS CAME UPON THE BURNING COMPRESSOR, ESDD THE STATION AND CALLED THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT TO THE STATION.    A DECISION WAS MADE TO LET THE FIRE BURN ITSELF OUT. NARRATIVE: ON 6/1/2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 10:30 AM, GULF SOUTH PIPELINE COMPANY LLC'S OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WERE ALERTED BY ENTERPRISE PRODUCTS PERSONNEL THAT ENTERPRISE'S WELL 11, WHICH IS ADJACENT TO GULF SOUTH'S PETAL GAS STORAGE NATURAL GAS CAVERNS PGS 10 AND PGS 12A, WAS EXPERIENCING AN INCREASE IN PRESSURE RESULTING IN A SURFACE LEAK.  GULF SOUTH WAS NEITHER INJECTING NOR WITHDRAWING GAS FROM WELLS PGS 10 OR PGS 12A, AND DECIDED NOT TO DO SO UNTIL THE PRESSURE ON ENTERPRISE'S WELL 11 HAD STABILIZED.     ON 6/2/2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 7:10 AM, GULF SOUTH AND ENTERPRISE DETERMINED THAT THE SURFACE LEAK ON ENTERPRISE'S WELL CONTAINED METHANE AND THAT A POSSIBLE SOURCE OF THE METHANE WAS EITHER CAVERN PGS 10 OR PGS 12A COMMUNICATING WITH ENTERPRISE'S WELL 11 BELOW GROUND, IN THE SALT FORMATION.  BECAUSE OF THIS POSSIBILITY, GULF SOUTH CALLED THE NRC. DURING THE INVESTIGATION OF THIS EVENT, GULF SOUTH IDENTIFIED THAT THE MAOP OF CAVERN PGS 10, WHICH IS 1550 PSIG AT THE SURFACE, INCREASED TO 1564 PSIG WITHOUT INJECTION OF GAS, AROUND THE SAME TIME OF THIS INCIDENT. GULF SOUTH PULLED GAS OUT OF BOTH CAVERNS PGS 10 AND PGS 12A OVER THE NEXT SEVERAL DAYS WHILE STAYING IN CONTACT WITH ENTERPRISE AND ENTERPRISE'S PRESSURE STABILIZED AFTER DOING SO.      PER BOARDWALK'S (GULF SOUTH'S) WELL CONTROL ERP, GULF SOUTH DECLARED A LEVEL 2 WELL CONTROL INCIDENT ON 6/2/2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 7:00 AM, DOWNGRADED THE INCIDENT TO A LEVEL 1 ON 6/3/2023 AT 8:15 AM, AND CLOSED THE INCIDENT ON 6/7/2023 AT 9:15 AM.     ADDITIONALLY, OUT OF AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION, GULF SOUTH HAS PERFORMED A MECHANICAL INTEGRITY TEST ON CAVERN PGS 12A AS WELL AS MULTIPLE PRESSURE, TEMPERATURE, AND DENSITY LOGS ON BOTH CAVERNS.    THE RESPONSE TO QUESTION B10A OF THIS REPORT IS SHOWN AS, "ONE INCH DEPTH OF COVER." THE LIMITATIONS OF THE REPORTING SYSTEM PROHIBIT ENTRY OF AN ACCURATE RESPONSE. THE CAVERN ROOF OF PGS 10 IS 2,163 FEET (25,956 INCHES) AND PGS 12A IS 4,284 FEET (51,408 INCHES) BELOW THE SURFACE.    THE RESPONSE TO QUESTIONS C4 AND C5 REFLECTS THE COMPLETION DATE OF CAVERN PGS 10; IT IS THE EARLIER OF THE TWO CAVERN COMPLETION DATES. THE COMPLETION DATE OF PGS 12A IS 7/22/2008.    THE RESPONSE TO QUESTION D3 REPRESENTS THE PIR OF CAVERN PGS 12A (THE LARGER OF THE TWO MAOPS). ADDITIONALLY, THE MAOP OF PGS 10 IS 543 FEET.    THE RESPONSE TO QUESTION E2 REPRESENTS THE MAOP OF CAVERN PGS 10; ADDITIONALLY, THE MAOP OF CAVERN PGS 12A IS 3181 PSIG. THE RESPONSE TO QUESTION E2B SHOWS THE MAOP ESTABLISHED DATE FOR CAVERN PGS 10. ADDITIONALLY, THE MAOP ESTABLISHED DATE FOR PGS 12A IS 7/22/2013.    THE RESPONSE TO QUESTION E3 SHOWS THAT THE PRESSURE EXCEEDED THE APPLICABLE ALLOWANCE IN §192.201 BECAUSE THE MAOP WAS EXCEEDED AND THE SYSTEMS SELECTIONS ARE LIMITED; HOWEVER, THE MAOPS ARE NOT ESTABLISHED BY §192.201 BUT BY API RP 1170.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE OPERATOR DID NOT FIND THAT AN INVESTIGATION OF THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WAS NECESSARY DUE TO: (PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION FOR WHY THE OPERATOR DID NOT INVESTIGATE)
INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE OPERATOR DEEMED THE INCIDENT INDEPENDENT OF CONTROLLER ACTIONS.
EQ_FAILURE_DETAILS: THERMAL RELIEF VALVE FUNCTIONED AS DESIGNED, HOWEVER THE VENT PIPING INSTALLATION DIRECTED GAS STREAM INTO GRADE SURFACE BLOWING MATERIAL CAUSING STATIC ELECTRICAL CHARGE IGNITING NATURAL GAS.
NARRATIVE: 16:13; THE DEHYDRATION SYSTEM TRIGGERED A FLAMEOUT/PUMP FAILURE ALARM.      A NEIGHBOR NOTIFIED A FORMER EMPLOYEE THAT THERE WAS AN ISSUE AT THE PLANT.  SAID NEIGHBOR COULD SEE SMOKE AT THE BACK OF THE FACILITY AND CALLED 911.    16:28; THE FORMER EMPLOYEE CALLED THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR TO INFORM HIM OF THE NEIGHBOR'S REPORT.    16:29; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR CALLED THE E&C TECHNICIAN TO DISCUSS INFORMATION AND ALARMS.  THE E&C TECHNICIAN WAS DISPATCHED TO THE STATION.      17:06; THE E&C TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ONSITE AND CALLED THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR TO INFORM HIM OF AN ACTIVE FIRE AT THE WEST SIDE DEHYDRATION SKID.  THE E&C TECHNICIAN IMMEDIATELY PERFORMED SITE SAFETY AWARENESS.    17:13; THE E&C TECHNICIAN MANUALLY ACTIVATED THE ESD SYSTEM AND THE FIRE SUBSIDED.    17:16; THE ESD ACTION WAS COMPLETE.  THE E&C TECHNICIAN ALLOWED THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT TO ENTER THE STATION AND STAGE ON THE BACKSIDE OF THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING.    17:17; THE E&C TECHNICIAN NOTICED THERE WAS A SMALL FLICKER OF FLAME COMING FROM THE GLYCOL DRAIN BARREL AND HEARD A NOISE HEARD COMING FROM THE PNEUMATIC DEVICES.     17:22; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR, THE E&C TECHNICIAN, AND THE OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN DETERMINED THE NOISE WAS THE AIR SUPPLY TO THE VAPOR RECOVER UNIT (VRU) CONTROLS.  THE EMPLOYEES ENTERED THE DEHYDRATION AREA WITH HANDHELD FIRE EXTINGUISHERS TO EXTINGUISH ANY FLAME OR FIRE AT THE GLYCOL BARREL.      17:35; THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR REPORTED TO THE OPERATIONS MANAGER THAT SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE HAD OCCURRED TO THE VRU UNIT AND DEHYDRATION SKID.    17:45; THE EMERGENCY RESPONSE LINE CALL, REQUIRED BY PROCEDURE OM 159, WAS CONDUCTED.  THE EVENT WAS DEEMED REPORTABLE TO THE NRC ON THE BASIS OF COSTS.  AT THIS TIME, THE SCENE WAS SECURED AND PRESERVED UNTIL A CORPORATE ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS TEAM WAS DEPLOYED TO THE STATION.      INVESTIGATION CONFIRMED THE FOLLOWING:    NO PERSONNEL WERE ON SITE AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT AND THE DEHYDRATION UNIT WAS NOT IN GAS DRYING SERVICE BUT WAS IN STANDBY SERVICE AS THE STATION WAS INJECTING GAS INTO THE STORAGE FIELD.     WITH THE HIGH AMBIENT TEMPERATURE AND NO WITHDRAWAL FROM STORAGE OCCURRING, THE MAIN BURNER WOULD HAVE LITTLE NEED TO OPERATE.  WITHOUT THE MAIN BURNER OPERATING, ONLY PILOT FUEL GAS WOULD BE USED BY THE SYSTEM.  WITH NO OUTLET FOR FUEL GAS TO BE USED, THE CONTACTOR WOULD BE A SOURCE OF ADDITIONAL GAS PRESSURE TO THE SEPARATOR WHICH IN TURN FEEDS THE FUEL GAS TRAIN TO THE SECONDARY LIQUID KNOCKOUT VESSEL, THE LOCATION OF THE THERMAL RELIEF VALVE.  SAID THERMAL RELIEF VALVE, TYPICALLY SET AT 125 PSIG, ACTIVATED WITH THE VENT PIPING DIRECTING GAS TOWARDS THE GROUND WHICH CONTAINS GRAVEL AND OTHER MATERIAL WHICH MOST LIKELY CAUSED STATIC ELECTRICAL CHARGES CREATING THE IGNITION SOURCE.  OBSERVATION FOUND THE HISTORIC VENT PIPING FOR THE THERMAL RELIEF VALVE WAS TERMINATED APPROXIMATELY ONE (1) FOOT ABOVE GRADE SURFACE WHICH DOES NOT MEET CURRENT KINDER MORGAN DESIGN CRITERIA.      FLAME RETARDANT TARPS ARE USED FOR WINTER PROTECTION OF THE DEHYDRATION EQUIPMENT.  DUE TO WIND, THE TARP BY THE KNOCKOUT VESSEL CAUGHT ON FIRE.  16:17 DATA SCAN SUPPORTS A SMALL INCREASE IN GAS FLOW FROM THE THERMAL RELIEF VALVE AT THE KNOCKOUT VESSEL.  16:17, A DROP IN AIR PRESSURE WITHIN THE AIR SYSTEM INDICATED THE FIRE HAD SPREAD TO THE VRU UNIT.  FIRE SPREAD TO THE TARP BY THE SEPARATOR.  16:30 DATA SCAN IDENTIFIES A LARGER GAS FLOW INCREASE WHICH IS SUSPECTED TO BE THE FIRE DAMAGED 1-INCH RELIEF VALVE ON THE SEPARATOR.    STATION GAS FLOW CONTINUES TO BYPASS THE DEHYDRATION AREA UNTIL ALL REPAIRS AND MODIFICATIONS ARE COMPLETED.  ON OR BEFORE NOVEMBER 1, 2023, A REPLACEMENT DEHYDRATION EQUIPMENT SKID WILL BE INSTALLED, ANY RELATED VENTS WILL BE VERIFIED AS MEETING KINDER MORGAN DESIGN CRITERIA OR MODIFIED AS NEEDED, AND A DESIGN REVIEW WILL BE CONDUCTED OF POSSIBLE INSTALLATION OF PRESSURE LIMITING DEVICE SENSING PRESSURE TO THE DEHYDRATION SKID TO SHUT IN THE CONTACTOR AS NECESSARY.

UPON ARRIVAL ONSITE, M6 MIDSTREAM LLC SYSTEM SUPERVISOR DISPATCHED FIELD OPERATORS TO BLOCK VALVES ON M6 MIDSTREAM LLC ASSETS. AT THE TIME OF ARRIVAL IT WAS UNKNOWN WHO OWNED THE PIPELINE SYSTEM THAT HAD RUPTURED AND CAUGHT FIRE DUE TO NUMEROUS PIPELINE OPERATORS IN THE SAME CORRIDOR. ONCE THE JO-04-01 (JONES LATERAL) PIPELINE SYSTEM WAS ISOLATED AND THE FIRE BEGAN TO DIE DOWN. EMERGENCY RESPONDERS WERE ALREADY ONSITE UPON M6 MIDSTREAM LLC'S ARRIVAL BUT WERE ONLY ON STANDBY AND WERE NOT UTILIZED TO EXTINGUISH THE FIRE. M6 MIDSTREAM LLC DETERMINED THE SAFEST ROUTE WAS TO LET THE PIPELINE FIRE TO BURN ITSELF OUT ONCE GAS WAS NOT PRESENT. THE FIRE EXTINGUISHED ITSELF AT APPROXIMATELY 23:00 ON MAY 24, 2023.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 22, 2023, AT 1409 HOURS, A PIPELINE TECHNICIAN WAS PERFORMING EXCAVATION STANDBY FOR A ROAD CONSTRUCTION PROJECT WHEN HE DISCOVERED DISCOLORED SOIL IN THE AREA OF THE M500A VENTURA TO FARMINGTON A LINE AT MILEPOST 98.47. HE IMMEDIATELY RETRIEVED HIS LEAK DETECTION DEVICE FROM HIS TRUCK AND PERFORMED A BAR HOLE LEAK DETECTION SURVEY. THE INITIAL INDICATIONS WERE IN THE 250 TO 300 PARTS PER MILLION RANGE. THIS IS A CASED CROSSING AND INDICATIONS FROM THE CASING VENT WERE APPROXIMATELY 21% LEL. THE GRADE 2 LEAK SHUT DOWN THE ROAD CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES SO THAT A LEAK INVESTIGATION COULD BEGIN. MNOPS WAS INITIALLY NOTIFIED AT 1546 HOURS, BECAUSE OF THE DECISION TO CLOSE THE ROAD AND SHUT DOWN CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITIES. THE SITE WAS SECURED. ON MAY 22, 2023, AT 18:13, THE DECISION WAS MADE TO REPLACE THE CROSSING, REMOVING THE CASING IN THE PROCESS. AT THIS POINT THE COST ESTIMATE WOULD LIKELY EXCEED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD. THE LEAK WAS BELIEVED TO BE FROM A CRACKED WELD INSIDE OF THE CASING. THE SUSPECTED WELD HAS BEEN SENT TO A LAB FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS.    A 36-INCH-LONG PIPE SAMPLE, WITH AN ACETYLENE GIRTH WELD LOCATED IN THE CENTER, WAS SUBMITTED TO AN INDEPENDENT LAB FOR ANALYSIS. LAB RESULTS WERE RECEIVED ON AUGUST 3, 2023. THE ACETYLENE WELD WAS INSPECTED AND FOUND TO BE SOUND WITH NO CRACKING. THE EXACT LEAK LOCATION WAS NOT FOUND IN THE SAMPLE SUBMITTED TO THE LAB. THE SAMPLE SUBMITTED HAD SIGNIFICANT EXTERNAL CORROSION PITTING. THE EVIDENCE OF EXTERNAL PITTING, AND THE SEVERITY OF THE PITTING IN SOME AREAS OF THE SUBMITTED SAMPLE, LEAD US TO BELIEVE THAT CORROSION PENETRATING THROUGH THE PIPE WALL AND GENERATING A PIN HOLE IN A DISCARDED PIECE OF PIPE CAUSED THE LEAK.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THIS PIPELINE IS NOT MONITORED BY SCADA.
NARRATIVE: A POSSIBLE LEAK/FIRE WAS DISCOVERED ON 5/11/2023 AT 14:03 BY OPERATIONS TECHNICIANS CONDUCTING ANNUAL LINE PATROLS ON LINE O-470. TECHNICIANS WERE CHANGING OUT SIGN MARKERS IN THE O-470 ROW WHEN THEY FELT HEAT COMING FROM UNDERNEATH THE ROCKS THAT COVERED PIPELINE IN THE ROW. TECHNICIANS IMMEDIATELY BACKED AWAY FROM THE LOCATION. TECHNICIANS WENT TO BOTH THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVE LOCATIONS TO BEGIN ISOLATING THE PIPELINE.     OPERATIONS TECHNICIANS NOTIFIED THE OPS MANAGER AT 15:45, WHO THEN NOTIFIED THE OPS DIRECTOR OF THE SITUATION. A CONFERENCE CALL WAS CONDUCTED (BEGINNING AT 16:00) TO DEVELOP A PLAN TO ISOLATE AND REMEDIATE THE SECTION OF PIPE WITH THE LEAK.     THE NRC (#1367017) WAS NOTIFIED AT 16:21 ON 5/11/2023.  A 48HR NRC NOTIFICATION WAS PLACED AT 12:48 ON 5/14/2023.  A FINAL NRC NOTIFICATION WAS PLACED AT 11:29 ON 5/15/2023; THIS UPDATE WAS TO CLARIFY THAT THE PIPELINE INVOLVED IN THIS LEAK INCIDENT WAS A TRANSMISSION PIPELINE.    A PLAN WAS PUT IN PLACE TO LOWER PRESSURE FROM 728PSIG (THE PRESSURE AT TIME OF DISCOVERY) TO 200PSIG, IN AN EFFORT TO KEEP ANY POTENTIAL FLAME BURNING UNTIL THE EXACT LOCATION OF THE LEAK COULD BE DETERMINED. OPS TECHNICIANS BEGAN BLOWING DOWN THE PIPELINE AT 17:34 AND GOT THE LINE PRESSURE DOWN TO 600PSIG BY 18:11 WHILE CONTRACTORS MOBILIZED TO THE SITE. CONTRACTORS UTILIZED A TRACKHOE WITH AN EXTENDED BOOM TO PULL ROCKS AWAY FROM THE LEAK LOCATION...AS ROCKS WERE PULLED AWAY, THE FLAME/FIRE WAS VISIBLE; AS CONTRACTORS WERE WORKING THE PIPELINE WAS BROUGHT DOWN TO 200PSIG. THE FIRE WAS EXTINGUISHED JUST BEFORE 24:00 ON 5/11/2023. AIR MOVERS WERE THEN UTILIZED TO COMPLETE THE BLOW DOWN.    THE CAUSE OF THE LEAK WAS IDENTIFIED AS EXTERNAL CORROSION (PITTING) AND THE FIRE/FLAME WAS LIKELY IGNITED BY A LIGHTNING STRIKE ON 5/11/2023. A 41' SEGMENT OF PIPE WAS SPOOLED IN TO REMEDIATE THE LEAK LOCATION. THE PIPELINE WAS BROUGHT BACK IN TO SERVICE ON 5/15/2023 AT 16:30.    NO BUILDINGS INTENDED FOR HUMAN OCCUPANCY WERE WITHIN 220 YARDS OF THE SEGMENT AND NO DOMESTIC CUSTOMERS WERE IMPACTED BY THE OUTAGE.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 4, 2023 AT 2:30 P.M. CST, A THIRD PARTY DISCOVERED BUBBLES IN THE GULF OF MEXICO AND CONTACTED WITT O'BRIEN'S RESPONSE, WITT O'BRIEN SUBSEQUENTLY MADE THE NOTIFICATION TO THE NRC AT 3:58 PM CST.  PHMSA NOTIFIED ENBRIDGE GAS CONTROL (TETLP) OF THE EVENT AND PROVIDED THE COORDINATES OF THE LEAK.  TETLP BEGAN INVESTIGATING THE LEAK AND NOTIFYING PRODUCERS TO SHUT IN PRODUCTION ON THE SYSTEM AT APPROXIMATELY 6:00 PM ON MAY 4, 2023.   TETLP MADE THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION TO THE NRC ON MAY 5, 2023 AT 6:50 PM CST UPON CONFIRMED DISCOVERY (BASED ON THE COORDINATES, ASSUMPTION THAT THE LEAK WAS ON TETLP'S LINE 40-B-4 AND ESTIMATED REPAIR COSTS WHICH EXCEEDED THE PHMSA REPORTING THRESHOLD OF AN INCIDENT).    TETLP WAS UNABLE TO SECURE A DIVING VESSEL CONTRACTOR UNTIL MAY 8, 2023 DUE TO CONTRACTOR AVAILABILITY IN THE GULF COAST REGION. AT APPROXIMATELY 8:00 AM CST ON MAY 9, 2023 TETLP CONFIRMED THAT THE LEAK WAS ON TETLP'S 24 INCH, LINE 40-B-4. TETLP IMMEDIATELY MOBILIZED A LIFT BOAT TO START ISOLATION AND BLOWDOWN.  LINE 40-B-4 WAS ISOLATED AND DEPRESSURIZED ON MAY 11, 2023. THE LEAK WAS REPAIRED BY INSTALLING A MECHANICAL COUPLING ON MAY 20, 2023.  A LEAK TEST WAS SUCCESSFULLY PERFORMED ON THE MECHANICAL COUPLING AND THE LINE RE-PRESSURIZED AND RETURNED BACK TO SERVICE ON MAY 20, 2023.    THE LEAK WAS 1/2 IN. DIAMETER IN A FIELD JOINT LOCATED AT THE 6 O'CLOCK POSITION, THAT WAS CAUSED BY INTERNAL CORROSION. THE ROOT CAUSE IS BELIEVED TO BE DUE TO THE INTERNAL CORROSION AT THE WELD ON THE LOW POINT OF THE LINE.

NARRATIVE: ON APRIL 28, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 00:39 HRS. CST, LIGHTNING STRUCK THE CORINTH COMPRESSOR STATION WHICH IS LOCATED IN ALCORN COUNTY, MISSISSIPPI. THE LIGHTNING STRIKE CAUSED DAMAGE TO A SMALL CONTROL FUSE ON THE PLC AND THEREFORE CAUSED THE ACTIVATION OF THE ESD SYSTEM AT 00:40 HRS. CST.  TC ENERGY WAS NOTIFIED BY THE PUBLIC OF A POTENTIAL FIRE AFTER THE ACTIVATION OF THE ESD SYSTEM. IT WAS LATER CONFIRMED LIGHTNING IGNITED THE GAS ON HORSEPOWER BLOCK 1 (HPB1) BLOWDOWN SILENCER. IT IS UNCLEAR IF THE ORIGINAL LIGHTNING STRIKE THAT DAMAGED THE FUSE ALSO STARTED THE FIRE OR IF THERE WAS A SECOND LIGHTNING STRIKE AFTER THE ESD WAS INITIATED.  LOCAL EMS RESPONDED TO SITE. NRC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE WITHIN 1-HOUR OF CONFIRMATION OF INCIDENT. THE EVENT WAS REPORTED TO NRC (NRC 1 HR. REPORT #: 1365859 AND NRC 48-HR. UPDATE REPORT#: 1365979) AT 02:18 HRS. CST. OPERATIONS RESPONDED TO THE SCENE AND IDENTIFIED THE FIRE AND GAS RELEASE. THE SOURCE OF THE GAS WAS ISOLATED, AND THE FIRE WAS ALLOWED TO BURN DOWN TO A MINIMUM WHERE THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT EXTINGUISHED THE REMAINING FLAME. OPERATIONS RETURNED THE CORINTH COMPRESSOR STATION TO SERVICE AT 15:45 HRS. CST.

NARRATIVE: ON 4/25/23, MOUNTAINWEST PIPELINE RELEASED 5,640 MSCF OF NATURAL GAS DUE TO AN INCORRECTLY INSTALLED SOLENOID WIRING FOR A SUCTION VALVE ON COMPRESSOR UNIT 2.  THE SOLENOID WAS NOT PROPERLY TESTED AT THE TIME OF INSTALL.      AT APPROXIMATELY 9:50, MOUNTAINWEST PIPELINE GAS CONTROL WAS ALERTED FROM A NEIGHBORING PLANT ABOUT GAS BEING VENTED ON UNIT 2 AT FIDLAR COMPRESSOR STATION. OPERATIONS ARRIVED ON-SITE AT 9:55 AND PERFORMED AN ESD AT 10:47 ON THE STATION AFTER ATTEMPTING TO FIX THE ISSUE WITH THE AUTOMATION'S GROUP.  COMPRESSOR UNIT 2 WAS NOT ACTIVE AT THE TIME AND HAD NOT BEEN ACTIVE FOR 5 YEARS.  WORK WAS BEING PERFORMED ON THE SOFTWARE OF THE SOLENOID TO UPGRADE THE FIRMWARE WHICH RESULTED IN THE SUCTION VALVE OPENING INSTEAD OF CLOSING. THIS RESULTED IN GAS FLOWING THROUGH THE SYSTEM AND OUT THE VENT VALVE.  ATTEMPTS TO CLOSE BOTH THE SUCTION VALVE AND VENT VALVE WERE UNSUCCESSFUL LEADING TO THE ESD.      NOTES/ESTIMATIONS:  A4) TIME OF INCIDENT REPORTING CRITERIA WAS MET WAS ESTIMATED BASED FLOW RATE AND TIME IT WOULD TAKE TO REACH 3 MMSCF.  A7) AMOUNT OF GAS LOSS WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN INLET METERS VS. OUTLET METERS DATA AT THE STATION.

PIPELINE CONTROL IMMEDIATELY CONTACTED THE INDIVIDUAL ON-CALL.  8:45 AM TECHNICIAN ARRIVED AT THE FACILITY.  8:52 AM RELEASE STOPPED  8:55 AM. ONCE CONTAINED, THE TECHNICIAN INSPECTED THE PRESSURE RELIEF VALVE (PRV) AND FOUND A TUBING FITTING CRACK BETWEEN THE PRV PILOT AND THE DOME OF THE PRV. THE PISTOL ON THE PRV WAS DAMAGED DUE TO OPERATING FOR AN EXTENDED PERIOD OF TIME. IT IS CURRENTLY BEING REPAIRED. WILLIAMS IS CONDUCTING AN INTERNAL INVESTIGATION TO IDENTIFY A SOLUTION THAT WILL PREVENT A REPEAT INCIDENT.     UPDATE 8/29/2023   AS OF JUNE OF 2023 PRV SENSING LINES HAVE BEEN REMOTE MOUNTED TO ISOLATE FROM VIBRATION TO REDUCE THE LIKELIHOOD OF SIMILAR EVENTS.
