INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: LEAK TOO SMALL TO DETECT
NARRATIVE: GAS RELEASE CALCS:    WE PRESSURED UP THE PIPELINE FROM 0 PSIG TO 305 PSIG USING THE UPSTREAM SEGMENT 11375 WHICH WAS AT 440 PSIG PRIOR TO EQUALIZATION AND 336 PSIG AFTER EQUALIZATION. 10793 HELD PRESSURE FOR A COUPLE DAYS AND THEN PRESSURE BEGAN DROPPING OVERNIGHT AND WE RECEIVED NOTIFICATION FROM TARGA IN THE MORNING THAT THERE APPEARED TO BE A LEAK IN THE CANAL NEAR THEIR FACILITY. AT THIS TIME THE AFFECTED SEGMENT WAS ISOLATED AT WD79A AND THEN THE SEGMENT WAS DEPRESSURIZED THROUGH THE TARGA FLARE AND THE LEAK LOCATION.    WE USED THE FOLLOWING PARAMETERS FOR THE REPORTED VOLUME:  - A STARTING PRESSURE OF 305 PSIG ON 10793 AND 336 PSIG ON 11375 WHEN PRESSURE STARTED DROPPING  - 11375 WAS ISOLATED FROM 10793 WHEN IT HAD DROPPED DOWN TO 314 PSIG.  - DIFFERENCE IN 336 PSIG AND 314 PSIG IN 11375 (91,091 FT OF 23.04 ID; 186,178 FT OF 22.966 ID) WAS INCLUDED IN THE GAS RELEASE CALC  - THE LEAKING SECTION HAS AN ID OF 21 INCHES ALONG THE LENGTH OF SEGMENTS 1082, 1083, AND MOST OF 10793 (96,693 FT). IT HAS AN ID OF 19 INCHES ALONG THE 20 INCH REPAIR SEGMENT OF 10793 (10,307 FT)  - THE DIFFERENCE IN GAS VOLUME IN THIS PIPELINE VOLUME BETWEEN 314 PSIG AND 0 PSIG WAS INCLUDED IN THE CALC  - RESULTING STANDARD GAS VOLUME OF THE PRESSURE CHANGES IS 5.91 MMSCF  - TARGA REPORTED A FLARE VOLUME OF 3.37 MMSCF  - THE RESULTING ESTIMATED GAS RELEASE IS 2.54 MMSCF    THE VGS 22' PIPELINE HAD A LEAK IN ON 1/15/2021 WHICH WAS REPORTED, MANAGED, AND REPAIRED. THE NATURE OF REPAIR INCLUDED INSTALLATION OF TWO PLIDCO MECHANICAL SPLIT SLEEVE REPAIR CLAMPS, COMPLETED ON 3/20/3021. ADDITIONAL WORK WAS PERFORMED ON THE PLATFORM WD 79A, REPLACING THE TOPSIDE PIPING BETWEEN VGS 24' PIPELINE PSN 11375 AND VGS 22' PSN 10793, WHICH WAS COMPLETED 3/10/2023.     THE SYSTEM WAS BEING PRESSURED BACK UP TO PERFORM A STANDING PRESSURE TEST WHEN A LEAK OCCURRED IN RED PASS CANAL ON 3/23/2023 AT 10:38. THE LEAK WAS REPORTED AS BUBBLING IN RED PASS CANAL. THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED AT WD 79A AND REMAINING GAS WAS EMERGENCY FLARED AT TARGA VENICE.    IT WAS DETERMINED THIS LINE WAS NOT SUITABLE TO BE REPAIRED FOR RETURN TO SERVICE AND THE TEAM MOVED TO EXECUTE A FLUSH AND FILL OF THE SYSTEM, IN ORDER TO BEGIN A POTENTIAL ABANDON-IN-PLACE PROCESS. A DIVE CREW CLOSED THE ISOLATION VALVE TO WD 26 IN PREPARATION FOR THE FLUSH AND FILL ACTIVITIES. ON 4/20/2023, THE FLUSH AND FILL PROCESS WAS COMMENCED WITH PLANS TO PUSH A FOAM PIG WITH 110% OF VOLUME BETWEEN TARGA VENICE AND WD 79A, WITH ALL FLUIDS BEING MOVED ACROSS THE WD 79A PLATFORM TO PSN 11375, NEVER LEAVING THE PIPING SYSTEM. THE FLUSH AND FILL OPERATION WAS COMPLETED ON 4/24/2023 AT 17:55. A SUCCESSFUL STATIC SHEEN TEST WAS PERFORMED.    THE REPORT WAS DELINQUENT DUE TO THE PREPARER HAD BEEN OUT SICK AND DID NOT NOTIFY THE TEAM OF THE DUE DATE OF THE REPORT UPDATE.

EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: THE SENSING LINE FROM THE VALVE BODY TO THE PILOT WAS PLUGGED WITH ICE.
NARRATIVE: A WBI EMPLOYEE ARRIVED AT WORK ON THE MORNING OF MARCH 23 AND NOTICED GAS BLOWING FROM THE RELIEF VALVE.  THE EMPLOYEE CALLED ANOTHER WORKER FOR ASSISTANCE AND CALLED WBI GAS CONTROL TO REPORT THE INCIDENT.  THE TWO EMPLOYEES WERE ABLE TO GET THE VALVE TO RESET AND STOP THE BLOWING GAS.  THE EMPLOYEES FOUND THE SENSING LINE FROM THE VALVE BODY TO THE PILOT WAS PLUGGED WITH MULTIPLE ICE PIECES.  THE REGULATOR IS USED TO CONTROL THE FLOW OF STORAGE GAS FROM THE STORAGE FIELD TO THE OLD DEHYDRATION SYSTEM.  BECAUSE OF THE LOW FIELD WITHDRAWAL RATE, THE OLD DEHYDRATION SYSTEM WAS NOT BEING USED.  THE GAS IN THE SENSING LINE WAS STAGNANT AND AMBIENT TEMPERATURES WERE BELOW FREEZING, CAUSING ICE TO BUILD UP IN THE SENSING LINE.  THE STATION OPERATING PRACTICE GOING FORWARD WILL BE TO ISOLATE THE OLD DEHYDRATION SYSTEM WHEN IT IS NOT REQUIRED FOR LOW FIELD WITHDRAWAL RATES.      SUPPLEMENTAL CHRONOLOGY:  3/22  5:38PM GAS STARTED VENTING.  6:55PM THE REPORTING THRESHOLD OF 3,000 MCF WAS MET.  3/23  5:35AM A WBI EMPLOYEE IDENTIFIED BLOWING GAS WHEN THEY SHOWED UP TO START WORK THAT DAY AND REPORTED IT TO GAS CONTROL.  6:04AM STATION PERSONNEL STOPPED VENTING GAS.  6:05AM THE EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT TEAM WAS NOTIFIED OF THE RELIEF VALVE VENTING SITUATION.  8:27AM THE EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT TEAM DETERMINED THAT THE AMOUNT OF GAS VENTED EXCEEDED 3,000 MCF.  9:03AM THE INITIAL REPORT TO THE NRC WAS PHONED IN.

NARRATIVE: AT APPROXIMATELY 01:15 SANDHILLS PLANT OPERATOR RECEIVED AN ALARM THAT THE WADDELL COMPRESSOR STATION SHUT DOWN DUE TO LOW DISCHARGE PRESSURE. HE NOTIFIED THE OPERATIONS MANAGER THAT THERE WAS A POTENTIAL RUPTURE ON THE WADDELL DISCHARGE 12 INCH PIPELINE DUE TO THE SHARP DECLINE IN PRESSURE. FIELD PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED TO BEGIN CLOSING MANUAL BLOCK VALVES ON THE PIPELINE. PLANT OPERATIONS PERSONNEL CLOSED THE MANUAL BLOCK VALVE AT THE SANDHILLS PLANT INLET (DOWNSTREAM OF THE RUPTURE LOCATION), AT 01:40. FIELD PERSONNEL COMPLETED CLOSING 2 MANUAL BLOCK VALVES AT PONDEROSA COMPRESSOR STATION (DOWNSTREAM OF RUPTURE LOCATION) AND WADDELL COMPRESSOR STATION (UPSTREAM OF RUPTURE LOCATION) AT 02:00.  ONCE ALL THE BLOCK VALVES WERE CLOSED AND PIPELINE ISOLATED FIELD PERSONNEL BEGAN GROUND PATROLS TO LOCATE THE RUPTURE. THE RUPTURE SITE WAS LOCATED AT APPROXIMATELY 03:08 AND VISUAL CONFIRMATION WAS MADE THAT THE LINE WAS TARGA'S PIPELINE. THE RUPTURE AREA WAS MONITORED USING 4 GAS MONITORS AND DETERMINED ALL PRODUCT HAD BEEN EVACUATED FROM LINE AND AREA WAS SAFE AT 03:10. THERE WERE NO FIRES, EXPLOSIONS, ROAD CLOSURES, EVACUATIONS OR LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONSE BECAUSE OF THIS RUPTURE. THE INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO TX RRC AS A SIGNIFICANT EVENT DUE TO THE H2S CONCENTRATION IN THE LINE AND THE LINE RUPTURING. THE CAUSE OF THE RUPTURE WAS DETERMINED TO BE INTERNAL CORROSION. THIS WAS DETERMINED BY VISUAL INSPECTION, PREVIOUS OPERATION HISTORY AND PRIOR METALLURGICAL TESTING ON DIFFERENT SECTIONS OF THIS PIPELINE. TO PREVENT AND MITIGATE RECURRENCE THIS PIPELINE HAS BEEN DISCONNECTED FROM ALL SOURCES OF HYDROCARBONS AND PERMANENTLY ABANDONED.

NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 15, 2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 3:10 PM, ENERGY TRANSFER PERSONNEL IDENTIFIED A LEAK IN THE B3LTE FACILITY (28.069311 -99.923707) ON THE B3LTE SUCTION WEBB A PIPELINE. THIS IS A 12-INCH TYPE C GAS GATHERING LINE IN A RURAL AREA LOCATED IN WEBB COUNTY NEAR LAREDO, TX. OPERATIONS PROCEEDED WITH LINE ISOLATION AND UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES WERE LOCKED OUT AND TAGGED OUT BY 3:20 PM. PIPE REPLACEMENT WAS MADE ON APRIL 26, 2023 AT 5:32 PM. LINE WAS PUT BACK IN SERVICE AS OF 3:30 PM ON APRIL 28, 2023.     INVESTIGATION REVEALED THAT THE APPARENT CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS DUE TO INTERNAL CORROSION (MIC). TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE, ENERGY TRANSFER COMPANY WILL WORK WITH SM ENERGY (PRODUCER) TO REVIEW GAS ANALYSIS AND MAKE ADJUSTMENTS AS NEEDED TO ENSURE BIOCIDE TREATMENT IS ADEQUATE.     AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT, REPORTING CRITERIA WAS NOT MET. AFTER FURTHER CALCULATIONS AND INVESTIGATING, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE PROPERTY DAMAGE AND REPAIR COST WAS GOING TO EXCEED THE $129,300 REPORTING CRITERIA. ON MARCH 20, 2023, THIS INCIDENT WAS TELEPHONICALLY REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) AT 5:22 PM. A COURTESY CALL WAS ALSO MADE TO THE TEXAS RAILROAD COMMISSION AT 5:30 PM.     PHMSA AID / CURTIS HUFF REQUESTED WE UPDATE A4 TO MATCH THE TIME IN A13, BUT WE DECLINED TO MAKE THAT CHANGE BECAUSE IT WOULD BE INACCURATE.  HERE, THE FAILURE WAS IDENTIFIED (A13) FIVE DAYS BEFORE THE INCIDENT BECAME REPORTABLE (A4).  UPON IDENTIFICATION OF THE FAILURE, IT WAS REASONABLE TO BELIEVE IT WAS NOT REPORTABLE AS THE PROPERTY DAMAGE REPORTING THRESHOLD WOULD NOT BE EXCEEDED FOR THIS PINHOLE LEAK.  IT WAS NOT UNTIL 5 DAYS LATER THAT IT WAS DETERMINED THAT LIQUIDS RELEASED WITH THE GAS LEFT COMPANY PROPERTY AND AT THE RESULTING INCREASE IN THE REPAIR COST ESTIMATES THEN WERE DETERMINED TO FAR EXCEED WHAT WAS EXPECTED, CAUSING REPORTABILITY TO BE TRIGGERED AT THAT TIME.  AFTER SUBMITTING THE FINAL REPORT, AID/HUFF AGAIN CAME BACK AND REQUIRED THAT A4'S DATE BE CHANGED, AND HE QUOTED THE INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE FORM FOR TYPE R GATHERING INSTEAD OF TYPE C AS IS PRESENT HERE.  BECAUSE AID WILL NOT ALLOW US TO CLOSE THE REPORT WITHOUT MAKING THE CHANGE, A4 WAS UPDATED AS REQUIRED BY AID TO REFLECT THE SAME TIME AS A13.

CORROSION_TYPE_DETAILS: NEGATIVE RECTIFIER HEADER LEAD CAME LOOSE RESULTING IN LOCALIZED CORROSION BENEATH THE CONNECTION.
NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 9, 2023, TEXAS EASTERN TRANSMISSION, L.P. (TETLP) WAS CONDUCTING AN ANOMALY DIG ON LINE 14 CLIN-KOSC VS-4, M.P.  356.89, WHEN THE CREW IDENTIFIED INDICATIONS OF A LEAK (I.E., BUBBLING).  A PIN HOLE LEAK WAS CONFIRMED WITH A SOAP SOLUTION AT APPROXIMATELY 12:07 CT. AT APPROXIMATELY 14:20 CT IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT REPAIRS WOULD LIKELY EXCEED THE PHMSA DAMAGE/REPAIR THRESHOLD, AND THE EVENT WAS SUBSEQUENTLY CALLED INTO THE NRC AT 14:47 CT.   AT APPROXIMATELY 16:50 CT THE UPSTREAM VALVE WAS CLOSED, AND THE DOWNSTREAM COMPRESSOR STATION (CLINTON) WAS USED TO PULL THE PRESSURE IN THE LINE DOWN. AT 18:10 CT THE DOWNSTREAM VALVE WAS CLOSED, AND THE LINE WAS THEN COMPLETELY BLOWN DOWN TO MAKE THE REPAIR.     THE LEAKING PIPE WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED AND THE LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON MARCH 12, 2023, 17:20 CT.  THE REMOVED SECTION WAS SENT TO A LAB FOR FURTHER METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS.     TETLP HAD ASSESSED THE LINE WITH AN MFL-A TOOL IN EARLY 2021. THE ANOMALY IN QUESTION WAS REPORTED AS HAVING A 66% DEPTH AND WAS ISSUED A DUE DATE OF APRIL 30, 2023.  EXCAVATION BEGAN MARCH 8, 2023, AND DURING THE DIG PROCESS THE ANOMALY WAS FOUND TO BE LEAKING.  FURTHER EXCAVATION VERIFIED THAT A NEGATIVE LEAD TO THE RECTIFIER HAD COME LOOSE RESULTING AN INCREASED CORROSION GROWTH RATE UNDER THE CONNECTION.      06/19/2023 THERE WERE NO CLEAR INDICATIONS OF A PROBLEM IN EITHER THE RECTIFIER READINGS OR THE TEST LEAD READINGS AT THE RECTIFIER, AND THERE WAS NO ADDITIONAL CORROSION DAMAGE IN THE AREA.  THE CORROSION WAS CONCENTRATED UNDER THE LOOSE NEGATIVE GROUND BED LEAD WHERE CURRENT WAS LEAVING THE PIPE AND RETURNING TO THE GROUND BED.

CAUSE: MATERIAL FAILURE OF PIPE OR WELD
NARRATIVE: ON 3/8/2023 WILLIAMS PIPELINE CONTROL WAS NOTIFIED BY A LANDOWNER OF A GAS SMELL NEAR HIS PROPERTY IN THE AREA OF A WILLIAMS PIPELINE. TECHNICIANS RESPONDED TO THE AREA AND BEGAN INVESTIGATING TO DETERMINE THE SOURCE OF THE SMELL. TECHNICIANS UTILIZED AN INFRARED LEAK DETECTION TOOL AND CONFIRMED A LEAK FROM THE PIPELINE. ONCE CONFIRMED THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED USING BLOCK VALVES AND THE GAS WAS EVACUATED FROM THE PIPE. AFTER GAS WAS REMOVED FROM THE SYSTEM AND THE LEAK WAS STOPPED, THE PIPELINE WAS EXCAVATED AND THE SOURCE OF THE LEAK WAS DETERMINED TO BE A CRACK ON MAINLINE B.     IN-FIELD HARDNESS WAS PERFORMED AT THE CRACK LOCATION TO CONFIRM A HARD SPOT DEFECT WAS PRESENT AT THE LOCATION OF THE CRACK. THE HARD SPOT MEASURED 364 BHN X 6.5" LONG X 6" WIDE.     ON OCTOBER 7, 2021 WILLIAMS CONTRACTED ROSEN TO INSPECT MAINLINE B (MLV 180B10 TO MLV 185B5) WITH THEIR HARD SPOT ILI (DMG) TECHNOLOGY TO ASSESS FOR THE HARD SPOT THREAT. THERE WERE A TOTAL OF 6 HARD SPOT ANOMALIES REPORTED BY ROSEN AND 4 OF THOSE 6 HARD SPOT FEATURES WERE EXCAVATED/EVALUATED FOR VERIFICATION. THE HARDNESS VALUES CONFIRMED FROM THESE 4 FEATURES RANGED FROM 192-208 BHN, WHICH WAS SLIGHTLY HARDER THAN BASE HARDNESS (~170-180 BHN). ALL 4 FEATURES WERE RECOATED PER WILLIAMS REPAIR PROCEDURE. THE HARD SPOT DEFECT THAT CRACKED (AND LEAKED) AT MP 1558.66 WAS NOT REPORTED BY ROSEN FROM THEIR 2021 HARD SPOT ILI. RE-ANALYSIS IS ONGOING WITH ROSEN BASED ON THE HARD SPOT DEFECT FOUND. LAB TESTING ON HARD SPOT WITH CRACK NOT YET COMPLETED AS PIPE WAS SENT TO TDW FOR PULL TESTING WITH THEIR HARD SPOT ILI TOOL TO VERIFY CAPABILITIES. WE HAVE NOT CONFIRMED HYDROGEN STRESS CRACKING.

NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 3, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 8:34 P.M. ET THE ACCIDENT STORAGE COMPRESSOR STATION ESD'ED RELEASING MORE THAN 3 MMCF OF NATURAL GAS.  EARLIER THAT DAY THE STATION LOST COMMERCIAL POWER AT APPROXIMATELY 6:15 P.M.  THE ACCIDENT STORAGE COMPRESSOR STATION IS EQUIPPED WITH TWO AXILLARY POWER GENERATORS. WHILE RETURNING TO COMMERCIAL POWER ONE OF THE UNITS FAILED TO SWITCH BACK TO COMMERCIAL POWER ISOLATING THE GAS IN A UNIT DISCHARGE.   AS A RESULT, A 10-INCH RF FLANGE GASKET FAILED ON THE UNIT DISCHARGE PIPING RELEASING GAS WITHIN THE COMPRESSOR BUILDING.  THE ESD WAS INITIATED DUE GAS REACHING 40% LEL WITH GAS LEVEL REACHING A PEAK OF 75% LEL WITHIN THE COMPRESSOR STATION.  THE GASKET WAS REPLACED, AND THE STATION WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE ON MARCH 4, 2023.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: INVESTIGATION IDENTIFIED NO CONTROLLER ISSUES. CONTROLLER ACTIONS WERE CONSISTENT WITH COMPANY PROTOCOLS.
NARRATIVE: ON MARCH 3, 2023 AT APPROXIMATELY 14:52, A NATURAL GAS RELEASE OCCURRED AT THE 20150013 ATHLON DISCHARGE 12-INCH PIG LAUNCHER SITE LOCATED 23 MILES SOUTHEAST OF ODESSA, TX IN MIDLAND COUNTY.    WHILE PREPARING TO LAUNCH A CLEANING PIG, THE OPERATOR MOVED ALL VALVES INVOLVED TO THEIR PROPER POSITIONS AS PER SOP. THE OPERATOR VISUALLY AND AUDIBLY WITNESSED THE PIG TRAP BLOW DOWN. ONCE THE PIG TRAP WAS COMPLETELY BLOWN DOWN THE OPERATOR BEGAN TO OPEN THE PIG TRAP DOOR AS PER SOP. THE OPERATOR WALKED AWAY FROM THE PIG TRAP DOOR TO RETRIEVE THE CLEANING PIG FROM HIS TRUCK AND HEARD A HISSING SOUND COMING FROM THE PIG TRAP. THE HISSING SOUND WAS GROWING LOUDER AND THE OPERATOR REMOVED HIMSELF FROM THE AREA TO BE SAFE. THAT IS WHEN THE GAS PRESSURE CAUSED THE PIG TRAP DOOR TO OPEN CAUSING THE RELEASE OF GAS FROM THE 16-INCH PIG TRAP DOOR.     AT APPROXIMATELY 16:35, IT WAS CALCULATED AND CONFIRMED THAT THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS AT 3.69 MMCF, WHICH EXCEEDED THE 3 MMCF REPORTING CRITERIA AND THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED AT 16:49.    THE BETTIS ACTUATOR AND VERSE VALVE WERE REMOVED AND SENT FOR INSPECTION AND REPAIRS.      A NEW MANUAL GEAR OPERATOR WAS INSTALLED AND AT 14:00 ON 04MAR THE PIPELINE WAS PUT BACK INTO SERVICE.    **JUNE 1, 2023, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE 1-INCH MAIN LINE SUPPLY GAS BALL VALVE WAS LEAKING BY AND LIKELY RE-SUPPLIED THE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY WITH ENOUGH PRESSURE TO ACTUATE THE VALVE OPEN ALLOWING GAS TO RE-ENTER THE PIG LAUNCHING BARREL THUS CAUSING THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS RELEASE.    THE LEAKING 1-INCH BALL VALVE WAS REPLACED WITH A NEW ONE.      THIS WAS AN ISOLATED INCIDENT AND TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE AND THIS SITE, THE ACTUATORS WERE REMOVED AND REPLACED WITH MANUAL GEAR OPERATORS.     THE SITE SPECIFIC PIGGING PROCEDURES HAVE BEEN UPDATED TO INCLUDE AREAS WITH ACTUATORS TO LOCK OUT THE SUPPLY SOURCE BLOWDOWN VALVE IN THE OPEN POSITION IN CASE THERE IS A LEAKING VALVE AND TO PREVENT GAS FROM RE-ENTERING THE PIG LAUNCHER BARREL.

NARRATIVE: IN THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF FEBRUARY 16TH, SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS MOVED THROUGH THE STATE OF ARKANSAS. AT APPROXIMATELY 7:45AM AN OPS TECHNICIAN WITH THE WEST POINT TEAM OVERHEARD ON HIS SCANNER THAT THERE WAS A FIRE ON A PIPELINE ROW NEAR THE BALD KNOW SPORTS COMPLEX, IN BALD KNOB, AR. THE TECHNICIAN IMMEDIATELY RECOGNIZED THAT THIS FIRE WAS LIKELY ON THE JM-10 ROW (IN HCA) AND QUICKLY RESPONDED TO THE LOCATION, ARRIVING AT 8:00AM. WHILE ON THE WAY TO THE SCENE, THE TECHNICIAN CONTACTED HIS OPS MANAGER AND SUPERVISOR, THE PIPELINE SPECIALIST, AND COMPLIANCE SPECIALIST COVERING THIS AREA.     THE FOLLOWING NRC NOTIFICATIONS WERE MADE FOR THIS EVENT:  NRC# 1360128 (INITIAL) AT 8:14 AM ON 3-16-23  NRC# 1360130 (UPDATED LOCATION) AT 8:29 AM 3-16-23  NRC# 1360277 (48HR UPDATE) 3-17-23  NRC# 1360366 (FINAL UPDATE) 3-18-23    WHEN THE TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ON SCENE HE SAW A 3' FLAME COMING FROM JM-10; THE FLAME WAS DUE TO A LIGHTNING STRIKE. THE DECISION WAS MADE TO LOWER PRESSURE ON THE LINE FROM 300 PSI TO 200 PSI AND TO MONITOR THE LEAK AND FLAME WHILE KEEPING THE LINE IN SERVICE UNTIL A STOPPLE BY-PASS COULD BE COMPLETED; THIS WAS DONE TO ENSURE GAS SERVICE WAS NOT INTERRUPTED FOR THE COMMUNITIES OF BALD KNOB AND JUDSONIA. OPS PERSONNEL WERE STATIONED AROUND THE CLOCK AT THE LEAK SITE, AND AT BOTH THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVE SITES, SO THAT THE LINE COULD BE ISOLATED IF CONDITION OF THE LEAK CHANGED FOR THE WORST.     STOPPLE BY-PASS EQUIPMENT ARRIVED ON SITE FRIDAY MORNING AND CREWS WORKED TO INSTALL THE STOPPLE FITTINGS. BY 9PM, ON FRIDAY 2-17-23, GAS WAS ON THE BY-PASS LINE. THE SECTION OF PIPE WITH THE LEAK WAS BLOWN DOWN AND THE FLAME WAS EXTINGUISHED.     ON SATURDAY, 2-18-23, THE ISOLATED SECTION WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED.   -AT 12:39PM, WELDS WERE COMPLETE ON THE NEW PIPE AND X-RAY BEGAN.   -AT 2:03PM, NEW PIPE WAS PURGED AND PACKED. REMOVAL OF STOPPLES BEGAN.  -AT 5:02PM, PRESSURE WAS RETURNED TO NORMAL AT 300 PSI    *REMOVED 'SUSPECTED' LIGHTNING STRIKE LANGUAGE FROM PART H - NARRATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE INCIDENT. THE LEAK WAS CAUSED BY A LIGHTING STRIKE.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE FAILURE WAS A LOCAL AUTOMATED DUMP VALVE THAT DID NOT CLOSE PROPERLY. THERE WAS NO INPUT FROM THE CONTROLLERS TO OPEN OR CLOSE THE VALVE.
EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: ROCK/DEBRIS IN VALVE THAT CAUSED THE VALVE NOT TO CLOSE
NARRATIVE: AT 9:41AM, SUNDAY FEBRUARY 5, 2023, GAS CONTROL NOTICED A DECLINE OF 135 PSIG AT RAGER STORAGE ALONG THE SP-7912 PIPELINE. THE NEXT POLL SHOWED ANOTHER 40 PSIG DECREASE. AT 9:45AM, GAS CONTROL SHUT IN ALL FLOW COMING OUT OF RAGER STORAGE. AT 9:51AM, COMPLIANCE-ON CALL RECEIVED A CALL FROM GAS CONTROL SAYING THAT THEY NOTICED A SUDDEN DROP OF PRESSURE ALONG THE SP-7912 PIPELINE AND THAT A TECHNICIAN WAS EN ROUTE. COMPLIANCE SUPERVISION WAS IMMEDIATELY NOTIFIED AFTERWARD. AT 11:13AM, COMPLIANCE WAS NOTIFIED THAT A 2-INCH DUMP VALVE WAS STUCK OPEN AND WAS VENTING TO ATMOSPHERE. AFTER TALKING WITH OPERATIONS, THE FOLLOWING TIMEFRAME WAS ESTABLISHED. AT APPROXIMATELY 9:30AM, THE DUMP VALVE STARTED VENTING GAS. THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS NOTIFIED AND ARRIVED ON SCENE AT 10:02AM. AT 10:34AM THE FIRE DEPARTMENT NOTED THAT THE VENTING SOUND STOPPED.   OPERATIONS ARRIVED ON SITE AT 11AM. THE DUMP VALVE OPENED INITIALLY BECAUSE THE LIQUID LEVEL CONTROLS REACHED THEIR THRESHOLD, WHICH SENT A SIGNAL TO THE VALVE TO OPEN. IT IS SPECULATED THAT THERE WAS A ROCK OR OTHER DEBRIS CAUGHT IN THE VALVE THAT WAS NOT ALLOWING IT TO CLOSE AFTER REMOVING THE LIQUID. SINCE THE VALVE SHUT BEFORE OPERATIONS ARRIVED ON SITE, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF WHAT EXACTLY OCCURRED, BUT OPERATIONS WAS ABLE TO OPEN AND CLOSE THE VALVE WITHOUT ISSUE AFTER THEY ARRIVED ONSITE.

NARRATIVE: AT 10:09AM MST ON FEBRUARY 5, 2023, A FAULTY HAZARDOUS GAS DETECTOR IN THE CONTROL ROOM OF EL PASO NATURAL GAS (EPNG) EUNICE B COMPRESSOR STATION CAUSED THE COMPRESSOR STATION TO UNDERGO AN EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD). DURING THE SHUTDOWN, SUCTION VALVE VB1 DID NOT CLOSE FULLY CAUSING GAS TO CONTINUE TO VENT. THIS LED TO A RELEASE OF 5,459 MCF GAS RELEASE. AT 11:03AM, AN EPNG EMPLOYEE ARRIVED AT SITE, CLOSED THE VALVE, STOPPING THE GAS RELEASE, AND RETURNED THE STATION TO NORMAL OPERATION. THERE WERE NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES. THE INVESTIGATION INTO THIS VALVE MALFUNCTION DETERMINED THAT THERE WAS FLAWED PROGRAMMING IN THE PLC LOGIC WHICH HAS BEEN RESOLVED.

NAME: ENABLE GAS TRANSMISSION, LLC
STATUS_WHEN_IDENTIFIED: NORMAL OPERATION, INCLUDES PAUSES DURING MAINTENANCE

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE LINE WAS LEAKING; THIS WAS NOT A RUPTURE EVENT.
CAUSE: MATERIAL FAILURE OF PIPE OR WELD
NARRATIVE: LOCAL OPS PERSONNEL RECEIVED A PHONE CALL FROM A HUNTER ON SATURDAY MORNING FEBRUARY 4TH, 2023. THE HUNTER WAS IN A VERY REMOTE LOCATION AND CAME ACROSS THE OM-1 PIPELINE ROW; HE NOTICED VAPOR COMING FROM THE GROUND AND SAW ICE COVERING THE GROUND IN THE PIPELINE ROW. OPS PERSONNEL DROVE OUT TO THE LOCATION TO VERIFY A POTENTIAL LEAK; AT 1030AM THAT MORNING OPS VERIFIED THAT WE HAD A POTENTIAL NATURAL GAS LEAK ON OM-1 AT THIS LOCATION. DUE TO THE HISTORY OF LAND MOVEMENT ON OM-1, OUR PERSONNEL BACKED AWAY FROM THE SITE OF THE LEAK AND NOTIFIED MANAGEMENT AND REGULATORY COMPLIANCE OF THE ISSUE. AN NRC NOTIFICATION (NRC#1359244) WAS MADE AT APPROXIMATELY 1045AM....ALTHOUGH THE LEAK WAS RELATIVELY MINOR, OPS KNEW THAT ANY REPAIR ON THE PIPELINE WOULD EXCEED $129,000 DUE TO THE REMOTE ACCESS AND RUGGED TERRAIN. OPS PERSONNEL MOBILIZED TO THE WINSLOW AND TURKEY FARM VALVE TO ISOLATE THE PIPELINE SEGMENT; THE PIPE WAS BROUGHT DOWN TO ROUGHLY 44# BY THAT AFTERNOON. COMMERCIAL BEGAN NOTIFYING CUSTOMERS OF THE OUTAGE, AS THE REPAIR WAS EXPECTED TO TAKE AN EXTENDED AMOUNT OF TIME. THE LINE WAS COMPLETELY FLAT BY NOON ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 6TH AND THE CONTRACTOR BEGAN EXCAVATING LEAK SITE; EXCAVATION WORK WAS VERY SLOW, AS THE GROUND WAS HARD/FROZEN.       ON 2-7-23, THE PIPE WAS EVALUATED AND IT APPEARS THE LEAK WAS CAUSED BY STRESS IN THE PIPE (LAB WILL CONFIRM CAUSE OF LEAK)  ON 2-10-23, A SECTION OF THE EXCAVATED PIPE WAS SENT OFF TO A METALLURGICAL LAB FOR FURTHER ANALYSIS/RESEARCH OF THE LEAK CAUSE.  ON 2-15-23, 2 40' REPLACEMENT PIPE JOINTS WERE HYDROTESTED IN PREPARATION FOR INSTALLATION.  ON 2-17-23, NEW PIPE REPLACEMENT SECTION TIE-INS COMPLETED (X-RAYS PASSED)  ON 2-21-23, THE LINE WAS PURGED AND RETURNED TO NORMAL OPERATING SERVICE.       SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT DATED 8.30.2023    PART C HAS BEEN UPDATED WITH THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION:  -CHANGED TYPE OF RELEASE FROM 'OTHER' TO 'CRACK'    PART G HAS BEEN UPDATED WITH THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION:  - APPARENT CAUSE WAS CHANGED FROM G8 TO G5  - SUB-CAUSE DETERMINED BY METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS  - SUB-CAUSE IS ENVIRONMENTAL CRACKING-RELATED  - ADDITIONAL FACTORS WERE 'OTHER' (DUCTILE OVERLOAD)  - POST CONSTRUCTION PT WAS 1250 PSIG    PART H HAS BEEN UPDATED WITH THE FINDINGS FROM THE LAB:    EXAMINATION OF THE PIPE SAMPLE REVEALED SEVERAL COLONIES OF CIRCUMFERENTIAL CRACK-LIKE INDICATIONS. THE LEAK FRACTURE SURFACE APPEARED CONSISTENT WITH CIRCUMFERENTIAL STRESS CORROSION CRACKING (CSCC), PROGRESSING APPROX. 0.080 INCH DEEP. THE REMAINING FRACTURE APPEARED CONSISTENT WITH DUCTILE OVERLOAD, INCLUDING NECKING ALONG THE INTERNAL PIPE SURFACE. PIPE MATERIAL WAS CONSISTENT WITH API 5LX (1968) GRADE X60 MATERIAL.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
NARRATIVE: ON FEBRUARY 4, 2023, AN UNINTENTIONAL RELEASE OF 6.7 MILLION CUBIC FEET OF NATURAL GAS OCCURRED WHEN A 2-INCH 2-PIECE, THREADED VALVE PULLED APART, ON THE HIGH-PRESSURE DUMP LINE ON THE WELL HEAD SEPARATOR. A TECHNICIAN WAS NEARBY AT THE TIME OF THE RELEASE. NO INJURIES, NO FATALITIES AND NO DAMAGES WERE REPORTED.      THE ISOLATION PLAN GENERATED FOR THE SECTION REQUIRED LATERAL VALVES TO BE CLOSED AND THE RELEASE WAS ISOLATED WITHIN 78 MINUTES. AFTER THE NATURAL GAS RELEASE, THERE WAS A SMALL RELEASE OF CRUDE OIL THAT IS A BYPRODUCT OF THE STORAGE WELL SYSTEM. A PRIMARY CAUSE OF THE EQUIPMENT FAILURE HAS YET TO BE DETERMINED AS THE INCIDENT IS CURRENTLY UNDER INVESTIGATION.  A SUPPLEMENT REPORT WILL BE FILED UPON THE COMPLETION OF THE INVESTIGATION.       UPDATE 7/3/2023 FAILURE INVESTIGATION FINAL SUMMARY REPORT     THE RESULTS OF THE ANALYSIS INDICATE THAT THE BALL VALVE AND THREADED CONNECTION FAILED DUE TO EXPANSION OF THE FEMALE THREADED SECTION ON THE BALL VALVE, MOST LIKELY AS A RESULT OF WATER FREEZING INSIDE THE VALVE. THE EXPANSION LED TO PARTIAL DISENGAGEMENT OF THE MALE THREADS ON THE THREADED CONNECTION AND THE FEMALE THREADS ON THE VALVE BODY. AN AXIL TENSILE STRESS, LIKELY FROM THE FREEZING WATER ACTING ON THE VALVE AND THREADED CONNECTION RESULTED IN DEFORMATION OF THE OF MALE THREADS FROM THE THREADED CONNECTIONS. WHICH SEPARATED THE VALVE BODY FROM THE MALE THREADED CONNECTION.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE GAS CONTROLLER DEPLOYED A FIELD EMPLOYEE TO THE SITE TO INVESTIGATE ISSUES INDICATED BY THE PUBLIC AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE OFFICIALS.  DATA DID NOT INDICATE ABNORMAL OPERATING CONDITIONS.
NARRATIVE: ON FEBRUARY 1, 2023 A SEVERE COLD WEATHER STORM WITH HEAVY RAIN AND SNOW MIX PASSED THROUGH THE BROWNSVILLE, TN AREA. AT 02:50 CT, A MEMBER OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND LOCAL LANDOWNER HEARD GAS VENTING FROM BROWNSVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION YARD AND MADE A CALL TO 811, WHICH WAS DISPATCHED TO THE CALL TO TC ENERGY GAS CONTROL CENTER. IN RESPONSE, A TECHNICIAN WAS DEPLOYED TO THE COMPRESSOR STATION TO INVESTIGATE THE REPORTED EVENT.     UPON ARRIVAL AT THE SITE AT 04:35 CT, THE TECHNICIAN FOUND THAT THE REGULATOR ON VALVE 501-33-J HAD FROZEN, RESULTING IN AN OPEN VALVE RELEASING GAS TO ATMOSPHERE AND MANUALLY CLOSED THE OPEN VALVE. VALVE 501-33-J IS A BLOWOFF VALVE ASSOCIATED WITH THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN (ESD) SYSTEM.     FOLLOWING THE EVENT, STATION TECHNICIANS INSPECTED THE VALVE, POWER/PILOT GAS LINES, REGULATOR COMPONENTS, LINES, FITTINGS AND MECHANICAL FUNCTIONALITY. IT WAS DETERMINED THAT ICE FORMATION FOUND INSIDE THE REGULATOR BODY AND SPRING COMPONENTS CAUSED THE REGULATOR TO FAIL TO OPERATE AND RESULTED IN A PARTIALLY CLOSED ESD VALVE. THE VALVE REGULATOR IS EQUIPPED WITH SPARE VALVE DISK REVERSAL CAPABILITIES, AND THE SPRING-LOADED DISK WAS REPLACED AND REGULATOR PLACED BACK INTO NORMAL OPERATION ON FEBRUARY 1, 2023.

DOWNSTREAM_OPRTNL_CNTRL_DETAIL: CONTROLLING FLOW WAS NOT NECESSARY AS PLEDCO FITTING HELD LEAK IN PLACE UNTIL IT COULD BE CUT OUT.

CAUSE_DETAILS: DAMAGE BY OPERATOR OR OPERATOR'S CONTRACTOR NOT RELATED TO EXCAVATION AND NOT DUE TO MOTORIZED VEHICLE/EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
OPERATION_TYPE: DAMAGE BY OPERATOR OR OPERATOR'S CONTRACTOR NOT RELATED TO EXCAVATION AND NOT DUE TO MOTORIZED VEHICLE/EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
NARRATIVE: WHEN THE AREA WAS EXCAVATED IN 2022 TO REPAIR THE LEAK, IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE PIPING INSTALLED UNDER THE ROAD DID NOT ALIGN WITH ANOTHER SECTION OF PIPE AND A WRINKLE BEND EXISTED BETWEEN THE TWO SECTIONS. RATHER THAN REWORKING THE AREA SO THAT THE PIPES WOULD MORE CLOSELY ALIGN, FRONTIER BELIEVES THAT THE CONTRACTOR WHO INSTALLED THE PIPE IN 1999 USED EQUIPMENT TO MANIPULATE THE LOCATION OF THE PIPE THROUGH PULLING AND/OR PUSHING TO ALIGN THE PIPES SO THAT THEY COULD BE JOINED VIA WELDING. THE SECTION OF PIPE WITH THE WRINKLE BEND IS BELIEVED TO HAVE BEEN INSTALLED WITH A DIRECTIONAL DRILL AS IT IS UNDER A ROAD SO THE CONTRACTOR DID NOT WANT TO OPEN CUT THE ROAD TO CORRECT THE ISSUE. DURING THE PROCESS TO MANIPULATE THE PIPING, THE WRINKLE BEND WAS CREATED WHICH CRACKED THE COATING AND OVER TIME ALLOWED FOR CORROSION TO CREATE A HOLE INSIDE OF THE WRINKLE BEND. FRONTIER HAD A FAILURE TO PINPOINT AND REPAIR THE LEAK IN 2018 WHEN WE DUG WHERE WE WERE INITIALLY GETTING GAS READINGS. WE DID NOT FIND ANYTHING ON THE TRANSMISSION LINE, THEREFORE WE ASSUME THOSE IN CHARGE AT THE TIME THOUGHT IT HAD TO BE ON OUR PLASTIC DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM LOCATED IN THE SAME VICINITY. THIS SHOULD HAVE BEEN MONITORED AND TRACKED AS A LEAK FROM THIS POINT ON, BUT THAT WAS NOT THE CASE. COMPLIANCE TASKS SUCH AS LEAK SURVEY/VALVE MAINTENANCE FOUND THIS LEAK AGAIN BETWEEN THE YEARS OF 2018-2022, BUT IT WAS STILL ASSUMED TO BE ON THE PLASTIC SYSTEM AND WAS NOT PROPERLY WRITTEN UP BY THOSE IN CHARGE AT THE TIME. HIGH GAS READINGS WERE FOUND DURING 2022 LEAK SURVEY, AND THOSE CURRENTLY IN CHARGE IMMEDIATELY PUT PLANS IN PLACE TO DIG WHERE THE GAS READINGS WERE POINTING US TOWARDS. THE PLEDCO WAS USED IN AUGUST 2022 TO STOP THE LEAK WE FOUND IN THE WRINKLE BEND. THE PLEDCO SHOULDN'T HAVE BEEN USED ACCORDING TO MANUFACTURER'S SPECIFICATIONS, WHICH IS A LESSON LEARNED.  THE PLEDCO FAILED IN JANUARY 2023, AND WE CALLED TDW TO STOPPLE TO REPLACE THE PIPE. THE GAS LOST CALCULATION IN THIS REPORT IS AN ESTIMATE OVER THE 5 YEARS WE ARE AWARE OF THAT THE PIPE WAS LEAKING.

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 16, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 07:00, A FLORIDA GAS TRANSMISSION TECHNICIAN DISCOVERED A COMPRESSOR UNIT VENT VALVE WAS VENTING NATURAL GAS TO ATMOSPHERE AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION 24, NEAR TRENTON, FL. THE TECHNICIAN ISOLATED THE COMPRESSOR AT APPROXIMATELY 0730 HRS. ACCORDING TO COMPANY SCADA, THE VENT VALVE HAD BEGUN VENTING AT APPROXIMATELY 13:00 ON JANUARY 15, 2023, BUT NO OTHER SCADA ALARMS WERE TRIGGERED. THE UNIT WAS SHUT DOWN REMOTELY BY GAS CONTROL AT 11:00 ON JANUARY 15, 2023. THE COMPRESSOR UNIT AUTOMATICALLY VENTS AFTER TWO (2) HOURS OF SHUTDOWN. THE SHUTDOWN AND ALL VALVE POSITIONS INDICATED CLOSURE; THEREFORE, NO ALARMS WERE TRIGGERED.    THE VOLUME RELEASED WAS CALCULATED TO BE APPROXIMATELY 102.6 MMSCF NATURAL GAS. THE DETERMINATION TIME FOR THIS ESTIMATE WAS APPROXIMATELY 14:52 ON JANUARY 16, 2023, WHICH WAS FOLLOWED BY THE NRC NOTIFICATION THAT OCCURRED AT APPROXIMATELY 15:44.    THE CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS LATER CONFIRMED TO BE A SUCTION SIDE ISOLATION VALVE NOT FULLY SEATING AFTER THE COMPRESSOR UNIT WAS SHUTDOWN. SPECIFICALLY, IT APPEARS THAT THE VALVE ACTUATOR MALFUNCTIONED RESULTING IN THE VALVE NOT FULLY SEATING, THEREBY ALLOWING PIPELINE PRESSURE TO BLEED BY INTO THE COMPRESSOR UNIT CAUSING THE NATURAL GAS TO BE VENTED TO ATMOSPHERE. THE SUCTION SIDE VALVE WAS FULLY CLOSED MANUALLY BY THE TECHNICIAN TO STOP FLOW OF GAS THROUGH THE COMPRESSOR. THE LOGIC TO ALLOW MORE TIME FOR VALVE CLOSURE WAS DEVELOPED AND INSTALLED THE DAY OF THE RCFA INVESTIGATION TO PREVENT RECURRENCE.

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 12TH AT 3:25 PM KINDER MORGAN TEXAS PIPELINE (KMTP) RECEIVED A CALL OF A POSSIBLE GAS LEAK FROM A CENTERPOINT ENERGY EMPLOYEE. AT THE TIME IT WAS UNKNOWN WHETHER THIS LEAK WAS FROM THE KMTP LINE OR THE CENTERPOINT ENERGY LINE BECAUSE BOTH LINES RUN IN PARALLEL.   ON JANUARY 13TH AT 9:47AM, IN AN EFFORT TO LOCATE THE POTENTIAL GAS LEAK, KMTP PROCEEDED TO BLOWDOWN THE LINE AND SUBSEQUENTLY EXPOSED THE PIPELINE SEGMENT IN ORDER TO FIND THE LEAK. THE LEAK WAS CONFIRMED TO BE ON THE KMTP LINE ON THE SAME DAY AT 7:22 PM. KMTP BEGAN THE REMEDIATION ACTIVITIES ON JANUARY 14TH, AND THE 7 FT SEGMENT OF PIPE WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED.  THE CUT-OUT SEGMENT OF THE PIPE WAS SENT TO THE METALLURGY LAB FOR FURTHER ASSESSMENT. THE METALLURGICAL REPORT CONCLUDED THAT EXTERNAL CORROSION CAUSED THE LEAK IN THE PIPE. HOWEVER, THE CORROSION MECHANISM COULD NOT BE DETERMINED THROUGH THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. KMTP CONDUCTED AN IN-LINE INSPECTION ON THE PIPELINE PRIOR TO THE INCIDENT BUT DID NOT RECEIVE THE FINAL REPORT UNTIL AFTER THE INCIDENT OCCURRED. THE IN-LINE INSPECTION DID DETECT, AND REPORT, METAL LOSS AT THE FAILURE LOCATION. KMTP PUT THE LINE BACK IN SERVICE ON JANUARY 14TH.

NARRATIVE: ON JANUARY 2, 2023, AT APPROXIMATELY 1206 HOURS, AN FGT TECHNICIAN WAS NOTIFIED BY A DOWNSTREAM CUSTOMER THAT A RELIEF VALVE AT THE CLEARWATER SOUTH REGULATOR STATION WAS CURRENTLY ACTIVATED.  THE CLEARWATER STATION IS SHARED WITH ANOTHER OPERATOR (CLEARWATER GAS COMPANY).  THE FGT TECHNICIAN PROMPTLY RESPONDED TO THE AREA AND ISOLATED THE SOURCE AT APPROXIMATELY 1300 HRS.  LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT AND POLICE RESPONDED TO A 3RD PARTY NOTIFICATION, AND THEY BLOCKED TRAFFIC IN BOTH DIRECTIONS UNTIL THE COMPANY TECHNICIAN ISOLATED THE REGULATOR RUN.  ACCORDING TO SCADA, THE RELIEF VALVE BEGAN VENTING AT APPROXIMATELY 1030 HRS THAT DAY.  THE PRESSURE EXCEEDED THE HI ALARM LIMIT THAT WOULD HAVE INITIATED CLOSER MONITORING BY GAS CONTROL HOWEVER THE PRESSURE DID NOT EXCEED THE HIHI CRITICAL ALARM LIMIT THAT WOULD HAVE GENERATED A CALL OUT AND FIELD INVESTIGATION.         THE VOLUME RELEASED WAS CALCULATED TO BE APPROXIMATELY 3.45 MMSCF NATURAL GAS.  THE DETERMINATION TIME FOR THIS ESTIMATE WAS APPROXIMATELY 1452 HRS.  NRC NOTIFICATION OCCURRED AT APPROXIMATELY 1551 HRS.  THE CLEARWATER SOUTH REGULATOR STATION DOES CONTAIN COVERED SEGMENTS IN THE COMPANY IMP, HOWEVER THE PIPING ASSOCIATED WITH THIS INCIDENT IS NOT A COVERED SEGMENT AS DETERMINED BY METHOD 2 (PIR).    UPON INVESTIGATION THE CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS CONFIRMED AS A DAMAGED DIAPHRAGM IN A MOODY PILOT ON THE REGULATOR WHICH ALLOWED HIGHER PRESSURE FLOW THROUGH THE STATION WHICH ACTIVATED THE RELIEF VALVE.  THE RELIEF CAPACITY WAS VALIDATED DURING THE RELEASE VOLUME CALCULATION.  THE DAMAGED EQUIPMENT WAS IMMEDIATELY REPLACED BY THE TECHNICIAN.     TO PREVENT RECURRENCE THE COMPANY IS REVIEWING THE HIHI CRITICAL ALARM SETTING AT THIS LOCATION IN RELATION TO RELIEF VALVE SETTING AND WILL RESET THE HIHI ALARM AT A LEVEL BELOW THE RELIEF VALVE SETTING.  THIS WILL RESULT IN A CALL OUT TO THE FIELD TO PERFORM INVESTIGATION BEFORE THE RELIEF ACTIVATES OR SHORTEN THE LENGTH OF TIME A RELIEF IS ACTIVATED.

OTHER_COMPRESSOR_DETAILS: FUEL RAIL DEVELOPED A CRACK FROM CONTACT WITH HANDRAIL CAUSING A FUEL LEAK
NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 30, 2022 AT APPROXIMATELY 01:15 LOCAL TIME, A FIRE OCCURRED AT THE DOMINATOR COMPRESSOR STATION ON UNIT #6, 30 MILES WEST OF JAL, NEW MEXICO IN LEA COUNTY.    OPERATIONS RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE 911 DISPATCHER AND THE FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS DISPATCHED TO THE STATION. THE OPERATIONS MECHANIC, WHO IS ALSO A VOLUNTEER FIRE FIGHTER, ARRIVED WITH THE FIRE DEPARTMENT AND ESD'D THE STATION.    A DECISION WAS MADE TO LET THE FIRE BURN ITSELF OUT.    AT 06:00 MOUNTAIN TIME, THE STATION WAS SAFE TO ENTER AND OPERATIONS MADE AN INITIAL ASSESSMENT AND IT WAS DETERMINED THE REPAIRS AND DAMAGES WOULD EXCEED THE $123,900.00 REPORTABLE DOLLAR CRITERIA.    AT APPROXIMATELY 15:30, UNIT #6 WAS ISOLATED AND THE STATION WAS PUT BACK ONLINE BY USING THE OTHER COMPRESSORS.    THE UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS CALCULATED AT 1500 MCF.    THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE IS STILL UNDER INVESTIGATION.    COMPONENTS HAS BEEN SENT TO THE LAB FOR ANALYSIS TO DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE. AND UNIT #6 WILL BE SENT TO THE MANUFACTURER TO BE DISMANTLED AND EXAMINED.    A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED ONCE THE DETERMINATION HAS BEEN MADE.    **3/22/2023 UPDATE**    AFTER THE COMPLETION OF THE LAB ANALYSIS, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE WAS DUE TO A LEAK ON THE COMPRESSOR FUEL RAIL.     THE INVESTIGATION REVEALED THAT THE FUEL RAIL AND HANDRAIL WERE IN CONTACT FOR SOME PERIOD OF TIME BASED ON THE APPEARANCE OF WEAR ON THE HANDRAIL.  THE HANDRAIL EXPERIENCED VIBRATIONS OVER TIME, EVENTUALLY SHEARING THE BOLT PROVIDED AND INCREASING THE CONTACT BETWEEN THE FUEL RAIL AND HANDRAIL, THUS CAUSING THE FUEL RAIL TO LEAK FUEL GAS AND IGNITE FROM A HEAT SOURCE IN CLOSE PROXIMITY.     TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE, OPERATIONS WILL BE VISUALLY INSPECTING, IDENTIFYING, AND CORRECTING UNITS THAT MAY HAVE SIMILAR INSTALLATIONS AND PERFORM PACKAGE ASSESSMENTS DURING ROUTINE PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE.

NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 22, 2022, AT 2106 FIELD OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN WAS NOTIFIED OF A VENTING RELIEF VALVE IN THE OGDEN COMPRESSOR STATION. IT WAS DETERMINED TO BE THE RELIEF VALVE DOWNSTREAM OF VALVE 113 ON THE DES MOINES A-LINE (IAB65001). ACCORDING TO DATA TRENDS THE RELIEF VALVE VENTED 20 TIMES FOR AN AVERAGE OF 40 SECONDS EACH FROM 2100 HOURS TO 2116 HOURS. HIGHEST PRESSURE RECORDED WAS 456 PSIG AT 2109 HOURS. AT 2116 HOURS THE PRESSURE RETURNED TO NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURE OF 355PSIG.     THE INVESTIGATION REVEALED THE CAUSE OF THE OVERPRESSURE WAS DUE TO CONSTRUCTION DEBRIS IN THE WHISPER TRIM CAGE OF THE VALVE 49 REGULATOR. THE DEBRIS CAUSED THE REGULATOR TO NOT CLOSE COMPLETELY. THE TEAM HAD MIDWEST VALVE TO INSPECT THE VALVE AND FOUND THE DEBRIS IN THE REGULATOR CAGE. THE CAGE WAS CLEANED OF ALL DEBRIS. THE STEM AND SEAT WERE VERIFIED TO HAVE NO DAMAGE. NEW SOFT GOODS AND SEALS WERE INSTALLED IN THE REGULATOR. WORK WAS COMPLETED ON JANUARY 5, 2023. THE CONTRACTOR TESTED THE VALVE FOR PROPER OPERATION AND FOUND NO ISSUES WITH THE VALVE OPENING AND CLOSING.    04/05/23 UPDATE - THE DEBRIS FOUND IN THE WHISPER TRIM CAGE WAS MOST LIKELY FROM PIGGING OPERATIONS NOT CONSTRUCTION. ALSO, THE DES MOINES A-LINE (IAB65001) WAS MISTAKENLY LISTED AS A 380 MAOP. IT SHOULD READ 500 MAOP. IN 2016 NORTHERN HAD AN INCIDENT ON THE M640A BUSHTON TO TESCOTT MAINLINE NEAR I-70 IN KANSAS. NORTHERN WAS ISSUED A CORRECTIVE ACTON ORDER. AS A RESULT, NORTHERN VOLUNTARILY LOWERED THE MAOP ON ALL BARE, DRESSER COUPLED MAINLINES. NORTHERN HAS BEEN ABANDONING AND REMOVING THOSE MAINLINES. THE CAO DID NOT INCLUDE BRANCH LINES SO DES MOINES SHOULD NOT HAVE BEEN INCLUDED. THIS BRANCHLINE IS BEING REPLACED WITH A NEW 16-INCH C-LINE (IAB65003). THAT PIPELINE WILL BE IN-SERVICE BY NOVEMBER 1, 2023. AT THAT TIME THE A-LINE WILL BE SHUT OFF AND READIED FOR ABANDONMENT. THAT SHOULD ELIMINATE THE LAST 16-INCH AND ABOVE BARE, DRESSER COUPLED PIPELINE.

EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: UNIT VALVE WAS FROZEN, CAUSING IT TO NOT OPERATE PROPERLY WHEN GIVING COMMANDS.
NARRATIVE: ON DECEMBER 19, 2022 WILLIAMS GAS CONTROL SENT A START COMMAND TO UNIT 1 AT VERNAL COMPRESSOR STATION, LOCATED ON THE NORTHWEST PIPELINE SYSTEM. SHORTLY AFTER THE START COMMAND WAS SENT, GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A VOLTAGE IMBALANCE ALARM. GAS CONTROL FURTHER INVESTIGATED THIS ALARM VIA THE HMI SYSTEM AND NOTICED THAT THE UNIT 1 LOADING VALVE WAS ISSUING A COMMAND DISAGREE ALARM. IT WAS ALSO NOTED AT THIS TIME THAT THE HMI SCHEMATIC SHOWED THE LOADING VALVE WAS OPEN (IT SHOULD'VE BEEN CLOSED). GAS CONTROL CALLED THE LOCAL ON-CALL TECHNICIANS TO HEAD TO THE SITE TO INVESTIGATE. WHEN OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE THEY WERE ABLE TO CONFIRM THAT THE LOADING VALVE WAS IN FACT OPEN AND VENTING GAS TO THE ATMOSPHERE. IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE VALVE WAS FROZEN IN THE OPEN POSITION. THE TECHNICIANS QUICKLY ACTED TO CLOSE THE LOADING VALVE BY WARMING THE VALVE AND MANUALLY MOVING IT TO THE CLOSED POSITION, CAUSING THE VENTING TO STOP. ONCE THE VALVES WERE COMPLETELY THAWED, OPERATIONS AND GAS CONTROL MANUALLY AND REMOTELY OPENED AND CLOSED THE VALVE TO ENSURE THAT PROPER FUNCTION HAD BEEN RESTORED. OVER THE NEXT SEVERAL HOURS, OPERATIONS WORKED WITH TECH SERVICES TO DEVELOP AN ACCURATE ESTIMATE FOR THE QUANTITY OF GAS THAT WAS RELEASED. A FINAL NUMBER OF 3,790 MSCF WAS CALCULATED. SINCE THE INITIAL OCCURRENCE, THE VALVE HAS OPERATED CORRECTLY EACH TIME AN OPEN / CLOSE COMMAND HAS BEEN ISSUED.    ADDITIONAL DETAIL ADDED 02/13/23: THE LOADING VALVE IS A 2' VALVE THAT IS USED TO PURGE AND BRING THE COMPRESSOR UP TO PIPELINE PRESSURE BEFORE THE MAIN UNIT VALVES CAN BE OPENED.  THIS IS A FAIL CLOSED VALVE THAT USES PNEUMATIC POWER TO OPEN.  ICING WAS PRESENT IN THE OPERATOR AND PNEUMATIC VENT, HOLDING THE VALVE IN THE OPEN POSITION.  AFTER HEAT WAS APPLIED TO REMOVE THE ICE, THE VALVE WAS ABLE TO BE MOVED AND CYCLED NORMALLY.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: CONTROLLER ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WERE NOT A CAUSE OF OR A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR TO THE INCIDENT. GAS CONTROL ISOLATED TL-442 11 MINUTES AFTER INITIATION OF THE RUPTURE BY REMOTELY CLOSING VALVE-420JJ.
NARRATIVE: ON 11/28/22 AT APPROX 23:22, TL-442 RUPTURED AT STA 5+00 UNDER AN ACCESS ROAD ON COMPANY PROPERTY IN PINE GROVE, WV. INITIAL NOTICE WAS RECEIVED FROM AN OUTSIDE PARTY AT APPROX 23:23. TL-442 IS A 16 IN OD TRANSMISSION PIPELINE APPROX 0.30 MI LONG AND TRANSPORTS NATURAL GAS FROM HASTINGS CS TO MOCKINGBIRD HILL CS. THE MAOP OF TL-442 IS 990 PSIG. A PRESSURE RESTRICTION OF 960 PSIG WAS ESTABLISHED IN 2003 FOR TL-442 DUE TO ITS CONNECTION TO TL-430 AT HAUGHT JCT. TL-442 WAS IN NORMAL OPERATION WITH AN OP OF 947 PSIG WHEN THE INCIDENT OCCURRED. AT APPROX 23:30, OPS PERSONNEL CALLED 911. ALSO AT APPROX 23:30, THE C-300 UNIT AT HASTINGS CS SHUTDOWN AND THE ASSOCIATED UNIT VALVES CLOSED, ISOLATING TL-442 UPSTREAM OF THE RUPTURE. AT APPROX 23:33, GAS CONTROL REMOTELY CLOSED VALVE-420JJ, ISOLATING TL-442 DOWNSTREAM OF THE RUPTURE. AT APPROX 23:50, OPS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT THE SITE. THE RUPTURE RESULTED IN AN APPROX 40 FT LENGTH OF PIPE OPENING CIRCUMFERENTIALLY AND LONGITUDINALLY (NOT SEAM ORIENTED) AND BOTH ENDS FOLDING UP AND COMING OUT OF THE GROUND; APPROX 23,403 MCF OF GAS LOSS; DAMAGE TO THE ACCESS ROAD; AND DEBRIS BEING BLOWN ON THE RIGHT-OF-WAY, S.R. 20, AND A RAILROAD. S.R. 20 AND THE RAILROAD WERE TEMPORARILY CLOSED AT 23:40 TO REMOVE DEBRIS. THE GAS DID NOT IGNITE AND THERE WERE NO FIRES, INJURIES, FATALITIES, OR EVACUATIONS. IT WAS DETERMINED ON 11/29/2022 AT 00:44 THAT THE INCIDENT INVOLVED AN UNINTENTIONAL ESTIMATED VOLUME OF GAS LOSS OF 3 MMSCF OR MORE. INITIAL NOTIFICATION (#1353645) WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 11/29/22 AT 01:04. FOLLOW UP NOTIFICATION (#1353781) WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 11/30/22 AT 11:46 TO CONFIRM THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION AND UPDATE THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE TL-442, THE GPS COORDINATES, THE ESTIMATED VOLUME OF UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS, AND THE TEMPORARY CLOSURE (AND REOPENING) OF S.R. 20 AND THE RAILROAD IN ORDER TO REMOVE DEBRIS. THE ESTIMATED VOLUME OF UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS LATER CONFIRMED TO BE 23,403 MCF. TL-442 WAS RESTARTED ON 12/30/22 AT 14:09 AFTER COMPLETION OF THE FOLLOWING REMEDIATION: APPROX 485 FT OF STATION PIPING AT HASTINGS CS ASSOCIATED WITH TL-442 WAS REMOVED AND REPLACED ALONG WITH VALVE-425D TO RESUME THE FLOW OF GAS BETWEEN HASTINGS CS AND MOCKINGBIRD HILL CS THROUGH TL-597 AND INTO TL-430 WHILE THE REMEDIATION OF TL-442 WAS COMPLETED. BOTH ENDS OF THE PIPE WERE CUT AT THE RUPTURE ON TL-442, UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM OF THE HINGED AREAS WHERE THE PIPE FOLDED UP AND CAME OUT OF THE GROUND. THE TWO PIECES OF PIPE THAT WERE CUT-OUT AT THE RUPTURE WERE SENT TO KIEFNER FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS AND REPLACEMENT PIPE WAS INSTALLED. THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS REPORT FROM KIEFNER DETERMINED THAT EXTERNAL CORROSION WHERE DISBONDED POLYETHYLENE COATING WAS SHIELDING CATHODIC PROTECTION WAS THE APPARENT CAUSE OF THE FAILURE. PIPE WAS CUT-OUT AT SELECTED LOCATIONS TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO THE INSIDE OF TL-442 FOR WIRELINE INSPECTIONS. WIRELINES USING CAL AND MFL TOOLS WERE CONDUCTED TO ASSESS THE INTEGRITY OF TL-442 AND REPLACEMENT PIPE WAS INSTALLED AT THE ACCESS LOCATIONS. INITIAL VERIFICATION DIGS WERE COMPLETED ON EITHER SIDE OF THE RUPTURE TO ASSIST IN VALIDATING THE ACCURACY OF THE DATA FROM THE WIRELINES. BASED ON THE DATA FROM THE WIRELINES, THE RESULTS FROM THE INITIAL VERIFICATION DIGS, AND THE EVALUATION OF ANOMALIES AS DESCRIBED BELOW, APPROX 950 FT OF TL-442 WAS REPLACED. THIS INVOLVED REPLACING TWO SECTIONS OF TL-442, FROM VALVE-425D TO THE SOUTH SIDE OF FISHING CREEK AND FROM THE NORTH SIDE OF S.R. 20 TO MOCKINGBIRD HILL JCT. THE ANOMALIES LOCATED IN THESE TWO SECTIONS WERE EVALUATED AS THE PIPE WAS EXCAVATED PRIOR TO REMOVAL AND FURTHER VERIFIED THE ACCURACY OF THE DATA FROM THE WIRELINES. APPROX 120 FT OF TL-442 LOCATED IN A CASING UNDER THE RAILROAD AND S.R. 20 WAS REMOVED AND REPLACEMENT PIPE WAS INSTALLED. AN ANOMALY LOCATED ON THE CASED PIPE THAT WAS REMOVED WAS EVALUATED AND FURTHER VERIFIED THE ACCURACY OF THE DATA FROM THE WIRELINES.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 21, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 06:00 HOURS, A STATION MECHANIC REPORTING TO WORK AT THE CONSUMERS ENERGY'S  FREEDOM COMPRESSOR STATION OBSERVED GAS BLOWING AT/NEAR THE CONDENSATE STORAGE TANK.  FREEDOM COMPRESSOR STATION IS LOCATED AT 12201 E. PLEASANT LAKE ROAD, MANCHESTER, (WASHTENAW COUNTY) MICHIGAN.    THE EMPLOYEE RESPONDED BY UTILIZING ISOLATION VALVE (30SDV-012) TO STOP THE GAS FROM BLOWING AND BEGAN A PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION TO DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF THE GAS RELEASE. A REVIEW OF THE SECURITY FOOTAGE AND CONFIRMATION OF HISTORIAN DATA DETERMINED THE RELEASE BEGAN ON SUNDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 05:48 HOURS.     THE INITIAL INVESTIGATION ON NOVEMBER 21, 2022 DETERMINED THE FILTER SEPARATOR DUMP VALVE (30GV-528) DID NOT CLOSE AFTER THE CONDENSATE DUMP OPERATION AND ALLOWED GAS TO FLOW FREELY FROM THE FILTER-SEPARATOR TO THE CONDENSATE STORAGE TANK AND RELEASE TO ATMOSPHERE. CONSUMERS ENERGY PERSONNEL INITIATED AN ABNORMAL EQUIPMENT ALIGNMENT/CONFIGURATION REQUEST (AEC) TO ISOLATE THE FILTER-SEPARATOR CONDENSATE SYSTEM TO ALLOW FOR MANUAL OPERATION.    FURTHER INVESTIGATION DETERMINED THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE RELEASE WAS DUE TO THE FILTER SEPARATOR SOUTH SUMP LEVEL CONTROLLER (LC_3001500).  THE LEVEL CONTROLLER SENT A PNEUMATIC SIGNAL TO THE DUMP VALVE WHICH OPENED THE VALVE TO INITIATE THE CONDENSATE DUMP PROCESS.  AFTER THE DUMP OPERATION WAS COMPLETE, THE SIGNAL TO CLOSE THE VALVE WAS NOT INITIATED (REMOVED) CAUSING THE DUMP VALVE TO REMAIN OPEN.       ON DECEMBER 7, 2022, CONSUMERS ENERGY (CE) PERSONNEL MET WITH THE LOCAL MAGNETROL LEVEL CONTROLLER SALES ENGINEER TO TROUBLESHOOT THE LEVEL CONTROLLER OPERATION. DURING THE INVESTIGATION IT WAS DISCOVERED THE LEVEL ADJUSTMENT KNOB WAS SET AT 1 WHICH WAS LOWER THAN RECOMMENDED FOR THIS OPERATION. STATION PERSONNEL PROCEEDED TO INCREASE THE LEVEL CONTROL SET POINT WHILE MONITORING SYSTEM RESPONSE.  AFTER ADJUSTING THE LEVEL TO A SET POINT OF 5, WHICH WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN ACCEPTABLE LEVEL SET POINT FOR OPERATION.    CE PERSONNEL WILL CONTINUE TO EVALUATING GAS COMPRESSION STATION LEVEL CONTROLLERS AND GAS CONTROL NOTIFICATION METHODS TO ALARM IN THE EVENT OF GAS RELEASE ASSOCIATED WITH PNEUMATIC LEVEL CONTROL DEVICES. THE UNIT WILL REMAIN IN BY-PASS MODE UNTIL THE EVALUATION IS COMPLETED AND ACTIONS TO MITIGATE ARE IMPLEMENTED.    THE EVALUATION OF THE LEVEL CONTROLLER AND GAS CONTROL NOTIFICATION METHODS AT THE FREEDOM COMPRESSOR STATION WAS COMPLETED.  AS A RESULT, LOW LEVEL LIQUID ALARMS AS WELL AS CONDENSATE TANK ACOUSTIC MONITORING WERE IMPLEMENTED. THE AEC WAS REMOVED AND THE UNIT IS NO LONGER IN BY-PASS MODE, NORMAL OPERATION RESUMED ON JANUARY 24, 2023.    CE HAS COMPLETED PROBLEM SOLVING SESSIONS REGARDING CONDENSATE DRAIN SYSTEM ALARMING AND FILTER-SEPARATOR COMMISSIONING/OPERATIONAL TESTING.  LONG-TERM COUNTERMEASURES WERE GENERATED WITH A PLAN TO INCORPORATE FLEETWIDE.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: AT THIS TIME THE CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS DUE TO PLC POWER AND RELAY FAILURE, NOT CONTROL ROOM ACTION.
NARRATIVE: COLUMBIA GULF TRANSMISSION, LLC, A TC ENERGY COMPANY, OPERATES AND MAINTAINS STANTON COMPRESSOR STATION LOCATED IN STANTON, KENTUCKY. ON SATURDAY NOVEMBER 19TH, 2022 A TC ENERGY CLEANING CONTRACTOR HEARD GAS VENTING INTO THE ATMOSPHERE WHILE PERFORMING THEIR DUTIES AT STANTON COMPRESSOR STATION. THE CONTRACTOR IMMEDIATELY CONTACTED COMPRESSOR STATION PERSONNEL; THE STATION PERSONNEL RESPONDED TO THE GAS RELEASE AT STANTON COMPRESSOR STATION AND ADDRESSED THE UNINTENDED RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS THAT WAS VENTING FROM UNIT 108 BLOWDOWN DISCHARGE STACK. THE STATION PERSONNEL INSPECTED AND SECURED THE AREA TO ENSURE THAT IT WAS SAFE; THEN THEN IDENTIFIED VALVES TO ISOLATE THE GAS LINE. THE LINE WAS ISOLATED AT 14:30 EASTERN TIME. ONCE THE LINE WAS ISOLATED THE STATION OPERATING PERSONNEL BEGAN TROUBLESHOOTING THE ISSUE. ALL ALARMS WERE CLEARED AT THE CONTROL PANEL, FUNCTIONAL TESTS WERE COMPLETED WITH NO NEW ERRORS GENERATED TO ENSURE NORMAL OPERATION OF THE STATION.    PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION IDENTIFIED THE PROBABLE CAUSE THAT LED TO THIS UNINTENDED RELEASE AT UNIT 108 WAS DUE TO A LOSS OF POWER TO A PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC CONTROLLER (PLC) AND RELAY FAILURE. THE LOSS OF POWER TO THIS PLC CAUSED THE COMPRESSOR UNIT BLOW DOWN VALVE (AOV 8810) TO REMAIN OPEN. THE VALVE REMAINED OPEN FOR 50 MINUTES RESULTING IN AN ESTIMATED TOTAL GAS LOST OF 5,328.8 MCF. THE PRESSURE OF THE LINE AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT WAS 779 LBS.    RELAY K340-2B DID NOT UNLATCH FROM ITS N/C STATE AFTER LOSING ITS POWER OR DE-ENERGIZED STATE TO N/O WHICH WOULD HAVE ALLOWED UNIT SUCTION/DISCHARGE VALVES TO BECOME CLOSED OR INTO A FAIL TO CLOSE/SHUT PHASE PER DESIGN. THIS WOULD HAVE PREVENTED GAS FROM VENTING TO ATMOSPHERE VIA UNIT FOUR INCH BLOWN DOWN SILENCER STACK. WITH POWER RESTORED & RELAY REPLACED, THE REMAINING SECTION OF CIRCUIT WAS TESTED TO MAKE SURE PROPER PANEL FEEDBACK, CONTINUITY TEST TO YARD VALVES COMPONENTS WAS VERIFIED AND SUCCESSFUL.

NARRATIVE: ON 11/18/2022, AT 09:37, A 3 INCH EMERGENCY SHUT DOWN (ESD) VALVE UNINTENTIONALLY OPENED AT THE COLORADO INTERSTATE GAS (CIG), MUDDY GAP COMPRESSORS STATION. AT 22:14, CIG GAS CONTROL CALLED THE FIELD OPERATOR ON DUTY AND COMMUNICATED THAT PRESSURE WAS TRENDING DOWN AND A VENT VALVE MAY BE OPEN AT THE MUDDY GAP COMPRESSOR STATION. AT 23:30, CIG FIELD OPERATOR ARRIVED ON SITE AND DISCOVERED AN ESD VALVE WAS OPEN. THE OPERATOR CLOSED THE VALVE. THE INCIDENT WAS DEEMED REPORTABLE. THE NRC NOTIFICATION WAS ISSUED, AND THE INTERNAL INVESTIGATION STARTED.     THE INTERNAL INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THAT THE ESD VALVE SOLENOID FAILED DUE TO AN ACCUMULATION OF WATER AND DEBRIS FROM THE STATION SUPPLY AIR SYSTEM THAT FROZE CAUSING THE VALVE TO OPERATE ABNORMALLY SENDING A PNEUMATIC OPEN SIGNAL TO THE ESD VALVE. IN ADDITION, THE VALVE STATUS WAS NOT IN SCADA AND THE CONTROLLERS DID NOT GET AN ALARM NOTIFYING THEM THAT THE VENT VALVE WAS OPEN.       IN ORDER TO PREVENT REOCCURRENCE CIG OPERATIONS CLEANED AND RE-TESTED THE VALVE SOLENOID. OPERATIONS IS REVIEWING PROCESSES AND PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE MEASURES TO VERIFY THAT THE MUDDY GAP CS SUPPLIED AIR SYSTEMS ARE DRY THROUGHOUT THE ENTIRE SYSTEM. IN ADDITION, OPERATIONS ADDED VALVE STATUS PLC LOGIC ALARMS IN SCADA FOR THE MUDDY GAP ESD VALVES.

NARRATIVE: ON NOVEMBER 9, 2022 OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ON SITE AT THE KEMMERER COMPRESSOR STATION HEARD WHAT APPEARED TO BE A COMPRESSOR RELIEF VALVE RELIEVING GAS. UPON FURTHER INVESTIGATION THE OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN DISCOVERED THE UNIT 1 PSV WAS RELIEVING. IN ORDER TO REMEDY THE SITUATION AS QUICKLY AS POSSIBLE, THE TECHNICIAN SHUT THE UNIT DOWN, ISOLATED IT FROM PRESSURE, AND BLEW IT DOWN. IT WAS NOTED BY THE TECHNICIAN AT THE TIME THAT THE RELIEF VALVE WOULD NOT RESEAT ITSELF AFTER DECREASING PRESSURE. IT WAS ALSO NOTED THAT THE RELIEF VALVE HAD OPENED UP PREMATURELY AT A PRESSURE OF AROUND 770PSI, WHERE THE SET PRESSURE ON THE RELIEF VALVE WAS 928 PSI. SINCE OPERATIONS DIDN'T KNOW EXACTLY WHEN THE RELIEF VALVE STARTED RELEASING GAS, THEY WORKED WITH TECH SERVICES AND GAS CONTROL TO ATTEMPT TO FIND ANY DATA SIGNIFYING WHEN THE RELEASE STARTED TO OCCUR. HMI TRENDS AND STATION CCTV CAMERAS WERE UTILIZED TO TRY AND DETERMINE WHEN THE UNIT STARTED TO RELIEVE, BUT WERE UNSUCCESSFUL IN DETERMINING THE EXACT TIME. AFTER REVIEWING THAT DATA THROUGHOUT THE DAY ON NOVEMBER 9TH AND NOT FINDING A DEFINITIVE ANSWER, IT WAS DECIDED TO CALCULATE A GAS LOSS ESTIMATION FROM THE MOMENT THE OPERATOR ARRIVED ON SITE AT 06:45 TO THE TIME THE UNIT WAS COMPLETELY ISOLATED AND GAS HAD STOPPED RELEASING AT 07:22. ONCE THIS TIMEFRAME WAS DECIDED, THE GAS LOSS CALCULATION WAS PROMPTLY RUN AND FOUND TO BE IN EXCESS OF THE 3MMSCF THRESHOLD. THAT GAS LOSS ESTIMATION WAS THEN COMMUNICATED TO THE SECURITY OPERATIONS CENTER AT WILLIAMS AND WAS IMMEDIATELY REPORTED TO THE NRC THAT AFTERNOON.    OVER THE COURSE OF THE FOLLOWING WEEKS, OPERATIONS WORKED WITH GAS CONTROL, SCADA, AND TECH SERVICES TO INVESTIGATE WHY THE VALVE HAD OPENED AT A LOWER PRESSURE THAN DESIGNED. IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE PILOT ON THIS PSV FAILED DUE TO A HARMONIC FREQUENCY AT THE STATION. IN ORDER TO REMEDY THE SITUATION, OPERATIONS WORKED TO GET A NEW AND DIFFERENT PILOT INSTALLED ON THIS PSV THAT WOULDN'T BE SUSCEPTIBLE TO PREMATURE RELIEVING AS A RESULT OF HARMONICS. OPERATIONS SUCCESSFULLY INSTALLED THE NEW PILOT ON THE PSV AND RETURNED THE COMPRESSOR UNIT TO SERVICE ON 11/18/2022.

NARRATIVE: EQUITRANS RAGER MOUNTAIN STORAGE FIELD WAS SHUT-IN ON 11/1/2022 TO PERFORM ROUTINE SHUT-IN INVENTORY VERIFICATION TESTING. ON NOVEMBER 6, 2022, EQUITRANS PERSONNEL WAS NOTIFIED OF AN ISSUE AND ARRIVED ON SITE AT GSW2244 AT APPROXIMATELY 4:15 PM. UPON ARRIVAL, PERSONNEL FOUND GAS ESCAPING FROM THE 9 5/8" CASING THROUGH A 2" VENT (ANNULAR VENT). THE SITE WAS SECURED AND AN INVESTIGATION BY EQUITRANS AND CUDD PRESSURE CONTROL ENSUED. STORAGE GAS FROM THE 7" PRODUCTION CASING WAS COMMUNICATING WITH THE 9 5/8" INTERMEDIATE CASING AND VENTING TO ATMOSPHERE. EQUITRANS AND CUDD COORDINATED TO SAFELY ADDRESS THE MIGRATING GAS USING A DYNAMIC KILL PROCEDURE. ON 11/19/2022, A TEMPORARY PLUG WAS SET IN THE 7" CASING FOLLOWED BY A SECOND TEMPORARY PLUG AND APPROXIMATELY 270' OF CEMENT ON 11/20/2022. AN INVESTIGATION IS UNDERWAY TO DETERMINE THE ROOT CAUSE FAILURE ANALYSIS (RCFA) OF THE NATURAL GAS RELEASE.  IN THE INTERIM, EQUITRANS PLANS TO PERFORM THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES TO INFORM THE RCFA AND ENSURE WELL INTEGRITY PER PHMSA SAFETY REGULATIONS AND API 1171.      1- INSTALLING REAL TIME PRESSURE MONITORS ON THE ANNULUS OF ALL RAGER MOUNTAIN GAS STORAGE WELLS.  2- REANALYZING THE 2016 VERTILOG WIRELINE RESULTS OF THE PRODUCTION CASING FROM WELL 2244 USING THE MOST RECENT SOFTWARE.  3- CONDUCTING A NEW WIRELINE SURVEY (ASSESSMENT) OF THE PRODUCTION CASINGS FOR THE OTHER WELLS WITH A HIGH DEFINITION, METAL LOSS VERTILOG TOOL.  4- EVALUATING ANY OTHER MECHANICAL GAS MIGRATION PATHWAYS BETWEEN THE PRODUCTION AND INTERMEDIATE CASING  5- REVIEWING EXISTING STORAGE WELL EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS AND RESPONSE PROCEDURES BASED ON LESSONS LEARNED FROM SHUTTING IN WELL 2244 TO IDENTIFY AND IMPLEMENT POTENTIAL IMPROVEMENTS.  ***FEBRUARY 2023 SUPPLEMENT***   NOTIFICATION OF THE INCIDENT CLARIFICATION: AT 3:28 PM ON 11/6/2022 EQUITRANS WAS NOTIFIED BY ANOTHER OPERATOR IN THE AREA (PEOPLES) WHO RECEIVED A NOTIFICATION FROM CAMBRIA COUNTY 911 SERVICE. EMERGENCY RESPONDERS WERE ON SITE WHEN EQUITRANS ARRIVED AT 4:15 PM. EQUITRANS EMPLOYEES VERIFIED THE ASSET WAS EQUITRANS GSW 2244 AND WERE IN COMMUNICATION WITH EMERGENCY RESPONDERS ON SITE.     ***MAY 2024 SUPPLEMENT***    THE RCFA WAS SUBMITTED TO PHMSA ON AUGUST 24, 2023. AN UNREDACTED COPY IS AVAILABLE IN PHMSA'S PUBLIC READING ROOM. THE RCFA INCLUDES, AMONG OTHER THINGS, THE DIRECT AND ROOT CAUSES OF THE INCIDENT AND THE ALLOTMENT OF INVENTORY CHANGE. EQUITRANS LATER SUBMITTED A REMEDIAL WORK PLAN AND AN INJECTION PLAN TO PHMSA SEEKING AUTHORITY TO RESUME INJECTIONS AT RAGER USING ALL WELLS EXCEPT THREE, WHICH REMAIN DISCONNECTED. ON OCTOBER 2, 2023, PHMSA APPROVED THE INJECTION PLAN. EQUITRANS BEGAN INJECTION AT RAGER ON OCTOBER 5, 2023. ON NOVEMBER 16, 2023, PHMSA ISSUED A LETTER TO EQUITRANS APPROVING ITS REQUEST TO REMOVE ALL PRESSURE RESTRICTION AT RAGER.    IN THIS FINAL REPORT, EQUITRANS ESTIMATES GAS COSTS BASED ON A HYPOTHETICAL UNIT PRICE TO REPLACE THE ENTIRETY OF THE ESTIMATED 1.29 BCF OF INVENTORY REDUCTION. EQUITRANS INCLUDES THIS INFORMATION FOR REPORTING PURPOSES ONLY; INCLUDING SUCH INFORMATION DOES NOT REQUIRE OR IMPLY THAT EQUITRANS WILL INCUR ANY SUCH COSTS.

NARRATIVE: ON 11/04/22 AT APPROXIMATELY 5:45AM A THIRD-PARTY OPERATOR WHILE CONDUCTING UNDER WATER (770FT) INSPECTIONS WITH A REMOTELY OPERATED VEHICLE (ROV) ON THEIR SUBSEA TIE-IN WITHIN 20 FEET OF OUR PIPE AND VALVE SITE; NOTICED BUBBLES COMING FROM A HOT TAP COMPLETION VALVE ON OUR (HIGH POINT GAS GATHERING) MC 109 LATERAL (BSEE 14970). THE OPERATOR CONTACTED OUR ENGINEERS TO REPORT THE RELEASE. FROM REPORTS THE BUBBLES BEING RELEASE ARE MINOR. THE AREA WAS FLOWN, AND NO BUBBLES WERE VISIBLE ON THE WATER SURFACE. THIS WAS ALSO CONFIRMED BY THE BOAT PRESENT ON LOCATION. CONTROL CENTER DID NOT SEE A PRESSURE DROP EITHER. AT 10:30AM IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE MONETARY AMOUNT FOR REPAIR WOULD EXCEED THE THRESHOLD FOR IMMEDIATE NOTIFICATION AND A CALL WAS PLACED TO THE NRC AT 10:53AM ON 11/04/22 (NRC # 1351712). ON 11/07/22 AT 7:10AM A 48HR UPDATE TO THE NRC WAS PLACED (NRC# 1351906). ON 11/18/22 A SATURATION DIVE CREW ARRIVED ON SITE TO PREFORM WORK FOR THE OPERATOR THAT IDENTIFIED THE RELEASE WITHIN 20FT OF THE RELEASE. HIGH POINT GAS GATHERING WAS ABLE TO CONTRACT THE DIVE CREW TO INVESTIGATE AND REPORT ON THE RELEASE. THE PIN HOLE LEAK SEEMS TO BE COMING FROM THE STOPPLE WELD-O-LET.  DUE TO THE DEPTH (770FT) AND THE AVAILABILITY OF DIVE BOAT/CREWS AND MINIMAL SIZE OF THE RELEASE; REPAIR PLANS/LOGISTICS ARE STILL BEING DEVELOPED AND SUBMITTED TO BSEE FOR APPROVAL. APPROXIMATELY 0.601 MCF OF NATURAL GAS HAS BEEN RELEASED. A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE FILED ONCE REPAIRS ARE COMPLETE.    *UPDATE* 10/29/24  AFTER BEING DELAYED BY THE MULTIPLE HURRICANES AND STORMS IN THE GULF OF MEXICO, WE WERE ABLE TO GET ON SCENE AND GET DIVERS DOWN TO THE 792FT AT THE END OF THE WEEK OF OCT 14TH. BUT UNFORTUNATELY, DUE TO UNFAVORABLE WEATHER CONDITIONS STARTING OCT 22 WE HAD TO LEAVE THE AREA. DIVERS WERE ABLE TO CONFIRM THAT THE LEAK HAS NOT WORSEN AND IS STILL A VERY MINOR LEAK (NO BUBBLES REACHING THE SURFACE AND NO SHEEN). THE CREW WAS ABLE TO COMPLETE SOME PREPARATIONS FOR THE REPAIR BUT HAD TO LEAVE DUE TO WEATHER.  AGAIN, DUE TO THE DEPTH, TIME OF YEAR, LEAK SIZE, WEATHER CONDITIONS I DO NOT HAVE A TIME FRAME FOR 2024 AND WILL LIKELY MOVE INTO SPRING 2025. REPAIR PLAN WILL BE TO INSTALL A TEMPORARY LAUNCHER, INSERT A STOPPLE IN THE TRAP, PUSH IT TO JUST PAST THE LEAKING FLANGE, ENGAGE THE STOPPLE, REPLACE THE LEAKING COMPONENT, DISENGAGE THE STOPPLE, AND LET THE PRODUCT PRESSURE PUSH THE STOPPLE BACK INTO THE TRAP, REMOVE THE LAUNCHER REPLACE BLIND FLANGE.     OCTOBER 2025, A SATURATION DIVE SPREAD ARRIVED ON LOCATION AND STARTED THE REPAIR PROCESS AND THE LEAK WAS STOPPED ON 10/22/25 AND REPAIRS COMPLETED PER THE ORIGINAL APPROVED REPAIR PLAN.

DURING THE OUTAGE, THE 1401 LINE, WHICH RUNS PARALLEL TO THE DAMAGED 1400 LINE, WAS ABLE TO OPERATE UNDER NORMAL CONDITIONS TO AVOID ANY SIGNIFICANT DISRUPTIONS TO OUR CUSTOMERS.

NARRATIVE: ON 10/30/22 AT APPROXIMATELY 02:50, AN INDIVIDUAL NOT AFFILIATED WITH THE COMPANY AND NOT A CONTRACTOR WORKING FOR THE COMPANY DROVE A VEHICLE OFF THE ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF WEST LUCAS STREET (HIGHWAY 52) AND PISGAH ROAD IN FLORENCE, SOUTH CAROLINA. AFTER LEAVING THE ROAD, THE VEHICLE COLLIDED WITH AND DAMAGED THE FLORENCE TEC TOWN BORDER STATION (13-110) THAT IS SITUATED APPROXIMATELY 40 FT FROM THE SIDE OF THE INTERSECTION. THE VEHICLE CAME TO A STOP ON TOP OF THE STATION. THE FUNCTION OF THE STATION IS METERING AND REGULATING THE NATURAL GAS BEING SUPPLIED TO THE CITY OF FLORENCE. THE STATION WAS IN NORMAL OPERATION WITH AN OPERATING PRESSURE OF APPROXIMATELY 610 PSIG WHEN THE INCIDENT OCCURRED. THE COLLISION RESULTED IN THE ENTIRE STATION BEING SHEARED OFF AT THE RISERS AND NATURAL GAS BEGAN BLOWING TO THE ATMOSPHERE. THE INDIVIDUAL DRIVING THE VEHICLE WAS NOT INJURED AND SAFELY EXITED THE VEHICLE. THE VEHICLE DID NOT IGNITE THE BLOWING NATURAL GAS. AT APPROXIMATELY 03:07, GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A SCADA LOW LOW PRESSURE ALARM ON THE STATION. AT APPROXIMATELY 03:14, GAS CONTROL DISPATCHED OPERATIONS PERSONNEL TO INVESTIGATE THE SCADA LOW LOW PRESSURE ALARM AT THE STATION. AT APPROXIMATELY 03:32, GAS CONTROL RECEIVED NOTIFICATION FROM THE FLORENCE COUNTY 911 DISPATCHER. AT APPROXIMATELY 04:04, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT THE STATION AND ASSESSED THE SITUATION. AT APPROXIMATELY 04:07 OPERATIONS PERSONNEL AT THE SITE REQUESTED ADDITIONAL SUPPORT TO HELP ISOLATE THE STATION. IT WAS DETERMINED AT 07:41 THAT THE INCIDENT LIKELY INVOLVED AN ESTIMATED PROPERTY DAMAGE OF $129,300 OR MORE WHICH WOULD CLASSIFY IT AS REPORTABLE INCIDENT. INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER AT 08:13. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1351225 TO THE INITIAL NOTIFICATION OF THE INCIDENT. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL STARTED TO ISOLATE THE STATION AT APPROXIMATELY 08:05. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL COMPLETED THE ISOLATION OF THE STATION AT APPROXIMATELY 08:21. THE ISOLATION OF THE STATION WAS ACCOMPLISHED BY HYDRAULICALLY PINCHING THE INLET RISER WHERE IT HAD BEEN SHEARED OFF BY THE VEHICLE AND WELDING ON A CAP WITH A NEEDLE VALVE AS A PRECAUTIONARY MEASURE TO RELIEVE ANY POTENTIAL OVER PRESSURIZATION. THE TAP VALVE TO THE STATION WAS ALSO CLOSED AND THE ENTIRE STATION WAS REMOVED FROM THE SITE. A FOLLOW UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION WAS MADE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ON 10/31/22 AT 16:16 TO CONFIRM THE INITIAL TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION AND PROVIDE AN UPDATE ON THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE STATION AND THE ESTIMATED VOLUME OF UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS. THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER ASSIGNED INCIDENT REPORT #1351347 TO THE FOLLOW UP TELEPHONIC NOTIFICATION. FOLLOWING INCIDENT REPORT #1351347, THE ESTIMATED VOLUME OF UNINTENTIONAL GAS LOSS WAS LATER CONFIRMED TO BE 1757 MCF. THE FOLLOWING REMEDIATION ACTIONS WERE COMPLETED: THE ENTIRE STATION WAS REBUILT, THE REBUILT STATION WAS INSTALLED AT THE SITE, AND THE REBUILT STATION WAS RESTARTED AND RETURNED TO SERVICE ON 12/20/22 AT 15:00. ENHANCED PROTECTION MEASURES FOR THE STATION ARE PLANNED TO BE INSTALLED.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: NO INDICATION OR EVIDENCE OF CONTROL ROOM OR CONTROLLER CONTRIBUTION TO THE INCIDENT; OPERATIONS (I.E., PRESSURES AND FLOWS) IN THE AREA WERE NORMAL AND WITHIN DESIGNED RANGES.
NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 22, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 9:12 AM EST, AN EMPLOYEE AT THE REUNION COMPRESSOR STATION IDENTIFIED GAS BLOWING THROUGH THE STATION BLOWDOWN STACK. THIS WAS NOT A PLANNED ACTIVITY AND INDICATED AN ISSUE. HE EXAMINED THE STATION AND IDENTIFIED AN ICE BUILDUP ON THE 2-INCH COMPRESSOR UNIT #1 LOADING VALVE. UNIT #1 WAS NOT IN OPERATION AT THE TIME BUT DID HAVE GAS AT A PRESSURE OF 150 PSIG LEAKING THROUGH THE VALVE INTO THE STATION BLOWDOWN SYSTEM.    EARLIER UNDER NORMAL OPERATION UNIT #1 WAS SHUT DOWN TO SWITCH TO UNIT #2. THROUGH THIS PROCESS THE UNIT LOADING VALVE ON UNIT #1 FAILED TO TRAVEL TO THE FULLY CLOSED POSITION BUT DID INDICATE IT WAS CLOSED ON THE SYSTEM CONTROLS DUE TO A FAULTY LIMIT SWITCH. THE VALVE FAILING TO FULLY CLOSE ALLOWED FOR GAS TO VENT THROUGH THE STATION BLOWDOWN SYSTEM OVERNIGHT. THE RESTRICTED FLOW PATH CREATED A RESTRICTION AND PRESSURE DROP THAT CAUSED THE FORMATION OF ICE AT THE VALVE. THE FAULTY LIMIT SWITCH ON THE 2-INCH LOADING VALVE WAS ALSO REPLACED.    UNIT #1 REMAINED OFF THROUGHOUT THIS TIME, UNIT #2 REMAINED RUNNING TO MEET ALL NEEDED CAPACITIES. THE FAILED COMPONENTS WERE REPLACED BEFORE RETURNING UNIT #1 TO NORMAL OPERATION.    THE GAS LOSS BASED UPON THE RESTRICTION AND DURATION WAS CALCULATED TO BE 7.08 MMSCF. INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE ACTIONS OF THE CONTROLLER IN THIS INCIDENT DID NOT WARRANT AN INVESTIGATION.  THE CONTROLLER NOTIFIED FIELD PERSONNEL OF A UNIT BEING DOWN AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION.  THE INCIDENT OCCURRED WHEN THE FIELD PERSONNEL TRIED TO START UNIT 442 AND THE LOAD LINE VALVE FAILED.
OTHER_COMPRESSOR_DETAILS: SEAL FAILURE AS WELL AS PLC PROGRAMMING CONTRIBUTED TO THIS INCIDENT.
NARRATIVE: ON OCTOBER 21ST, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 19:00 HOURS, TECH WAS DISPATCHED TO BENNINGTON CS UNIT 447 TO TROUBLESHOOT UNIT 447 GOING DOWN.  TECH CORRECTED THE ISSUE, PUT IT BACK ONLINE AND DEPARTED. UNIT 447 WENT BACK DOWN ON OIL TEMPERATURE ON THE SAME DATE AT APPROXIMATELY 20:30 HOURS. AT APPROXIMATELY 20:35 HOURS, TECH ADVISED GAS CONTROL TO START UNIT 442 REMOTELY. TECH THEN WENT BACK TO STATION TO RESET THE OIL COOLER TEMPERATURE SHUTDOWN ON UNIT 447. DURING THIS TIME, UNIT 442 FAILED TO START DUE TO NOT BEING PRESSURIZED. AT APPROXIMATELY 21:05, TECH THEN STARTED UNIT 447 AND PUT BACK ONLINE AND ADVISED GAS CONTROL THAT UNIT 442 WAS UNAVAILABLE DUE TO THE LOAD VALVE FAILURE.  ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 24TH, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 07:00 HOURS, TECH ARRIVED AT THE STATION AND WAS REVIEWING ALARMS THAT OCCURRED OVER THE WEEKEND.  HE NOTICED THAT THE LOAD VALVE FAILURE ALARM ON UNIT 442 WAS STILL ACTIVE.  TECH WENT OUT TO UNIT 442 TO PHYSICALLY INSPECT WHY THE ALARM WAS STILL ACTIVE.  TECH DISCOVERED THAT UNIT 442'S LOAD VALVE WAS PARTIALLY OPEN AND VENTING GAS.  AT 08:25 HOURS, TECH MANUALLY CLOSED THE LOAD VALVE ON UNIT 442 TO PREVENT IT FROM VENTING AND NOTIFIED HIS SUPERVISOR OF THE INCIDENT AT APPROXIMATELY 08:30 HOURS.  SUPERVISOR THEN INITIATED AN INVESTIGATION OF THE INCIDENT TO TRY AND DETERMINE HOW LONG THE VALVE HAD VENTED GAS AND WHAT CAUSED THE INCIDENT.  A BALANCE SEGMENT WAS DONE ON BENNINGTON COMPRESSOR STATION TO DETERMINE THE VOLUME OF GAS VENTED.  IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE VALVE HAD VENTED GAS FROM OCTOBER 21ST, 2022, AT 21:02 HOURS TO OCTOBER 24TH, 2022, AT 08:25 HOURS (APPROXIMATELY 59 HOURS AND 23 MINUTES). ON OCTOBER 24TH AT APPROXIMATELY 14:10 HOURS, SUPERVISOR NOTIFIED DOT COMPLIANCE PERSONNEL.  AT THIS POINT, IT WAS DETERMINED TO BE A REPORTABLE INCIDENT.  INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO NRC ON OCTOBER 24TH, 2022, AT 14:20.  ON OCTOBER 25TH, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 13:00 HOURS, PLC PROGRAMMER REVIEWED THE PROGRAMMING ON UNIT 442 AND DETERMINED THERE WAS NOT A LOGIC IN THE PLC THAT WOULD PREVENT THIS FROM HAPPENING.  ON OCTOBER 26TH, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 10:00 HOURS, PLC PROGRAMMER AND STATION I&E TECH, MADE THE PLC LOGIC CHANGES THAT WOULD PREVENT THIS FROM HAPPENING AGAIN. WE NOW HAVE A TIMED LOCKOUT ON THE UNIT THAT IF IT DOES NOT START IN A CERTAIN PROGRAMMED TIME, THE VALVE WILL AUTOMATICALLY CLOSE THE VENT VALVE AND GIVE AN ALARM OF THE LOCKOUT.  THE UNIT WAS LEFT LOCKED AND TAGGED OUT UNTIL SUCH PLC PROGRAMMING COULD BE TESTED AND VERIFIED TO BE ACCURATE.  ON DECEMBER 1ST, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 08:00 HOURS, LOGIC WAS TESTED ON THE PLC FOR UNIT 442, AND PROVEN TO BE EFFECTIVE.  THIS CHANGE WILL PREVENT FURTHER INCIDENTS FROM OCCURRING.  A SPECIFIC CAUSE OF THE LOAD VALVE FAILURE COULD NOT BE PINPOINTED THEREFORE, A NEW LOAD VALVE (NEW VALVE AND ACTUATOR) WAS ORDERED AND IS WAITING TO BE INSTALLED.  UNIT 442 IS LOCKED AND TAGGED OUT OF SERVICE UNTIL A STATION OUTAGE CAN BE SCHEDULED TO REPLACE THE LOAD VALVE ON UNIT 442.     NOTE:  AS DIRECTED BY AID/RODRIGUEZ, THE "EARLIEST TIME A REPORTING CRITERIA WAS MET" WAS CHANGED ON 4/26/2023.  THE ORIGINAL DATE AND TIME, 10/24/2022 14:20, WAS CHANGED TO 10/21/2022 21:02 AS DIRECTED TO DO PER AID.

NARRATIVE: THIS REPORT IS FOR THE FIXED FACILITY EVENT THAT OCCURRED ON OCTOBER 11, 2022:    AT 9:45 PM ON OCTOBER 11TH, ARKOMA 1 COMPRESSOR STATION WAS IN NORMAL OPERATIONS AT STEADY STATE CONDITIONS WHEN ENGINE/COMPRESSOR UNIT #2203 SHUT DOWN ON HIGH ENGINE COOLANT TEMPERATURE. THE ON-SITE OPERATOR INSPECTED THE UNIT AND INITIATED A BLOW DOWN TO PREPARE IT FOR RE-START.     AT APPROXIMATELY 10:12 PM, DURING THE UNIT BLOW DOWN, TWO (2) LEL GAS MONITORS ACTIVATED WITHIN THE ENCLOSED COMPRESSOR BUILDING, ACTIVATING AN ESD WHICH TRIPPED ALL COMPRESSORS (4 TOTAL) AND SHUT ELECTRICAL POWER TO THE SITE AS DESIGNED.     THE OPERATOR EVACUATED THE BUILDING. GAS WAS RELEASED AND IGNITED, AND THE BUILDING BECAME ENGULFED IN FLAMES AT APPROXIMATELY 10:16 PM. THERE WERE MULTIPLE LOSS OF CONTAINMENT (LOPC) POINTS AND EQUIPMENT FAILURES.    THE EVENT WAS CLASSIFIED AS AN ENCLOSED VAPOR CLOUD DEFLAGRATION FOLLOWED BY A FIRE.    THE INITIAL CAUSE OF THE ESD AND THE INITIATING IGNITION SOURCE COULD NOT BE DEFINITIVELY IDENTIFIED. IT IS SUSPECTED THAT LOPC ASSOCIATED WITH UNIT #2202 INITIATED THE ESD.    INCIPIENT FIRES FUELED BY THE RESIDUAL ENGINE OIL, COOLANT, AND INSULATION CONTINUED TO BURN UNTIL THE FIRE WAS EXTINGUISHED ON OCTOBER 12, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 11:00 AM CST.    ROOT CAUSE: WE HAVE DETERMINED THAT MULTIPLE FACTORS CAUSED THE EVENT, INCLUDING FAULTY AND MISSING CHECK VALVES AND LOW-PRESSURE BRAIDED HOSES EXPOSED TO HIGH PRESSURE FROM THE VENT SYSTEM.    PATH FORWARD: WE HAVE SINCE COMPLETED ISOLATING INDIVIDUAL VENTILATION LINES FROM THE PRE-LUBE PUMPS AND STARTERS, WHICH WILL SEPARATE HIGH-PRESSURE FROM LOW-PRESSURE COMPONENTS.  FOR THREE DAYS, WE ALSO HAD 20 + PEOPLE GO FLANGE TO FLANGE COMPLETE A PROCESS HAZARD ANALYSIS, CONFIRMING ALL THE DESIGNS AND P&IDS ARE CORRECT AND CORRECTED WHERE NEEDED.    RE-DESIGNING SKIDS TO REMOVE AS MANY THREADED CONNECTIONS AS POSSIBLE.    IMPLEMENTED AND SCHEDULED REPLACEMENTS OF WORN ITEMS SUCH AS BRAIDED HOSES.    INSTRUMENT AND COMPRESSOR START GAS HAVE BEEN CONVERTED TO AIR.    THE NEW BUILDING WILL BE OPEN WITH NO WALLS FOR IMPROVED VENTILATION.    ONCE THE COMPRESSOR STATION IS COMMISSIONED, A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT TO THIS ONE WILL BE SUBMITTED.

NARRATIVE: ON 10/07/22 AT APPROXIMATELY 11:04AM WHILE FIELD PERSONNEL WERE FLYING BACK FROM DUTIES AT ANOTHER LOCATION, THEY SPOTTED BUBBLES ON THE SEA SURFACE WITH NO SHEEN IN CLOSE PROXIMITY TO ONE OF OUR PIPELINES (HIGH POINT GAS GATHERING (HPGG), SP160 BSEE 4283). AT THAT TIME CONTROL CENTER DID NOT SEE A PRESSURE DROP. A CALL WAS PLACED TO THE NRC AT 6:12PM ON 10/07/22 (NRC# 1349308) TO REPORT AN UNKNOWN RELEASE AS WELL TO THE COAST GUARD TO ISSUE A BROADCAST WARNING TO ALL MARINE TRAFFIC. THIRD COAST HPGG SHUT IN PRODUCTION AND ISOLATED THE LATERAL TO STABILIZE AND MONITOR THE PRESSURE. THIRD COAST ALSO CONTRACTED A VESSEL TO BE AT THE RELEASE SITE TO MONITOR AND KEEP TRAFFIC AT A SAFE DISTANCE. ON 10/8/22 AFTER THE PRESSURE SETTLED DUE TO PRODUCTION AND RECEIVING SHUTTING DOWN; A SLOW PRESSURE DROP WAS OBSERVED. IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE RELEASE WAS COMING FROM OUR PIPELINE. ON 10/08/22 AT 6:54PM A CALL WAS PLACED TO THE NRC (NRC# 1349385) TO TAKE OWNERSHIP OF THE RELEASE AND UPDATE WITH KNOWN INFORMATION. HPGG KEPT THE LATERAL ISOLATED FROM THE MAINLINE UPSTREAM; DOWNSTREAM OF THE RELEASE WAS BLINDED. CONTROL CENTER HAD PRODUCTION MAINTAIN FLOW IN THE LINE TO KEEP A 150PSI TO 200PSI ON THE LINE TO PREVENT WATER ENTERING UNTIL REPAIRS COULD BE MADE. ON 10/10/22 AT 2:40PM A 48HR CALL WAS PLACED TO THE NRC (NRC# 1349499) TO UPDATE WITH NEW INFORMATION. DUE TO AVAILABILITY, A DIVE BOAT COULD NOT ARRIVE ON LOCATION UNTIL 10/16/22 TO LOCATE THE RELEASE. DIVERS LOCATED THE RELEASE AND CONFIRMED THAT IT WAS COMING FROM THE SP160 LATERAL. DUE TO WEATHER, THE DIVE BOAT HAD TO RETURN TO THE DOCK. ON 10/20/22 THE DIVE BOAT WAS BACK ON LOCATION DIVING AND MAKING THE REPAIR. ON 10/23/22 AT 2:46AM THE REPAIR WAS MADE WITH A CLAMP AND THE RELEASE STOPPED. APPROXIMATELY 4.983 MILLION CUBIC FEET OF NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED. THE DIVERS THEN MOVED UPSTREAM ISOLATING THE PIPELINE VIA A SUBSEA VALVE SETS FROM THE PRODUCER. THE SP160 LATERAL WILL BE PURGED OF HYDROCARBONS AND KEPT ISOLATED.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
EQ_FAILURE_DETAILS: THE HYDRAULIC PUMP USED TO CLOSE THE JAWS OF THE SQUEEZER FAILED. THIS FAILURE CAUSED THE JAWS TO OPEN ON THE SQUEEZERS THE CONTRACTOR RENTED.
NARRATIVE: IT IS CENTRIC'S OPINION THAT THE PRIMARY ROOT CAUSE OF THE INCIDENT WAS EQUIPMENT FAILURE (HYDRAULIC SQUEEZERS). THROUGH INVESTIGATION IT WAS REPORTED THAT SOMETHING MALFUNCTIONED IN THE HYDRAULIC PUMP, CAUSING THE JAWS OF THE SQUEEZERS TO RELEASE AND ALLOW GAS TO ENTER THE HOLE. BROADHEAD HAS REQUESTED MAINTENANCE RECORDS FROM EQUIPMENT SUPPLIER GAJESKE, INC. BUT THESE RECORDS HAVE NOT YET BEEN PROVIDED BY GAJESKE, INC. CENTRIC WAS INITIALLY INFORMED THAT NO CLAMPS WERE PROVIDED WITH THE RENTAL. AFTER FOLLOW-UP QUESTIONING CENTRIC RECEIVED THE FOLLOWING FROM BROADHEADS LAWYER "THE LOCKING DEVICES WERE PROVIDED BUT THEY WERE NOT USED." LEADING US TO IDENTIFY THIS AS A CONTRIBUTING FACTOR. OTHER CONTRIBUTING FACTORS INCLUDE THAT THE CONTRACTORS WERE NOT WEARING APPROPRIATE PPE (IN CONTRAVENTION TO THEIR CONTRACT WITH CENTRIC), AND HAZARD RECOGNITION WAS INADEQUATE BY THE CONTRACTOR STAFF ON SCENE.

NARRATIVE: WHILE PERFORMING ROUTINE ROUNDS A MONROE GAS STORAGE EMPLOYEE ARRIVED AT THE T (T IS REFERRED TO AS WHERE THE WELL ROAD FROM THE EAST GATE MEETS THE WELL ROAD THAT LEADS LEFT TOWARDS WELL #7 AND RIGHT TOWARD WELLS #15,16,17, &9) APPROXIMATE AT 1646 AND DISCOVERED THE FIRE COMING FROM WHAT WE NOW KNOW WAS THE 12' EAST LATERAL. AFTER ASSESSING THE LOCATION OF THE INCIDENT, THE EMPLOYEE BEGIN CALLING IN ADDITIONAL PERSONNEL AND EMERGENCY OFFICIALS TO ASSIST WHILE SHUTTING DOWN THE FACILITY ONCE ADDITIONAL PERSONNEL ARRIVE THE LINE WAS ISOLATED MANUALLY AND ALLOWED TO BURN ITSELF OUT. MONROE GAS STORAGE EMPLOYEES DIRECTED THE FIRE DEPARTMENT TO THE INCIDENT AREA AND REQUESTED THAT THEY ACTIVELY COOLED THE SURROUNDING AREA TO ENSURE THE FIRE WAS CONTAINED. THE FIRE WAS OUT, AND INCIDENT CONTAINED APPROXIMATELY 40 MINUTES AFTER DISCOVERY.     THE IDENTIFIED INTERNAL CORROSION WAS CONSISTENT WITH MESA CORROSION, TYPICALLY OCCURRING IN A CARBON DIOXIDE (CO2) ENVIRONMENT LIKELY EXACERBATED VIA THE PRESENCE OF SALT WATER AND SAND (RESULTING IN AN UNDER-DEPOSIT PHENOMENON).  TO MITIGATE THE DAMAGE FROM THE FAILURE AND IDENTIFIED CORROSION MGS PERFORMED THE FOLLOWING: REPLACED IN KIND 42FT NORTH OF POINT OF FAILURE AND 801 FT WEST OF POINT OF FAILURE. REPLACED IN KIND ONE 90-DEGREE ELBOW. MGS ALSO INSTALLED A 2 INCH LOW POINT DRAIN. THE DETERMINATION OF HOW MUCH OF PIPE NEEDED TO BE REPLACE WAS MADE USING A COMBINATION OF VISUAL INSPECTION, HAND-HELD UT GAUGE AND GUIDED WAVE TECHNOLOGY. GUIDED WAVE TECHNOLOGY WAS UTILIZED UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM OF REPLACED PIPE, OF WHICH NO ADDITIONAL INTEGRITY ISSUES WERE FOUND AND NO FOLLOW-UPS WERE RECOMMENDED.   THE 2 INCH LOW POINT DRAIN WAS THE FIRST PHASE OF STEPS MGS IS TAKING TO PREVENT RECURRENCE, THE SECOND INCLUDES DEVELOPING AND IMPLEMENTING A PIGGING AND CHEMICAL TREATMENT PLAN PRIOR TO BRINGING THE 12' EAST LATERAL IN SERVICE.

INT_VISUAL_EXAM_DETAILS: PIPE WALL THINNING DUE TO INTERNAL CORROSION.  DETERMINED BY METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS.
NARRATIVE: ON 10/03/22 AT 00:18, THE 24-INCH ST PETER DRIP LINE IN THE WAVERLY STORAGE FIELD FAILED RESULTING IN A FIRE. THE PIPE APPEARS TO HAVE FAILED AT THE 6 O'CLOCK POSITION ON THE SOUTH END OF THE PIPE.    APPROXIMATELY 7.1 MMCF OF NATURAL GAS WAS RELEASED. THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED AND BLOWDOWN BY AREA OPERATIONS RESULTING IN THE FIRE BEING EXTINGUISHED.    LOCAL EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT CLOSED ROADS AND EVACUATED TWO RESIDENTS NEAR THE FAILURE SITE TO PREVENT TRAFFIC FROM ENTERING THE AREA.    THE FAILED SECTION OF PIPE WAS REMOVED AND THE LINE WAS RESTARTED ON 10/06/22 AT 11:00.    THE FAILED SECTION OF PIPE WAS SENT TO THIRD PARTY LABORATORY FOR ANALYSIS. THE RESULTS OF THE ANALYSIS INDICATED THAT THE RUPTURE OCCURRED BY DUCTILE OVERLOAD AT THE APPROXIMATE 6:15 ORIENTATION IN AN AREA OF SIGNIFICANT WALL THINNING FROM INTERNAL CORROSION.  THE INTERNAL CORROSION THAT PRECEDED THE RUPTURE WAS LIKELY DUE TO THE PRESENCE OF CORROSIVE GASES IN THE PIPELINE.    TO PREVENT RECURRENCE, A NEW DRIP DESIGN WAS PREPARED TO BRING THE DRIP ABOVE GROUND SO IT CAN BE MONITORED MORE CLOSELY.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: CONTROLLERS DID NOT RECEIVE ANY ALERTS OR ALARMS DUE TO THE POWER LOSS.
NARRATIVE: AT 20:40 ON SATURDAY OCTOBER 01, 2022, NORTHERN RECEIVED A CALL FROM THE OPERATIONS COMMUNICATION CENTER (OCC) STATING THAT A PASSERBY HEARD A LOUD NOISE FROM CHANCELLOR ETHANOL TBS. THE LOCAL TECHNICIAN WAS SENT TO THE STATION TO INVESTIGATE. THE TECHNICIAN CONTACTED GAS CONTROL AND DISCOVERED THAT NO ALARMS WERE REPORTING, AND SCADA WAS NOT COMMUNICATING.  NORTHWESTERN (THE LDC) WAS ONSITE AND REPORTED THAT GAS WAS BLOWING.   NORTHERN'S TECHNICIAN ARRIVED ON SCENE AT APPROXIMATELY 21:15 AND FOUND THE RELIEF VALVE RELIVING STACKS HALF OPEN. THE TECHNICIAN CHECKED PRESSURES FROM THE ELECTRONIC FLOW MEASUREMENT (EFM) AND NOTICED THAT NO DISPLAYS WERE WORKING. HE ALSO NOTICED THE EFM WAS NOT BEING POWERED. THE TECHNICIAN CORRECTED THE RELIEF VENTING BY LOWERING THE SET POINT OF THE SECONDARY PNEUMATIC VALVE CONTROLLER.  THE PRIMARY REGULATOR IS CONTROLLED WITH A DIGITAL VALVE CONTROLLER WHICH IS POWERED WITH 24 VOLTS. WHEN THE POWER WAS LOST TO THE DIGITAL VALVE CONTROLLER, THE REGULATOR FAILED OPEN CAUSING THE RELIEF TO VENT DUE TO THE SECONDARY REGULATOR BEING SET SLIGHTLY HIGHER THAN THE RELIEF. IF THE RELIEF WAS SET HIGHER THAN THE SECONDARY REGULATORS, THE RELIEF MAY NOT HAVE OPENED.  ON OCTOBER 3, 2022, FURTHER INVESTIGATION WAS CONDUCTED CONCERNING THE POWER FAILURE TO THE STATION. IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE SOLAR PANELS WERE NOT WIRED CORRECTLY TO CHARGE THE BATTERIES. THE BATTERIES WERE ONLY GETTING 19 VOLTS TO CHARGE 24-VOLT BATTERIES. THE BATTERIES WERE FULLY CHARGED WHEN THE STATION WAS PUT INTO SERVICE ON SEPTEMBER 28, 2022. THE BATTERIES SLOWLY DEPLETED FROM NOT BEING CHARGED CORRECTLY. UPON FURTHER REVIEW OF THE REGULATOR, THIS MODEL REGULATOR HAS A LOW VOLTAGE PROTECT FUNCTION THAT DISCONNECTS ALL THE LOAD ONCE THE BATTERIES REACH 23 VOLTS. NORTHERN NATURAL GAS SETS A LOW VOLTAGE ALARM AT 22 VOLTS. DUE TO THE VOLTAGE REGULATOR DISCONNECTING POWER AT 23 VOLTS, NO ALARMS WERE RECEIVED, NOR COULD ANY OTHER MONITORING BE PERFORMED REMOTELY.

QUALIFIED_INDIVIDUALS: YES, THEY WERE QUALIFIED FOR THE TASK(S)
NARRATIVE: ON 29 SEPTEMBER 2022, TC ENERGY TECHNICIANS WERE PERFORMING A CORRECTIVE WORK ORDER TO REPLACE THE HYDRAULIC FLUID IN THE 501-45A-34 VALVE. WHILE PERFORMING THE WORK, AN INCIDENT OCCURRED RESULTING IN INJURIES AND A RELEASE OF GAS. THE INCIDENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER, #1348627. THE ANR 2-501 PIPELINE MP582.6 TO MP585.1 - 2-501 LOOP SOUTH END TO MAINLINE VALVE 46 (ISOLATED SEGMENT), HAS BEEN BLOWN DOWN AND ISOLATED. IN ADDITION, TC ENERGY HAS SUSPENDED QUALIFICATIONS ON COVERED TASKS ASSOCIATED WITH THE WORK BEING PERFORMED ASSOCIATED WITH THE INCIDENT AND CONDUCTED DRUG TESTING.    THE PIPELINE SEGMENT REMAINED OUT OF SERVICE UNTIL REPAIR OF THE PIPELINE WAS COMPLETED.  REPAIR TO THE PIPELINE INCLUDED THE REPLACEMENT OF THE VALVE AND VALVE OPERATOR.  THE PIPELINE WAS RESTARTED AND RETURNED TO FULL SERVICE ON JANUARY 13, 2023 AT 11:39 CST.    THE PRIMARY CAUSE OF THE FAILURE HAS BEEN DETERMINED TO BE THE RESULT OF THE TECHNICIAN FAILING TO CONFIRM MANUFACTURER'S REPAIR PROCEDURE AND OPENING THE VALVE DURING CORRECTIVE MAINTENANCE TO REPLACE THE HYDRAULIC HAND PUMP.  FOLLOWING THE REPAIR, WHEN THE VALVE WAS ACTUATED USING THE HAND PUMP, HYDRAULIC OIL BEGAN TO MOVE TO THE OPEN SIDE OF THE VALVE STEM AND THE VALVE GATE DISENGAGED AND MOVED IN AN UNCONTROLLED MANNER.  THE RESULT OF THIS ACTION CAUSED A FAILURE IN THE VALVE ACTUATOR CONNECTION AND A RELEASE OF PIPELINE FLUIDS AND HYDRAULIC OIL TO THE SURROUNDING AREA.
