NARRATIVE: ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 14TH THE ANNUAL ESD TEST WAS PERFORMED AT COMPRESSOR STATION 430.  ONCE COMPLETED THE TWO TECHNICIANS WERE PUTTING THE STATION BACK IN SERVICE AND WERE REPRESSURIZING THE SUCTION AND DISCHARGE HEADERS UTILIZING THE 4" LOADING LINES AROUND THE STATION SIDE GATES. TECHNICIAN 1 WAS TAKING CARE OF THE SUCTION HEADER, AND TECHNICIAN 2 WAS TAKING CARE OF THE DISCHARGE HEADER.  ONCE THE VALVES WERE PARTIALLY OPENED, AND THE LINES WERE BEING PRESSURED UP TECHNICIAN 1  NOTICED A WASP NEST NEAR HIM AND RETURNED TO HIS VEHICLE TO GRAB A CAN OF WASP SPRAY.  HE SPRAYED DOWN THE NEST AND RETURNED THE CAN TO HIS TRUCK.  HE THEN PROCEEDED TO TECHNICIAN 2 TO DISCUSS THE NEXT STEPS IN THE PROCEDURE.  THE LINES CONTINUED TO PACK UP TO LINE PRESSURE.  TECHNICIAN 1 FAILED TO RETURN TO AND CLOSE THE 4" LOADING LINE ON THE SUCTION HEADER.  BOTH TECHNICIANS CONTINUED AND BROUGHT THE STATION BACK INTO SERVICE.  ON SATURDAY, SEPTEMBER 17TH A THUNDERSTORM ROLLED THROUGH THE AREA, AND A LIGHTNING STRIKE CAUSED A STATION EBD.  GAS CONTROL CALLED TECHNICIAN 1 WHO WAS WORKING ON EQUIPMENT AT ANOTHER STATION WHEN PIPELINE CONTROL CALLED HIM TO MAKE HIM AWARE OF THE BLOWDOWN EVENT. THE STATION WAS NOT ONLINE AT THE TIME, SO THE EMPLOYEE INFORMED GAS CONTROL HE WOULD SWING BY ON HIS WAY HOME.  THE EMPLOYEE WRAPPED UP WHAT HE WAS DOING AND LOGGED IN REMOTELY TO THE STATION HMI AND NOTICED ONLY THE SUCTION HEADER HAD BLOWN DOWN, BUT THE TRANSMITTER WAS STILL SHOWING 300 PSI.  TECHNICIAN 1 RECOGNIZED THIS WAS ABNORMAL AND CALLED THE MANAGER OF OPERATIONS.  THE MANAGER OF OPERATIONS LOGGED INTO THE SECURITY CAMERAS AND WAS ABLE TO DETERMINE THAT GAS WAS STILL BLOWING FROM THE SUCTION LINE BLOWDOWN VENT.  THIS WAS APPROXIMATELY 30 MINUTES AFTER THE INITIAL CALL THAT THE STATION HAD BLOWN DOWN.  TECHNICIAN 1 STARTED HEADING TO THE STATION BUT WAS AN HOUR AWAY, MANAGER OF OPERATIONS CALLED TECHNICIAN 2, AND HE ALSO STARTED HEADING TO THE STATION.  BOTH TECHS ARRIVED AT STATION 430C AT APPROXIMATELY THE SAME TIME AND FOUND THE 4" SUCTION LOADING VALVE PARTIALLY OPEN FROM THE ESD TEST THAT WAS PERFORMED ON WEDNESDAY.  GAS VENTED FOR 90 MINUTES AT 300PSI FOR A TOTAL OF 8.145MMSCF  THE LIGHTNING STRIKE HAD BLOWN 2 FUSES AND BURNED UP THE SOLENOID ON THE SUCTION BLOW-DOWN VALVE, WHICH IS WHY IT ACTUATED AND THE DISCHARGE BLOWDOWN DID NOT.  EMPLOYEES WERE NOT ABLE TO MAKE REPAIRS THAT EVENING AS THE THUNDERSTORM CONTINUED WITH HEAVY RAIN AND LIGHTNING.  EMPLOYEES RETURNED THE NEXT MORNING AND MADE REPAIRS AND PUT THE STATION BACK IN SERVICE.   AN INVESTIGATION WILL BE CONDUCTED AND MOVING FORWARD THE LOADING VALVES ON THE STATION SUCTION AND DISCHARGE WILL BE ADDED TO THE SAFETY CRITICAL DEVICE LIST AND WILL HAVE CAR SEALS INSTALLED THAT WILL REQUIRE REMOVAL AND INSTALLATION ANYTIME THE VALVES ARE OPERATED IN ORDER TO CONFIRM THEY ARE LEFT IN THE CORRECT POSITION.

NARRATIVE: DUE TO CHARACTER LIMITS, SEE ORIGINAL REPORT 20220107 FOR INCIDENT DESCRIPTION.    *UPDATE 10/31/22* THE LAB COMPANY (STRESS ENGINEERING) THAT THE SAMPLES WERE SENT TO IS CURRENTLY PREFORMING METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS ON BOTH SAMPLES THAT THIRD COAST SENT. THE CHIEF INCIDENT INVESTIGATOR THAT THIRD COAST CONTRACTED TO INVESTIGATE THE LAKE LERY INCIDENT IS STILL CONDUCTING THE INVESTIGATION AND INTERVIEWING WITNESSES. MEETINGS WITH THIRD COAST, THE INVESTIGATOR AND THE US COAST GUARD HAS PRODUCED A MARINE CASUALTY INVESTIGATION BY THE COAST GUARD. REPAIRS TO THE 20' ARE UNDER WAY AND ARE NEAR COMPLETION INCLUDING BACKFILLING THE HOLE THAT WAS CREATED. TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE THE PIPELINE WILL BURIED, HOLE BACK FILLED AND 2 LEVELS OF ARTICULATING CONCRETE MATES WILL BE PLACED ON TOP ALONG THIS SECTION OF PIPE.    *UPDATE 01/05/23* THE 20' GATE 6 TO TOCA LINE HAS BEEN DEWATERED, CUT, AIR GAPPED, AND CAPPED FROM ANY PRESSURE SOURCE AND PLACED UNDER A NITROGEN BLANKET.     *UPDATE 02/7/23* STRESS ENGINEERING (SES) PERFORMED SEVERAL EXAMINATIONS OF THE PIPE SAMPLES SENT FOR ANALYSIS INCLUDING LASER SCANNING, VISUAL EXAMINATION, SCANNING ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (SEM), ENERGY DISPERSIVE X-RAY SPECTROSCOPY (EDS), X-RAY DIFFRACTION (XRD), CHEMICAL ANALYSIS, MECHANICAL TESTING AND METALLOGRAPHIC EXAMINATION. THE TWO TRANSVERSE FRACTURES ON THE TWO PIPE SEGMENTS (NORTH AND SOUTH) ORIGINATED AT THE 6 O'CLOCK POSITION. THE TWO HELICAL, CIRCUMFERENTIAL FRACTURES LIKELY OCCURRED FOLLOWING A LONGITUDINAL RUPTURE ALONG THE BOTTOM OF THE 13-FEET LENGTH PIPE SEGMENT(S) THAT WAS/WERE DISPLACED AND NEVER RECOVERED. PITTING CORROSION WAS FOUND ON THE INSIDE SURFACE OF THE TWO PIPE SEGMENTS ANALYZED BY SES. THE PITS WERE FOUND TO BE AS DEEP AS 50% OF THE WALL THICKNESS IN THE NORTH SECTION. THE INSIDE SURFACE SCALE CONTAINED IRON CARBONATE AND IRON SULFIDE, WHILE THE DEPOSITS FROM THE PITS CONTAINED IRON CARBONATE AND IRON OXIDE ' WHICH ARE TYPICAL OILFIELD CORROSION PRODUCTS FROM ACID GASES. THE TENSILE PROPERTIES MET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR GRADE X52 STEEL PER API 5LX (1953) SPECIFICATIONS. THE MICROSTRUCTURE OF THE PIPE BASE MATERIAL WAS TYPICAL OF GRADE X52 CARBON STEEL. WITHOUT THE MISSING PIPE SEGMENT(S) AND THE ABILITY TO EXAMINE IT/THEM, SES CANNOT CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF THE RUPTURE, NOR CAN IT RULE OUT CERTAIN POSSIBLE CAUSES.    MULTIPLE ATTEMPTS WERE CONDUCTED USING A COMBINATION OF DIVERS AND HYDROGRAPHIC/MAGNETOMETER UP TO 1 MILE FROM RELEASE POINT; LOCATING/MARKING AND INVESTIGATING BY DIVER HEAVY METAL DEBRIS TARGETS. THE 13-FOOT PIECE WAS NEVER FOUND AND RECOVERED.

NARRATIVE: 8.31.22 WHILE CUTTING GRASS ON A TRACTOR, A LANDOWNER RAN OVER A 1" TAP VALVE THAT IS SUSPECTED TO HAVE BECOME EXPOSED DUE TO THE RECENT RAINFALL AMOUNTS IN THE AREA. THE TRACTOR CAUSED DAMAGE TO A PUNCHING TEE BELOW GROUND BY OPENING A 1/8" HOLE IN THE THREADS OF THE TEE. THE LANDOWNER THEN CALLED ET SYSTEM CONTROL AND NOTIFIED THEM OF THE SITUATION. AT 1630 SYSTEM CONTROL NOTIFIED MEMBERS OF THE EMMET TEAM AND THEY CONFIRMED THE DAMAGE AND LEAK AT 1727 OF THE SAME DAY. THE DECISION WAS MADE TO LOWER THE LINE PRESSURE FROM 350 TO 200PSI AND MONITOR UNTIL REPAIRS COULD BEGIN ON 9.1.22, DUE TO THE NEED TO MAINTAIN DELIVERY TO A CUSTOMER. THE INITIAL BELIEF WAS WE WOULD NOT EXCEED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD, AS WE BELIEVED WE COULD EXPOSE THE VALVE AND SHUT THE FLOW OFF. FURTHER INVESTIGATION OF THE DAMAGED FITTING REVEALED BROKEN THREADS BELOW THE VALVE. ONCE WE DISCOVERED THIS WE MADE THE REPORT, AS THE REPORTING CRITERIA WOULD BE MET BEFORE THE REPAIRS COULD BE COMPLETED. REPAIRS BEGAN AT APPROXIMATELY 1730 ON 9.1.22 AND CONTINUED INTO 9.2.22. THE LEAK WAS TERMINATED AT 0300 ON 9.2.22. ET EMPLOYEES AND CONTRACTORS INSTALLED A THREE-WAY STOPPLE FITTING AND REPLACED A 5' SECTION OF THE PIPE TO COMPLETE THE REPAIR. THE DAMAGED COMPONENT WAS REMOVED FROM THE SYSTEM. THERE WERE NO INJURIES OR INTERRUPTION TO CUSTOMERS AS A RESULT OF THIS EVENT.

NARRATIVE: A EMERGENCY LOCATE REQUEST WAS SENT IN FROM SUMMIT UTILITIES AT APPROXIMATELY (15:45) ON AUGUST 16, 2022. OUR OPERATIONS TECHNICIAN RESPONDED TO THE SCENE AT (16:15) AND DISCOVERED A GAS READING OF 3% LEL IN THE ATMOSPHERE....MORE TECHNICIANS ARRIVED AND BEGAN BAR HOLE TESTING WHICH REVEALED READINGS AS HIGH AS 50% GAS. THE PIPELINE (BM-21) IS IN THE VICINITY OF 2 DUPLEXES AND RUNS UNDER 2 CONCRETE DRIVEWAYS TO THESE DUPLEXES; THE SUSPECTED LEAK WAS UNDER ONE OF THESE CONCRETE DRIVEWAYS. THE DECISION WAS MADE TO EVACUATE THE RESIDENTS OF THE 2 DUPLEXES AS A PRECAUTION; THIS DECISION WAS MADE AT APPROXIMATELY 18:40 ON AUGUST 16, 2022.   AFTER EVACUATING THE RESIDENTS, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL FORMED A PERIMETER AND MONITORED GAS LEVELS WHILE WAITING FOR EQUIPMENT TO ARRIVE TO ISOLATE AND BLOW DOWN THE AFFECTED PIPELINE SEGMENT. OPERATIONS ISOLATED 8,661' OF PIPE BY PLACING A STOPPLE PLUG IN A STOPPLE FITTING LOCATED AT LINE PLUS 540+95 AND BY CLOSING BLOCK VALVE 43120 AT GREENBRIER TOWN BOARDER STATION LOCATED AT LINE PLUS 627+56; THE LINE WAS BLOWN FLAT AT 22:50 ON AUGUST 16, 2022.   CONTRACTORS MOBILIZED TO THE SITE ON THE MORNING OF AUGUST 17, 2022....AFTER HEAVY RAIN MOVED OUT OF THE AREA, THE CONTRACTORS BEGAN REMOVING BOTH CONCRETE DRIVEWAYS. THE PIPE WAS UNCOVERED ON THE AFTERNOON (AUGUST 17TH) AND A SMALL PINHOLE LEAK WAS DISCOVERED IN A GIRTH WELD. THE PIPE WAS CUTOUT AND 44' OF 0.237: WT, 4.5" DIA, X52 GRADE PIPE WAS INSTALLED IN ITS PLACE.  THE PIPE WAS PURGED AND LOADED AND THEN BROUGHT BACK INTO SERVICE ON THE MORNING OF AUGUST 18TH, 2022.

NARRATIVE: AT APPROXIMATELY 1:40 A.M. ON AUGUST 14TH, 2022, GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A LOW FLOW ALERT FOR EQUITRANS' ZINK COMPRESSOR STATION. GAS CONTROL THEN NOTICED AN APPROXIMATE 250 PSIG DROP IN DISCHARGE PRESSURE ON COMPRESSOR #5 AT THE STATION. THE ON CALL OPERATION EMPLOYEE WAS DISPATCHED AND FOUND A RELIEF VALVE WAS VENTING AT THE STATION. THE RELIEF VALVE CEASED VENTING AT APPROXIMATELY 3:15 A.M. PRESSURE AT THE RELIEF VALVE AT THE TIME OF THE FAILURE WAS APPROXIMATELY THE STATION DISCHARGE PRESSURE OF 1521 PSIG. ONCE THE RELIEF VALVE BEGAN VENTING, THE PRESSURE AT THE RELIEF VALVE DROPPED TO THE STATION SUCTION PRESSURE OF APPROXIMATELY 1040 PSIG. A CALCULATION WAS PERFORMED AND IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE GAS LOSS EXCEEDED THE 3 MILLION CUBIC FOOT THRESHOLD FOR REPORTING AS PER CFR49 PART 191.3. AFTER FURTHER REVIEW, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT AN O-RING FAILED IN THE VALVE SEAT WHICH IS COMMONLY CAUSED BY VIBRATION.     THE INITIAL INTERPRETATION WAS THAT A 48 NOTICE WAS REQUIRED ONLY WHEN CHANGES TO REPORTED DATA WERE KNOWN, BUT IT HAS SINCE BEEN UNDERSTOOD THAT A 48 HOUR NOTICE IS REQUIRED ON ALL INCIDENTS EVEN IF IT JUST CONFIRMS THE INITIAL REPORTED DATA.

NARRATIVE: ON 8/11/22, AN INCIDENT OCCURRED ON THE MCMC 1204 PIPELINE. OPERATIONS RESPONDED AND DISCOVERED A PIPELINE RUPTURE APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES SOUTHWEST OF KINGMAN, KS. THE PIPELINE WAS ISOLATED, BLOWN DOWN, AND DAMAGED SECTION OF PIPE REMOVED FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS. GAS LOSS WAS CALCULATED AT 4.11 MMCF.     UPDATE:   THE THIRD PARTY FAILURE ANALYSIS REPORT ON THE RUPTURED PIPE SEGMENT INDICATED WITHIN THE PIPE BODY MATERIAL, THERE WERE INTERNAL DEFECTS AND AREAS WITHIN THE MICROSTRUCTURE OF LOW MATERIAL PROPERTIES.  THESE WERE MOST LIKELY DUE TO VINTAGE STEEL MAKING PROCESSES AND PIPE MANUFACTURING METHODS (ESTIMATE PIPE MANUFACTURED IN THE 1940S).  THE DEFECTS AND CRACKS SEEN TODAY PROBABLY EITHER INITIATED AND / OR BECAME BIGGER DURING THE PIPE'S CRUDE OIL SERVICE PRIOR TO 1955 IN UNKNOWN OPERATING CONDITIONS.  THESE INTERNAL DEFECTS, WHILE NOT AN ISSUE AT THE START OF THE PIPELINE NATURAL GAS SERVICE IN 1955, WERE EITHER ALREADY INTERNAL CRACKS OR WERE INTERNAL CRACK INITIATION SITES.  OVER THE COURSE OF ALMOST SEVENTY YEARS, THE NORMAL FLUCTUATION IN PIPELINE PRESSURES GREW AND / OR INITIATED INTERNAL CRACKS TO THE POINT THAT THE EFFECTIVE PIPE WALL THICKNESS WAS NOT ENOUGH TO CONTAIN NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURES.  THIS LED TO THE PIPE WALL BULGE THAT CREATED CRACK INITIATION SITES ON THE INTERNAL PIPE SURFACE THAT BECAME CRACKS PROPAGATED BY FATIGUE AND EVENTUALLY LED TO THE PIPE SEGMENT RUPTURE.  THE LAP WELD SEAM DID NOT APPEAR TO BE A FACTOR IN THIS INCIDENT.   SUBMITTED BY BRENT THEEDE 3/15/2023.

AT 00:51, ENTERPRISE ISOLATED AND BLEW DOWN THE STATION BY PUSHING THE ESD BUTTON. THE GLASSCOCK POLICE DEPARTMENT AND THE STANTON VOLUNTEER FIRE DEPARTMENT ARRIVED AT THE STATION ON 00:49 AND 01:30, RESPECTIVELY. AT 02:25, ENTERPRISE DETERMINED THAT THE EVENT WOULD MEET THE DEFINITION OF AN INCIDENT BASED ON ANTICIPATED COSTS OF THE UNIT 1 FIRE. UNITS 1 AND 2 ARE LEASED BY ENTERPRISE FROM ARCHROCK AND THE COSTS TO REPAIR OR REPLACE THE UNITS ARE NOT AVAILABLE TO ENTERPRISE.  THE NRC AND TXRRC WERE NOTIFIED OF THE EVENT BY ENTERPRISE AT 02:47 AND 02:52, RESPECTIVELY. ON 8-6-2022 AT 06:45, THE FIRE SELF-EXTINGUISHED.  UNIT 2, WHICH IS ADJACENT TO UNIT 1, WAS DAMAGED BY THE FIRE; HOWEVER, NO STATION PIPING WAS INVOLVED IN THE FIRE. MACK STATION WAS OUT OF SERVICE FROM 8-6-2022 TO 8-28-2022. UNIT 1 WAS REMOVED FROM MACK STATION AND A REPLACEMENT FOR UNIT 1 WAS PLACED INTO SERVICE ON 8-29-2022. UNIT 2 WAS REPAIRED AND PLACED INTO SERVICE ON 9-23-2022.      AN INVESTIGATION OF THE INCIDENT DETERMINED THAT AN ABNORMAL VIBRATION FROM THE SECOND STAGE DISCHARGE RECEIVER CAUSED BOLTS ON THE PIPE FLANGES TO LOOSEN WHICH CAUSED A LOSS OF CONTAINMENT. MISC_DETAILS: THE INVESTIGATION TEAM CONCLUDED THAT THERE WAS LIKELY DEBRIS THAT FOUND THEIR WAY INTO THE SEAT OF THE VALVE. THIS DEBRIS COULD'VE ORIGINATED FROM HYDROTEST PROJECTS THAT HAD BEEN TAKING PLACE NEAR THE COMPRESSOR STATION EARLIER THIS YEAR. THIS REMAINS THE MOST LIKELY CAUSE, AND IS STILL SEEN AS A "HYPOTHETICAL" CAUSE AS NO DEBRIS WERE FOUND AFTER FURTHER INVESTIGATION.
NARRATIVE: ON JULY 28TH, 2022 THERE WAS AN IMPROPER SEALING OF A UNIT VALVE AT NORTHWEST PIPELINE WILLARD COMPRESSOR STATION. WILLIAMS OPERATIONS RECEIVED A CALL FROM SKAMANIA COUNTY 911 DISPATCH AT 22:10 LOCAL TIME REPORTING A STRONG SMELL OF GAS FROM A NEIGHBOR NEAR THE COMPRESSOR STATION. WILLIAMS OPERATIONS PERSONNEL IMMEDIATELY RESPONDED TO THE CALL AND ARRIVED ON-SITE AT 23:50 LOCAL TIME TO ASSESS THE SITUATION.     WHILE ON-SITE IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT THE VENT VALVE ON UNIT ONE AT THE COMPRESSOR STATION WAS SHOWING CLOSED ON THE HMI BUT WAS STILL VENTING GAS. IN AN EFFORT TO STOP THE GAS FROM VENTING AS QUICKLY AS POSSIBLE OPERATIONS TECHNICIANS AT THE SITE CONSULTED WITH PIPELINE CONTROL AND THE OPERATIONS SUPERVISOR AND AGREED TO ISSUE A START COMMAND TO UNIT ONE. ISSUING THIS START COMMAND WAS FOUND TO CAUSE ALL VALVES TO POSITION THEMSELVES CORRECTLY, THUS STOPPING THE GAS FROM VENTING TO THE ATMOSPHERE. SHORTLY AFTER, A STOP COMMAND WAS SUBSEQUENTLY ISSUED TO THE SAME UNIT, WITH OPERATIONS NOTING THAT ALL VALVES ALSO CORRECTLY POSITIONED THEMSELVES AGAIN. IT WAS NOTED BY PIPELINE CONTROL THAT THIS UNIT WOULD NOT BE NEEDED FOR THE REST OF THE NIGHT, SO UNIT ONE WAS LEFT OFF AND UNAVAILABLE UNTIL THE NEXT DAY.     ONCE THE UNIT HAD BEEN SHUT DOWN AND NO ADDITIONAL VENTING WAS CONFIRMED, OPERATIONS CONTACTED THE SECURITY OPERATIONS CENTER (SOC) AT 01:40 LOCAL TIME. AT THIS TIME, THE SOC RAN GAS LOSS ESTIMATION CALCULATIONS AND WAS ABLE TO CONFIRM DISCOVERY THAT THE 3MMSCF RELEASE THRESHOLD WAS REACHED. SHORTLY AFTER, AT 01:52, AFTER A CONVERSATION BETWEEN THE SOC AND PIPELINE SAFETY, IT WAS DETERMINED TO REPORT THE INCIDENT TO THE NRC. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL THAT RESPONDED TO THE INCIDENT WENT HOME TO REST BEFORE ARRIVING ON-SITE THE NEXT MORNING TO CONTINUE THE TROUBLESHOOTING PROCESS.     THE FOLLOWING MORNING OPERATIONS AND TECH SERVICES EMPLOYEES WORKED TO TROUBLESHOOT THE VALVE POSITION INDICATORS AND WERE NOT ABLE TO RECREATE THE PROBLEM THAT CAUSED UNIT ONE SUCTION VALVE TO REMAIN CRACKED DURING A REMOTE STOP. TO BETTER ALERT OPERATION PERSONNEL OF A SIMILAR INCIDENT OCCURRING, PIPELINE CONTROL PLACED A TEMPORARY PRESSURE ALARM TO MONITOR CASE PRESSURE. OVER THE COURSE OF THE FOLLOWING WEEKS, OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ATTEMPTED TO RECREATE THE ISSUE TO BETTER UNDERSTAND WHAT CAUSED THE INITIAL INCIDENT.     MULTIPLE EFFORTS TO RECREATE THE ISSUE WERE UNSUCCESSFUL AND A FULL ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION HAS SINCE TAKEN PLACE. THE FINDINGS OF THE INVESTIGATION REVEALED THAT THE UNIT 1 BLOWDOWN VALVE HAD BEEN LEAKING 12 HOURS PRIOR TO PIPELINE CONTROL RECEIVING NOTIFICATION FROM THE EMERGENCY DISPATCH. THE INVESTIGATION INTO WHY THE BLOWDOWN VALVE FAILED TO SEAL FOUND THAT THERE WAS LIKELY SOME DEBRIS IN THE SEAT OF THE UNIT VALVE THAT CAUSED IT TO NOT SEAL PROPERLY. IT'S BELIEVED THAT THESE DEBRIS MAY HAVE ORIGINATED FROM PIPELINE CLEANING / PIGGING EFFORTS AS PART OF A SERIES OF HYDROTEST PROJECTS THAT HAD RECENTLY OCCURRED IN THE AREA. IT WAS ALSO FOUND THAT POWER TURBINE PERCENT SPEED AND SUCTION/DISCHARGE PRESSURES WERE NOT UTILIZED TO SHOW INDICATION OF A POTENTIAL ISSUE WITH THE COMPRESSOR. THESE ITEMS WEREN'T SPECIFICALLY REVIEWED AT THE TIME AS THERE WERE NO ALARMS GIVEN TO PIPELINE CONTROL. TO PREVENT THIS FROM HAPPENING IN THE FUTURE, WILLIAMS IS IMPLEMENTING A CONTINUOUS VENTING UNIT ALARM WITHIN SCADA, STATION, AND UNIT CONTROL SYSTEMS.

NARRATIVE: 7/23/22, SEA ROBIN GAS CONTROL RECEIVED NOTIFICATION OF A SHEEN IN THE EI 330 BLOCK AREA. THE THIRD-PARTY PRODUCER WHO OBSERVED THE SHEEN NOTIFIED THE NRC AND THOSE WHO HAVE LINES IN THE AREA.    7/24/22, ET MADE AN OVERFLIGHT TO CONFIRM SHEEN. FINDINGS WERE REPORTED TO THE NRC AT 17:36, NRC#1342409.    7/25/22, THE PRODUCER WHO ORIGINALLY SPOTTED THE SHEEN HAS A DSV ON CONTRACT AND ARE IN THE PROCESS OF INVESTIGATING THE SOURCE. THEY ARE JUST ARRIVING IN THE FIELD BUT WILL UPDATE US AS THEY DETERMINE OWNERSHIP.    7/26/22, THE DSV SHOWS THE LEAK SOURCE IS AT A CROSSING BETWEEN THE SEA ROBIN WEST 8-INCH LINE AND A THIRD PARTY 6-INCH LINE. DUE TO THE LIMITATIONS OF THE DSV THAT RESPONDED, THEY WERE UNABLE TO SAFELY ACCESS THE CROSSING TO VERIFY WHICH LINE IS AT FAULT. THE SEA ROBIN WEST 8-INCH LINE IS CONNECTED TO THE SEA ROBIN WEST 24-INCH, WHICH IS CURRENTLY IN THE PROCESS OF DEPRESSURIZATION DUE TO THE LEAKING FITTING IN SMI128. THAT DEPRESSURIZATION WORK WILL CONTINUE AS WILL OUR ATTEMPTS TO LOCATE A DSV WITH THE NEEDED CAPABILITIES. THE NRC 48-HOUR UPDATE WAS MADE, AND THE REPORT WAS UPDATED WITH OVERFLIGHT FINDINGS. NRC#1342624.    8/19/2022, WE HAVE BEEN UNABLE TO SOURCE A DSV TO RESPOND TO OUR INCIDENTS. THE RESPONSIBLE PARTY FOR THIS RELEASE IS YET TO BE DETERMINED.    8/21/2022, ENERGY TRANSFER PLACED A CALL TO THE NRC TO TAKE OWNERSHIP OF THE RELEASE AND UPDATE RESPONSIBLE PARTY TO SEA ROBIN PIPELINE. NRC# 1345136. CONDENSATE FOUND LEAKING FROM A PINHOLE LEAK AT THE 5 O'CLOCK POSITION.     8/22/2022, THE REPAIR CLAMP HAS BEEN INSTALLED. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL BEGINNING THE PRESSURIZATION OF PIPELINE TO PERFORM LEAK CHECKS AT MULTIPLE STAGES. NO LEAKS WERE OBSERVED BY THE DIVERS OR OVERFLIGHT PERSONNEL THROUGHOUT EACH INCREASE IN PRESSURE POINTS.     8/23/22, THE LINE WAS BACK TO FULL PRESSURE (EQUALIZED) AT 16:39.

NARRATIVE: ON 7/22/22 AT APPROXIMATELY 10:10, A PRODUCER CONTACTED ENERGY TRANSFER OPERATIONS PERSONNEL TO REPORT A LOSS OF PRESSURE AND WELL SHUT IN BIENVILLE PARRISH. THE 2 WELLS REPORTED TO BE SHUT IN WERE BOTH INLETS INTO THE ENERGY TRANSFER PIPELINE REFERENCED AS 20101088 TRISTATE 9047 CHK WEYERHAEUSER 15-1. OPERATIONS PERSONNEL WERE MOBILIZED AND ENROUTE TO SHUT MANUAL VALVES TO ISOLATE THE SEGMENT OF PIPELINE. THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVES WERE SHUT IN AND ENERGY TRANSFER PERSONNEL LOCATED A PIPELINE RUPTURE ON THE WEYERHAEUSER 15-1. ONCE ISOLATED, THE PIPELINE WAS ALLOWED TO BLOWDOWN AT THE RUPTURE SITE. A SEGMENT OF PIPE WAS LOCATED OUTSIDE OF THE RUPTURE AREA AND WAS PRESERVED TO SEND FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS.    REPORTS WERE MADE TO THE LA STATE POLICE ON 722/22 AT 10:22 AND LA DNR ON 7/22/22 AT 11:46. NRC NOTIFICATIONS WERE MADE (1342244 & 48-HR FOLLOW UP 1342376).    METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS IS BEING COMPLETED ON THE SAMPLE RECOVERED FROM THE RUPTURE SITE.  THE SEGMENT OF PIPE AFFECTED HAS BEEN REPLACED AND WAS PLACED BACK IN SERVICE ON 8/5/22 AT 18:00.    METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS INDICATED INTERNAL ENVIRONMENTAL CRACKING AS THE CAUSE LIKELY DUE TO INTERACTION BETWEEN CORROSION INHIBITOR AND COMPONENTS OF THE GAS STREAM.     SINCE THE TIME OF THE FAILURE THE PIPELINE WAS SUBJECTED TO A HYDROSTATIC PRESSURE STRENGTH AND SPIKE TEST.

CORROSION_LOCATION_DETAILS: 6 O'CLOCK POSITION ON THE PIPE. THIS SEGMENT WAS ON A DOWNHILL SLOPE OF THE RIGHT-OF-WAY.
NARRATIVE: DURING THE MORNING OF THE INCIDENT, THUNDERSTORMS WERE MOVING THROUGH THE AREA AND THE DOWNSTREAM AMINE TREATMENT PLANT WAS STRUCK BY LIGHTING AND LOST POWER SUBSEQUENTLY SHUTTING IN AT AROUND 08:30. AT 09:00, OPERATIONS RECEIVED A NOTICE FROM EMERGENCY RESPONDERS THAT A WELL WAS BLOWING OUT OR RELIEVING NEAR HWY 4.  SUPERVISORS AND OPERATORS BEGAN TO MAKE THEIR WAY AROUND TO THAT SIDE OF THE FIELD TO INVESTIGATE BUT TOOK EXTRA TIME DUE TO TREES BEING ACROSS THE ROAD. SOON THEREAFTER IT WAS DETERMINED THAT A WELL WAS NOT BLOWING OUT, BUT A PIPELINE HAD RUPTURED IN THAT AREA.  THE EXACT LOCATION OF RUPTURE WAS UNKNOWN AT THE TIME, BUT THE PIPELINE WAS IDENTIFIED AND OPERATIONS BEGIN ISOLATING THE WELLS AND THE PIPELINE BETWEEN 09:20 AND 09:25.  LOCAL EMERGENCY RESPONDERS EVACUATED 2 HOMES AS A PRECAUTION AND SHUT DOWN HWY 4 ONCE THE LEAK LOCATION WAS IDENTIFIED. NO INJURIES OR DAMAGE TO PROPERTY OCCURRED.  THE EXACT LOCATION OF THE FAILURE WAS CONFIRMED AROUND 09:30 AND THE PIPELINE WAS COMPLETELY ISOLATED BY 09:40.  NRC WAS NOTIFIED AT 10:30, BUT THE CALLER SAT ON HOLD FOR 20 MINUTES AND DID NOT SPEAK TO AN OPERATOR TILL AROUND 10:50.  FURTHER EVALUATION OF THE RUPTURE IDENTIFIED INTERNAL CORROSION WITH PITTING.  DAMAGED SECTION OF PIPE WAS CUT BACK TO CLEAN PIPE AND SENT OFF TO BE EVALUATED WHICH WAS DETERMINED TO BE MICROBIAL INDUCED CORROSION.

NARRATIVE: COLUMBIA GULF TRANSMISSION, LLC OPERATES AND MAINTAINS CLEMENTSVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION LOCATED IN LIBERTY, KENTUCKY. ON THURSDAY JULY 14TH, 2022 THE MONITORING CENTER CALLED OUT STATION PERSONNEL TO INVESTIGATE THE SHUTDOWN OF 3 COMPRESSOR UNITS AT THE STATION AFTER GAS CONTROL HAD RECEIVED UNIT FAULT INDICATIONS. STATION PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE AND DISCOVERED GAS BLOWING AT THE SIDE GATE OF THE STATION, THEY THEN CHECKED THE HMI CONTROL PANEL AND SAW THE 3 COMPRESSOR UNITS WERE STILL ONLINE AND RUNNING AND PROCEEDED TO SHUT THOSE UNITS DOWN. THE STATION PERSONNEL THEN WENT BACK TO THE HMI TO REVIEW ALARMS AND VALVE MOVEMENTS, AT THAT POINT THE BLOWDOWN HAD STOPPED.    OTHER STATION TECHNICIANS WERE CALLED IN TO ASSIST AND AFTER TROUBLESHOOTING, DISCOVERED A SHORTED CLOSED LIMIT WIRE ON VALVE ACTUATOR 206-1PV. THE SHORTED WIRE TRIPPED A 24VDC BREAKER THAT FEEDS POWER TO DISCRETE I/O MODULES, WHICH ALSO CAUSED THE COMMS TO NOT FUNCTION. AFTER THE REPAIR OF THE SHORTED WIRE ROUTINE SAFETY CHECKS WERE COMPLETED WHICH INCLUDED A CHECK OF THE ESD SYSTEM TO ENSURE SAFETY AND RELIABILITY. ALL ALARMS WERE CLEARED AT THE CONTROL PANEL, FUNCTIONAL TESTS WERE COMPLETED WITH NO NEW ERRORS GENERATED TO ENSURE NORMAL OPERATION OF THE THREE UNITS. AN INVESTIGATION IS STILL UNDERWAY INTO THE CAUSE OF THE RELEASE. THE CALCULATED VOLUME OF GAS RELEASED WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 8,072.6 MCF    THE CHRONOLOGY OF EVENTS ON JULY 14TH, 2022 ARE AS FOLLOWS (ALL TIMES WERE RECORDED IN LOCAL CENTRAL DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME):  - 3:17 AM: A  UNIT FAULT INDICATION WAS RECEIVED BY TC GAS CONTROL;  - 3:50 AM: TC OPERATIONS PERSONNEL ARRIVED AT THE SITE AND LOCAL RESPONDERS WERE AT THE SITE;  - 4:04 AM: TC REGULATORY COMPLIANCE MADE A REQUEST FOR VALVE SIZE, PRESSURE, AND TIME OF RELEASE;  - 5:31 AM: TC MONITORING CENTER WAS NOTIFIED OF TOTAL GAS LOST, WHICH AT THE TIME, WAS CALCULATED TO BE 5792.8 MCF BY TC OPERATIONS MANAGER;  - 5:35 AM: TC MONITORING CENTER NOTIFIED TC REGULATORY COMPLIANCE OF TOTAL GAS LOST;  - 5:38 AM: NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER WAS NOTIFIED OF THE INCIDENT;  - 5:45 AM: PHMSA ACCIDENT INVESTIGATION DIVISION WAS NOTIFIED OF THE INCIDENT.    THE DURATION OF THE GAS RELEASE WAS DETERMINED FROM OUR CONTROL SYSTEMS TO BE 29 MINUTES.    INSPECTIONS REVEALED THAT THE MAIN BREAKER POWERING THE REMOTE I/O PANEL THAT CONTROLS VALVE 206-1BD WAS TRIPPED (CB244). FURTHER INVESTIGATION DISCOVERED DAMAGED AND SHORTED WIRING FEEDING VALVE 206-1PV WHICH WAS CONFIRMED TO BE THE CAUSE OF BREAKER CB244 FAULTING.    NOTE REGARDING THE REVISED TIME IN QUESTION A4 ABOVE: IN RESPONSE TO THIS QUESTION THE TIME PROVIDED HERE IS AT THE DIRECTION OF PHMSA AID AND IS NOT AN ADMISSION; WE DID NOT HAVE SUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO DETERMINE WE HAD A REPORTABLE INCIDENT UNTIL 05:31.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 12, 2022, TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE (TGP) WAS NOTIFIED BY LOCAL EMS PERSONNEL THAT THERE HAD BEEN A RUPTURE IN THE VICINITY OF TGP'S 300 PIPELINE SYSTEM IN MCKEAN COUNTY, PA, WITH THE PRESENCE OF A VISIBLE FIRE.  TGP CONFIRMED THE PRESENCE OF THE RUPTURE IN THE 309 VALVE SECTION OF THE 300-1 PIPELINE AND FOUND THAT AUTO-CLOSE VALVES HAD OPERATED AS DESIGNED TO ISOLATE THE RUPTURE LOCATION.  THE FIRE WAS CONTAINED AND ALLOWED TO BURN OUT.      METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS REVEALED THAT THE LINE RUPTURED FROM NEAR-NEUTRAL-PH STRESS CORROSION CRACKING. HISTORICAL IN-LINE INSPECTION (ILI) ASSESSMENT TECHNOLOGIES AVAILABLE ON THE PIPELINE ARE NOT DESIGNED TO DETECT STRESS CORROSION CRACKING. AFTER THE INCIDENT, TGP COMPLETED AN EMAT ILI ASSESSMENT, WHICH IS DESIGNED TO DETECT CRACKING. BASED ON THE RESULTS OF THAT ASSESSMENT TGP WILL REMEDIATE ACCORDINGLY.     SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED WITH UPDATES WHEN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS AVAILABLE.

NARRATIVE: ON JULY 7, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 10:37AM, ENERGY TRANSFER GAS CONTROL OBSERVED A SUDDEN DROP IN FLOW AT THE SWEENY ENTERPRISE METER, DOWNSTREAM OF THE OLD OCEAN (SWEENY TO BURLESON COUNTY), 24-INCH PIPELINE. GAS CONTROL IMMEDIATELY INITIATED AN EMERGENCY AREA SHUTDOWN BY SHUTTING DOWN BELLVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION AND NOTIFYING OPERATIONS PERSONNEL. ET PERSONNEL WERE IMMEDIATELY DISPATCHED TO THE UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM VALVE AT 10:40AM. THE AUTOMATED UPSTREAM BLOCK VALVE WAS CLOSED AT 11:00AM AND THE DOWNSTREAM BLOCK VALVE WAS MANUALLY CLOSED AT 12:00 PM ISOLATING THE VALVE SECTION AND LETTING THE FIRE BURN OUT. AT 11:45AM, ET PERSONNEL ARRIVED ON SITE. A HALF MILE RADIUS WAS SECURED FROM THE PUBLIC WITH THE HELP OF LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT. THE FIRE WAS PUT OUT AT 12:15PM. ONCE THE AREA WAS DEEMED SAFE, REPAIRS WERE INITIATED. THE FAILED SECTION OF PIPE WAS CUT OUT AND REPLACED AND LINE WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE AS OF 8:00 PM ON JULY 9, 2022. THE CAUSE FOR THE FAILURE WAS DETERMINED TO BE EXTERNAL CORROSION DUE TO STRAY CURRENT INTERFERENCE.    TO PREVENT RE-OCCURRENCE, ET HAS REPLACED SEGMENTS OF BOTH PIPELINES, PUT IN PERMANENT ELECTRODES, TEST STATIONS, AND INSTALLED A CRITICAL BOND BETWEEN BOTH PIPELINES. THE BOND WILL BE MONITORED EVERY TWO MONTHS NOT TO EXCEED 2.5 MONTHS.     THIS PIPELINE IS A CLASS 1 TRANSMISSION PIPELINE LOCATED IN FORT BEND COUNTY NEAR WALLIS, TEXAS. THIS FACILITY IS UNDER THE JURISDICTION OF THE TEXAS RAILROAD COMMISSION. ON JULY 7, 2022, THIS INCIDENT WAS TELEPHONICALLY REPORTED TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER (NRC) AT 11:55AM. A COURTESY CALL WAS ALSO MADE TO THE TEXAS RAILROAD COMMISSION AT 12:15 PM INCIDENT #2649.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE CONTROLLER'S ACTIONS DID NOT CAUSE OR CONTRIBUTE TO THE EVENT.
OTHER_CONTROL_RELIEF_DETAILS: LOSS OF COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE STATION & ESD PLCS AND THE I/O MODULES.  FAILURE OF THE ESD I/O MODULE TO "HOLD-LAST STATE" ON THE ESD SIDE-VENT VALVES.
NARRATIVE: ON 6/25 AT 9:12AM, TRANSCOLORADO GAS TRANSMISSION COMPANY LLC'S OLATHE COMPRESSOR STATION LOCATED IN MONTROSE COUNTY, COLORADO EXPERIENCED AN ABNORMAL EVENT WHILE IN STANDBY MODE WITH NO COMPRESSION IN OPERATION AT THE TIME.   THE STATION EXPERIENCED A COMMUNICATION FAILURE BETWEEN THE STATION PLC, ESD PLC AND THE I/O MODULE CONTROLLING THE ESD VALVES.  THE I/O MODULE CONTROLLING THE ESD VALVES ALSO FAILED TO "HOLD LAST STATE" UPON LOSS OF COMMUNICATION.  THIS RESULTED IN THE STATION VENT VALVE OPENING (INSTEAD OF REMAINING CLOSED) AND AN UNPLANNED GAS RELEASE EVENT FOR 112 MINUTES. THE TOTAL VOLUME RELEASED WAS  24,659.50 MCF.    THE IMMEDIATE CAUSE OF THE EVENT WAS DETERMINED TO BE THE FAILURE OF THE I/O CONTROLLER DESIGNED TO PROVIDE A "HOLD LAST STATE" COMMAND THAT KEEPS THE STATION VALVES IN THE CURRENT STATE WHEN COMMUNICATIONS ARE LOST.  INVESTIGATION REVEALED THAT THERE WERE SHORT LOSSES OF COMMUNICATION PRIOR TO THIS EVENT, BUT THE I/O MODULE "HOLD LAST STATE" SETTING ALWAYS FUNCTIONED AS DESIGNED.  ANOTHER CONTRIBUTING CAUSE WAS IDENTIFIED TO BE LOSS OF COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE STATION & ESD PLCS AND THE I/O MODULES.  THE INTERMITTENT LOSS OF COMMUNICATION WAS CAUSED BY POOR CONNECTIONS ON THE COMMUNICATION WIRES AT THE PLCS, I/O MODULES, AND SURGE-SUPPRESSION EQUIPMENT.   ALL CONNECTIONS WITH THE COMMUNICATIONS WIRE HAVE BEEN CHECKED AND CORRECTED AS REQUIRED.   THERE HAS BEEN NO COMMUNICATIONS FAILURES SINCE.  THE I/O MODULE WAS ALSO REPLACED.

NARRATIVE: PIPELINE 1656 IS A TRANSMISSION PIPELINE THAT IS OPERATED AND MAINTAINED BY COLUMBIA GAS TRANSMISSION, LLC (COLUMBIA) WITH AN OPERATOR ID 2616. COLUMBIA HAS IDENTIFIED A LEAK ON PIPELINE 1656 UNDER A TOWNSHIP ROADWAY NEAR THE TOWN OF GREENCASTLE, FRANKLIN COUNTY, PENNSYLVANIA. PIPELINE 1656 IS A 6-INCH DIAMETER, CARBON STEEL, 0.28-INCH WALL THICKNESS, BARE STEEL PIPELINE, WHICH IS CATHODICALLY PROTECTED IN THE AREA OF THE REPORTED LEAK. LINE 1656 HAS A MAXIMUM ALLOWABLE OPERATING PRESSURE OF 175 PSIG AND ITS OPERATING PRESSURE AT THE TIME OF DISCOVERY WAS APPROXIMATELY 165 PSIG.   WHILE PERFORMING SCHEDULED CATHODIC PROTECTION SURVEYS, COLUMBIA OPERATIONS DISCOVERED THE PRESENCE OF NATURAL GAS IN A TEST STATION NEAR THE 100 BLOCK OF PENNSINGER ROAD, GREENCASTLE, PA. GAS MIGRATION STUDIES WERE PERFORMED, AND THE LOCAL DISTRIBUTION COMPANY PERFORMED GAS DETECTION SURVEYS WITHIN THE TWO NEARBY HOMES. NO NATURAL GAS WAS FOUND IN OR AT THE FOUNDATIONS OF EITHER HOME. THE LINE PRESSURE WAS REDUCED TO 100 PSIG TO MAINTAIN LOCAL MARKET. BELL HOLES WERE OPENED ON EITHER SIDE OF PENNSINGER ROAD, AND GAS MIGRATION SURVEYS HAVE BEEN PERFORMED TWICE DAILY SINCE DISCOVERY.   AS OF JULY 7, 2022, PIPELINE 1656 HAS BEEN BLOW DOWN TO 0 PSIG AND 19 LDC CUSTOMERS HAVE BEEN PLACED ON TEMPORARY BOTTLED COMPRESSED NATURAL GAS (CNG) SERVICE. IN ADDITION, A CNG TANKER HAS BEEN PLACED TO KEEP THE TOWN OF HAGERSTOWN, MD IN SERVICE AS NEEDED. CURRENTLY HAGERSTOWN IS BEING SUPPLIED BY AN ALTERNATIVE SUPPLY.   WORK WAS COMPLETED TO REPLACE 232 FEET OF FBE COATED CARBON STEEL PIPE ON JULY 18, 2022. THE PIPELINE HAS BEEN PURGED AND PLACED BACK INTO SERVICE AT 4:49PM EASTERN TIME ON JULY 18, 2022.    THE INCIDENT INVESTIGATION REPORT WAS FINALIZED ON JANUARY 17, 2023.  THE CAUSAL AND ROOT CAUSE FACTORS OF THIS PROCESS EQUIPMENT FAILURE INDICATE GENERAL EXTERNAL CORROSION LED TO A THROUGH WALL ANOMALY RESULTING IN THE RELEASE OF NATURAL GAS. THE CORROSION CAN LIKELY BE ATTRIBUTED TO INSUFFICIENT CATHODIC PROTECTION AT THE LEAK LOCATION OVER THE LIFE SPAN OF THE PIPE. FOR APPROXIMATELY THE FIRST 20 YEARS OF SERVICE THIS SECTION OF BARE STEEL PIPE OPERATED WITH NO CATHODIC PROTECTION IN PLACE.

NARRATIVE: ON JUNE 17, 2022, AT APPROXIMATELY 2:14 A.M. CDT THE LAHASKA REGULATING STATION (MR 78127) BEGAN DRIFTING OPEN. THIS EVENTUALLY RESULTED IN THE PRESSURE EXCEEDING THE DOWNSTREAM MAOP.  AT 5:01 A.M. CDT RELIEF VALVES BEGAN OPENING APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES DOWNSTREAM AT THE LAMBERTVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION (CS).  THE VOLUME RELEASED EXCEEDED THE 3 MMCF PHMSA REPORTING THRESHOLD.  THE UPSTREAM EAGLE CS SERVES TWO PARALLEL LINES, LINE 1 (20-INCH) AND LINE 2 (36 & 20-INCH). THE MAOP OF LINE 1 IS 656 P.S.I.G. AND LINE 2 IS 1000 P.S.I.G. AT THE DISCHARGE OF EAGLE CS. APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES UPSTREAM OF THE LAMBERTVILLE CS THE MAOP OF LINE 2 IS LOWERED TO 656 P.S.I.G. AT MR 78127.      THE CONTROL VALVES AT MR 78127 HAD BEEN SHUT-OFF ON THE PREVIOUS MONDAY, JUNE 13, 2022 FOR PLANNED MAINTENANCE WORK. PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION INDICATES THE CONTROL SUPPLY GAS WAS SHUT OFF DURING THE MAINTENANCE WORK ON THURSDAY JUNE 16, 2022 AND SUPPLY GAS GRADUALLY BLED-OFF OVERNIGHT WHICH ALLOWED THE CONTROL VALVES TO DRIFT OPEN. PRESSURES ON LINE 2 DOWNSTREAM OF MR 78127 REACHED 731 P.S.I.G. OR 111% OF THE 656 P.S.I.G. MAOP (66.7% SMYS) AT LAMBERTVILLE COMPRESSOR STATION. PRESSURES ON SEGMENT OF LINE 1 ALSO EXCEEDED MAOP, BUT LESS THAN ALLOWABLE BUILDUP.  THE EFFECTED SEGMENTS WERE LEAK SURVEYED WITH LEAK DETECTION EQUIPMENT. IN ADDITION, A REVIEW OF ILI DATA CONFIRMED THERE WERE NO ACTIONABLE ANOMALIES AT THE ELEVATED PRESSURES IN THE AFFECTED SEGMENTS. THE EVENT REMAINS UNDER INVESTIGATION.      OCTOBER 11, 2022  PART E11 REGARDING THE INVESTIGATION INTO THE CONTROL ROOMS ACTIONS WAS UPDATED.  PART G APPARENT CAUSE WAS UPDATED  PART H - THE INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THAT CONTROL GAS VALVES WERE LEFT IN THE WRONG POSITION DURING MAINTENANCE ACTIVITY.  PROCEDURES WILL BE UPDATED, AND TRAINING WILL BE PROVIDED TO PREVENT RECURRENCE.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
NARRATIVE: DCP'S TYPE C GAS GATHERING 16-IN 'V' OIL CENTER DISCHARGE PIPELINE RUPTURED AND DCP WAS NOTIFIED OF THE RUPTURE BY THE COUNTY SHERIFF. DCP PERSONNEL RESPONDED PROMPTLY AND ISOLATED THE LINE. THE FAILED PIPE SEGMENT WAS SENT FOR METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS AND RESULTS INDICATED LOCALIZED INTERNAL CORROSION PITTING DUE TO THE PRESENCE OF HYDROGEN SULFIDE IN THE PRODUCT STREAM. DCP'S INITIAL COST ESTIMATE WAS BELOW THE REPORTING THRESHOLD, BUT ACTUAL REMEDIATION COSTS EXCEEDED THE INITIAL ESTIMATE, REQUIRING DCP TO REPORT ONCE FINAL COSTS WERE AVAILABLE. REMEDIATION COST WERE DETERMINED TO MEET INCIDENT REPORTING THRESHOLD ON DECEMBER 16, 2022, AND THE NRC WAS NOTIFIED AT THAT TIME.     THE PIPELINE WAS REPAIRED AND REMAINS OUT OF SERVICE. THE PIPELINE IS AIR GAPPED AND DISCONNECTED FROM ALL SOURCES OF PRODUCT.

NAME: ENERGY TRANSFER COMPANY
STATUS_WHEN_IDENTIFIED: NORMAL OPERATION, INCLUDES PAUSES DURING MAINTENANCE
INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE OPERATOR DID NOT FIND THAT AN INVESTIGATION OF THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WAS NECESSARY DUE TO: (PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION FOR WHY THE OPERATOR DID NOT INVESTIGATE)
INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: INVESTIGATION IDENTIFIED NO CONTROLLER ISSUES. CONTROLLER ACTIONS WERE CONSISTENT WITH COMPANY PROTOCOLS.
NARRATIVE: ON JUNE 2, 2022, THERE WAS A NATURAL GAS RELEASE ON THE 99991001 WTG BOOSTER 10IN TX-WINKLER_BLK B5 SUR 3,8,13,14,17,24, A TYPE C GATHERING LINE LOCATED 4.5 MILES SOUTH OF KERMIT, TX IN WINKLER COUNTY.    THE LINE SEPARATED AND RELEASED 1,667 THOUSAND CUBIC FEET OF NATURAL GAS DUE TO A PIPE FAILURE FROM A DEFECT DUE TO INTERNAL CORROSION.    ON JUNE 14, 2022, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE AND REPAIRS WAS GOING TO EXCEED THE $129,300.00 REPORTING CRITERIA. THIS EVENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC # AND TRRC #    2000 FEET OF PIPE WILL BE REPLACED WITH NEW PIPE AND TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE, OPERATIONS WILL HAVE MEASUREMENT CONDUCT MONTHLY CHECKS ON THE THREE MONITORS FEEDING THE SYSTEM AND LOWER THE PPM HIGH AND HIGH HIGH ALARM SET POINTS.    THE AFFECTED PORTION OF THE PIPE WAS SENT TO THE LAB, WAITING FOR RESULTS.  A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT WILL BE SUBMITTED WITH LAB RESULT FINDINGS.    ON JULY 9, 2022, THE PIPELINE REPLACEMENT WAS COMPLETE AND AT 12:00 THE PIPELINE WAS PUT BACK IN SERVICE.    ON NOVEMBER 2, 2022, THE RESULTS OF THE LAB METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS INDICATED SIGNIFICANT WALL LOSS DUE TO INTERNAL CORROSION.    THE RESULTS OF THE METALLURGICAL ANALYSIS INDICATE THAT THE RUPTURE INITIATED IN THE PIPE BODY AT THE 12:00 ORIENTATION AND TRAVERSED THROUGH THE PIPE FOR 70-IN BEFORE ARRESTING. SIGNIFICANT WALL LOSS FROM INTERNAL CORROSION WAS OBSERVED IN THE TOP HALF OF THE PIPE DUE TO AN UPSET WITH THE PRODUCERS WHICH ALLOWED OXYGEN IN THE PIPE WHICH CREATED THE INTERNAL CORROSION. THE FAILURE WAS NOT ASSOCIATED WITH THE LONGITUDINAL SEAM.     TO PREVENT THIS FROM OCCURRING IN THE FUTURE, OPERATIONS WILL BEGIN PIGGING THIS LINE TWICE A WEEK TO BETTER DISPERSE INJECTED CORROSION INHIBITOR AND ENSURE LINE IS CLEAN.  OXYGEN SENSORS HAVE ALSO BEEN INSTALLED ON THIS LINE TO CLOSELY MONITOR THE OXYGEN CONTENT OF GAS STREAM.    ADDITIONALLY, OUR CHEMICAL COMPANY HAS BEEN INSTRUCTED TO CHECK INSTALLED COUPONS FOR CHEMICAL RESIDUE QUARTERLY AND PERFORM BACTERIA TESTS AND MAKE NECESSARY ADJUSTMENTS AS NEEDED TO THE ONGOING CHEMICAL TREATMENT INJECTION PROGRAM.    THIS SYSTEM HAS ALSO BEEN BUDGETED FOR AN ILI INSPECTION FOR 2023.

NAME: ENABLE GAS TRANSMISSION, LLC
STATUS_WHEN_IDENTIFIED: NORMAL OPERATION, INCLUDES PAUSES DURING MAINTENANCE

RUPTURE_DETAILS: SECTION OF PIPE WAS EJECTED DUE TO RUPTURE. ORIGIN AND INTERACTION WITH SEAM UNKNOW AT THIS TIME. WILL UPDATE AFTER LAB ANALYSIS.
EST_COST_OTHER_DETAILS: LAB COST FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS REPORT.
INTERNAL_INSPECTION_DETAILS: NO LAUNCHERS OR RECEIVERS INSTALLED ON PIPELINE.
ACCIDENT_DETAILS: NOTIFIED BY 3RD PARTY PRODUCER OF A LOW FLOW.
INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE FACILITY WAS NOT MONITORED BY A CONTROLLER(S) AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT
NARRATIVE: ON 5/24/2022 AT APPROXIMATELY 3:30AM, THE COMPANY WAS NOTIFIED BY A PRODUCER OF A LOW PRESSURE FROM AN INPUT TIED TO OT-13. ASSUMING THERE WAS A LEAK, PERSONNEL WERE DISPATCHED BY MANAGEMENT TO ISOLATE THE LINE AND LOCATE ANY POSSIBLE ISSUES. THREE VALVES AND A CHECK VALVE WERE UTILIZED TO ISOLATE THE SECTION WITH THE FINAL VALVE BEING CLOSED AT 6:30AM. AT 7:30AM A RUPTURE WAS FOUND IN A RURAL AREA APPROXIMATELY 1350FT SOUTH OF BEAR CREEK LOOP RD. NEAR BONANZA, ARKANSAS. INTERNAL INCIDENT COMMUNICATIONS WERE MADE, AND THE EVENT WAS DETERMINED TO BE NON-REPORTABLE DUE TO ESTIMATES MADE BY MANAGEMENT AND ENGINEERING SUPPORT FOR THE COST OF REPAIR/CLEAN-UP AND RELEASE VOLUME. AN ENVIRONMENTAL CLEAN-UP CONTRACTOR WAS DISPATCHED TO THE SITE AND ASSESSED ACCESS TO THE LOCATION, TERRAIN, AND THE TIMELINE FOR THE CLEAN-UP. COST ESTIMATES BASED ON PRIOR COMPANY EXPERIENCE REQUIRED SUBSTANTIAL ADJUSTMENTS AFTER INPUT FROM CONTRACTOR. UPON RECEIVING THE UPDATED INFORMATION, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE COST OF REPAIR/CLEAN-UP WOULD EXCEED $122K AND AN IMMEDIATE REPORT WAS MADE TO THE NRC ON 5/25 AT 10:56AM. A 44.5IN SECTION OF PIPE WAS EJECTED DUE TO THE RUPTURE AND THE DAMAGED PIECE WAS COLLECTED AND SENT TO THE LAB FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS ON 5/28. THE LAB HAS ESTIMATED THAT THE FAILURE ANALYSIS REPORT WILL BE COMPLETED BY THE WEEK OF 6/27. THE RUPTURE WAS REMEDIATED BY REPLACEMENT ON 5/26 AND RETURNED TO PARTIAL SERVICE ON 5/27 AT 3:30PM. OT-13 WILL REMAIN AT (200PSI) TO SUPPORT A LOCAL DISTRIBUTION COMPANY (16-20 CUSTOMERS) WITH A RETURN TO NORMAL OPERATING PRESSURE PENDING THE OUTCOME OF FAILURE ANALYSIS AND ADDITIONAL INTEGRITY ASSESSMENT ACTIONS. SUPPLEMENTAL: LAB RESULTS FROM THE INITIAL FAILURE WERE RECEIVED ON 7/18 AND CONCLUDED THE RUPTURED OCCURRED DUE TO EXTERNAL CORROSION. OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT DECIDED TO PRESSURE TEST OT-13 AND MODIFY TO MAKE THE LINE PIGGABLE. DURING A PRESSURE TEST ON 8/23, A SECTION OF THE LINE FAILED AT (35.174671, -94.388078), APPROXIMATELY 3.2 MILES SOUTH OF THE INITIAL FAILURE. AFTER REPAIRS WERE MADE, A SUCCESSFUL PRESSURE TEST WAS COMPLETED ON 9/14. OT-13 WAS RETURNED TO NORMAL OPERATION AT 750PSI ON 9/30. LAB RESULTS FROM THE PRESSURE TEST FAILURE WERE RECEIVED ON 11/11 AND CONCLUDED THAT THE RUPTURE OCCURRED DUE TO EXTERNAL CORROSION.

EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: ESD PNEUMATIC SWITCH FAILED DUE TO DEBRIS ACCUMULATION
NARRATIVE: ON MAY 22, 2022 AT 18:44, COLORADO INTERSTATE GAS CO (CIG) GAS CONTROL RECEIVED AN ALARM INDICATING THAT VENT VALVE VO-26 AT THE WAMSUTTER COMPRESSOR STATION (CS) OPENED. CONSEQUENTLY, GAS CONTROL CONTACTED OPERATIONS PERSONAL REGARDING THE ALARM. AT 19:30, CIG OPERATOR ARRIVED ON SITE AND CONFIRMED DISCOVERY OF THE LEAK. AT 19:34, CIG OPERATOR MANUALLY CLOSED THE VENT VALVE VO-26 AND COMMUNICATED THE DETAILS OF THE EVENT TO HIS SUPERVISOR. CIG INITIATED THE EMERGENCY RESPONSE LINE (ERL) AND DURING THE CALL, THE EVENT WAS DEEMED REPORTABLE.    THE ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THAT THE SUPPLY SIDE OF THE GAS PNEUMATIC ESD SWITCH ON VENT VALVE VO-26, ACCUMULATED DEBRIS ALLOWING THE VALVE TO OPEN INADVERTENTLY. CIG OPERATIONS REPLACED THE SWITCH AND TESTED THE VALVE FOR PROPER OPERATION. THE STATION WAS RETURNED TO SERVICE THE SAME DAY.

INCIDENT_AREA_SUBTYPE: TYPICAL ABOVEGROUND FACILITY PIPING OR APPURTENANCE

INVESTIGATION_STATUS: NO, THE OPERATOR DID NOT FIND THAT AN INVESTIGATION OF THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS OR CONTROL ROOM ISSUES WAS NECESSARY DUE TO: (PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION FOR WHY THE OPERATOR DID NOT INVESTIGATE)
INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: THE OPERATOR DEEMED THE INCIDENT INDEPENDENT OF THE CONTROLLER(S) ACTIONS.
EQ_ADDITIONAL_OTHER_DETAILS: RELIEF VALVE FAILED DUE TO DEBRIS AND FAILED INTERNAL SOFT GOODS.
NARRATIVE: ON MAY 19, 10:40 PM LOCAL TIME, GAS CONTROL RECEIVED A CALL ABOUT A NOISE COMPLAINT ASSOCIATED WITH TGP'S COMPRESSOR STATION NO. 17 (I.E., CS 17). GAS CONTROL CALLED TGP OPS SPECIALIST WHO ARRIVED ON SITE AT 10:44 PM LOCAL TIME TO INVESTIGATE THE NOISE. UPON ARRIVAL THE OPS SPECIALIST NOTICED THAT RELIEVE VALVE NO. 17-4D WAS VENTING NATURAL GAS. CONSEQUENTLY, THE OPERATIONS SPECIALIST CLOSED THE BLOCK VALVE UNDER THE RELIEF VALVE THEREBY STOPPING THE RELEASE AT 10:52 LOCAL TIME. THIS CALCULATED TO BE 1,828 MCF AND WAS DETERMINED TO BE LESS THAN THE PHMSA 3,000 MCF AND NOT REPORTABLE AT THE TIME.  ON JUNE 22, 2022, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT THE TOTAL RELEASE TIME DID NOT INCLUDE THE ACTUAL INITIAL NOTIFICATION WHEN GAS CONTROL RECEIVED THE NOISE COMPLAINT AND THE INITIAL CALCULATED VOLUME UTILIZED THE INCORRECT RELIEF VALVE ORIFICE SIZE.  THEREFORE, THE TOTAL TIME OF THE RELEASE CHANGED FROM 8 MINUTES TO 26 MINUTES AND, THE TOTAL RELEASE VOLUME WITH THE REVISED ORIFICE SIZE EXCEEDED THE REPORTING THRESHOLD. THE NEW GAS LOSS VOLUME CHANGED FROM 1,828 MCF TO 10,814 MCF.  THE EVENT WAS REPORTED TO THE NRC ON JUNE 22, 2022 AT 15:40 PM LOCAL TIME.  THE INVESTIGATION INTO THE RELEASE REVEALED THAT THE RELIEF VALVE FAILED IN THE OPEN POSITION DUE TO DEBRIS AND FAILED SOFT GOODS INTERNAL TO THE PILOT VALVE, WHICH CONTROLS THE RELIEF VALVE. ONCE THE PILOT WAS CLEANED, SERVICED AND REBUILT THE VALVE AND PILOT VALVE WAS SUCCESSFULLY TESTED AND RETURNED TO SERVICE.

NARRATIVE: ON MAY 12TH AT 0555, THE PIPELINE SUPERVISOR SENT OPERATIONS PERSONNEL TO INVESTIGATE A LEAK INDICATION.  AT AROUND 0630, A POSSIBLE GAS LEAK WAS VISUALLY IDENTIFIED AND OPERATIONS PROCEEDED TO ISOLATE THE PIPELINE.   BY 0707, THE DOWNSTREAM AND UPSTREAM MANUAL VALVES WERE CLOSED, ISOLATING THE PIPELINE.  AT 0900, OPERATIONS WAS ABLE TO SAFELY APPROACH THE LEAK SITE AND DETERMINED THE LEAK SIZE AND ORIGIN. BASED ON THE SIZE OF THE LEAK, ENTERPRISE CALCULATED THAT THE RELEASE VOLUME EXCEEDED 3 MMSCF AND THE NRC WAS IMMEDIATELY NOTIFIED.    ON MAY 13TH LINE 15 WAS EXCAVATED AND ENTERPRISE CONFIRMED THE RELEASE WAS DUE TO A FAILURE OF A 3RD PARTY CHECK VALVE CAP. THE CHECK VALVE WAS REMOVED AND THE CONNECTION WAS CAPPED.

INVESTIGATION_STATUS_DETAILS: VISUAL CONFIRMATION IN THE FIELD INDICATED THERE WAS A RUPTURE. THERE WAS NO CONTROL ROOM ERROR INVOLVED IN THE INCIDENT.
NARRATIVE: ON MAY 11, 2022, GAS CONTROL NOTICED AN UNANTICIPATED DROP IN PRESSURE ON LINE S2 AT 11:30 AM AND NOTIFIED LOCAL OPERATIONS AT 11:34 AM.  UPON NOTIFICATION, OPERATIONS IMMEDIATELY BEGAN TO EVALUATE POTENTIAL CAUSES FOR THE DROP IN PRESSURE AND BEGAN MOBILIZING RESOURCES, WHILE ALSO REACHING OUT TO OFFICIALS ALONG THE SECTIONS OF THE PIPELINE.  WITH NO APPARENT OPERATIONAL CAUSE FOR THE PRESSURE DROP, GAS CONTROL TOOK MEASURES CONSISTENT WITH AN ABNORMAL CONDITION AND CLOSED A REMOTE CONTROL VALVE LOCATED AT BUSBY GATE AT 11:43 AM.      AFTER DISCUSSIONS WITH LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT AT 12:14 PM, ATMOS ENERGY PROCEEDED TO THE LOCATION OF THE INCIDENT AND ARRIVED ONSITE AT 12:54 PM. UPON ARRIVAL, ATMOS ENERGY DISCOVERED A PIPE RUPTURE ADJACENT TO LACY PIGGING STATION.      IN ADDITION TO CLOSING THE REMOTE VALVE, LOCAL OPERATIONS CLOSED THE MANUALLY OPERATED VALVE AT THE LACY PIGGING STATION TO FURTHER SEGMENT LINE S2 AND MINIMIZE GAS RELEASE.    BASED UPON INFORMATION ATMOS ENERGY RECEIVED FROM LOCAL OFFICIALS, FIRST RESPONDERS CLOSED ROADS AND EVACUATED RESIDENTS IN THE AREA OUT OF AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION. ATMOS ENERGY DOES NOT HAVE DETAILS ON THAT EVACUATION BUT HAS ESTIMATED A NUMBER IN RESPONSE TO QUESTION A23.    SUPPLEMENTAL:   PART A QUESTION 16, THE LINE WAS NO LONGER IN SERVICE AS OF MAY 11, 2022 AT 11:43 AM CST, AND IS BEING ABANDONED. WHILE THIS LINE IS IN THE PROCESS OF BEING REPLACED, ATMOS ENERGY CUSTOMERS ARE BEING SERVED THROUGH OTHER FACILITIES AND NO CUSTOMERS ARE AFFECTED BY THE ABANDONMENT OF THE LINE. FOR THIS REASON, THE RESTART TIME REFLECTED IN THIS QUESTION IS THE SAME TIME THAT THE LINE WAS SHUT DOWN.    AFTER THE INCIDENT OCCURRED, ATMOS ENERGY FOLLOWED ITS PROCEDURES AND INSPECTED THE PIPE IN QUESTION.  THE METALLURGICAL REPORT RESULTING FROM THE INVESTIGATION CONCLUDED THAT MICROBIOLOGICALLY INFLUENCED CORROSION (MIC) AND CHLORIDE CORROSION WERE THE PRIMARY CORROSION MECHANISMS.
